It requires explicit `--base-url`, `--source-sha`, `--worker-version-id`, and fresh `--out`, verifies exact health before auth, creates exactly one disposable authenticated user plus anonymous contexts, writes only the closed sanitized visual set, and completes its own primary cleanup proof before success. Focused tests exercise every runner branch and sentinel path; script typecheck and full-suite 100% coverage include it before Unit32 freezes HEAD. Unit37 invokes this exact ProcessCommand under logical name `production-visual-dogfood`, then uses the execution-contract-defined `production-visual-qa-review:visual_qa` and `production-visual-design-review:design_visual` AgentCommand prompts against the live site and immutable artifacts; a finding that needs no code may retry Unit37, while a code finding must enter the post-merge successor-task protocol and can never mutate this already-merged branch or edit artifacts in place.
- Visual artifact content is exact and no-extra-fields. `VisualCaptureId` is the ordered 29-member set `guest-home-mobile|guest-home-desktop|guest-home-wide|cook-session-mobile|cook-session-desktop|continue-cooking-mobile|continue-cooking-desktop|saved-controls-mobile|saved-controls-desktop|saved-kitchen-mobile|saved-kitchen-desktop|saved-recipes-mobile|saved-recipes-desktop|tag-authoring-mobile|tag-authoring-desktop|tag-discovery-mobile|tag-discovery-desktop|tag-display-detail-mobile|tag-display-detail-desktop|tag-display-list-mobile|tag-display-list-desktop|tag-display-card-mobile|tag-display-card-desktop|developer-docs-mobile|developer-docs-desktop|api-playground-mobile|api-playground-desktop|mcp-guide-mobile|mcp-guide-desktop`. One immutable registry derives basename `<viewport>-<surface>.png` from each id and maps surfaces to the actual changed route exactly: guest-home/continue-cooking/saved-kitchen -> `home`; cook-session -> `cook_recipe`; saved-controls/tag-display-detail -> `recipe_detail`; saved-recipes -> `saved_recipes`; tag-authoring -> `recipe_edit`; tag-discovery -> `my_recipes`; tag-display-list -> `recipes_index`; tag-display-card -> `user_kitchen`; developer-docs -> `developer_docs` at canonical `/api`; api-playground -> `api_playground` at canonical `/api/playground`; mcp-guide -> `mcp` at `/mcp`. Redirect aliases are tested but do not duplicate screenshots. Mobile is 390x844, desktop 1440x900, wide 1920x1080. Guest-home and mcp-guide use fixture `guest`; developer-docs uses public `guest`; api-playground uses `authenticated` with one saved recipe containing source URL, nonsequential steps, scalable quantity, course, and custom tags. Saved-recipes mobile uses `saved-populated-search` and must show multiple saved results plus the active search/filter state; saved-recipes desktop uses `saved-empty-search` and must show the authenticated no-match state; every other non-display id except tag-display uses `authenticated`; tag-display mobile uses `dense` and desktop uses `empty`. `VisualCapture={id,route,viewport,fixture,screenshot,screenshotSha256,status:'passed',authFormCount:0,passwordInputCount:0,sensitiveTextMatchCount:0}` must match that registry exactly. `VisualReport={version:1,sourceSha,workerVersionId,captures,cleanupOutcomePath:'cleanup-outcome.json'}` uses exact expected source/Worker values and the ordered 29 captures. `VisualMetric={captureId,screenshotSha256,viewportWidth,viewportHeight,scrollWidth,horizontalOverflowPx,overlapCount,occludedControlCount,clippedTextCount,nextSectionVisible}` matches the capture registry; screenshotSha256 must equal that id's VisualCapture hash and decoded PNG bytes, dimensions/scrollWidth/horizontalOverflowPx/counts are nonnegative safe integers, horizontalOverflowPx is `max(0,scrollWidth-viewportWidth)` and at most 1, all three counts are zero, and nextSectionVisible is true exactly for guest-home captures and null otherwise. `VisualMetrics={version:1,metrics}` contains the exact capture order and therefore binds every metric to the validated PNG hash. `absurdity-ledger.md` is exact UTF-8 bytes `# Visual Absurdity Ledger\nversion: 1\nstatus: closed\nready: 0\nreviewer_gated: 0\nfindings: none\n`; no free-form prose is accepted.
- Every screenshot must decode as one PNG with exact registry dimensions, one IHDR/IEND, no bytes after IEND, and no `tEXt`, `zTXt`, `iTXt`, or `eXIf` chunk; malformed dimensions/chunks/CRC/trailing data reject the artifact before hash acceptance. Unit29.2's literal fixtures use tiny deterministic valid registry-dimension PNG builders and corruption cases without importing or executing the later visual runner.
- After the runner atomically closes its exact 38-file tree, form one entry per direct child file with `path` equal to that output-relative basename only (no slash, prefix, task-relative, or absolute form) and sha256 equal to its bytes; sort entries by path using UTF-8/BINARY order. `visualArtifactSetSha256` is SHA-256 over UTF-8 `JSON.stringify` of that exact ordered `{path,sha256}` list with declared field order and no replacer/whitespace. Each visual reviewer runs against that immutable set; reviewer output is accepted only as exact CONVERGED or the canonical sanitized FINDINGS JSON. A FINDINGS verdict writes and hashes `review-findings.v1.json`, then fails its command/stage. On CONVERGED, the executor writes one canonical UTF-8 report with exact eight newline-terminated lines `# Spoonjoy Production Visual Review`, `version: 1`, `role: <visual_qa|design_visual>`, `agent_id: <canonical lowercase UUID>`, `verdict: clean`, `source_sha: <mergeSha>`, `visual_artifact_set_sha256: <sha256>`, `open_findings: 0`. Any extra line/field, invalid agent id, different source/set hash, non-clean verdict, or nonzero finding rejects the report before hashing. This format applies respectively to `visual-qa-review.md` and `visual-design-review.md` and stores no finding text.
- Each primary or recovery cleanup pass has `deadline=started+90_000`, 30 total I/O attempts, nested 20 cook HTTP attempts. Before any attempt fail when `now >= deadline`, total count already 30, or cook count already 20; then increment applicable counters and use timeout `min(10_000,deadline-now)`. Logical operationAttempt starts 1 and increments on retry, resetting only when phase/target changes. Network, 408/425/5xx, and 429 retry; Retry-After is valid only `/^[1-9][0-9]*$/` integer seconds, otherwise absent; delay is valid Retry-After else `min(500*2^(operationAttempt-1),5000)`. Fail before sleep when `now+delay >= deadline`. D1/admin failures retry only the same statuses or exact case-insensitive tokens `rate limit`, `timed out`, `ETIMEDOUT`, `ECONNRESET`, `fetch failed`; malformed/unmatched/nonzero is fatal. Primary/recovery budgets reset independently.
- Cleanup state machine is exact. GET residue 200 must parse the standard envelope and exact diagnostic `data` schema; malformed envelope/data, corruption, or other 4xx is fatal, while retry classes above retry. Cleanup targets only its predeclared purgeTarget. Residue current matching that target issues DELETE; DELETE 202 or either GET/DELETE 409 `session_purging` waits exactly 1000ms, increments operationAttempt without resetting phase/target, and then spends the next bounded I/O attempt on canonical residue GET. The same deadline/attempt guards run before that sleep and GET; no other polling interval is legal. 409 stale_attempt with any other current id and every 404 are fatal identity/proof failures. Residue current null with matching unexpired lastPurged still issues one idempotent confirmation DELETE, which must return bodyless 204; any required-target path passes only after this exact terminal 204, then one final residue. Residue current null with live-state/projection/socket data, missing/mismatched lastPurged, or any DELETE for a non-target is fatal. Final proof requires presentKeys subset exactly `history:v1`, `coordinator:v1`, `replay-tombstones:v1`; history true; current/five live-state keys/sockets zero; coordinator current null with logical expiry; tombstone max expiry at/before coordinator expiry. Capture this retained-DO proof and exact A retirement, require no nonterminal/activeKey D1 row, then use the existing scoped Wrangler D1 command runner to execute one parameterized userId plus retired-attempt-id DELETE against CookSessionIndex only. The command may delete exactly the retired A row and never B/another user/table; it runs under the same bounded D1/admin retry budget, atomically returns changes plus remaining user rows, and is followed by a zero-row query. Record the immutable projection-retirement receipt inside pre-delete proof, then delete user. Visual has no retired attempt, skips mutation, and records the zero/empty receipt. After deletion make no owner DO call; prove User and all CookSessionIndex rows zero and rely on mandatory User-existence auth to make retained metadata inaccessible. Release claims distinguish relational/content-bearing/live-state zero from permanent content-free history plus bounded/logically expiring identifier-bearing idempotency metadata.
- Cleanup runs in finally. Primary failure records phase/evidence, waits exactly 5 seconds only if `now+5000 < deadline` (otherwise starts recovery immediately), then one recovery with fresh budgets. Recovery checks user existence first. Absent user makes zero owner calls, reuses immutable pre-delete DO proof and the prepared user-delete mutation journal, writes the truthful reconciled user-delete proof only when the original response was lost, then rechecks user absence plus all-D1-zero and post-delete evidence. Present user repeats the state machine, including projection-response reconciliation. Exact flags remain: clean functional+primary exits zero; functional failure with clean primary exits nonzero/residueClean true; recovered cleanup exits nonzero/residueClean+cleanupRecovered true; two failures residueClean false/cleanupIncomplete true.

**CookSessionIndex Projection Schema**
- D1 model `CookSessionIndex` is progress-free: `attemptId String @id`, `userId String`, `recipeId String`, nullable unique `activeKey String?`, `status String`, `recipeFingerprint String`, `recipeTitle String`, `recipeCoverImageUrl String?`, `revision Int`, `projectionSeq Int`, `lastProjectionId String @unique`, `startedAt DateTime`, `updatedAt DateTime`, `completedAt DateTime?`, and `abandonedAt DateTime?`; user/recipe foreign keys cascade on hard delete. Indexes are `(userId,status,updatedAt)`, `(userId,recipeId,startedAt)`, and `(recipeId)` plus the unique active key/projection id constraints. Active-step, scale, checked-count, checklist, and recipe-snapshot fields are forbidden.
- Persisted queue entries are a discriminated union. UPSERT is `{ version: 1, projectionId: "<attemptId>:<sequence>:UPSERT", attemptId, sequence, kind: "UPSERT", payload }`, where payload is exactly `{ userId, recipeId, status: "ACTIVE"|"COMPLETED"|"ABANDONED", recipeFingerprint, recipeTitle, recipeCoverImageUrl: string|null, revision, startedAt, updatedAt, completedAt: string|null, abandonedAt: string|null }`. DELETE is `{ version: 1, projectionId: "<attemptId>:<sequence>:DELETE", attemptId, sequence, kind: "DELETE", payload: { userId, recipeId } }`; PURGING is never UPSERTed. Sequence/revision fields are nonnegative integers, fingerprints/ids/status/timestamp nullability match the snapshot, and every timestamp emitted by current code is canonical `Date.toISOString()` text. Initial create sequence/revision is 0; progress/terminal UPSERT carries only resulting revision plus metadata; purge DELETE uses current revision + 1. Every enqueue, dequeue, lifecycle load, and alarm validates the exact wrapper plus every entry's version, fields, projectionId components, immutable owner/recipe/attempt, sequence/payload revision equality for UPSERT, timestamp/status shape, and FIFO cross-entry invariants. Any malformed persisted wrapper/head/tail remains byte-for-byte queued and has the exact fail-closed precedence above; `alarm()` throws and nothing is dropped or sent to D1. Against an existing D1 row, matching `lastProjectionId` is an exact no-op; greater sequence applies; lower sequence is a no-op; equal sequence with a different projectionId is projection corruption, throws, and never overwrites/deletes. A missing-row UPSERT applies; a missing-row DELETE replay is a no-op. A greater DELETE removes the row; lower is a no-op; equal-different is corruption. Applied UPSERT sets activeKey only for ACTIVE and records lastProjectionId. FIFO orders old terminal, new active, and purge across attempts.
- Queue-wide invariants are exact: length is `0..1_024`; projectionId is unique; every payload userId/recipeId equals the deterministic object's owner/recipe; each attempt occupies at most one contiguous block; and within each block entries retain append order with strictly increasing sequence, at most one DELETE, DELETE last, and no UPSERT after DELETE. The first still-queued sequence need not be zero because successful heads are deleted. Old-attempt blocks precede later-attempt blocks, including old terminal before new create; validation never re-sorts.
- Projection queue capacity is both 1,024 entries and 1,500,000 canonical UTF-8 bytes, with the final slot and enough bytes for one exact current-attempt DELETE reserved exclusively for purge. Before any ordinary append, serialize the candidate queue plus a synthetic DELETE using the current owner/recipe/attempt and maximum legal sequence `2_147_483_647`; acceptance requires current length `< 1,023` and that reserved wrapper remain within the byte limit. Otherwise return `503 projection_backlog`, `Retry-After: 60`, and record no state/ledger/revision. ACTIVE resume/same-terminal no-op/exact replay append nothing and remain available. A previously accepted queue therefore guarantees purge can append at length at most 1,023, yielding at most 1,024; an existing purge DELETE is reused, and no other transition can append while PURGING. A structurally valid but already oversized queue is corruption/500 rather than capacity. Successful alarm dequeue reopens count/byte capacity; no entry is evicted or coalesced.
- Every start/progress/terminal/purge transition that appends a projection calls top-level SQLite DO storage writes plus `setAlarm(Date.now())` inside the same explicit `ctx.storage.transaction`; no external I/O occurs inside. Alarm and state/ledger/revision/queue commit atomically. `setAlarm` failure aborts everything, records no mutation id, and returns `500 internal_error`, so client retry is fresh. The first alarm is immediate. One FIFO-gated alarm invocation validates all persisted state, then independently runs every due channel: it processes at most 32 projection heads sequentially and, while current is PURGING, performs one complete socket sweep when the socket channel is due. For each projection head it performs exactly one idempotent AtomicSqlPort batch, then one DO transaction verifies/removes that exact unchanged head and clears only `retry-state.projection`; it may continue through head 32. A socket sweep attempts PURGING send then close for every attached socket independently; zero remaining sockets clears only `retry-state.sockets`, while any thrown operation or remaining socket advances only that channel. Projection and socket work due in the same alarm are both attempted even when the other fails. After both channels, PURGING finalization runs only when its DELETE barrier is gone and the socket set is empty. The next alarm is immediate when a projection queue remains after 32, otherwise the minimum of both retry channels and coordinator/tombstone metadata deadlines; no remaining work deletes the alarm. D1 success followed by DO-commit failure leaves the head and relies on D1 replay no-op under Cloudflare alarm retry.
- Projection and socket channels use independent consecutive-failure transitions. First through fifth failures for that channel persist `failures=1..5` and delays 2/4/8/16/32 seconds; when its stored failures is already five, every later failure keeps five and delays 60 seconds indefinitely. A successful projection head clears only projection retry before a later head can establish a fresh sequence; a successful socket sweep that reaches zero clears only socket retry. Initial purge fanout that leaves/throws on any socket establishes socket failure one even if projection work also fails. Each head-removal transaction is already durable before the next head; the final retry-state/alarm transaction is computed from the post-work queue/socket state and atomically updates both channels plus the one minimum alarm. If that final transaction fails, prior successful head removals and their per-head projection-channel clears remain valid; the thrown alarm retries from the newly persisted queue and last committed retry state without inventing either channel. Card GET rearming is repair only, never the sole creation path. Unit13 tests all due/not-due cross-products, independent counter/reset behavior, minimum-alarm precedence, socket-only PURGING retries, and finalization after both barriers clear.

**SavedRecipe**
- `SavedRecipe(userId, recipeId, createdAt, syncUpdatedAt)` uses composite primary key `(userId, recipeId)`, required foreign keys to User and Recipe with `ON DELETE CASCADE`, `createdAt NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, required canonical `syncUpdatedAt`, and indexes `(userId, createdAt, recipeId)` and `(userId, syncUpdatedAt, recipeId)`. `NativeSyncClock(accountId,lastIssuedAt)` has a cascading User relation and supplies the monotonic account floor. Explicit unsave hard-deletes only SavedRecipe while its owning atomic caller writes the native-sync tombstone.
- Migration `0027_saved_recipes.sql` backfills each active `(Cookbook.authorId, RecipeInCookbook.recipeId)` once with `createdAt = MIN(RecipeInCookbook.createdAt)` across that author's matching cookbook memberships and `syncUpdatedAt` allocated from the normalized complete account high-water. It uses an explicit `JOIN Recipe ON Recipe.id = RecipeInCookbook.recipeId WHERE Recipe.deletedAt IS NULL`, conflict-ignore semantics, and no replacement/deletion. `addedById` does not determine ownership. Rerunning is harmless and preserves the first saved chronology and sync timestamp.
- Adding cookbook membership through `recipe-detail.server.ts`, `cookbooks.$id.tsx`, `spoonjoy-api.server.ts`, or `api-v1.server.ts` uses one shared D1-safe helper that submits membership create/upsert and SavedRecipe upsert together through one AtomicSqlPort batch; Prisma transactions and independent side writes are forbidden for this helper. Removing membership never unsaves; explicit unsave never removes cookbook membership. Re-save is idempotent. Soft-deleted recipes remain referenced but are hidden until restored.
- `/saved-recipes` and owner-only My Kitchen sections use SavedRecipe. My Kitchen shows at most 12 active saves ordered `(SavedRecipe.createdAt DESC, recipeId DESC)` plus a link to `/saved-recipes`; the dedicated route remains complete. Saved search is a viewer-scoped SQL path: join/EXISTS SavedRecipe before ordering, never post-filter an already-limited global result. A blank query returns every active saved recipe ordered `(SavedRecipe.createdAt DESC, recipeId DESC)`. A nonblank query applies the existing recipe FTS parser, intersects before ordering, returns every match ordered by existing BM25 rank, then title `COLLATE NOCASE`, then recipeId; the web route introduces no limit or pagination. Tests exceed the global 50-result maximum to prove completeness. Private save state is never written to global `SearchDocument` metadata or duplicate documents.
- Web save uses the existing `POST /recipes/:id` FormData action with exact intent `saveRecipe` or `unsaveRecipe`; recipe id comes only from the route. Before dispatch, inspect all entries: if any `intent` entry is either save value, the body is valid only when it contains that one string entry and nothing else; mixed-order `intent=other&intent=saveRecipe`, repeated save values, an extra key, a File, or blank returns bodyless `400`. When no save value appears, existing non-save intents retain current field handling, but a missing, blank, or otherwise unknown first intent returns bodyless `400` after authentication. It requires cookie auth through existing `requireUserId(request, "/login", env)`, so an unauthenticated POST throws `302 Location: /login?redirectTo=/recipes/<id>` before parsing the body. Success is JSON `200 { ok: true, intent, recipeId, saved }` with `Cache-Control: private, no-store`, `Pragma: no-cache`, and `Vary: Cookie`; it calls idempotent save/unsave without a client mutation id. Missing/soft-deleted recipe is bodyless `404`; already desired state is 200. The fetcher disables only this control while pending, does not optimistically flip, updates only when the response intent and recipeId match its submitted command, and on a route error, network failure, malformed/mismatched 200 body, or non-200 preserves prior state with inline `Couldn't update saved recipes.` plus Retry of the same idempotent intent. Cookbook controls are unaffected.
- Public v1 adds exact private authenticated routes `GET /api/v1/me/saved-recipes` requiring `kitchen:read` and `PUT|DELETE /api/v1/me/saved-recipes/{recipeId}` requiring `kitchen:write`. Existing cookie-session and bearer principals are accepted through the existing v1 principal resolver; absent/insufficient principals use existing errors, and every response uses private no-store. GET accepts at most one limit/cursor; repeated, blank cursor, or blank/invalid limit is validation error. Limit defaults 20/max50 and order is createdAt DESC, recipeId DESC. Cursor is unpadded `v1.<base64url>` using `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`; decoded UTF-8 must be byte-exact `JSON.stringify({ createdAt, recipeId })` with only those keys/order, canonical Date.toISOString round-trip, nonempty recipeId, and raw re-encoding equality. Padding, invalid UTF-8/alphabet, noncanonical JSON/ISO, or extra/missing/type-invalid keys is invalid. Predicate is strict `createdAt < cursor.createdAt OR (createdAt = cursor.createdAt AND recipeId < cursor.recipeId)` independent of cursor-row existence. Query requests limit+1; return first limit, hasMore iff extra row, and nextCursor from last returned row only when hasMore, else null. Response cursor echoes accepted raw or null. Recipes are active viewer saves with isSaved true.
- PUT and DELETE on that exact mutation path use existing v1 idempotency and accept `clientMutationId` from JSON body, then `X-Client-Mutation-Id`, then query string, in that precedence order; unknown body fields are rejected. Both return `200` with `{ recipeId, saved: true|false, mutation: { clientMutationId, replayed: false } }`; existing idempotency replay changes only `replayed` to true while retaining the recorded status/body. PUT of an already-saved recipe and DELETE of an already-unsaved active recipe are successful. Missing or soft-deleted recipes return `404`; unsave never changes cookbook membership.
- Personalized REST recipe `isSaved` is a separate optional projection privilege, never an implication of public recipe access. Cookie-session and trusted environment principals receive their user's boolean. A bearer principal receives a boolean only when its normalized granted scopes contain exact `kitchen:read`; `public:read`, `recipes:read`, `kitchen:write` without `kitchen:read`, and every other read-capable scope receive null and perform no SavedRecipe lookup. Invalid tokens and principals lacking the endpoint's ordinary recipe-read scope retain existing errors. Every valid authenticated request, including a public-only bearer whose value is null, is `private, no-store` with Pragma and deduplicated `Vary: Cookie, Authorization`; only a request with no principal uses the existing public Cache-Control/ETag behavior.

**RecipeTag**
- `RecipeTag` is exact: `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL` / Prisma `String @id @default(cuid())`; required `recipeId`, `label`, `normalizedLabel`, `kind`, `source`, `createdById`; and required `createdAt`/`updatedAt` defaulting to `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`updatedAt @updatedAt` in Prisma). `recipeId` references Recipe with `ON DELETE CASCADE`; `createdById` references User with `ON DELETE CASCADE`. SQL CHECKs constrain kind to `COURSE|CUSTOM` and source to `MANUAL`. Unique `(recipeId, normalizedLabel)` enforces normalized identity in addition to the partial one-COURSE index. Supporting indexes are `(recipeId, kind, normalizedLabel, id)`, `(kind, normalizedLabel, recipeId)`, and `(createdById)`. Hard-deleting either parent removes tags; soft-deleting a recipe preserves but hides them.
- `kind` is `COURSE` or `CUSTOM`; `source` is `MANUAL` in this release. Only the recipe owner may mutate tags. Manual tags are canonical immediately and have no acceptance transition.
- Course is optional/singular with values main/side/appetizer/dessert; a CUSTOM normalizedLabel equal to one of those four is reserved and invalid. Before normalization reject any unpaired UTF-16 high/low surrogate; never replacement-normalize it. Custom display canonicalization uses exactly whitespace U+0009..000D, U+0020, U+0085, U+00A0, U+1680, U+2000..200A, U+2028, U+2029, U+202F, U+205F, U+3000; U+FEFF is preserved. Strip leading/trailing members, collapse each interior maximal run to ASCII space, preserve all else/casing, enforce 1..40 Unicode scalar values, then `display.normalize("NFKC").toLowerCase()`. Scalar comparator iterates well-formed code points, compares numeric values, then shorter sequence, never localeCompare; SQL uses BINARY. Tests include unpaired `\uD800`/`\uDC00` rejection, `A< U+FEFF < B` preservation, composed/decomposed NFKC identity, supplementary U+1F600 ordering after BMP U+FFFF, and prefix-before-longer ordering.
- Database invariants are exact. Partial unique index permits one COURSE. BEFORE INSERT and BEFORE UPDATE OF `kind,normalizedLabel,recipeId` triggers raise literal `recipe_tag_custom_limit` only when NEW is a distinct CUSTOM and the target recipe already has ten other CUSTOM rows (UPDATE excludes OLD.id); duplicate conflict-ignore never trips the limit. AFTER INSERT/DELETE and actual UPDATE of label/normalizedLabel/kind/source set parent Recipe.updatedAt to SQL `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`; same-second equality is legal because RecipeTag contentHash, not parent time alone, drives search freshness. Validation maps course cardinality/value to `Choose one valid course.`, malformed/length custom to `Custom tags must be 1 to 40 characters and use well-formed Unicode.`, reserved labels to `Custom tags cannot use a course name.`, and >10 to `Add no more than 10 custom tags.` Recipe create/edit return status 400 exact `{ errors:{ tags:<message> }, submittedTags:{ course:string, customTags:string[] } }`. Unique/limit race throws `RecipeTagConflictError` and returns 409 same shape with `Tags changed at the same time. Review and try again.` Submitted raw rows/casing are preserved; no partial write.
- Tag mutations and new recipe creation use current-state declarative SQL through one AtomicSqlPort batch. Full replacement deletes absent custom/course rows first, inserts each desired normalized row with conflict-ignore to preserve the row/casing that exists at execution time, and then applies the recipe update; new recipe creation inserts Recipe plus desired tag/course rows in that same port batch. Prisma nested creates and Prisma transactions are forbidden. D1 commit order is serialization order: simultaneous full replacements may both succeed; the later commit owns complete normalized membership/course, while a normalized row retained across both commits keeps the casing/id present at the later transaction's execution. A normalized row deleted by one transaction and newly inserted by a later transaction uses that later submitted casing. There is no unowned delta add/remove API. Tests force both replacement orders, retained-overlap casing, delete/re-add casing, rollback, response loss, and injected unique/limit trigger conflicts mapped to the declared 409.
- Migration `0028_recipe_tags.sql` also creates raw-SQL singleton `SearchRebuildLease(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(id = 'current'), lease TEXT NULL, expiresAtMs INTEGER NULL)` and conflict-ignores row `('current',NULL,NULL)`; Unit 24 applies it once and Unit 27 never rewrites it. Acquisition conditionally sets lowercase UUID and `now+60000` where null/expired and succeeds on one changed row; release matches UUID. Immediately before replacement capture one guardNowMs. Every delete/insert/metadata statement uses `lease=<uuid> AND expiresAtMs>guardNowMs`; no later clock enters the transaction, so all apply or all no-op.
- Accepted tags project exactly into SearchDocument: recipe metadata adds course plus custom display labels, body appends those labels in normalizedLabel/id order, and title/subtitle remain unchanged. RecipeTag source content hash is exact `sha256:<lowercase hex>` over UTF-8 `JSON.stringify(rows)` where rows come from active-recipe tags ordered `recipeId BINARY, kind BINARY, normalizedLabel BINARY, id BINARY` and each object has exact key order `{ id, recipeId, label, normalizedLabel, kind, source }`. The Search source fingerprint adds RecipeTag count/latestAt plus that hash; createdById/timestamps are excluded because they do not shape documents.
- One-statement structural completeness uses expected key CTE `active Recipe -> (recipe,id) UNION ALL Cookbook -> (cookbook,id) UNION ALL User -> (chef,id) UNION ALL active ShoppingListItem -> (shopping-list-item,id)` and grouped actual `(entityType,entityId)` from SearchDocument. The single returned row contains exact `expectedCount,actualRowCount,actualDistinctKeyCount,missingCount,extraCount,duplicateKeyCount,currentMetadataCount,metadataDocumentCount`. Complete means expected=actual rows=actual distinct, missing/extra/duplicate zero, currentMetadataCount one, and metadataDocumentCount=actualRowCount; query/table/type failure is incomplete. This checks key-set structure, not deliberate cached content corruption when sources are unchanged.
- Query algorithm is deterministic. Read source fingerprint plus that one-statement completeness. Complete+fresh queries immediately. Complete+stale attempts lease once at t=0; on failed acquisition because another unexpired holder exists, query old complete immediately. Incomplete attempts lease at t=0; after each failed acquisition wait exactly 25,50,100,200,400,800,1600ms, then run completeness first (query immediately if complete), then attempt lease again. After the 1600ms cycle, another failure with incomplete index throws `SearchIndexUnavailableError`; `recipes._index.tsx` and `search.tsx` return 503 text `Search is temporarily unavailable.`, v1 returns `503 service_unavailable` same message, and every MCP search operation returns retryable same text. Unit27.1 owns all consumers and response tests.
- A holder runs at most three cycles. Each cycle reads a source fingerprint, builds, executes fixed-guard atomic replacement, then reads fresh-time lease ownership, source fingerprint, and one-statement completeness in that order. Owned+matching+complete may query, then best-effort release; release failure is logged and natural 60s expiry remains, but response is valid. Source changed while still owner starts next cycle. Lost/expired lease or a failed guarded transaction becomes non-holder: if one-statement check is complete, query that old/new complete index immediately; if incomplete, follow remaining wait/acquisition schedule. After three source-change cycles, query any complete index or throw unavailable. Statement/lease/release exceptions follow the same complete-index fallback; no request queries an incomplete index. Direct REST projections remain immediate; anonymous cache policy remains.
- Discovery filters use at most one `course` and repeated `tag`. Course absent or one exact `course=` means no filter (All emits empty); one of four means filter; repeated course even empty or any other value is 400. Raw empty tag and a tag that becomes empty solely through the exact whitespace canonicalizer are ignored. Nonempty malformed-surrogate/overlength/reserved invalid tag is 400. Valid tags normalize/dedupe/sort/cap ten. Filter controls emit empty course for All and canonical display labels for tags. Search/scope/course/tags combine AND before rank/limit; unfiltered ordering stays.
- My Recipes, owner My Kitchen recipe results, and Search use one explicit GET filter form. Course is a native/menu select with `All courses` plus the four values. Custom tags are enumerated checkboxes, not free-form: options are distinct canonical CUSTOM tags on active recipes visible to that route/owner scope before q/course/tag filtering, grouped by normalizedLabel, choosing the lowest tag id's display label and sorted by normalizedLabel then id. Search shows controls for `scope=all|recipes`; supplied filters under another scope are valid but yield no non-recipe matches. `Apply filters` submits; controls never auto-submit. Forms preserve existing `q`, Search `scope`, and every unrelated query parameter as hidden fields while replacing course/tag. Removing one selected chip preserves all other parameters and tags; `Clear filters` removes only course/tag; route-level Clear Search removes q but preserves course/tag/scope. My Recipes Previous/Next links clone the current query in original pair order, preserve `q`, `course`, every repeated `tag` including its order, and every unrelated parameter, replace all existing `page` entries with one target page when target >1, and omit `page` when target is 1. At most ten boxes may be selected; the eleventh is disabled and server validation remains authoritative.
- Integrated main's My Recipes path remains bounded in SQLite. `searchMyRecipes` keeps its owner/deletedAt boundary, 50-row page size, one-row lookahead, `updatedAt DESC,id DESC` order, and existing q semantics across title/description/servings/owner username/ingredient name. Course/custom-tag predicates are added to that SQL before order/limit/offset; combined q/course/tag requests may not materialize the owner's corpus or call the legacy batched ingredient loader. Visible tag options use a separate owner-scoped pre-q/course/tag query. `test/lib/my-recipes-search.server.test.ts` and `test/routes/my-recipes-query-boundary.test.ts` are permanent regression oracles for outgoing SQL/arguments, pagination, privacy, and the no-unbounded-query boundary.
- Every task edit to `app/routes/_index.tsx` preserves integrated main's guest landing contract: the brand signal/headline/support copy remain visible, the `Collect` continuation remains inside the first viewport, the hero is at most viewport height minus 128px, the headline precedes rather than overlaps continuation content, and there is no horizontal overflow at 390x844, 1440x900, or 1920x1080. `e2e/home-hero-viewport.spec.ts` is the executable oracle and joins the final discovery visual gate plus the full release E2E run.
- Recipe create/edit forms submit exactly one `course` field (`""` means null; missing/repeated/invalid is validation error) and repeated `customTag` fields. Blank custom rows are omitted; first occurrence wins display casing; normalized duplicates collapse; parsed canonical tags sort by normalized label then submitted index. Parse/validate all fields before writes. Create uses one nested Recipe create with nested canonical tag creates. Edit passes the desired set to the current-state declarative transaction: delete rows absent from the desired normalized set, conflict-ignore desired inserts so execution-time existing ids/casing win, and apply the recipe update last. Any failure rolls back recipe and every tag change; validation returns HTTP 400 and trigger races return the exact HTTP 409 tag-conflict response with submitted course/custom rows and no partial write.

**Work Suite Ownership And Baseline**
- The Desk task is adopted before further reviewer passes at exact `/Users/arimendelow/desk/spoonjoy-v2/clem-feedback-e2e/task.md`; artifacts root exists at its `artifacts/`. Frontmatter is schema 1, title/task `clem-feedback-e2e`, track `spoonjoy-v2`, requester/validator `ari`, repo `spoonjoy-v2` local path `~/Projects/spoonjoy-v2-clem-feedback`, and one active adopted iteration. Before initial Work Doer entry, `planning_complete` remains false and task/track remain `drafting` while the doing-doc reviewer chain is open; the final Work Planner convergence handoff alone sets doing status `READY_FOR_EXECUTION`, task `planning_complete:true`, task/track `processing`, exact reviewed/gate metadata, and byte-equal mirrors, then commits and pushes both repos. If Unit0's mandatory fetch later discovers main drift, its planner detour marks planning `NEEDS_REVIEW` and doing `drafting` but leaves the already-started Desk task/track `processing` with `planning_complete:true`; reconvergence restores `approved`/`READY_FOR_EXECUTION` and reviewed/gate metadata before Unit0 restarts. Unit0 never performs a lifecycle transition itself. The task body records branch, source/planning/doing paths, and reviewed commit; track.md has the same task/status row. “Match” means all those exact values and existing paths. Every later lifecycle transition likewise updates Desk task/doing/track and is committed/pushed before downstream work.
- Through Unit31, the active Desk iteration at `spoonjoy-v2/2026-07-15-adopted/` is a synchronized mirror, not a competing authority: `feedback-source.md`, `planning.md`, `doing.md`, and `continuity.md` must be byte-equal after every reviewer checkpoint to exact repository files `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-clem-feedback-source.md`, `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-planning-clem-feedback-e2e.md`, `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-doing-clem-feedback-e2e.md`, and `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-doing-clem-feedback-e2e/AUTOPILOT-STATE.md`. Until the explicit Unit32 transfer, that task-local AUTOPILOT file is the sole Clem continuity authority; repository-root `AUTOPILOT-STATE.md` belongs to audit-remediation and must not be read, mirrored, or changed by this task. Unit0a verifies those four exact `diff -q` comparisons plus task active-iteration path, reviewed-content checkpoint, current gate, and track row before any dependency setup or red test; mismatch blocks execution until both repos are synchronized, committed, and pushed. At Unit32, before freezing HEAD, the final tracked continuity commit records that implementation Units0-31 are complete and transfers release continuity to task-root `release-evidence.v1.json` plus adapter-created `release-continuity.v1.json`; Desk task.md remains the lifecycle locator and active iteration continuity.md remains the frozen transfer source. From successful transfer onward release-continuity JSON intentionally diverges, is committed/pushed after every terminal StageRun, and satisfies Work Merger's Arc/AUTOPILOT update requirement. The tracked task-local AUTOPILOT, doing status/emojis/checklists, and planning checklist remain immutable release snapshots after `BRANCH_HEAD_SHA` capture; generic Work Doer/Work Merger tracked-progress writes are explicitly routed to the external Desk equivalents so they cannot mutate the tested SHA. The task's preserved pre-adoption bundle uses `../_planning/` from the task directory and is historical only.
- The first successful Unit34 PR ensure triggers the identity task's required `processing -> validating` transition before review/merge, crash-journaled across both repos by active-root `desk-validating.attempt.v1.json`. `CancellationCandidate={remoteWinnerSha,localCandidateSha,convergenceSha}` is exact. `DeskValidatingAttempt={version:1,identitySha256,task,prUrl,lifecycleAt,productHeadBeforeSha,productDoingPath,deskDoingPath,taskPath,trackPath,productDoingBeforeSha256,productDoingDoneSha256,deskDoingBeforeSha256,deskDoingDoneSha256,taskBeforeSha256,taskDoneSha256,trackTargetRowBeforeSha256,trackTargetRowDoneSha256,phase,productCommitSha,cancellationCandidates,cancellationObservedRemoteSha,startedAt,updatedAt}` requires task and every path byte-equal ReleaseTaskIdentity; phase is `prepared|cancelling|product_done|cancelled`, immutable fields frozen, and cancellation candidates append-only at most eight. Done transforms repository doing READY_FOR_EXECUTION->VALIDATING with PR, byte-equal Desk doing, task processing->validating with PR, and only that identity's track row while preserving unrelated rows. Executor pushes prepared, product doing commit, product_done checkpoint, then Desk task/doing/track and removes attempt. Recovery accepts only predecessor or one unique direct doing-transform child; a competing doing-preserving advance enters recorded winner-tree cancellation. Wrong identity, multiple child, changed doing, wrong ancestry/tree, or unprovable index fails closed. Cancelled consumes attempt/supersedes run and loops Unit32-33; product_done freezes the commit. After transfer repository doing is immutable, with final Desk derivative owned by DeskArchive.
- Current-main integration is a pre-Unit0 gate, not deferred until release convergence. This plan has merged and re-audited `origin/main@1bea760b`: `edf22ce1`'s dual Apple OAuth callback/env/deployment contract remains unchanged, `42267511`'s bounded My Recipes SQL search/pagination is extended rather than replaced, and `2f392840`'s guest hero viewport-reveal contract is preserved. It also retains `b07d787e`'s browser+APIRequest candidate-readiness repair and `dcf296bd`'s redirect-safe CDP routing plus side-effect-free browser POST mutation-channel probe. New overlap is explicit: `405d6149` keeps cover schema/orchestration in `recipe-cover-schema.server.ts` and `recipe-cover-service.server.ts`, with REST/MCP adapters importing that service and no duplicated cover wrappers; every task group that edits either adapter runs `test/lib/recipe-cover-service-architecture.test.ts` as a must-remain-green test in its red and verification commands. `1bea760b` keeps `scripts/advisory-scan.ts` measured and typed, preserves the canonical `advisory` CI job plus production prerequisite, and remains covered by its script/workflow tests. Unit0a first fetches `origin/main`; if that exact ref is not already an ancestor of HEAD, it performs no red test or implementation: merge the fetched ref into this agent branch, re-audit every overlap, update planning/doing/continuation state, and repeat fresh Quality plus alternating Scrutiny convergence before restarting Unit0a. Unit32 repeats integration for release-time drift.
- `scripts/cleanup-local-qa-data.mjs` adds mutually exclusive `--assert-clean`: it runs the dry-run target query, requires every exact `{item,count}` row including blockers to parse once with nonnegative safe integer count zero, and exits nonzero on unknown arguments, `--apply` combination, any positive/missing/duplicate/malformed row, or command error. It never mutates D1/R2. Plain dry-run remains inspection-only and is never release proof. Unit0a first adds the complete red local/QA/production parser/exit/nonmutation tests and Unit0b implements the oracle before any baseline invokes it; Unit31 only verifies the already-implemented broad oracle alongside smoke's additional exact per-user DO/R2 proof.
- Fresh-worktree dependency setup for Unit0a is exactly `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; `pnpm prisma:generate`; it is setup, not a product assertion, and may not change tracked files. After Unit0b makes the cleanup oracle green, Unit0c performs baseline setup/assertions in this exact order: `DATABASE_URL="file:./test.db?connection_limit=1&socket_timeout=60" pnpm prisma:push`; `pnpm exec wrangler d1 migrations apply DB --local`; `pnpm cleanup:qa -- --assert-clean`; `pnpm run typecheck`; `pnpm run build`; exact `pnpm exec vitest run test/routes/shopping-list-route.test.ts test/routes/api-v1-shopping-mutations.test.ts test/lib/mcp/spoonjoy-tools.server.test.ts test/routes/recipes-id.test.tsx test/routes/index.test.tsx test/routes/my-recipes.test.tsx test/routes/my-recipes-query-boundary.test.ts test/lib/my-recipes-search.server.test.ts test/lib/search.server.test.ts test/routes/api-v1-recipes.test.ts test/components/navigation/mobile-nav.test.tsx test/lib/apple-oauth.server.test.ts test/lib/env.server.test.ts test/lib/oauth-route.server.test.ts test/routes/auth-apple.test.ts test/routes/redwood-functions-auth-oauth.test.ts test/lib/recipe-cover-service-architecture.test.ts test/lib/recipe-cover-service.server.test.ts test/scripts/advisory-scan.test.ts test/release-workflow-security.test.ts`; and exact `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/home-hero-viewport.spec.ts`. Schema absence is setup, not a product regression. No tracked change is allowed from dependency/schema generation.
- Unit32's exact local sequence is: `pnpm cleanup:qa -- --assert-clean`; `pnpm prisma:generate`; `DATABASE_URL="file:./test.db?connection_limit=1&socket_timeout=60" pnpm prisma:push`; `pnpm exec wrangler d1 migrations apply DB --local` twice; `pnpm run api:playground:generate`; `git diff --exit-code -- app/lib/generated/api-v1-playground.ts`; `pnpm run typecheck:scripts`; `pnpm run typecheck`; `pnpm run test:coverage`; `pnpm run test:workers:coverage`; `pnpm run test:e2e`; `pnpm run build`; `pnpm cleanup:qa -- --assert-clean`; `git diff --exit-code`; `git diff --cached --exit-code`; and require empty `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all`. The second migration run must report no unapplied migration. Every command has an individual manifest attempt/log and zero warnings.
- Invoke `work-merger` exactly once at Unit34, passing this doing doc and branch. That single full-workflow invocation owns checkpoints Units34-37 through PR, CI, merge, automatic deploy, smoke, cleanup, Desk closure, and worktree cleanup. Unit36 is a checkpoint inside the same invocation, never a second work-merger call. If main advances, the same invocation integrates it and loops Units32-35 as specified.
- Release convergence begins only after the feature units are complete. It may run `git fetch origin main` but never `git pull`; when `origin/main` advances, merge that exact fetched commit into the agent branch, resolve there, and rerun the frozen gates. The canonical local `main` branch remains untouched and no inbound integration enters it before GitHub performs the protected merge. This is the release-phase implementation of the repository's local-to-GitHub one-way rule.
- `test/config/clem-feedback-boundaries.test.ts` is the automated source-boundary oracle. It parses the registered app routes plus first-party root/navigation/recipe-index/new/detail/edit/My Recipes/My Kitchen surfaces and their rendered link/form/action labels, and fails on any user-facing web import route/control/action, AI tag suggestion/control/contract, `Pebble` identifier/copy/branch, or mobile-dock route change outside the frozen baseline. Developer/API/agent import surfaces are explicitly allowed. Unit29 runs this oracle and records the source-row disposition map; a manual diff inspection supplements but cannot replace it.

**Deployment Attestation**
- Measured pure module `scripts/vite-build-sha.ts` resolves exact lowercase 40-hex HEAD, rejects dirty/missing/invalid git output, fails when unavailable outside development, and returns literal `development` only for explicit development. `vite.config.ts` is executable configuration and is measured through focused import coverage; its invocation plus `define` wiring for `__SPOONJOY_BUILD_SHA__` is asserted behavior, not a coverage exclusion. Public DB/auth-free `GET /health` returns exact JSON `{ status:"ok", service:"spoonjoy", buildSha }`, header `X-Spoonjoy-Build-Sha: <same value>`, `Cache-Control: no-store`, and `Content-Type: application/json`; production/QA reject `development`.
- All probes are immediate at t=0, then fixed intervals while scheduled time `<= deadline`; no new request starts later. Health fetch uses cache-busting query, `cache:"no-store"`, and a fresh 4-second AbortController timeout, so the final scheduled sample ends by deadline+4s; network/abort/non-200/malformed/mismatched body/header records one failed Health sample and polling continues. Health samples are t=0,5,...,180s. GitHub workflow REST calls use 10-second request timeouts: source-CI and production-run discovery are t=0,10,...,300s, while the already-selected production run and its deploy job are watched at t=0,10,...,1800s. Production discovery stops as soon as at least one exact automatic candidate exists in `queued|in_progress|completed`; it does not wait for completion. Cloudflare deployment capture uses one 30-second request. Exhaustion or malformed oracle fails its stage; timers are fake-clock tested and always cleared.
- Production's staged MCP canary performs no product/stateful mutation until candidate-version routing is stable. Integrated main commits `b07d787e` and `dcf296bd` already repair the proven `e7b0e9ec` mismatch with two consecutive three-channel cycles: real-browser navigation and APIRequest both GET protected-resource metadata, while a second real browser loads then submits the no-store, side-effect-free `/.well-known/spoonjoy-release-readiness` React Router action to prove the browser mutation channel. That readiness POST is transport-only and explicitly allowed before stability; signup, auth grants, D1/R2 writes, and every other product mutation remain forbidden. Redirect-safe CDP interception adds the override to every same-origin hop and never leaks it cross-origin. Unit29.1 preserves those regressions and tightens stability to the final contract here. Each cycle requires all three responses HTTP 200 with exact candidate `X-Spoonjoy-Worker-Version`; three consecutive successful cycles run one second apart within one 120-second deadline. Any network/non-200/missing/old-version result resets the count to zero, each request is capped at `min(10 seconds, remaining deadline)`, and equality to the deadline fails. Every later same-origin canary request still sends the override and asserts the candidate header. The orchestrator's rollback remains authoritative on any later mismatch.
- QA base is exactly `https://spoonjoy-v2-qa.mendelow-studio.workers.dev`. Commands are exact: `pnpm cleanup:remote:qa:apply`; `pnpm cleanup:remote:qa -- --assert-clean`; `pnpm run qa:preflight`; `pnpm run deploy:qa`; health to branchHeadSha; `node scripts/smoke-api-live.mjs --target-env qa --base-url https://spoonjoy-v2-qa.mendelow-studio.workers.dev --out /Users/arimendelow/desk/spoonjoy-v2/clem-feedback-e2e/artifacts/release-<ReleaseRun.run>/unit-33/stage-<StageRun.run>/qa-api-smoke/attempt-<Command.attempt>/out`; `node scripts/smoke-live.mjs --target-env qa --base-url https://spoonjoy-v2-qa.mendelow-studio.workers.dev --release-identity-sha256 <identitySha256> --release-attempt-path <attemptPath> --out /Users/arimendelow/desk/spoonjoy-v2/clem-feedback-e2e/artifacts/release-<ReleaseRun.run>/unit-33/stage-<StageRun.run>/qa-lifecycle-smoke/attempt-<Command.attempt>/out`; crash-safe `qa-cleanup-reconcile` immediately after deployment capture and before broad cleanup; final `pnpm cleanup:remote:qa -- --assert-clean`. `--keep-smoke-data` is forbidden; smoke owns mutation cleanup in finally, and only assert-clean plus smoke's exact per-user proof are release oracles.
- Production base is exactly `https://spoonjoy.app`; workers.dev is not a release oracle. Commands after exact workflow/deploy/health are `pnpm run production:readiness`; crash-safe `production-cleanup-reconcile`; `pnpm cleanup:production -- --assert-clean`; API/lifecycle/visual smoke; final `pnpm cleanup:production -- --assert-clean`. The reconcile command is exact `node scripts/smoke-live.mjs --target-env production --reconcile-cleanup --failed-attempts-json <J> --release-identity-sha256 <identitySha256> --release-attempt-path <attemptPath> --out <O>`, where J is canonical compact JSON of BINARY-sourceIdentitySha256/attemptPath-sorted unique CleanupReconcileTarget values selected from the current identity and its hash-valid repair-predecessor chain, or `[]`; at most one target may still be incomplete because no next smoke may start until it is reconciled. Before signup each QA/production smoke creates a random 128-bit lowercase-hex recoveryNonce, derives memory-only email `codex-smoke-<first32 lowercase hex emailDigest>@example.com`, where emailDigest is HMAC-SHA-256 of UTF-8 token bytes and `spoonjoy-smoke-email-v1\0<recoveryNonce>`, and derives exact password `Sj1!<passwordDigest>`, where passwordDigest is unpadded base64url HMAC-SHA-256 over distinct message `spoonjoy-smoke-password-v1\0<recoveryNonce>`. Raw email, token, and password exist only in child memory and request bodies, never argv, stdout/stderr, artifacts, diagnostics, or persisted state. Exact `CleanupRecoveryIdentity={targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',targetBaseUrl,userId:string|null,recoveryNonce,emailSha256,credentialDerivation:'hmac-sha256-v1',credentialKeySha256}` stores only its declared nonsecret target/user/nonce fields plus lowercase SHA-256 of the derived email and, as the sole explicit secret-digest exception, the raw D1 token; recovery reconstructs and hash-checks email/password in memory. A failed/incomplete cleanup alone writes compact canonical no-LF `cleanup-incomplete-diagnostic.json` with exact `CleanupIncompleteDiagnostic={version:1,phase,targetEnvironment,targetBaseUrl,sourceIdentitySha256,attemptLocator,recoveryIdentity,cleanupPhasePath,cleanupPhaseSha256,purgeTargets,completedTargets,remainingTargets,failureCode,createdAt}`; attemptLocator is a TaskArtifactLocator and remaining paths are source-task-relative, target arrays are exact BINARY-sorted subsets/partition, and the file is immutable after atomic write. Reconcile first compares the current token hash during side-effect-free preflight, derives the same password, reauthenticates only the recorded user, hash-validates diagnostic/phase/targets and its separate successor lineage and cleanup proof chains, and resumes the same purge/projection/user-delete state machine idempotently with the cleanup logs below. A missing or changed token returns typed preflight `credential_missing` with zero mutation; no alternate/global cleanup is attempted. Every selected attempt must produce a nonnull already_clean/reconciled proof and broad assert-clean must pass before a fresh Unit37 user is created. Recovery reruns the same J/output attempt read-first; no fresh smoke can strand prior residue. Normal smoke commands use resolver-owned identity.deskTaskRoot output paths. Unit30 updates `smoke:live` default/package script to canonical domain. Smoke owns finally cleanup; broad assert-clean plus exact per-attempt proof establish zero product residue while allowing only declared inaccessible DO metadata.
- The `cleanup-incomplete-diagnostic.json` chronology and schema replace “failed/incomplete alone writes” and the abbreviated schema above. Exact `CleanupRecoveryIdentity={targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',targetBaseUrl,userId:string|null,recoveryNonce,emailSha256,credentialDerivation:'hmac-sha256-v1',credentialKeySha256}` and `CleanupIncompleteDiagnostic={version:1,phase,targetEnvironment,targetBaseUrl,sourceIdentitySha256,attemptLocator,recoveryIdentity,cleanupPhasePath,cleanupPhaseSha256,purgeTargets,completedTargets,remainingTargets,failureCode,createdAt}` use phase `prepared|user_created|targets_recorded|cleanup_incomplete`. Before signup the runner atomically creates prepared with null userId/cleanupPhase fields/failureCode and empty arrays. After signup and each target/cleanup checkpoint it crash-safely rewrites the complete file through a token-qualified fsynced candidate, preserving createdAt/nonce/key hash and advancing phase; userId becomes nonnull at user_created, target arrays form a BINARY-sorted exact partition at targets_recorded, cleanupPhase fields become nonnull when the phase log exists, and failureCode becomes nonnull only at cleanup_incomplete. If the child dies before a later phase, the executor accepts/hashes the latest complete schema-valid phase as the retained failed-attempt diagnostic; prepared recovery reauthenticates by derived email and rediscovers the exact disposable user/targets, or proves signup never occurred. Successful cleanup atomically removes the journal before the passed closed output is finalized, so it never enters the 14/38-file passed sets. A same-token partial candidate beside an intact main is removed and rebuilt because no dispatch precedes main readback; a same-token initial candidate without main is removed because signup cannot precede prepared-main readback; foreign, multiple, wrong-token, or corrupt-main state fails closed; tests fault before/after signup, response loss, every rewrite, process signal, TERM/KILL, and successful removal.
- Cleanup recovery identity and diagnostic target binding are replaced by exact `CleanupRecoveryIdentity={targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',targetBaseUrl,userId:string|null,recoveryNonce,emailSha256,credentialDerivation:'hmac-sha256-v1',credentialKeySha256}` and `CleanupIncompleteDiagnostic={version:1,phase,targetEnvironment,targetBaseUrl,sourceIdentitySha256,attemptLocator,recoveryIdentity,cleanupPhasePath,cleanupPhaseSha256,purgeTargets,completedTargets,remainingTargets,failureCode,createdAt}`. Environment/base must be QA/QaBaseUrl or production/ProductionBaseUrl and byte-equal between diagnostic, recovery identity, reconcile target, and executing ProcessSpec; attemptLocator is the exact task-artifact locator for the failed command. Target/environment/base participate in every diagnostic hash and target key. Cross-environment, cross-base, raw physical path, or omitted locator recovery rejects before credential or provider access.
- Deployment capture calls exact single-page Cloudflare endpoint `GET https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/$CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID/workers/scripts/<script>/deployments` with bearer token, using script `spoonjoy-v2-qa` for QA and `spoonjoy-v2` for production. The API schema has no pagination cursor; success requires exact successful envelope/result.deployments array and rejects duplicate/invalid ids, noncanonical created_on, HTTP/API error, or an unexpected pagination field. Normalize source only to `github_actions|wrangler|dashboard|unknown|null`, never provider text; normalize each result to `{id,createdAt:created_on,source}`, sort createdAt descending then id BINARY, and compute after-minus-before ids sorted BINARY. Empty difference is legal and makes no activity claim; a nonempty QA delta selects the unique maximum `(createdAt,id)` and likewise makes no causality claim. Exact health SHA is QA authority and the automatic workflow is production authority.
- Immediately before merge Unit36 captures normalized production deployments. Unit37 carries that immutable set, first discovers completed-success canonical `.github/workflows/ci.yml` push at mergeSha, then discovers automatic `.github/workflows/production-deploy.yml` workflow_run with the same head and exact triggering CI databaseId and runAttempt. Unit29.1 modifies the production workflow run-name to use exact `ci-run-${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}-attempt-${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}`; discovery parses that immutable id and rejects a source-only match. At first candidate poll retain exact path/event/head/trigger candidates in queued|in_progress|completed, sort createdAt then databaseId, and freeze newest; workflow_dispatch/wrong edge never qualify. watch-workflow polls selected id through 1800 seconds and requires completed/success; later runs never replace it. verify-deploy-job requires completed-success deploy. Terminal workflow preserves selected databaseId/path/event/head/trigger/url/createdAt while status/conclusion evolve. Download exact canary artifact, hash/parse production-release.json, and require no-extra-fields `status:"promoted"`, sourceSha mergeSha, treeHash branchHeadTreeSha, triggeringCiDatabaseId/runAttempt equal CiWorkflow.databaseId/runAttempt, valid candidate Worker version, and databaseRollbackSupported false. CI edge, workflow, job, artifact, canonical health, and source-tagged Worker jointly attest production; unrelated deployments remain evidence and are never guessed as causal.

**Release Evidence Manifest**
- In TrackDisposition/TrackArchive, `taskArchiveKind` is exactly `desk_archive|predecessor_supersede`; `taskArchiveSha256` is Sha256 of final `desk-archive.v1.json` bytes for desk_archive or final `predecessor-supersede.v1.json` bytes for predecessor_supersede, never a directory/tree hash. `scanDeskSha` is the fetched Desk main commit whose tree was scanned. The later ArchiveValidationReceipt records `trackDispositionCommitSha`, the already-existing commit containing the disposition; TrackDisposition never self-references its own commit SHA.
- Unit37 `workflow-discovery` argv is the registered form already listed plus exact ordered pairs `--triggering-ci-database-id <CiWorkflow.databaseId> --triggering-ci-run-attempt <CiWorkflow.runAttempt>` immediately before `--out`; every later workflow/artifact command verifies the same field transitively.
- Previously named release schemas are exact: `BranchProtection={repo,branch,strict,requiredLinearHistory,requiredContextNames,capturedAt}`; `CheckSet={repo,prNumber,sourceSha,checks}`; `ReviewThreadSet={repo,prNumber,sourceSha,unresolvedCount,threadIds}`; `MergeReadiness={repo,prNumber,testedBaseSha,sourceSha,treeSha,remoteBaseSha,headSha,headTreeSha,strict,requiredLinearHistory,requiredContextNames,checks,unresolvedThreadIds,ready}`; and `Continuation={repo,prNumber,sourceSha,treeSha,mergeSha,excludedHeadRef,openPrNumbers,openBranchRefs,openPrCount,openBranchCount,unresolvedThreadIds,followUpIssueIds,ready}`. All are no-extra-fields; openPrNumbers is numeric-ascending unique, every string array is UTF-8/BINARY-sorted unique, counts are nonnegative safe integers equal to their paired array lengths, times canonical, identifiers exact, and ready is the conjunction of the stated identity/merged/exclusion and all-zero invariants. Provider-only observations are exact no-extra-fields `QaDeployObservation={targetSha,inventoryDisposition:'delta'|'no_delta',deployment,health}`, `PullRequestObservation={number,url,state:'OPEN',headRef,headSha,baseRef,baseSha,title,bodySha256}`, and `MergeObservation={prNumber,state:'MERGED',sourceSha,testedBaseSha,squash}`; they deliberately contain no disposition or intent/root hashes. `EnsureProbe={version:1,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,observedAt,result,observation}` has result `exact_effect|absent_complete|indeterminate|conflict`; observation is the matching provider-only observation only for exact_effect and null otherwise. Unit29.2 alone materializes the full QaDeployOutcome/PullRequest/MergeOutcome by adding the authority-selected disposition, intentSha256, and rootIntentSha256 after validating the probe against the current attempt. Production `Workflow` and `ProductionRelease` additionally require `triggeringCiDatabaseId` and `triggeringCiRunAttempt`, equal the selected CiWorkflow databaseId/runAttempt; `.github/workflows/production-deploy.yml` has an exact run-name `ci-run-${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}-attempt-${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}`, which discovery parses to prove the CI-to-production workflow_run edge. QaDeployObservation delta requires nonnull normalized after-minus-before Deployment; no_delta requires deployment null plus exact target health. Check object arrays use the execution-contract-defined comparator and reject duplicate names.
- Reservation mode has exact domain `process|agent`. ReleaseContinuity.nextUnit is deterministic: it is null for `superseded|failed|passed`; for active it is the earliest unit `32..37` whose latest StageRun is missing, running, or failed; after Unit37's latest stage is passed but before finish-run it is null. Initial transfer is exactly 32. The lastTerminal triplet names the numerically latest existing terminal StageRun, with greatest run number inside that unit, and is all null only when none exists.
- Release ownership is immutable and task-specific. Exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTaskIdentity={kind,task,repairRootTask,repairPredecessorTask,repairDepth,branch,repoCwd,deskTaskRoot,taskSourcePath,taskArchivePath,trackPath,productPlanningPath,productDoingPath,productContinuityPath,deskDoingPath,prTitle,prBody}` uses `kind:'primary'|'repair_successor'`, normalized repo-relative document/Desk paths, absolute real non-symlink cwd/root paths, and nonempty title/body. Primary has repairRootTask equal task, repairPredecessorTask null, and repairDepth zero. A repair successor inherits repairRootTask, sets repairPredecessorTask to the immediate predecessor task, and sets repairDepth to predecessor depth plus one; depth is a safe integer and the chain rejects cycles, skipped depths, repeated task names, or a predecessor whose identity/hash cannot be resolved uniquely through active/task/archive/TrackArchive receipts. A repair successor derives task=`successorSlug`, branch=`worker/<successorSlug>`, repoCwd=`/Users/arimendelow/Projects/spoonjoy-v2-<successorSlug>`, deskTaskRoot=`/Users/arimendelow/desk/spoonjoy-v2/<successorSlug>`, source/archive/track/Desk-doing and reviewed planning/doing/continuity paths from the hash-valid successor task, prTitle=`Repair Spoonjoy post-merge <first12 successorKey>`, and prBody=`Repairs Spoonjoy post-merge failure <successorKey>; the reviewed execution contract is <productDoingPath>.` with no trailing LF. PostMergeSuccessorAttempt freezes only successorKey/slug/lineage while prepared, freezes task path plus immutable task-seed path/hash after task creation; mutable task.md may advance only through declared Desk lifecycle transforms, and freezes the complete identity/hash only at contract_approved; existing task/worktree/branch/doc/lineage drift rejects at every phase. Every manifest, ProcessSpec, AgentCommand prompt, PR head/title/body, continuation exclusion, validating transition, TerminalCleanup, DeskArchive, TrackDisposition, validator, and receipt reads only its manifest's identity. Fixed Clem literals elsewhere describe the primary identity only and are never inherited by a repair successor.
- Primary ReleaseTaskIdentity bytes are exactly kind `primary`, task `clem-feedback-e2e`, repairRootTask `clem-feedback-e2e`, repairPredecessorTask null, repairDepth `0`, branch `worker/clem-feedback-e2e`, repoCwd `/Users/arimendelow/Projects/spoonjoy-v2-clem-feedback`, deskTaskRoot `/Users/arimendelow/desk/spoonjoy-v2/clem-feedback-e2e`, taskSourcePath `spoonjoy-v2/clem-feedback-e2e`, taskArchivePath `spoonjoy-v2/_archive/clem-feedback-e2e`, trackPath `spoonjoy-v2/track.md`, productPlanningPath `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-planning-clem-feedback-e2e.md`, productDoingPath `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-doing-clem-feedback-e2e.md`, productContinuityPath `worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-doing-clem-feedback-e2e/AUTOPILOT-STATE.md`, deskDoingPath `spoonjoy-v2/clem-feedback-e2e/spoonjoy-v2/2026-07-15-adopted/doing.md`, prTitle `Ship Clem feedback end to end`, and prBody `Ships Clem's accepted feedback end to end; the reviewed doing contract is worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-doing-clem-feedback-e2e.md.`. Successor identity requires the same field order and the exact formulas above at contract_approved; no field may remain null at manifest init. Every `identitySha256` is lowercase SHA-256 of the UTF-8 bytes of compact `JSON.stringify` serialization of those exact fields in schema order, with no insignificant whitespace or final LF; no caller-supplied hash or alternate property order is accepted.
- The active manifest path is `<ReleaseTaskIdentity.deskTaskRoot>/artifacts/release-evidence.v1.json`; task archival moves it unchanged beneath that identity's taskArchivePath, and any whole-track archival re-roots it beneath the generation-qualified TrackArchive target. It has no extra fields and exact top-level `{version:1,identity:ReleaseTaskIdentity,writerId,revision,updatedAt,preReleaseArtifacts:PreReleaseArtifact[],terminalArtifacts:TerminalArtifact[],runs:ReleaseRun[]}`. `writerId` is one canonical lowercase UUID v4 created at initialization and immutable through archive: primary initializes at Unit32, while a repair successor initializes at manifest_ready before Work Doer. Primary preReleaseArtifacts and terminalArtifacts are empty; repair-successor membership follows the Round 59 closed registries. revision is a nonnegative safe integer and updatedAt is canonical ISO. Initialization stores revision zero and one clock reading; every successful state-changing manifest operation stores `revision+1` and `updatedAt=max(clockNow,previousUpdatedAt+1ms)` in the same atomic rewrite. An exactly idempotent/no-op call preserves both; recover increments once only when it repairs/interrupts state, while provisional winner-tree abandonment restores the pushed predecessor's unchanged pair. Revision/date overflow rejects before mutation. Canonical ISO means `new Date(x).toISOString()===x`; Sha is lowercase 40-hex; sha256 is lowercase 64-hex; required strings are nonempty. Every stored artifact/log/report/process/intent/arm/dispatch/handoff/outcome path is a normalized `/`-separated task-relative path beginning `artifacts/`, with no empty, `.`, or `..` segment, and resolves against identity.deskTaskRoot; the one root continuity path is exactly `release-continuity.v1.json`. No absolute stored artifact path is legal, so archive re-rooting preserves validity.
- Ordinary rewrites of an existing valid manifest use exact sibling `release-evidence.v1.json.tmp`, validate complete candidate bytes, fsync/close, rename over main, fsync the parent, and then remove any valid orphan ordinary temp; invalid main or temp fails closed. Initialization never uses that ordinary temp. It creates exact token-qualified `release-evidence.v1.json.init.<writerId>.tmp` plus no-extra-fields `manifest-init-candidate.v1.json={version:1,writerId,identitySha256,tempPath,tempSha256,initialManifestSha256,startedAt}` under the same same-host lock. Candidate is fsynced before the token temp; tempSha256 and initialManifestSha256 both bind the exact valid revision-zero bytes. Dead-owner recovery with missing main validates lock/candidate/identity: an absent or zero-byte owned temp is removed with candidate and retried using the same writerId; exact complete hash-valid bytes are promoted by rename and parent fsync; nonzero partial/mismatch, unbound temp, foreign token, multiple candidate, fixed ordinary temp, or malformed sidecar fails closed. If main is already exact, recovery removes only its matching init temp/candidate/lock. Thus every initialization crash has one deterministic promote/remove/fail-closed branch. The tested helper validates before and after every write.
- Release command execution uses `REPO_CWD=ReleaseTaskIdentity.repoCwd` and `DESK_TASK_ROOT=ReleaseTaskIdentity.deskTaskRoot`. A tested `resolveCommandAttemptPaths` accepts only a manifest-owned relative `artifacts/release-<positive>/unit-<32..37>/stage-<positive>/<logicalName>/attempt-<positive>` matching the active run/stage/command, resolves it below DESK_TASK_ROOT, and proves every existing parent real/non-symlink with no escape. Under manifest lock, start creates sibling `.attempt-<A>.<reservationToken>.tmp`, writes/fsyncs exact `attempt-reservation.v1.json={version:1,writerId,releaseRun,unit,stageRun,logicalName,attempt,mode,reservationToken}`, creates declared zero-byte scaffolding, and for ensure writes/fsyncs/hashes operation-intent plus ensure-arm. It fsyncs bottom-up, renames to `attempt-<A>`, fsyncs parent, then atomically records the matching command/token/artifacts in the manifest. Intent/arm authority remains provisional until exact remote Desk readback; no process may spawn earlier. Unreferenced recovery follows the exact Round 49 two-tree rule: zero-scaffold or byte-valid intent/arm reservation, with the latter removable only under unchanged remote predecessor, no remote arm/dispatch/handoff/gate/release/process/outcome, zero logs, and zero provider/child calls. Manifest-referenced provisional abandonment separately requires the execution-contract-defined winner-tree proof. Any extra/nonzero/malformed/unmatched/remote-armed tree fails closed. Referenced ordinary/armed attempts are terminalized only after exact liveness proof and never reused. Reviewer attempts use the same reservation protocol without process logs. Stored paths normalize to identity-task-relative artifacts and re-root only through identity DeskArchive/TrackArchive receipts.
- The unreferenced-reservation removal sentence above is replaced by this exact closed recovery rule. An exact next-attempt staging or final reservation may be removed when its tree is either reservation plus zero-byte declared scaffolding, or reservation plus zero-byte scaffolding plus the byte-valid operation-intent and ensure-arm named by that reservation. The second form additionally requires fetched origin/main still equals the frozen pre-attempt Desk predecessor, no manifest references the attempt, neither intent nor arm exists remotely, no dispatch/handoff/gate/release/process/outcome/artifact beyond those two files exists locally or remotely, both logs are zero, and the injected provider/child call count is zero. Any extra/nonzero log, remote trace, malformed cross-link, wrong predecessor, process evidence, or ambiguous state fails closed. This covers the crash after final-directory rename and before manifest rewrite without making a mutation-capable reservation adoptable.
- Provisional abandonment is a distinct nonterminal recovery operation, never an interrupted command. Local provisional commit L must be clean and contain only the exact next running ensure attempt atop pushed remote predecessor O; current origin/main must still have O's exact pre-attempt task tree. Recovery constructs/pushes the execution-contract-defined winner-tree merge K with tree O and parents O,L, ff-only advances local to K, and returns `abandoned:1` without adding a manifest command or continuity event. The next `start-command` therefore reuses the same attempt number with a fresh token/host/intent/arm. Any remote trace of the arm, nonzero log, process call, or tree mismatch makes abandonment illegal.
- `scripts/release-executor.ts` is the sole production ProcessCommand owner and package command `pnpm release:execute -- --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --command-json J`. Import-safe `createReleaseExecutor(deps)` performs the identical lifecycle in tests and production: side-effect-free environment/capability preflight, start-command, exact Desk commit/push/readback, optional ensure dispatch-CAS, prepare-spawn, spawn the nonmutating gate, record-process, release-spawn checkpoint, immutable release-file creation, gate-mediated child exec with streaming sanitizer and timeout enforcement, finish-command, terminal Desk commit/push/readback, and recovery/winner convergence. The gate and every generation are part of the same total timeout; actual registered argv cannot execute before its released checkpoint is remote-readable. Exported `runReleaseExecutorCli(argv,deps)` builds the complete result in memory and returns exact `{exitCode,stdout,stderr}` bytes without writing. The executable wrapper performs exactly one injected synchronous write of the selected nonempty stream and no write for the empty stream. On a passed terminal ProcessCommand it returns exit 0, exact compact finish-command success JSON plus newline on stdout, and empty stderr. On a terminal ProcessCommand failure it returns exit 1, empty stdout, and exact compact `{\"ok\":false,\"code\":\"process_failed\",\"failureReason\":<finite>}` plus newline on stderr. Argument, preflight, lifecycle, checkpoint, artifact, filesystem, or other executor failure returns exit 2, empty stdout, and exact compact `{\"ok\":false,\"code\":<argument|preflight|state|lock|schema|artifact|io|checkpoint>}` plus newline on stderr. A complete write preserves that exact contract; an injected short write or throw exits 2 and makes no byte-exact JSON claim because already-written process bytes cannot be retracted. No code path emits raw argv/path/prompt/secret/exception/stack/free text, and a persisted failed command can never produce exit zero. Units32-37 invoke this command for every ProcessCommand and never reproduce those steps manually. AgentCommand and terminal product/Desk cleanup remain outside this executor.
- Environment preflight is mandatory before state mutation, and its original in-memory deadlineStartedAt is persisted by start-command after success. Shared `preflightProcessEnvironment` returns exact public/secret proofs with the same PATH/HOME/TMPDIR/account/host/capability validation and nonserialization rules. Common gate/registered-child keys are PATH, HOME, CI=1, NO_COLOR=1, FORCE_COLOR=0, plus TMPDIR iff resolved. base is common; local-db adds exact `DATABASE_URL:'file:./test.db?connection_limit=1&socket_timeout=60'`; github uses HOME-backed gh; cloudflare adds account and HOME-backed Wrangler OAuth; cloudflare-rest additionally resolves exact Wrangler OAuth JSON to one memory-only bearer and maps it only into its registered Cloudflare REST child; cloudflare-d1 transports the raw D1 token through a fresh one-shot anonymous descriptor for the preflight probe and independently through a fresh descriptor for every later registered D1 command; only the child receives CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN; cloudflare-github uses both stores. Only the exact role, token, and authority-B64 internal keys defined below may supplement this table. Missing/invalid input rejects with zero mutation and a crash before start-command simply restarts preflight with no persisted attempt.
- Ensure dispatch is a second pushed compare-and-swap checkpoint, distinct from arm. After the armed `mutate_once` executor's production probe returns complete absence, it invokes exact `prepare-dispatch`, which creates/hashes `ensure-dispatch.v1.json`, records its task-relative path on the running ProcessCommand, commits, normal-pushes from the current arm checkpoint, fetches, and byte-verifies current origin/main before the registered provenance process may receive the sole mutation dependency. A cross-host handoff may transfer the same global root and retain `mutate_once` only when its normal push wins before any dispatch; rejected handoff/dispatch fetches the winner and forbids mutation. Once any authoritative dispatch exists, later attempts are `reconcile_only`; another host may observe an exact-effect probe in memory only but may not persist or materialize, certify absence/death, spawn, interrupt, replay, fail the run, or change dispatch. The dispatch host may replay or materialize only after exact role-tagged token-member liveness is false. Thus each operationKey has one immutable dispatch, at most one concurrent child, and at most one effective target state. Loss of the dispatch host blocks terminal progress rather than risking invented liveness.
- Unit29.2 is the sole file/manifest authority interpreter. It reads and schema/hash/cross-link validates the intent, arm, and dispatch files, then constructs canonical no-extra-fields `EnsureInvocationAuthority={version:1,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,releaseRun,reservationToken,executionHost,mode,rootIntentSha256,intentSha256,armSha256,dispatchSha256,dispatchProbeSha256}`. `mode` is `mutate_once|reconcile_only`; mutate_once requires current executionHost equal dispatch.executionHost and all hashes/identities byte-equal, while reconcile_only never exposes a mutation dependency.
