Unit29.1 provider code receives only this already-validated object (or null for the first read-only probe), parses its schema and checks operation argv identity, and is forbidden from reading manifest/intent/arm/dispatch/handoff/process paths or deciding liveness/replay. For a gate-mediated ensure child, Unit29.2 serializes unpadded base64url of the canonical UTF-8 object only as `SPOONJOY_RELEASE_ENSURE_AUTHORITY_B64`; the provider CLI parses it and exposes the named mutation dependency iff mode is mutate_once. This object, never path interpretation inside Unit29.1, is the complete serialized authority channel.
- Every ProcessCommand runs with cwd REPO_CWD through `createReleaseExecutor`, sanitized streams, and the seven exact policies: `base`, `local-db`, `github`, `cloudflare`, `cloudflare-rest`, `cloudflare-d1`, and `cloudflare-github`. Internal `SPOONJOY_RELEASE_ENSURE_AUTHORITY_B64` exists as opaque gate-to-child transport for ensures; `SPOONJOY_RELEASE_SPAWN_TOKEN` and `SPOONJOY_RELEASE_PROCESS_ROLE` exist on gate and child for liveness; `SPOONJOY_RELEASE_SECRET_FD` exists only in a `cloudflare-rest` or `cloudflare-d1` gate bootstrap until consumed as specified below. Every other SPOONJOY key is unset. Before each spawn, prepare-spawn appends exact `SpawnGeneration={generation,spawnToken,status,gatePath,releasePath,processIdentity,preparedAt,recordedAt,releasedAt,endedAt}`. Status/nullability remain prepared/recorded/released/aborted/ended as defined; role-tagged runtime members do not alter immutable generation history.
- Exact gate path is `<attempt>/spawn-<generation>-gate.v1.json` and exact release path is `<attempt>/spawn-<generation>-release.v1.json`. Gate bytes are immutable no-extra-fields `{version:1,releaseRun,unit,stageRun,logicalName,attempt,generation,spawnToken,executionHost,commandSha256,releasePath}`; releasePath is task-relative and commandSha256 hashes compact canonical JSON of the exact resolved execution argv passed after the gate's standalone `--`; the gate recomputes it before waiting. Release bytes are immutable no-extra-fields `{version:1,releaseRun,unit,stageRun,logicalName,attempt,generation,spawnToken,releasedAt}` and byte-equal the released manifest fields. Both are reservation infrastructure, not Artifacts, and the closed-set validator allows exactly the generation-declared files: gate always exists after prepare-spawn, release exists iff release authority was physically emitted. prepare-spawn atomically writes/fsyncs gate bytes plus the manifest generation and pushes/readbacks both before spawning.
- The executor spawns the same exact gate argv as detached group leader with token and role=gate. The gate validates file/token/command hash, waits for release, then spawns registeredArgv in the same PGID with the same token and role=child; ensure authority is inherited opaquely. It stays until child exit. Recovery accepts only the exact role/member matrices in Round 36 and treats any verified member as live. record-process persists gate identity; release-spawn pushes before release-file creation. TERM/KILL targets the verified group and completion requires every verified token member reaped.
- Gate/manifest crash classification retains exact orphan/spawn/release-file rules, but liveness is the complete role-tagged token member set. Synchronous spawn failure aborts prepared generation through finish-command spawn_error. A durable released generation with missing release file is recoverable only on its executionHost and only for the verified live gate; a dead set is reconciled before replay/materialization. Other hosts probe in memory only and never persist or terminalize. Replay marks every dead released generation ended then appends; postdispatch materialization does the same. Predispatch materialization instead requires zero generations/global dispatch. Only declared output specs receive out paths.
- Every ProcessSpec carries one exact total timeout started before preflight and covering every sequential preflight, filesystem, checkpoint, provider, gate, child, finish, and readback phase. One durable checkpoint has a 120000ms aggregate cap; normal lifecycle has five=600000ms and ensure lifecycle six=720000ms. Capability preflight is 30000ms and local file work 20000ms, so fixed overhead is 650000ms ordinary and 770000ms ensure. `operationPhaseCapMs`, rather than a contradictory child cap, includes every provider horizon, mutation child, final observation request, and gate wait: workflow-watch/deploy-job 1810000ms; QA ensure 1810000ms (=300000 pre-dispatch observation + 600000 mutation + 910000 post-dispatch reconciliation); PR/merge ensure 730000ms (=300000 + 120000 + 310000); CI/workflow discovery 310000ms; production visual 900000ms; lifecycle smokes and qa/production-cleanup-reconcile 360000ms; app/Workers coverage, E2E, and build 1200000ms; every other argv 300000ms. Exact totals are workflow-watch/deploy-job 2580000; qa-deploy-ensure 2700000; pr-ensure/merge-ensure 1620000; ci-workflow-discovery/workflow-discovery 1080000; production-visual-dogfood 1680000; lifecycle smokes and qa/production-cleanup-reconcile 1140000; app-coverage/workers-coverage/e2e/build 1980000; every other ProcessSpec 1080000. Thus the no-race maxima are respectively 2460000, 2580000, 1500000, 960000, 1550000, 1010000, 1850000, and 950000, each strictly below its total. The reconcile cap covers one incomplete cleanup's maximum 305 seconds plus termination; additional authorized ancestry entries must already be clean or are processed in separate fresh commands before any new smoke. Winner-convergence retries share the same total deadline and may exhaust it safely rather than extending time. Every async dependency accepts one AbortSignal and a remaining-time cap; no provider horizon is subordinate to a smaller phase cap. Each gate/child is one process group; deadline sends SIGTERM, waits exactly 5000ms, sends SIGKILL if needed, and reaps. TERM/KILL timeout results fail with null exit/warning. Unproven death leaves command running and same-host recovery resumes exact-generation kill/reap; another host cannot terminalize. Fake-clock tests exhaust every full phase sum, equality, checkpoint, TERM/KILL, descendant, recovery, and timer-cleanup branch.
- The sole executable manifest interface is `pnpm release:evidence -- <subcommand>` in import-safe `scripts/release-evidence-cli.ts`; all subprocesses receive argv arrays, never shell text. Every command requires flags in listed order; equals-style, duplicate/unknown/misordered flags, extras, empty values, and post-subcommand `--` reject before lock/mutation. Complete mutating grammar is: `init --manifest M --identity-json J`; `recover --manifest M --writer-id W`; `start-run --manifest M --writer-id W --tested-base-sha T --branch-head-sha H --branch-head-tree-sha B`; `start-stage --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --start-sha S`; `start-command --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --command-json J`; `prepare-spawn --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A`; `record-process --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --generation N --pid I --pgid J --process-start-identity X`; `release-spawn --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --generation N`; `prepare-handoff --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --to-execution-host X`; `prepare-dispatch --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --probe-json E`; `materialize-ensure-outcome --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --probe-json E --authority predispatch_existing|postdispatch_reconciled`; `finish-command-without-process --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --reason predispatch_indeterminate|predispatch_conflict|dispatch_ambiguity`; `finish-command --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --generation N --process-result exit:<0..255>|spawn_error|signal|timeout:term|timeout:kill --stream-status clean|invalid_utf8|nul`; `start-agent-command --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --role Q`; `finish-agent-command --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --agent-id D --verdict CONVERGED`; `finish-agent-command --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --agent-id D --verdict FINDINGS --findings-json J`; `interrupt-agent-command --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt A --agent-id none|D --reason stalled|errored|tool_unavailable`; `append-artifact --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --kind K --path P`; `set-merge-sha --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --merge-sha S`; `record-pre-merge-disposition --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit 32|33|34|35|36 --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt none|A`; `record-post-merge-disposition --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt none|A`; `resume-external-wait --manifest M --writer-id W --wait-path P`; `resolve-pre-merge-blocker --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit 32|33|34|35|36 --stage-run G --logical-name L --disposition P`; `start-agent-runtime-probe --manifest M --writer-id W --wait-path P`; `finish-agent-runtime-probe --manifest M --writer-id W --wait-path P --probe-ordinal N --agent-id D --report-json J`; `resolve-post-merge-blocker --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --stage-run G --logical-name L --disposition P`; `finish-stage --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --status passed --end-sha S --evidence-json J`; `finish-stage --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit U --stage-run G --status failed --end-sha S`; `supersede-run --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --end-sha S`; `fail-run --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --end-sha S --reason dispatch_ambiguity|operator_abandonment|post_merge_finding|post_merge_repair`; and `finish-run --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --end-sha S`. The list also includes exact `transfer-continuity --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit 32 --stage-run G --tested-base-sha T --branch-head-sha H --branch-head-tree-sha B --out O`. Identity JSON is compact canonical ReleaseTaskIdentity and legacy init flags reject. Prepare/release-spawn obey gate-generation rules; prepare-dispatch requires absent_complete; materialize requires exact_effect provider observation and current authority; predispatch requires zero generations while postdispatch requires every released generation member dead; finish-command names latest generation. both disposition commands derive every decision/evidence byte from persisted evidence and have no decision override. Remaining lifecycle invariants are unchanged and byte-tested.
- Agent review is an exact non-process attempt lifecycle. `AgentCommand={mode:'agent',logicalName,attempt,reservationToken,status,role,promptSha256,requiredArtifactsSha256,outPath,agentId,verdict,interruptionReason,runtimeEpoch,retryOrdinal,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt,startedAt,endedAt,reportPath,reportSha256}` is no-extra-fields. `status` is `running|interrupted|failed|passed`; role is the frozen logical-name role; reservationToken and agentId are canonical lowercase UUIDs when nonnull; prompt/artifact/report hashes are Sha256; outPath/reportPath are task-relative and reportPath is inside outPath. Running has null agentId/verdict/interruptionReason/endedAt/report fields. Interrupted has canonical endedAt, reason `stalled|errored|tool_unavailable|recovery`, nullable agentId, and null verdict/report fields. Failed has nonnull agentId, verdict `FINDINGS`, canonical endedAt, null interruptionReason, and nonnull task-relative `review-findings.v1.json` path/hash. Passed has nonnull agentId, verdict `CONVERGED`, canonical endedAt, null interruptionReason, and the role-specific nonnull clean report path/hash. `start-agent-command` creates one fresh attempt/out directory, derives exact prompt/review-input hashes, appends the running command, and returns `{attempt,runtimeEpoch,retryOrdinal,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt,prompt,promptSha256,requiredArtifactsSha256,outPath}`. CONVERGED accepts only the token and writes/hashes/appends the canonical clean report; FINDINGS accepts only the Round 36 canonical JSON, writes/hashes/appends its sanitized finding report, and fails. Malformed/unstructured output fails schema without a repair artifact. Agent attempts have no process argv, executionHost, generations, exit/warning/failure/terminalSource, stdout, stderr, or log artifacts. runtimeEpoch and retryOrdinal are positive; retryOrdinal is sequential for the exact release/unit/logical-name/runtimeEpoch key and capped at two before the durable agent-runtime wait. deadlineStartedAt equals startedAt and deadlineAt is exactly +1800000ms.
- Every ProcessSpec stores exact `operands:{index,kind}[]`, BINARY/numeric unique and exhaustive for nonliteral positions; omitted indices are literal. Values after `--manifest|--out|--intent|--arm|--dispatch` are task_relative, values after `--workflow-path` are repo_relative, and git pathspecs after standalone `--` are repo_relative; these are the only nonliteral rules in the frozen registry and each concrete entry's array is source/byte-tested. Literal and repo_relative bytes remain unchanged, repo_relative resolves only under REPO_CWD, and task_relative alone stores normalized task paths and re-roots absolute. Sentinels are legal only at matching task_relative indices. `prepare-handoff` writes exact handoff/interrupt on source and target consumes it. prepare/record/release-spawn are per-generation/idempotent only for exact bytes. materialize requires exact_effect plus predispatch_existing with zero generations or postdispatch_reconciled with every released role-tagged member dead; it performs no provider mutation and terminalizes the command itself. fail-run reasons retain the finite domain above.
- Agent prompts are byte-frozen. Generic prompt is exact newline-terminated UTF-8: `Spoonjoy release review v1\nrole: <role>\nsource_sha: <sha>\nplanning_path: <ReleaseTaskIdentity.productPlanningPath>\ndoing_path: <ReleaseTaskIdentity.productDoingPath>\nrequired_artifacts_sha256: <sha>\nscope: <role-scope>\nrules: read-only; cite file and line for every finding; return exactly CONVERGED only when no findings; otherwise return canonical compact JSON with top-level verdict and findings conforming to the declared ReviewFinding schema\n`. Exact role-scope strings are `implementation=behavior, architecture, recovery, and integration correctness`; `test=coverage, oracle quality, boundary, race, and failure completeness`; `security_privacy=authentication, authorization, secrets, privacy, and destructive-operation safety`; `migration_config=schema, migration, Cloudflare, workflow, and deployment configuration`; `design_visual=responsive visual quality, interaction states, and design-system consistency`; `api_compatibility=REST v1, OpenAPI, MCP, generator, and backward compatibility`; and `visual_qa=live rendered routes, viewport evidence, interaction behavior, and artifact integrity`. Generic CONVERGED report bytes are exactly eight LF-terminated lines: `spoonjoy-release-review-v1`, `role: <role>`, `agent_id: <uuid>`, `verdict: clean`, `source_sha: <sha>`, `prompt_sha256: <sha256>`, `required_artifacts_sha256: <sha256>`, `finding_count: 0`. Unit37 visual CONVERGED reports use only the final seventeen-line `# Spoonjoy Production Visual Review` format defined by Round77/Round97; generic and visual parsers reject the other format. For Unit37 visual roles the prompt inserts `live_target: https://spoonjoy.app\nvisual_artifact_set_sha256: <sha256>\nvisual_rule: invoke visual-qa-dogfood read-only against the declared live target and immutable artifact set.\n` immediately before rules; Unit34 design_visual does not. `ReviewInput={sourceSha,requiredArtifacts,requiredArtifactsSha256,liveTarget,visualArtifactSetSha256}` is exact: `requiredArtifacts` is a BINARY-path-sorted unique `{path,sha256}[]`, its hash is SHA-256 of compact canonical JSON with field order path/sha256, and live/visual fields are both null outside Unit37 visual roles. Selection is registry-owned, never caller-supplied: Unit32 uses every Artifact from latest passed attempts of its first eighteen ProcessCommands; Unit34 uses every Artifact from latest passed `fetch-drift` and `pr-ensure`; each Unit37 visual review uses all 38 immutable BINARY-sorted direct-child visual-runner output Artifacts plus that command's stdout/stderr log Artifacts. `ProcessCommand={mode:'process',logicalName,attempt,reservationToken,executionHost,status,command,storedCommandSha256,envNames,timeoutMs,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt,spawnGenerations,terminalSource,outPath,intentPath,armPath,dispatchPath,startedAt,endedAt,exitCode,warningCount,failureReason,interruptionReason,stdoutPath,stderrPath}` is no-extra-fields. `failureReason` is null except failed, where it is exactly `spawn|timeout_term|timeout_kill|signal|sanitizer_invalid_utf8|sanitizer_nul|exit|warning|output|provider_indeterminate|provider_conflict|dispatch_ambiguity`; `interruptionReason` is null except interrupted, where it is `recovery|cross_host_handoff|operator_abandonment`. Running and interrupted have terminalSource null; finish-command terminalizes with `process`; materialize terminalizes with `materialized`; finish-command-without-process terminalizes with `without_process`. Passed/failed have canonical endedAt; predispatch materialized pass requires zero generations and intent host, while postdispatch materialized pass requires every released role-tagged member dead on the dispatch host. `StageRun.commands` remains ordered ProcessCommand|AgentCommand.
- The continuity adapter makes Unit32's first logical command executable without mutating frozen tracked files. It verifies identity task root/state and the active ReleaseRun/Unit32 StageRun, validates supplied identities against the manifest, emits only `{status:'ready'}`, and creates no final file. `ReleaseContinuity={version:1,identitySha256,task,branch,planningPath,doingPath,continuitySourcePath,writerId,manifestRevision,releaseRun,testedBaseSha,branchHeadSha,branchHeadTreeSha,mergeSha,releaseStatus,lastTerminalUnit,lastTerminalStageRun,lastTerminalStatus,nextUnit,manifestPath:'artifacts/release-evidence.v1.json',updatedAt}` and `ContinuityTransfer={version:1,identitySha256,task,branch,planningPath,doingPath,continuitySourcePath,writerId,manifestRevision,releaseRun,testedBaseSha,branchHeadSha,branchHeadTreeSha,mergeSha:null,releaseStatus:'active',lastTerminalUnit:null,lastTerminalStageRun:null,lastTerminalStatus:null,nextUnit:32,manifestPath:'artifacts/release-evidence.v1.json',continuityPath:'release-continuity.v1.json',continuitySha256,updatedAt}` are exact/no-extra-fields; task/branch/paths/hash byte-equal ReleaseTaskIdentity and manifestRevision/time byte-equal the persisted manifest. Initial transfer has null merge/terminal fields, active status, and nextUnit 32. Later mergeSha is Sha/null; releaseStatus is `active|superseded|failed|passed`; terminal fields are all null or exact unit/stage/status; nextUnit is null or 32..37 from latest stages/run status. Finish-command crash-safely writes continuity around the authoritative manifest rename; every later mutation refreshes it to the same persisted revision/time, no-op preserves both, and recover repairs only from validated manifest without advancing time. Desk task remains lifecycle locator; release-evidence plus release-continuity are post-transfer authority and archive unchanged. Pre-resolution argv remains exact transfer-continuity vector with only the final task-relative out sentinel replaced.
- Read-only grammar is exactly `validate --manifest M --mode active|archive`; it has no writer/lock/mutation, re-roots by mode, and validates manifest/continuity/evidence/hashes/closed set/sidecar zero. Every success is one compact no-extra-fields JSON plus newline on stdout and empty stderr. Exact success objects are: init `{writerId}`; recover `{writerId,interrupted,repaired,abandoned,handoffPath}`; start-run `{releaseRun,status:'active'}`; start-stage `{stageRun,status:'running'}`; start-command `{attempt,reservationToken,executionHost,argv,envNames,stdoutPath,stderrPath,outPath,intentPath,armPath,dispatchPath}`; prepare-spawn `{generation,spawnToken,gatePath,releasePath,status:'prepared'}`; record-process `{generation,status:'recorded',pid,pgid,processStartIdentity}`; release-spawn `{generation,status:'released'}`; prepare-handoff `{status:'interrupted',handoffPath}`; prepare-dispatch `{status:'prepared',dispatchPath}`; materialize-ensure-outcome `{status:'materialized',outPath,sha256}`; finish-command-without-process `{status:'failed',warningCount:0,failureReason:'provider_indeterminate'|'provider_conflict'|'dispatch_ambiguity'}`; finish-command `{status:'passed'|'failed',warningCount,failureReason}`; start-agent-command `{attempt,runtimeEpoch,retryOrdinal,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt,prompt,promptSha256,requiredArtifactsSha256,outPath}`; finish-agent-command `{status:'passed'|'failed',reportPath}`; interrupt-agent-command `{status:'interrupted'}`; append-artifact, append-pre-release-artifact, and append-terminal-artifact `{path,sha256}`; transfer-continuity `{status:'ready'}`; set-merge-sha `{mergeSha}`; record-post-merge-disposition `{classification,dispositionPath,dispositionSha256,retryOrdinal}` or zero-mutation `{classification:'preflight_recovered',dispositionPath:null,dispositionSha256:null,retryOrdinal}`; resume-external-wait provider returns `{status:'waiting',waitPath,waitSha256,waitOrdinal,notBefore}` or `{status:'resolved',resolutionPath,resolutionSha256,owningUnit,runtimeEpoch:null,nextRuntimeEpoch:null}`; resolve-pre/post-merge-blocker and authorize-pre/post-merge-blocker return blocked `{status:'blocked',dispositionPath,dispositionSha256}` where legal or resolved `{status:'resolved',resolutionPath,resolutionSha256,owningUnit}`; start-agent-runtime-probe `{probeOrdinal,runtimeEpoch,prompt,promptSha256,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt}`; finish-agent-runtime-probe returns waiting `{status:'waiting',waitPath,waitSha256,waitOrdinal,notBefore}` or resolved `{status:'resolved',resolutionPath,resolutionSha256,owningUnit,runtimeEpoch,nextRuntimeEpoch}`; record-pre-merge-disposition `{status:'retry_same_unit'|'external_wait'|'human_blocker'|'fix_current_branch',dispositionPath,dispositionSha256,evidencePath,evidenceSha256,retryOrdinal}`; finish-stage passed `{status:'passed',evidencePath}` or failed `{status:'failed',evidencePath:null}`; supersede-run `{status:'superseded'}`; fail-run `{status:'failed'}`; finish-run `{status:'passed'}`; validate `{valid:true,releaseRun,status}`. Successor initialize-manifest and terminal recovery/authorization use their Round 59 exact result objects. Every listed object rejects extra fields; paths/nulls/numbers follow their named schemas. CLI failure is nonzero, empty stdout, and one sanitized `{ok:false,code}` stderr line with finite `argument|preflight|state|lock|schema|artifact|io|checkpoint`; executor wrapper obeys its separate 0/1/2 contract. All dependencies are injectable and Unit29.2 byte-tests every result.
- One-time initialization is the sole mutation without an already-existing manifest writerId. Exact `init --manifest M --identity-json J` validates canonical ReleaseTaskIdentity bytes, generates the proposed writerId, acquires the ordinary hard-link lock with that writerId, and executes only the token-qualified candidate/temp protocol above. Dead same-host recovery reuses that writerId and deterministically promotes exact complete initial bytes or removes only absent/zero-byte owned temp state; live/cross-host/malformed/multiple/nonzero-mismatch state fails closed. Initialization never creates the ordinary rewrite temp and may never replace an existing nonidentical valid or invalid manifest.
- After initialization, every mutating helper call must present the manifest's exact writerId and acquire exact sibling `release-evidence.v1.lock` before reading the manifest. Acquisition writes, fsyncs, and closes exact owner `{version:1,writerId,token,executionHost,hostname,pid,processStartIdentity,acquiredAt}` at unique `release-evidence.v1.lock.<token>.candidate`; it hard-links candidate to absent lock, fsyncs parent, unlinks candidate, and fsyncs again. Token is lowercase UUID-v4; executionHost is the frozen host hash; hostname is nonempty informational text; pid positive; processStartIdentity exact; time canonical. Existing lock fails closed. Release rereads same writerId/token/executionHost then unlinks/fsyncs. Crash recovery permits lock+candidate only when byte-identical, token/filename match, and lstat proves equal device/inode. Live or EPERM exact pid/start identity fails closed; dead-owner recovery is legal only when current executionHost equals owner executionHost and exact start-identity probe proves ESRCH/reuse, never from hostname equality. It then uses token-qualified stale rename/fsync/reacquire. Different inode/content, extra sidecar, different executionHost, malformed owner, changed identity/token, link/rename race, or unknown probe fails hard. Lone dead candidate/lock uses same exact-host route. Read-only validation never touches sidecars. Every terminal StageRun checkpoint requires sidecar zero. Thus same-writer concurrency is locked and resume can recover only a proven dead exact-host predecessor.
- The sole exception to ordinary cross-host lock/liveness rejection is pre-dispatch ensure handoff. It never steals a lock: it begins from the clean pushed arm checkpoint after sidecar zero and uses normal Git push as CAS against prepare-dispatch. A winning handoff transfers the same root's mutate_once eligibility; a winning dispatch forbids handoff and leaves every other host read-only for exact-effect observation only.
- `ReleaseRun` is exact `{run,status,failureReason,createdAt,endedAt,testedBaseSha,branchHeadSha,branchHeadTreeSha,mergeSha,stages}`: run positive/sequential; status `active|superseded|failed|passed`; failureReason is exact `dispatch_ambiguity|operator_abandonment|post_merge_finding|post_merge_repair` iff failed and null otherwise; endedAt null iff active; captured SHAs immutable; mergeSha null through Unit35 then immutable. `stages` is exact unit32..unit37 arrays. At most one StageRun and one command attempt are running. A stage starts only after latest preceding units pass. Superseding first terminalizes work. Retryable failed stage leaves parent active and may append after pushed checkpoint. SHA/code change supersedes only before mergeSha. Failed is nonretryable after all running work terminalizes; either post-merge reason requires the pushed successor journal, with post_merge_finding restricted to reviewer finding. Passed requires every latest unit stage passed and nonnull mergeSha; terminal runs never receive later stages or change status.
- Every `StageRun` is exact `{ run,status,startedAt,endedAt,startSha,endSha,commands,artifacts,evidence }`: run is positive/sequential within its unit; status is `running|failed|passed`; endedAt/endSha are null iff running; evidence is null for running/failed and required for passed. Units32-35 use branchHeadSha for both SHAs. Unit36 starts at branchHeadSha and may fail only before `set-merge-sha`; after mergeSha is persisted it remains the same running stage until the exact merge effect, outcome, and evidence are reconstructed and it passes at mergeSha. Unit37 starts/ends at mergeSha and may append a retry StageRun only when no code/SHA change is required. Timestamps are monotonic within their parent run. A failed stage is immutable; only a later sequential same-unit StageRun may retry it while the parent remains active and all preceding units' latest stages still pass.
- ProcessCommand terminal semantics are exact: status `running|interrupted|failed|passed`, endedAt null iff running, and running/interrupted have null exitCode/warningCount/failureReason. Ordinary passed requires exit:0, clean stream, exitCode 0, warningCount 0, terminalSource `process`, null reason, and complete output. Predispatch or post-generation materialized passed requires terminalSource `materialized`, exitCode null, warningCount 0, null reason, empty sanitized logs, exact typed output, and its exact authority/liveness proof. finish-command-without-process failed requires terminalSource `without_process`, null exitCode, warningCount 0, empty sanitized logs, and the matching provider_indeterminate/provider_conflict/dispatch_ambiguity reason. Failed precedence is `spawn > timeout > signal > sanitizer > exit > warning > output`; spawn/timeout/signal/sanitizer have null exit/warning, while exit/warning/output store observed exit and count. Thus clean exit-zero empty policy with output fails output; nonzero JSON command fails exit without passed-only fiction. Paths are task-relative/immutable/distinct. Warning classification strips ANSI, splits streams, and counts each line once for repository WARN/warning/[WARNING]/Node/React/console.warn patterns. Fixtures cover every named warning, dirty git output, partial/missing output, gate/record/release, spawn, timeout/TERM/KILL, signal, UTF-8, and NUL; unrecognized text is not guessed. Crash recovery interrupts only pre-dispatch process/agent after liveness proof. Post-dispatch process remains running while any released role-tagged member lives, materializes exact effect after every released role-tagged member dies, or on exact dispatch host appends a generation and sequentially re-enters identical idempotent argv after exact reconciliation; only deadline ambiguity may fail it. AgentCommand retry always uses fresh agent. No command/evidence/artifact is overwritten.
- Cross-file recovery is deterministic at every write boundary. All command-owned JSON/text/report files use sibling `<basename>.tmp`, file fsync, rename, and directory fsync; command-owned out trees are closed before `append-artifact`, whose exact path+hash operation is idempotent and legal only while that attempt remains running. `finish-command` requires the complete spec-declared tree only to pass, except the `continuity` policy requires the child output tree absent and creates its declared ReleaseContinuity/ContinuityTransfer files itself from the new terminal revision/time before appending them. On failure it still rejects every undeclared path, symlink, credential-bearing byte, or malformed file that claims to be a closed Artifact, but missing/partial declared outputs remain immutable diagnostics and do not prevent terminalization; only semantically closed files are appended. It atomically appends accepted semantic output plus both sanitized log hashes with terminal status and is idempotent for the same process-result/stream-status/hash set. A crash before manifest rename leaves the command running and recovery marks it interrupted while retaining its immutable files, and a crash after rename is already terminal. `finish-agent-command` writes the canonical clean or FINDINGS report first. If recovery finds the sole schema-valid report under a matching running AgentCommand, it reconstructs agentId/verdict from that report and completes the exact passed-CONVERGED or failed-FINDINGS manifest transition; no report means interrupted and any extra/malformed report fails closed. `finish-stage passed` writes exact evidence before its manifest transition; recovery seeing one sole schema-valid/hash-complete `unit-<U>-evidence.json` for a still-running stage completes that pass, while no evidence leaves the idempotent finish call retryable and malformed/extra evidence fails closed. Failed-stage/run transitions create no evidence file and are idempotent by exact terminal identity. Manifest-first transitions (`set-merge-sha`, supersede/fail/finish-run) repair release-continuity from the authoritative manifest on recovery. A valid manifest always wins over its orphan manifest temp; a missing/invalid manifest never infers state from artifacts. Recovery scans every exact stage/attempt root and rejects unknown directories/files, noncanonical temps, or a file that cannot be classified by these rules. Unit29.2 injects a crash before and after every create/write/fsync/rename/unlink/manifest/continuity operation and proves byte-stable repair, no overwrite, no duplicate remote mutation, and sidecar-zero terminal validation.
- Each StageRun owns exact directory `artifacts/release-<ReleaseRun.run>/unit-<32..37>/stage-<StageRun.run>/` and its passed aggregate evidence is exact `unit-<U>-evidence.json` there, written only by `finish-stage`; failed/running stages have no such file; each command attempt owns exact immutable child `<logicalName>/attempt-<Command.attempt>/`. Stdout/stderr are exact `stdout.log`/`stderr.log` in that child, and no report/evidence/output path reuses another release, stage, or attempt directory. Artifacts are exact `{ kind:"log"|"report"|"screenshot"|"json",path,sha256 }`; each path follows the task-relative rule, is unique in its StageRun, exists, is a regular file, and hash-matches when appended. The helper owns an immutable required-logical-name plan per unit. Unit32's exact order is `release-continuity-transfer`, `local-cleanup-assert-before`, `prisma-generate`, `local-prisma-push`, `local-d1-migrate-first`, `local-d1-migrate-second`, `api-playground-generate`, `api-playground-diff`, `script-typecheck`, `app-typecheck`, `app-coverage`, `workers-coverage`, `e2e`, `build`, `local-cleanup-assert-after`, `worktree-diff`, `index-diff`, `worktree-status`, `review-implementation`, `review-test`, `review-security-privacy`, `review-migration-config`; the first eighteen are ProcessCommand and the final four are AgentCommand with matching roles; no other Unit32 logical name is legal. Unit33's required order is `qa-deployment-before`, `qa-cleanup-reconcile`, `qa-cleanup-apply-before`, `qa-cleanup-assert-before`, `qa-preflight`, `qa-deploy-ensure`, `qa-deployment-after`, `qa-health`, `qa-api-smoke`, `qa-lifecycle-smoke`, and `qa-cleanup-assert-after`. Unit34's exact order is `fetch-drift`, `pr-ensure`, `review-implementation`, `review-design-visual`, `review-test`, `review-security-privacy`, `review-migration-config`, `review-api-compatibility`. Unit35 requires `branch-protection`, `exact-head-ci`, and `unresolved-comments`. Unit36's order is `fetch-protection-drift`, `qa-health`, `production-deployment-before`, and `merge-ensure`. Unit37 requires `ci-workflow-discovery`, `workflow-discovery`, `workflow-watch`, `deploy-job`, `release-artifact-download`, `production-deployment-after`, `production-health`, `production-readiness`, `production-cleanup-reconcile`, `production-cleanup-assert-before`, `production-api-smoke`, `production-lifecycle-smoke`, `production-visual-dogfood`, `production-visual-qa-review`, `production-visual-design-review`, `production-cleanup-assert-after`, and `continuation-scan` in that order; `production-visual-qa-review` and `production-visual-design-review` are AgentCommand and every other Unit37 name is ProcessCommand. No logical name outside these lists is legal. The three remote mutation slots are discover-first ensure ProcessCommands with immutable operation-intent evidence; their retry semantics are defined below and no separate remote-effect reconciliation logical name exists. Each smoke process receives exact fresh empty `<command-attempt-directory>/out/`. API smoke creates exactly `api-smoke-results.json`; a primary-only release lifecycle smoke creates exactly `smoke-results.json`, `01-recipes-after-signup.png`, `02-recipe-detail.png`, `03-cook-mode.png`, `04-shopping-list.png`, `05-account-settings.png`, `06-cook-session-mismatch.png`, `cleanup-outcome.json`, `cleanup-phase-primary.json`, `cleanup-pre-delete-rows.json`, `cleanup-user-delete.json`, `cleanup-post-delete-rows.json`, and `cleanup-retained-do-allowlist.json`. A recovered diagnostic run additionally creates `cleanup-phase-recovery.json` but cannot pass Unit33/37. Reports contain task-relative paths/basenames only. Recursively enumerating regular files below each release smoke `out/` must equal its closed set with no extras, and every output file is appended and hashed; final Unit33/37 `smokeArtifactPaths` is the sorted exact union of its 14 task-relative primary-only smoke files. Production visual dogfood uses exactly one disposable authenticated visual user plus anonymous contexts and writes exactly 38 files to its fresh primary-only `out/`: `visual-report.json`, `visual-metrics.json`, `absurdity-ledger.md`; screenshots `mobile-guest-home.png`, `desktop-guest-home.png`, `wide-guest-home.png`; mobile/desktop pairs for `cook-session`, `continue-cooking`, `saved-controls`, `saved-kitchen`, `saved-recipes`, `tag-authoring`, and `tag-discovery`; mobile/desktop pairs for each `tag-display-detail`, `tag-display-list`, `tag-display-card`, `developer-docs`, `api-playground`, and `mcp-guide`; and the same six cleanup JSON basenames. Every file is hashed and every structured/text file parses under its exact schema. After screenshots and before user deletion, the visual runner uses its shared cleanup writer for exact per-user residue discovery, sole purgeTarget terminal-204 confirmation, D1-zero wait, pre-delete rows/retained-DO allowlist, user deletion, post-delete D1-zero, and derived no-post-delete-owner-call oracle; it cannot delegate that proof to the final broad assertion. A passed stage has no running latest attempt; every required logical name's latest attempt passed; each ensure outcome cross-links its immutable intent/disposition and exact remote state; no undeclared logicalName exists; all artifact paths validate; and evidence is exact.
- The immutable ProcessSpec registry is part of `release-evidence.ts`, has no runtime-discovered argv, and is byte-tested against this paragraph. In templates `M,W,R,U,G,A,T,H,B,S,P,C,D,V` resolve from validated manifest/predecessor artifacts; C is the selected CiWorkflow.databaseId and D is the selected production Workflow databaseId. `QB=https://spoonjoy-v2-qa.mendelow-studio.workers.dev`, `PB=https://spoonjoy.app`, `X=identitySha256`, `Y=@release-attempt-path@`, `CJ=@cleanup-targets-json@`, `CA=CiWorkflow.runAttempt`, `O=@release-out@`, `F=@release-intent@`, `Q=@release-arm@`, and `Z=@release-dispatch@`. The PR constants are exact `PR_TITLE=Ship Clem feedback end to end` and `PR_BODY=Ships Clem's accepted feedback end to end; the reviewed execution contract is worker/tasks/2026-07-14-1313-doing-clem-feedback-e2e.md.` with no trailing newline; `PR_BODY_SHA256` is lowercase SHA-256 of those exact UTF-8 body bytes. Each entry is exact `logicalName[environmentPolicy,outputPolicy]=JSON argv`; flags/order are mandatory. `logs` has only reservation and sanitized logs; `empty` requires both empty; `json:<file>:<schema>` has one atomic parsed JSON in fresh out; `ensure:<file>:<schema>` makes start-command create intent/arm and reserve dispatch path, while prepare-dispatch may later create/append EnsureDispatch and a passed attempt adds exact outcome; `api-smoke`, `lifecycle-smoke`, and `visual` use their closed sets; `cleanup-reconcile` validates the exact aggregate/per-target closed set. Every failed/interrupted ensure retains intent/arm; authoritative dispatch is retained when prepared; handoff is legal only pre-dispatch with cross_host_handoff; only a never-pushed provisional tree is abandoned. Mutation authority is the first global dispatch per operationKey, not first arm or ReleaseRun. Failed attempts use status-dependent partial-output rules and cannot satisfy required slots. No other spec/argv/cwd/policy/file/conditional command is legal.
- Ensure ProcessSpecs contain F, Q, and Z exactly once as values of `--intent`, `--arm`, and `--dispatch`. Global operation identity is `operationKeySha256`, derived from the immutable operation-specific parameters below rather than ReleaseRun. Under the manifest lock, `start-command` scans every ReleaseRun for the same key; rejects missing files/hashes, malformed links, multiple roots or dispatches, a wrong operation/target, concurrent running authority, and any mode inconsistent with global history. With no authoritative dispatch it creates `mutate_once` for the first root, for a same-host successor after every prior same-key attempt is terminal before dispatch, or for the winning pre-dispatch handoff; all retain the first root hash and only one may be running. A terminal pre-dispatch attempt does not consume mutation authority. With any authoritative dispatch it creates only `reconcile_only` and links that dispatch. Start writes/hashes the current OperationIntent and EnsureArm, reserves but does not create the canonical dispatch path, appends both Artifacts with the running ProcessCommand, and replaces F/Q/Z with canonical absolute paths. For the first root, OperationIntent.rootIntentSha256 is null while EnsureArm.rootIntentSha256 and every outcome rootIntentSha256 equal the current intent hash; every successor stores/uses that nonnull first-root hash. Intent and arm become authoritative only when current origin/main contains the exact arm/running checkpoint; dispatch becomes authoritative only after its separate winning push. No non-ensure command may contain F/Q/Z. An ensure subprocess may read only its supplied intent/arm, current or root dispatch when present, own out directory, internal token/host environment, and typed remote read dependencies; a mutator dependency exists only for the exact current authoritative dispatch.
- Unit32 ProcessSpecs are: `release-continuity-transfer[base,continuity:release-continuity-transfer.json:ContinuityTransfer]=["pnpm","release:evidence","--","transfer-continuity","--manifest",M,"--writer-id",W,"--release-run",R,"--unit","32","--stage-run",G,"--tested-base-sha",T,"--branch-head-sha",H,"--branch-head-tree-sha",B,"--out",O]`; `local-cleanup-assert-before[base,logs]=["pnpm","cleanup:qa","--","--assert-clean"]`; `prisma-generate[base,logs]=["pnpm","prisma:generate"]`; `local-prisma-push[local-db,logs]=["pnpm","prisma:push"]`; both `local-d1-migrate-first[base,logs]` and `local-d1-migrate-second[base,logs]` use `["pnpm","exec","wrangler","d1","migrations","apply","DB","--local"]`; `api-playground-generate[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","api:playground:generate"]`; `api-playground-diff[base,empty]=["git","diff","--exit-code","--","app/lib/generated/api-v1-playground.ts"]`; `script-typecheck[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","typecheck:scripts"]`; `app-typecheck[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","typecheck"]`; `app-coverage[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","test:coverage"]`; `workers-coverage[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","test:workers:coverage"]`; `e2e[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","test:e2e"]`; `build[base,logs]=["pnpm","run","build"]`; `local-cleanup-assert-after[base,logs]=["pnpm","cleanup:qa","--","--assert-clean"]`; `worktree-diff[base,empty]=["git","diff","--exit-code"]`; `index-diff[base,empty]=["git","diff","--cached","--exit-code"]`; and `worktree-status[base,empty]=["git","status","--porcelain=v1","--untracked-files=all"]`.

- Unit34 ProcessSpecs are: `fetch-drift[github,json:git-drift.json:GitDrift]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-drift","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--branch","main","--tested-base-sha",T,"--source-sha",H,"--tree-sha",B,"--out",O]`; `pr-ensure[github,ensure:pull-request.json:PullRequest]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","ensure-pr","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--head-ref",I.branch,"--head-sha",H,"--base-ref","main","--base-sha",T,"--title",I.prTitle,"--body",I.prBody,"--intent",F,"--arm",Q,"--dispatch",Z,"--out",O]`, where I is the exact manifest ReleaseTaskIdentity.
- Unit35 ProcessSpecs are: `branch-protection[github,json:branch-protection.json:BranchProtection]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-protection","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--branch","main","--required-context","workers-coverage","--out",O]`; `exact-head-ci[github,json:checks.json:CheckSet]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-checks","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--pr-number",P,"--source-sha",H,"--out",O]`; `unresolved-comments[github,json:review-comments.json:ReviewThreadSet]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-review-comments","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--pr-number",P,"--source-sha",H,"--out",O]`.
- Unit36 ProcessSpecs are: `fetch-protection-drift[github,json:merge-readiness.json:MergeReadiness]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-merge-ready","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--pr-number",P,"--tested-base-sha",T,"--source-sha",H,"--tree-sha",B,"--required-context","workers-coverage","--out",O]`; `qa-health[base,json:health.json:HealthSeries]` is the Unit33 health argv; `production-deployment-before[cloudflare-rest,json:deployments.json:DeploymentSnapshot]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","capture-deployments","--environment","production","--script","spoonjoy-v2","--out",O]`; `merge-ensure[github,ensure:merge-outcome.json:MergeOutcome]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","merge-pr","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--pr-number",P,"--source-sha",H,"--tested-base-sha",T,"--tree-sha",B,"--method","squash","--intent",F,"--arm",Q,"--dispatch",Z,"--out",O]`.

`workflow-discovery[github,json:production-workflow-discovery.json:WorkflowDiscovery]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","discover-production","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--source-sha",S,"--workflow-path",".github/workflows/production-deploy.yml","--triggering-ci-database-id",C,"--triggering-ci-run-attempt",CA,"--out",O]`; `workflow-watch[github,json:production-workflow-terminal.json:Workflow]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","watch-workflow","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--run-id",D,"--run-attempt",RA,"--source-sha",S,"--workflow-path",".github/workflows/production-deploy.yml","--out",O]`; `deploy-job[github,json:deploy-job.json:DeployJob]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-deploy-job","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--run-id",D,"--run-attempt",RA,"--source-sha",S,"--job-name","deploy","--out",O]`; `release-artifact-download[github,json:production-release.json:ProductionRelease]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","download-artifact","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--run-id",D,"--run-attempt",RA,"--source-sha",S,"--tree-sha",B,"--artifact-name","mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts","--out",O]`; `production-deployment-after[cloudflare-rest,json:deployments.json:DeploymentSnapshot]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","capture-deployments","--environment","production","--script","spoonjoy-v2","--out",O]`; `production-health[base,json:health.json:HealthSeries]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","verify-health","--base-url",PB,"--source-sha",S,"--worker-version-id",V,"--out",O]`; `production-readiness[cloudflare-github,logs]=["pnpm","run","production:readiness"]`; `production-cleanup-reconcile[cloudflare-d1,cleanup-reconcile]=["node","scripts/smoke-live.mjs","--target-env","production","--reconcile-cleanup","--failed-attempts-json",CJ,"--release-identity-sha256",X,"--release-attempt-path",Y,"--out",O]`; `production-cleanup-assert-before[cloudflare,logs]` and `production-cleanup-assert-after[cloudflare,logs]` both use `["pnpm","cleanup:production","--","--assert-clean"]`; `production-api-smoke[base,api-smoke]=["node","scripts/smoke-api-live.mjs","--target-env","production","--base-url",PB,"--out",O]`; `production-lifecycle-smoke[cloudflare-d1,lifecycle-smoke]=["node","scripts/smoke-live.mjs","--target-env","production","--base-url",PB,"--release-identity-sha256",X,"--release-attempt-path",Y,"--out",O]`; `production-visual-dogfood[cloudflare-d1,visual]=["pnpm","run","production:visual-dogfood","--","--base-url",PB,"--source-sha",S,"--worker-version-id",V,"--release-identity-sha256",X,"--release-attempt-path",Y,"--out",O]`; `continuation-scan[github,json:continuation.json:Continuation]=["pnpm","release:provenance","--","continuation-scan","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--pr-number",P,"--source-sha",H,"--tree-sha",B,"--merge-sha",S,"--excluded-head-ref",RB,"--out",O]`. Here `RA` is the frozen selected positive runAttempt. Every `capture-deployments` ProcessSpec is byte-tested to use `cloudflare-rest`; no `capture-deployments` registration under `cloudflare` is legal.
- Registry JSON schemas are exact/no-extra-fields. `ContinuityTransfer` is defined above. `DeploymentSnapshot={environment,script,capturedAt,deployments}` uses `qa|production`, exact script, canonical time, sorted Deployment[]. `GitDrift={repo,branch,remoteBaseSha,testedBaseSha,sourceSha,treeSha,ancestor,clean,pushed}` requires exact identities and all true. Each ensure derives operationKeySha256 from canonical UTF-8 JSON of exact operation parameters: QA `{operation:'qa_deploy',targetSha,script:'spoonjoy-v2-qa',baseUrl:QA_BASE}`; PR `{operation:'pr_create',targetSha,repo,headRef:identity.branch,baseRef:'main',title:identity.prTitle,bodySha256}`; merge `{operation:'squash_merge',targetSha,repo,prNumber,testedBaseSha,treeSha,method:'squash'}`.
- `OperationIntent={version:1,releaseRun,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,reservationToken,executionHost,mode,rootIntentSha256,rootDispatchPath,createdAt}` uses exact identities; mode is `mutate_once|reconcile_only`; root is null only for the first-ever root, otherwise immutable global root; rootDispatchPath is task-relative/nonnull exactly when a prior authoritative dispatch exists. `EnsureArm={version:1,releaseRun,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,reservationToken,executionHost,intentSha256,rootIntentSha256,createdAt}` carries current/nonnullable-effective-root hashes. `EnsureProbe={version:1,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,observedAt,result,observation}` uses the provider-only observation union defined above and never carries disposition/intent/root hashes. `EnsureDispatch={version:1,releaseRun,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,reservationToken,executionHost,intentSha256,armSha256,rootIntentSha256,probe,dispatchedAt,reconcileDeadlineAt}` embeds exact absent_complete/null-observation probe; deadline is dispatchedAt+900000ms for QA and +300000ms otherwise. `EnsureHandoff={version:1,releaseRun,operation,operationKeySha256,targetSha,fromHost,toHost,currentIntentSha256,rootIntentSha256,fromAttempt,baseDeskSha,claimedAt}` is legal only from a pushed arm with no dispatch. At most one authoritative dispatch exists per key across all runs.
- `PullRequest={number,url,state:'OPEN',headRef,headSha,baseRef,baseSha,title,bodySha256,disposition,intentSha256,rootIntentSha256}` and `QaDeployOutcome={targetSha,disposition,intentSha256,rootIntentSha256,inventoryDisposition,deployment,health}` are materialized only by Unit29.2 from a hash-valid exact_effect observation plus current authority; `MergeOutcome={prNumber,state:'MERGED',sourceSha,testedBaseSha,squash,disposition,intentSha256,rootIntentSha256}` follows the same rule. Created requires current authoritative dispatch and the child that first created the effect; existing is first-read exact effect before dispatch; reconciled is exact effect after dispatch/replay. BranchProtection, CheckSet, ReviewThreadSet, MergeReadiness, and Continuation retain the exact schemas above. `HealthSeries={baseUrl,sourceSha,workerVersionId,samples}` uses exact base/source, nullable Worker version only for QA, and complete scheduled Health[]; pass evidence requires every sample HealthSuccess while failed attempts retain HealthFailure. Existing CI/workflow/deploy/release/smoke/visual schemas remain with the exact workflow conclusion and triggering-CI domains above. Every output is atomic, semantically parsed, status-closed, and contains no credential/free text.
- Remote mutation authority is global by operationKey, not ReleaseRun. `start-command` creates current intent/arm and a reserved dispatch path, scans all prior runs, and retains mutate_once eligibility across terminal pre-dispatch retries without changing the first root. With any global dispatch, current mode is reconcile_only and intent names rootDispatchPath. Before dispatch, `createReleaseExecutor` calls production `probeEnsureOperation` with operation argv plus null authority and receives exact EnsureProbe. exact_effect materializes only through predispatch_existing on the intent host; after dispatch it materializes only through postdispatch_reconciled on the dispatch host after liveness proof; absent_complete alone enters prepare-dispatch; indeterminate/conflict follow fixed read-only horizons. A crash before spawn is legal: pre-dispatch may retry/handoff; post-dispatch the dispatch host may replay after proving no live process, while another host may probe in memory only and wait/block. Every re-entry probes first. Unit29.2 validates files and passes canonical EnsureInvocationAuthority; the subprocess parses that object and rejects authority path flags, never revalidating files. The guarantees are one dispatch, at most one concurrent child, and at most one effective target state, not one historical spawn.
- Ensure observation has persisted operation-specific horizons. Before dispatch every operation uses EnsureArm.createdAt offsets `[0,5000,10000,20000,40000,80000,160000,290000]` and deadline +300000ms. After dispatch PR create and squash merge use the same offsets from EnsureDispatch.dispatchedAt and +300000ms; QA deploy uses `[0,5000,10000,20000,40000,80000,160000,320000,640000,890000]` and +900000ms to contain provider rollout plus exact-health verification. Each request timeout is `min(10000,deadline-now)`; no request starts when `now>=deadline`, and a response at/after deadline cannot pass. GitHub reads follow every provider cursor until none, with at most 100 pages and 10000 records; cap, truncation, rate limit, network/timeout, malformed response, stale health, or unavailable oracle is indeterminate. exact_effect requires every operation identity/target invariant and includes canonical typed outcome; absent_complete requires a complete authoritative no-effect read; any wrong-identity target is conflict. Pre-dispatch indeterminate/deadline fails without spawn or consuming authority, so a later same-host attempt or winning handoff retains the first root. Post-dispatch exact_effect materializes the outcome; absent_complete/indeterminate continues to the immutable operation deadline and the dispatch host may then persist dispatch_ambiguity only after exact process-identity liveness is false. Cross-host returns finite state without mutation and must wait for dispatch host or exact effect. Fake-clock/crash tests cover every sample, equality, page cap, classification, and remaining-horizon resume.
- Arm, handoff, dispatch, completion, and takeover are Git compare-and-swap checkpoints. The production executor commits/pushes/fetches/byte-verifies the arm before probing. A recoverer requests handoff by running the exact source-attempt `prepare-handoff --to-execution-host <target>` grammar; its winning push atomically persists EnsureHandoff and cross_host_handoff, thereby fencing the old host before the target can consume it. The target's next attempt verifies handoff bytes/hash, source arm, target executionHost, root, and no dispatch, then retains mutate_once. If dispatch wins first, handoff push rejects and every other host is permanently read-only for absence. Every rejected push fetches/schema-validates the winning descendant; a losing clean commit L converges through merge K whose tree equals winner O and parents are O,L, normal-pushes K, then ff-only advances local. No reset/force/loser tree survives. Tests race handoff immediately before dispatch, dispatch before spawn, crash before process record, child running, safe same-dispatch replay, delayed provider visibility, accepted-response/outcome loss, later ReleaseRun with same operationKey, and winner convergence; dispatch count and effective target-state count are each at most one globally, with at most one concurrent child.
- Every Desk checkpoint uses one Git recovery matrix. Every `git add`, `git write-tree`, `git commit-tree`, `git push`, `git fetch`, `git merge-base`, `git rev-parse`, and index/worktree probe in this matrix has exact cwd `/Users/arimendelow/desk`; no command inherits identity.repoCwd or the caller cwd. Journal O/C per declared path are exact BlobState (present bytes/hash or absent). Legal worktree/index pairs are `(O,O)|(C,O)|(C,C)` including absence; create/delete/move use those declared transitions. Recovery materializes C, invokes exact `["git","add","--",...binarySortedDeclaredPaths]`, verifies the index, obtains `newTree` through exact `["git","write-tree"]`, and creates commits only through `git commit-tree`; no porcelain commit or shell is legal. `CheckpointOperation` is exact `manifest|command|continuity|terminal_cleanup|desk_archive|track_archive|archive_validation|repair_index|successor|slugger|cancellation|convergence`. Normal checkpoint message stdin is exact UTF-8 with final LF: `spoonjoy release checkpoint <operation>\n\nSpoonjoy-Release-Version: 1\nSpoonjoy-Release-Identity-SHA256: <identitySha256>\nSpoonjoy-Release-Operation: <operation>\nSpoonjoy-Release-Old-Tree: <oldTree>\nSpoonjoy-Release-New-Tree: <newTree>\nSpoonjoy-Release-Journal-SHA256: <journalSha256>\n`; argv is `["git","commit-tree",newTree,"-p",oldCommit]`. Convergence message is the same form with operation `convergence` followed before the final LF by `Spoonjoy-Release-Winner-Commit: <winnerCommit>\nSpoonjoy-Release-Loser-Commit: <loserCommit>\n`; argv is `["git","commit-tree",winnerTree,"-p",winnerCommit,"-p",loserCommit]` in that parent order. The six base trailer values derive only from the hash-valid identity, operation owner, parent tree, verified candidate tree, and compact canonical journal bytes; convergence winner/loser derive from the fetched winner and clean rejected candidate. Commit-tree environment is exactly `PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin`, `HOME=/Users/arimendelow`, `USER=arimendelow`, `LOGNAME=arimendelow`, `LANG=C`, `LC_ALL=C`, `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Spoonjoy Release`, `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=release@spoonjoy.app`, `GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=<journal.updatedAt>`, `GIT_COMMITTER_NAME=Spoonjoy Release`, `GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL=release@spoonjoy.app`, `GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=<journal.updatedAt>` with every other key unset. Legal postcommit is the unique direct child with exact tree, parent list, message bytes, trailers, identity, and timestamp; remote is O, that child, or a schema-valid descendant. Convergence additionally requires winnerTree, creates no content change, and is legal only after a rejected normal push. Undeclared dirt, partial bytes, any other worktree/index pair, wrong ancestry/tree/message/trailer/time, swapped parents, same-tree unrelated commit, incompatible winner, or ambiguous valid commit fails closed. Exact ref publication is closed: after an initial `['git','fetch','--prune','origin','refs/heads/main:refs/remotes/origin/main']`, let L be `refs/heads/main` and R be `refs/remotes/origin/main`. If L equals R, oldCommit=L. If exact `['git','merge-base','--is-ancestor',L,R]` exits zero and the Desk worktree/index are clean, fast-forward locally with `['git','update-ref','refs/heads/main',R,L]` then `['git','read-tree','--reset','-u',R]`, verify HEAD/index/worktree exactly R, and set oldCommit=R. A crash between those two commands resumes from ref R plus the provably clean L worktree/index and runs only read-tree. If L is ahead/diverged, the worktree/index is dirty, either ref is missing, or ancestry is indeterminate, checkpoint creation fails closed before candidate bytes. After commit-tree emits one lowercase commit SHA candidateCommit, run `['git','update-ref','refs/heads/main',candidateCommit,oldCommit]`, then normal non-force `['git','push','--porcelain','origin','refs/heads/main:refs/heads/main']`, then `['git','fetch','--prune','origin','refs/heads/main:refs/remotes/origin/main']`; accepted push is exit zero with exactly one parsed porcelain `ok` ref record and empty warning/error stream, while rejection/response loss always proceeds to the exact fetch. If fetched origin/main equals candidateCommit, publication succeeded. If it is a schema-valid descendant preserving the exact candidate declared-path bytes, it is the winner and convergence rules apply. If it still equals oldCommit after a proven rejected/no-effect push, the exact same candidate may retry. Any other remote is rescanned under the race rules. Local advancement/recovery is explicit: a crash after update-ref adopts only the unique hash-valid candidate child; when fetched winner is R rather than candidateCommit, run `['git','update-ref','refs/heads/main',R,candidateCommit]` followed by `['git','read-tree','--reset','-u',R]` only after the clean O/C matrix proves no undeclared dirt, then verify HEAD/index/worktree equal R before rebuilding. Convergence candidate K uses the declared two-parent commit-tree argv, the same update-ref/push/fetch sequence with expected old local winner, and no force/lease. Every command uses the closed Desk environment; stdout/stderr/exit and ref/tree/index/worktree results are persisted before phase advance. Push response loss, local-ref-update crash, fetch failure, rejected winner, and read-tree recovery are fault-tested.
- Release-provenance exposes shared `normalizeReleaseProvenanceArgv(fullArgv)` requiring exact prefix `["pnpm","release:provenance","--"]`, and both executable wrapper and in-process tests pass only the returned suffix to `createReleaseProvenanceCli(deps)`. Every Unit33-37 release-provenance ProcessSpec, not only ensures, goes through this production normalizer/API and its actual stdout/stderr/output tree before finish-command ingestion. `verify-drift` runs exact fetch/read. `ensure-qa-deploy` classifies complete Cloudflare inventory plus exact QA health; its sole mutation child is `["pnpm","run","deploy:qa"]`, and exact health SHA remains authority even when inventory returns no delta. `ensure-pr` requires exact identity head/base/title/body hash discovery and its sole mutation child is `["gh","pr","create","--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--head",I.branch,"--base","main","--title",I.prTitle,"--body",I.prBody]`. `merge-pr` accepts exact merged outcome or complete readiness and its sole mutation child is `["gh","pr","merge",P,"--repo","spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2","--squash","--match-head-commit",H]`. There is no alternate/admin/merge/rebase/force/auto/delete-branch/reconciliation mutator. `capture-deployments` uses the exact endpoint/schema; every GitHub adapter pins identities and classifies incomplete pagination as indeterminate.
- The two Unit37 review commands are ordered immediately after `production-visual-dogfood` and before final production cleanup. Each owns its own command-attempt out directory outside the visual runner tree and writes exactly one canonical hashed report, respectively `visual-qa-review.md` and `visual-design-review.md`; neither may edit or add a file under the immutable 38-file runner output. `VisualReviewer={role,agentId,verdict:'clean',artifactPath,reportSha256,visualArtifactSetSha256}` is no-extra-fields with role `visual_qa|design_visual`, distinct canonical lowercase UUID agentIds, the matching task-relative report path/hash, and the exact immutable runner-set hash. Unit37 cannot pass without exactly one reviewer of each role, both latest command attempts passed, both canonical reports parse/hash/cross-link, and zero open finding.
- Every Unit37 StageRun failure decision after merge is first persisted by exact derived-only `record-post-merge-disposition --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt none|A`. It writes/hashes/pushes immutable stage-owned `post-merge-failure-disposition.v1.json`, creates its own sanitized capability evidence when required, appends both hashes, refreshes continuity atomically, and is byte-idempotent; failed StageRun, disposition, and capability bytes never gain resolution fields after terminalization. `PostMergeTerminalCode` is exact `credential_missing|billing_required|platform_capability_missing|agent_runtime_unavailable|review_findings|github_rate_limited|github_5xx|cloudflare_5xx|dns_failure|network_timeout|eventual_consistency|cleanup_incomplete|spawn|timeout_term|timeout_kill|signal|sanitizer_invalid_utf8|sanitizer_nul|exit_nonzero|warning|output_invalid|provider_indeterminate|provider_conflict|dispatch_ambiguity|validation_failed|configuration_failed|provenance_failed`. Exact no-extra-fields `PostMergeFailureDisposition={version:1,releaseRun,unit:37,stageRun,logicalName,attempt,commandMode,terminalCode,evidencePath,evidenceSha256,retryOrdinal,classification,maxRetries,runFailureReason,repairCode,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,createdAt}` and has no resolution member. `human_blocker` is discriminated as preflight with `attempt:null,commandMode:'preflight'` or process with positive attempt and `commandMode:'process'`; credential/billing/platform codes alone qualify, while agent runtime is external_wait. Reviewer FINDINGS is repair_successor/post_merge_finding/review_finding; provider/network ordinals 1 and 2 are transient_retry/maxRetries 2; ordinal 3+ is external_wait; cleanup_incomplete ordinals 1 and 2 retry and ordinal 3 becomes repair_successor/post_merge_repair/cleanup; ordinary code defects use the exact repair mapping. If an attempt-none preflight is available when rerun, no disposition or evidence is written and the CLI returns exact `preflight_recovered`, allowing a fresh same-unit StageRun. Retry ordinals use persisted disposition history only; wait ordinals use separate wait-checkpoint history below. Unknown/forged combinations reject without mutation.
- Post-merge disposition derivation is total over `(terminalCode,retryOrdinal,logicalName,commandMode,environmentPolicy)` and uses no default branch. Exact tuple families are: `credential_missing|billing_required|platform_capability_missing -> {classification:'human_blocker',maxRetries:0,runFailureReason:null,repairCode:null}`; `agent_runtime_unavailable -> {classification:'external_wait',maxRetries:2,runFailureReason:null,repairCode:null}` after its two journal-owned attempts; `review_findings -> {classification:'repair_successor',maxRetries:0,runFailureReason:'post_merge_finding',repairCode:'review_finding'}`; `github_rate_limited|github_5xx|cloudflare_5xx|dns_failure|network_timeout|eventual_consistency|provider_indeterminate|provider_conflict|dispatch_ambiguity|spawn|timeout_term|timeout_kill|signal -> {classification:'transient_retry',maxRetries:2,runFailureReason:null,repairCode:null}` for ordinals 1..2 and `{classification:'external_wait',maxRetries:2,runFailureReason:null,repairCode:null}` for ordinal 3+; `cleanup_incomplete -> transient_retry/maxRetries:2/nulls` for ordinals 1..2 and `repair_successor/0/post_merge_repair/cleanup` for ordinal 3+; `sanitizer_invalid_utf8|sanitizer_nul|exit_nonzero|warning|output_invalid|validation_failed -> repair_successor/0/post_merge_repair/production_validation`; `configuration_failed -> repair_successor/0/post_merge_repair/configuration`; and `provenance_failed -> repair_successor/0/post_merge_repair/provenance`. Typed provider/credential/configuration signals take precedence over generic exit/output/process codes, so an adapter-positive capability failure never falls through to exit_nonzero. Capability binding is also total: preflight credential/platform evidence names the exact blocked CapabilityEntry and its declared probe; process `pr-ensure` maps to `github_pr_write/process:pr-ensure`, `merge-ensure` to `github_merge_write/process:merge-ensure`, QA deploy to `cloudflare_workers_edit/process:qa-deploy-ensure`, D1 cleanup/reconcile/lifecycle operations to `cloudflare_d1_edit/process:<logicalName>`, and a provider-positive Cloudflare billing response to `cloudflare_billing/process:<logicalName>`. Exact process-only capability domain is `github_pr_write|github_merge_write|cloudflare_workers_edit|cloudflare_d1_edit|cloudflare_billing`; it never appears in preflight available proofs. A terminal code/context without exactly one table row or one capability mapping is schema-invalid and writes nothing. Tests enumerate the Cartesian legal rows, every retry boundary, every illegal context, and precedence over generic process failures.
- Units32-36 use a separate pre-merge lifecycle and can never create a repair successor. Every failed stage first invokes exact derived-only `record-pre-merge-disposition --manifest M --writer-id W --release-run R --unit 32|33|34|35|36 --stage-run G --logical-name L --attempt none|A`, which writes/hashes/pushes one immutable stage-owned `pre-merge-failure-disposition.v1.json` before any retry or fix. Exact no-extra-fields `PreMergeFailureDisposition={version:1,releaseRun,unit:32|33|34|35|36,stageRun,logicalName,attempt,commandMode,terminalCode,evidencePath,evidenceSha256,classification,retryOrdinal,maxRetries,createdAt}` uses the same closed terminal-code domain, `attempt:null,commandMode:'preflight'` versus positive-attempt `commandMode:'process'|'agent'` discrimination, capability-evidence binding, and terminal-code precedence as PostMergeFailureDisposition, with classification `retry_same_unit|external_wait|human_blocker|fix_current_branch`. Credential, billing, and platform-capability codes are human_blocker/maxRetries 0; agent-runtime unavailability is external_wait and agent mode only after its two epoch-scoped attempts; provider/network/process transients are retry_same_unit/maxRetries 2 at ordinals 1..2 and external_wait at ordinal 3+; cleanup_incomplete is retry_same_unit at ordinals 1..2 and fix_current_branch at ordinal 3+; review findings (agent mode only), sanitizer/output/warning/exit, validation, configuration, and provenance failures are fix_current_branch/maxRetries 0. Unknown tuples reject. Before set-merge-sha, Unit36 obeys this matrix, but fix_current_branch is legal only after authoritative complete unmerged/no-effect observation; after set-merge-sha it can only reconcile/pass. retry_same_unit appends a fresh same-unit StageRun at unchanged HEAD; external_wait uses trigger-specific provider or agent-runtime recovery. A preflight human blocker uses resolve-pre-merge-blocker and the exact immutable PreMergeBlockerResolution only after a fresh successful read-only probe. A process-positive edit/billing blocker rejects read-only resolution and requires authorize-pre-merge-blocker; its operator authorization permits one fresh attempt without claiming capability success. fix_current_branch authorizes a tracked fix, supersedes the old ReleaseRun, and restarts Unit32. No Unit32-36 path writes post-merge repair files, initializes a successor, or enters repaired release-terminal.
- A repair_successor never reruns the defective tree without a reviewed repair. After the failed Unit37 stage disposition or passed-Unit37 terminal disposition push, Work Merger invokes strict measured `pnpm release:terminal -- --mode successor` actions. Exact `RepairBrief={code,artifactPath,artifactSha256,reviewerRole,promptSha256,dispositionPath,dispositionSha256,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,affectedSurfaces}` retains finite code `review_finding|provenance|cleanup|configuration|production_validation`; `affectedSurfaces={ui:boolean,api:boolean,migrationConfig:boolean}` is derived from the failed command/artifact registry and cannot be caller-supplied. The sole exact `PostMergeSuccessorAttempt`, suite-attempt, phase/null, runtime-wait, and implementation-evidence schemas are the Round 52 declarations below. The successor doing doc contains implementation-only units and excludes Units32-37. Crash recovery validates/completes or interrupts one bounded suite attempt before a fresh ordinal, never skips or duplicates a phase. Only implementation_complete may initialize the successor manifest and run universal Units32-37 once, followed by its PR/merge/production/terminal/archive/index flow.
- Successor completion evidence is a closed registry, never an arbitrary path/hash collection. Exact `SuccessorImplementationRegistry={version:1,successorKey,doingPath,doingSha256,groups,reviewRoles,createdAt}` has BINARY-sorted unique `groups:{groupId,testUnitId,implementationUnitId,verificationUnitId}[]` parsed from the approved successor doing doc: every implementation group appears exactly once as contiguous status-bearing `a` test, `b` implementation, and `c` verification headers with What/Output/Acceptance; no other implementation unit shape is legal. `reviewRoles` is the BINARY-sorted set containing `implementation`, `migration_config`, `security_privacy`, and `test`, plus `design_visual` iff RepairBrief.affectedSurfaces.ui and `api_compatibility` iff RepairBrief.affectedSurfaces.api; therefore the only legal global order is the applicable subsequence of `api_compatibility|design_visual|implementation|migration_config|security_privacy|test`. migration_config remains required and records clean no-impact when unaffected. `SuccessorImplementationUnitReceipt={version:1,successorKey,unitId,phase:'test'|'implementation'|'verification',status:'completed',sourceBaseSha,sourceHeadSha,treeSha,redEvidencePath,redEvidenceSha256,commands,artifactPaths,artifactSetSha256,completedAt}` exists exactly once for every registry unit. All sourceHeadSha/treeSha equal the final clean pushed implementation head/tree and sourceBaseSha equals implementationBaseSha; test phase alone has nonnull immutable red evidence proving its expected nonzero assertion before implementation, while implementation/verification have both red fields null. `commands:{argv,expected:'red'|'green',exitCode,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,warningCount}[]` is nonempty: test has at least one red command with exitCode 1..255 and then its final green replay; all other expected green commands have exitCode zero; every warningCount is zero. `SuccessorReviewReport={version:1,successorKey,role,agentId,verdict:'clean',sourceHeadSha,treeSha,completedAt}` exists exactly once for every registry reviewRole with distinct canonical agent ids. `SuccessorVerificationArtifact={version:1,successorKey,unitId,command,exitCode:0,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,warningCount:0,sourceHeadSha,treeSha,createdAt}` exists at least once for every verification unit and exactly matches its receipt commands/artifacts. Implementation evidence arrays must be the BINARY-sorted normalized task-relative paths of these exact closed sets; all bytes schema/hash-validate, all unit IDs/roles equal the registry with no omission/extra/duplicate, every source/tree equals the final implementation head/tree, every group is complete, and every set hash covers compact canonical `{path,sha256}[]` with no final LF. `reviewReportPaths` points to the complete projected SuccessorReviewReport bytes; the enclosing `{path,sha256}` review set supplies the only report hashes, so no report self-hashes. The registry path is exact task-relative `artifacts/post-merge-successor/implementation-registry.v1.json`; unit receipts/red evidence live only under `artifacts/post-merge-successor/units/`, review reports only under `artifacts/post-merge-successor/reviews/`, verification artifacts only under `artifacts/post-merge-successor/verification/`, and coverage only under its declared sibling directory. Every one is appended to the successor manifest as a PreReleaseArtifact before implementation evidence, so terminal ordinary-file validation sees no task-root extras.
- Changed coverage scope is derived independently from coverage instrumentation and retained as an exact closed artifact set. Exact no-extra-fields `CoveragePathSet={version:1,kind:'diff'|'production_executable'|'instrumented',paths,pathsSha256,createdAt}` uses BINARY-sorted unique repo-relative paths and hashes compact canonical `paths` bytes with no final LF. The sole basenames under task-relative `artifacts/post-merge-successor/coverage/` are `coverage-diff-paths.json`, `coverage-production-executables.json`, `coverage-instrumented-paths.json`, `coverage-scope.json`, `coverage-total.json`, `coverage-changed.json`, `coverage-command-results.json`, and `coverage-summary.json`. Exact no-extra-fields `CoverageScope={version:1,diffPathSetPath,diffPathSetSha256,productionExecutablePathSetPath,productionExecutablePathSetSha256,instrumentedPathSetPath,instrumentedPathSetSha256,changedExecutablePaths,changedExecutableSetSha256,allChangedExecutablePathsInstrumented:true,createdAt}` cross-hashes the first three files. `CoverageTotal={version:1,metrics,createdAt}` carries exact CoverageMetricSet. `CoverageChanged={version:1,changed,coverageScopePath,coverageScopeSha256,createdAt}` carries exact ChangedCoverage and hashes CoverageScope. `CoverageCommandSet={version:1,commands,createdAt}` carries the exact nonempty zero-warning CoverageSummary command array. `diff` is exact `git diff --name-only implementationBaseSha...implementationHeadSha`. The independent production registry is every tracked regular file matching `app/**/*.{ts,tsx}`, `workers/**/*.{ts,tsx}`, or `scripts/**/*.{ts,mts,mjs}`, plus present root files `vite.config.ts`, `vitest.config.ts`, `playwright.config.ts`, and `react-router.config.ts`, minus `**/*.d.ts` and exact path segments `test`, `tests`, `__tests__`, `fixtures`, `generated`, `node_modules`, `coverage`, and build-output directories; classification never reads instrumentation. changedExecutablePaths is the intersection of diff and production paths; every member must occur in the independent instrumented set. For both measured and not_applicable, changedExecutableSetSha256 hashes compact canonical changedExecutablePaths bytes. Measured is required for a nonempty set and reports positive 100% metrics over exactly it; not_applicable requires an empty set and the hash of exact bytes `[]`. `coverage-summary.json` contains exact CoverageSummary and is the sole value legal for PostMergeImplementationEvidence.coverageSummaryPath at `artifacts/post-merge-successor/coverage/coverage-summary.json`; CoverageSummary.artifacts is exactly the BINARY-sorted seven-entry subordinate `{path,sha256}` set for every other basename in that directory, excludes the summary itself to avoid a self-hash, and permits no substitution or extras; its commands, total, and changed members byte-equal the command, total, and changed artifact payloads. Every path/hash cross-link, set membership, classifier exclusion, metric, and no-extra-fields boundary is independently validated.
- The predecessor remains terminal-pending until the successor final receipt/index are pushed/read back; its frozen archive state may be validating_active, archive_in_progress, or done_archived_pending. The successor Work Merger invokes repair-intermediate at positive predecessor depth or repair-root at depth zero.
