The final control envelope repeats the same pair. Mixed nullability, field drift, attempt mutation, missing archived proof, kept-active locator, or receipt projection mismatch rejects.

### Round 66 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final authority for descriptor-loaded spawn code, adapter-state evidence, invalid-output recovery, literal retry prompts, rollover journal states, checkpoint hash preimages, terminal bootstrap, and track-move fencing.

- Spawn-gate code executes from authenticated descriptors, never a mutable path. Terminal validator creation emits standalone no-external-import CommonJS `release-spawn-gate.bundle.cjs`; exact no-extra-fields `SpawnSourceIdentity={device,inode,byteLength,sha256}` binds its no-follow regular-file stat and complete bytes to the validator source tree. Before gate authorization, release-terminal opens the bundle twice with `O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW`, requires two independent open descriptions at offset zero, fstats and hashes each with position-preserving reads, and requires both identities byte-equal the frozen expected identity. Exact gate argv is `[node.absolutePath,'-','--','--gate',gatePath,'--release',releasePath,'--startup',gateStartupPath,'--gate-spawn',gateSpawnPath,'--child-ready',childReadyPath,'--registration-bundle',actualBundlePath,'--registration-bundle-sha256',actualBundleSha256,'--spawn-token',spawnToken,'--child-source-fd','3','--',...registeredEffectArgv]`; registration bundle sentinel value indices remain 14 and 16. Gate source descriptor is stdin and the independently opened child descriptor is inherited only as fd 3; gate closes neither before readiness. Exact child argv is `[node.absolutePath,'-','--','--child','--role','child','--child-ready',childReadyPath,'--registration-bundle',actualBundlePath,'--registration-bundle-sha256',actualBundleSha256,'--spawn-token',spawnToken,'--',...registeredEffectArgv]`; sentinel value indices remain 9 and 11. The gate remaps inherited fd 3 to child stdin and closes its copy only after successful spawn. registeredEffectArgv byte-equals the already-declared effect registry array and is never shell-expanded. No process opens the source path.

- Final no-extra-fields `ProcessLaunchSpec={argv,cwd,sourceIdentity,sourceFdRole,registrationBundlePathIndex,registrationBundleSha256Index}` uses sourceFdRole gate_stdin|child_stdin and hashes the sentinel-normalized descriptor launch, while final `ProcessReady={version:1,platform,pid,pgid,spawnToken,role,instanceToken,launchedAtUnixNs,argvBufferBytes,title,registeredArgvSha256,sourceIdentity,readyAt}` byte-equals the matching registration's launch-spec hash and source identity. ProcessMemberRegistration adds sourceIdentity after registeredArgvSha256. Parent ProcessGateSpawn adds gateSourceIdentity and childSourceIdentity, both exact, before spawnedAt. A ready record is accepted only from the process executing the descriptor whose identity the parent already authenticated; bundle-path replacement after either open cannot change those bytes. The validator path may be removed only after both ready records and child_recorded are pushed/read back. Tests race replacement before/between/after opens, symlink/nonregular input, inode/device/hash/length drift, shared-offset descriptors, wrong fd mapping, early close, child substitution, malformed loaded-source attestation, and deletion before readiness. The Round 65 path-only TrustedSourceFile/ProcessLaunchSpec rule is invalid.

- Runtime probes encode the complete adapter domain. Final SuccessorAgentRuntimeProbe status is absent|paused|running|completed|errored|cancelled. absent is legal only for lifecycle probe before create-once preparation, has agentId and returnedBytesSha256 null, and after pushed/read-back evidence authorizes only preparation for the same invocationToken. paused is lifecycle-only, has nonnull agentId, null returned hash, and authorizes only immutable dispatch receipt publication or release/recovery for that same paused runtime. running has nonnull agentId and null returned hash. completed has nonnull agentId and returnedBytesSha256; errored|cancelled have nonnull agentId and null returned hash. Cancellation probes permit only running|completed|errored|cancelled and retain their global 1..3 ordinal budget. absent/paused never authorize cancellation, cleanup, interruption, attempt replacement, or another invocation token. Every suite/review status transition consumes exactly the longest contiguous typed probe prefix; this matrix replaces every narrower Round 65 status rule.

- Invalid completed output is terminal evidence for every runtime owner. Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorRuntimeTermination={version:1,successorKey,runtimeKind,suiteAttemptOrdinal,reviewAttemptOrdinal,role,dispatchPath,dispatchSha256,agentId,outcome,cancellationIntentPath,cancellationIntentSha256,cancellationReceiptPath,cancellationReceiptSha256,terminalProbePath,terminalProbeSha256,returnedReportPath,returnedReportSha256,runtimeCheckpointPath,runtimeCheckpointSha256,completedAt}` replaces SuccessorSuiteRuntimeTermination and SuccessorReviewRuntimeTermination. runtimeKind is planner_suite|doer_suite|planner_review|implementation_review with exact review nullability. outcome is runtime_errored|runtime_cancelled|invalid_output. errored/cancelled use the existing cancellation matrix and null returned-report fields; invalid_output requires a completed probe, null cancellation fields, and one immutable sanitized runtime copy whose bytes fail only that owner's declared output grammar. Exact suite paths are `suite/<planner|doer>/attempt-<N>-termination.v1.json` and `suite/<planner|doer>/attempt-<N>-runtime-invalid-output.txt`; planner-review paths are `suite/planner/attempt-<N>-review-<R>-termination.v1.json` and `suite/planner/attempt-<N>-review-<R>-runtime-invalid-output.txt`; implementation-review paths are its exact D/R/role root's `termination.v1.json` and `runtime-invalid-output.txt`. The invalid-output path is absent for other outcomes. Planner-suite, doer-suite, and planner-review termination interrupts and registers the owning suite with matching terminalOutcome; planner-review invalid output therefore advances to the next planner suite ordinal rather than inventing a fourth role ordinal. Implementation-review termination registers under its D/R/role root and alone authorizes R+1 in the same doer suite. Doer suite interruption always freezes the classified ledger/runtime checkpoint; planner suite interruption first completes its declared snapshot cleanup. PreReleaseArtifact/SuiteArtifactRef add runtime_termination and runtime_invalid_output. Prior-outcome sets contain the suite_attempt plus termination and the already-declared doer checkpoint/ledger pair when applicable. No malformed completed runtime can complete, disappear, or dead-end.

- Planner retry prompt authority is literal. Final exact UTF-8 one-final-LF bytes are `Spoonjoy repair successor planner v2\nsuccessor_key: <K>\nsource_path: <sourcePath>\nrepair_briefs_json: <briefsJson>\nrepair_brief_set_sha256: <briefSetSha256>\nprior_planner_outcomes_json: <priorJson>\nprior_planner_outcome_set_sha256: <priorSetSha256>\nplanning_path: <planningPath>\ndoing_path: <doingPath>\ncontinuity_path: <continuityPath>\nworktree: <worktreePath>\nskill: work-planner\nrules: preserve operator-locked scope; consume only the bound immediately-prior outcome set; create only implementation red/green/verification units; exclude Units32-37; run fresh reviewer convergence; write exactly the named planning and doing paths; return only validated artifact locators\n`. Ordinal one uses priorJson exact `[]`; every later ordinal uses the exact BINARY-path-sorted compact canonical `{path,sha256}[]` immediately-prior outcome set and its byte hash. findings|snapshot_conflict|promotion_conflict use their already-declared sets; runtime_errored|runtime_cancelled|invalid_output use exact prior suite_attempt plus SuccessorRuntimeTermination. The final planner history domain includes all six outcomes before the latest attempt, with no carry-forward approval, omitted termination, free-form prompt line, or undefined corresponding version.

- Branch conflict and rollover are representable journal states. Final no-extra-fields `PostMergeSuccessorAttempt={version:1,successorKey,rolloverPredecessorPath,rolloverPredecessorSha256,predecessorManifest,predecessorReleaseRun,predecessorStageRun,dispositionKind,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,dispositionPath,dispositionSha256,bootstrapPath,bootstrapSha256,phase,taskPath,taskSeedPath,taskSeedSha256,identityPath,identitySha256,planningPath,planningSha256,doingPath,doingSha256,handoffPath,handoffSha256,manifestPath,manifestSha256,manifestWriterId,suiteAttempts,runtimeWaitPaths,runtimeWaitSetSha256,implementationEvidencePath,implementationEvidenceSha256,branchConflictPath,branchConflictSha256,rolloverPath,rolloverSha256,replacementSuccessorKey,replacementJournalPath,replacementJournalSha256,createdAt,updatedAt}` replaces every prior form. Original attempts have null rollover-predecessor pair; fresh rollover attempts require both nonnull and hash-valid. Normal phases through implementation_complete retain their final matrix and keep the seven conflict/rollover/replacement fields null. branch_conflict is terminal from bootstrap or implementation_running, preserves the longest legal prefix, adds only the conflict pair, and permits no predecessor suite/runtime/product-branch/task mutation; the sole legal continuation is the read-only absence observation, rollover receipt, and fresh replacement prepared journal. rolled_over preserves that state and adds rollover pair plus replacement key/journal pair. The replacement journal is already pushed/read-back in prepared with rolloverPredecessorPath/hash equal the predecessor rollover receipt; no successor can cite itself.

- Conflict derivation and collision observations are exact and acyclic. conflictKind is bootstrap|doer_transition. acceptedBaseSha is implementationBaseSha for bootstrap and the conflicted transition's oldProductHeadSha for doer_transition; observedRemoteSha is the conflict receipt's exact 40-hex remote. `rolloverSeedSha256` hashes compact canonical `{version:1,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictKind,conflictReceiptPath,conflictReceiptSha256,acceptedBaseSha,observedRemoteSha,rolloverOrdinal}`. successorSlug is `<predecessorSuccessorSlug>-rollover-<first12 rolloverSeedSha256>`, branch is `worker/<successorSlug>`, worktreePath is `/Users/arimendelow/Projects/spoonjoy-v2-<successorSlug>`, and taskPath is `spoonjoy-v2/<successorSlug>/task.md`. Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorRolloverAbsenceObservation={version:1,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictReceiptPath,conflictReceiptSha256,rolloverOrdinal,rolloverSeedSha256,branch,worktreePath,taskPath,remoteBranchCommand,localBranchState,worktreeState,taskState,outcome:'absent',observedAt}` requires registered ls-remote no-match exit 2/empty streams, local ref absent, no-follow filesystem absence, and Desk-tree absence. Final rolloverKeySha256 hashes compact canonical `{rolloverSeedSha256,absenceObservationPath,absenceObservationSha256}` and is the new successorKey. Final replacement `SuccessorBranchConflictRollover={version:1,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictKind,conflictReceiptPath,conflictReceiptSha256,acceptedBaseSha,observedRemoteSha,rolloverOrdinal,rolloverSeedSha256,absenceObservationPath,absenceObservationSha256,rolloverKeySha256,successorKey,branch,worktreePath,taskPath,createdAt}` binds both stages without self-reference. Collision preserves its read-only observation and increments ordinal; no path is inferred. PreReleaseArtifact kinds add branch_conflict|branch_conflict_rollover and the predecessor/fresh PostMerge histories cross-link them. This is the complete state/derivation/lineage authority for the Round 65 rollover action.

- Grouped checkpoint hashes have literal preimages. checkpointUnitIds is the exact registry-order string array `[testUnitId,implementationUnitId]` for red_green or `[verificationUnitId]` for verification; checkpointUnitSetSha256 is lowercase SHA-256 of those exact compact canonical UTF-8 JSON bytes with no final LF. unitReceipts is the exact same-order object array with field order unitId,receiptPath,receiptSha256; unitReceiptSetSha256 hashes its compact canonical UTF-8 bytes with no final LF. Neither array is BINARY-resorted. Every receipt in one checkpoint repeats the same checkpoint array/hash; `--units-json` byte-equals its preimage. Empty, reordered, duplicated, extra, alternate field order, escaped-equivalent/noncanonical JSON, or hash drift rejects.

- Terminal execution has a retained trusted bootstrap before canonical-main ownership. `release-terminal.ts` adds bootstrap-only grammar `pnpm release:terminal -- --mode bootstrap --manifest M --writer-id W` invoked from identity.repoCwd while the clean pushed feature worktree still exists; normal/repaired/successor effects remain forbidden in this invocation. Its first effect is pushed/read-back Desk-control exact `TerminalBootstrapAttempt={version:1,identitySha256,executionHost,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,sourcePath,sourceSha256,bundlePath,bundleTempPath,phase,bundleSha256,commandResults,startedAt,updatedAt}` with phase prepared, null bundle hash, empty results, and both host paths absent. sourcePath is exact absolute `<identity.repoCwd>/scripts/release-terminal.ts`; sourceSha256 equals that file's Git blob bytes at sourceHeadSha. TrustedToolchain adds esbuildEntry:TrustedExecutable. Exact bundle argv is `[node.absolutePath,esbuildEntry.absolutePath,sourcePath,'--bundle','--platform=node','--format=cjs','--packages=bundle','--outfile='+bundleTempPath]` from identity.repoCwd under the frozen terminal bootstrap environment; Node built-ins alone may remain external. Exact smoke argv is `[node.absolutePath,bundlePath,'--bootstrap-self-test']` from a token-qualified empty mode-0700 directory and must return zero/zero warnings with no provider, Desk, product, or host-control mutation. The script validates every source blob against sourceHeadSha, fsyncs/renames the mode-0600 bundle, and atomically advances bundled then validated. Every registered command/result and temp/rename/fsync boundary is journaled and recoverable; a pre-attempt module-load failure has zero effect and may relaunch from the still-present worktree.

- Exact `TerminalBootstrapReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,executionHost,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,sourcePath,sourceSha256,bundlePath,bundleSha256,node:TrustedExecutable,commandResults,validatedAt}` atomically replaces the validated attempt and is pushed/read back before terminal ownership begins. Bootstrap success is exact `{mode:'bootstrap',receiptPath,receiptSha256,bundlePath,bundleSha256}`. Work Merger then launches only `[node.absolutePath,bundlePath,...normal|repaired|successor exact flags]` from canonical `/Users/arimendelow/Projects/spoonjoy-v2`; the bundle validates the receipt/hash/inode before parsing and immediately chdirs canonical. The feature worktree cannot be removed before receipt publication; after validator creation, recovery may additionally verify the byte-identical validator copy, but the host-control bundle remains the sole launch authority through recursive successors, cleanup, archive, Slugger, and final result. It is removed only after the final ReleaseTerminalResult and terminal Desk state are pushed/read back, when no retry is legal. Missing canonical package/script entries are irrelevant. The old direct canonical `pnpm release:terminal` owner is invalid; the same measured release-terminal source and coverage gate own bootstrap plus the three effect modes, so the fourteen/fifteen executable registry does not change.

- Track move fencing is external, acyclic, and recoverable before host-journal creation. Final TrackArchiveLinearizationAttempt adds `checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256` immediately after phase and adds phase fence_published. prepared/push_inflight have all three null. After push_inflight commit/fetch/readback yields checkpointDeskSha C, release-terminal freezes exact no-extra-fields `TrackLinearizationFence={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,trackGeneration,scanDeskSha,pushInflightAttemptPath,pushInflightAttemptSha256,checkpointDeskSha,publishedAt}` at deterministic sibling `attempt-<N>-fence.v1.json`. One atomic Desk commit with parent C adds that fence and rewrites only the attempt to fence_published with checkpointDeskSha C and fence path/hash. The fence hashes the prior push_inflight attempt bytes, while the new attempt hashes the fence, so no cycle exists.

- Recovery fetches main and locates exactly one first descendant F of C whose tree diff is only prior-attempt -> exact fence_published attempt plus absent -> exact fence receipt; F is fenceDeskSha. A later descendant is acceptable only if both bytes remain unchanged. Zero/multiple candidates, changed bytes, non-descendant remote, or another diff blocks/rescans without private-index mutation. TrackLinearizationCommandJournal adds checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,fenceDeskSha and may be created only after that graph proof; every field is recoverable from external evidence if no host journal exists. read-tree/commit-tree parent and lease expected SHA are F, not C. External observed attempt and final receipt add fencePath,fenceSha256,fenceDeskSha and preserve them. A crash before/after checkpoint response, fence candidate write/commit/push/readback, graph resolution, or journal persistence therefore resumes one exact prefix under concurrent Desk advancement.

### Round 67 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final authority for descriptor-backed executable images, retained terminal source, grouped-red production, suite runtime outputs, rollover observations, canonical-main reconciliation, and complete concurrent track fencing.

- Every executable image is descriptor-backed. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorEntry={identity,fd,transport}` uses identity:SpawnSourceIdentity, positive fd, and transport alias|stdin. Exact `DescriptorExtra={role,identity,fd}` uses role bootstrap_source_copy|child_source and a positive fd. Final no-extra-fields `DescriptorLaunchSpec={version:1,platform,executable,executableFd,entry,extras,argv,cwd,envNames}` uses platform darwin|linux, executable:TrustedExecutable, executableFd exact 3, entry null for native binaries or one DescriptorEntry, extras a BINARY-role-sorted unique array, absolute cwd, and BINARY-sorted unique envNames. All declared fds are distinct except stdin transport uses fd 0; no undeclared fd >=3 is inherited. The adapter opens executable, entry, and extras with `O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW`, validates complete device/inode/mode/uid/size/hash/version or source identity through position-preserving reads, and keeps independent descriptions open. Actual executable argv[0] is `/dev/fd/3` on Darwin or `/proc/self/fd/3` on Linux; alias entry operands use their matching descriptor alias and stdin maps only that entry to fd 0. DescriptorProcessAdapter persists the complete spec/hash in the owning process intent before secret acquisition or spawn, post-spawn fstats/hashes the same parent descriptors, and closes them only after spawn outcome plus registered process identity is durable. The kernel therefore resolves authenticated inodes, not replaceable directory entries.

- TrustedExecutable version probes, gh/git/tar/mv/ouro/pnpm/Node/wrangler/esbuild/provider launches, process gate/child, and every secret-bearing child use DescriptorProcessAdapter; script entries use trusted Node as descriptor-backed executable plus a separately authenticated entry descriptor. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorSpawnReceipt={version:1,owner,launchSpecSha256,executableIdentity,entryIdentity,extraIdentities,pid,pgid,processIdentity,spawnedAt}` has entryIdentity null or the exact entry identity and extraIdentities the DescriptorLaunchSpec role/identity/fd projection in its exact order; it is appended to the existing owner journal after successful spawn and before any release/secret descriptor. Linux `/proc/<pid>/exe` must resolve the same executable device/inode; Darwin relies on the descriptor exec alias plus ProcessIdentity/title handshake and the parent-held post-spawn descriptor proof. Replacement before/after validation, path unlink/rename, alias mismatch, inherited extra fd, executable/entry offset drift, child image mismatch, or missing receipt rejects. The Round 61 path spawn in the old TrustedExecutable clause and every later pathname bundle launch are invalid.

- Gate/child descriptor assignment is literal. Parent gate launch uses executable Node fd 3, alias entry gate bundle fd 4, and extra child_source fd 5; exact gate argv replaces its former source operand with fd-4 alias and uses `--child-source-fd 5`. ProcessRegistrationBundle freezes a child DescriptorLaunchSpec using the same authenticated Node identity at fd 3 and the byte-identical child bundle identity as alias entry fd 5. Gate retains fd 3 and fd 5, launches child through their aliases, and closes fd 5 only after child spawn receipt; gate/child ProcessReady sourceIdentity equals its respective entry identity. Terminal bootstrap uses stdin entry fd 0 plus bootstrap_source_copy extra fd 4. No Round 66 fd-3 child-source or pathname script vector remains legal.

- Terminal bootstrap reads no mutable module graph. Final `scripts/release-terminal.ts` is one self-contained module with no import except `node:*` built-ins and no dynamic/package/file code loading; its bootstrap and effect behavior live in that one measured file. Work Merger opens the clean pushed source twice and uses DescriptorProcessAdapter to launch trusted Node with source descriptor one as exact TypeScript stdin and source descriptor two inherited at fd 4: actual argv is `[nodeFdAlias,'--input-type=module-typescript','-','--','--mode','bootstrap','--manifest',M,'--writer-id',W,'--bootstrap-source-fd','4']`. Both source identities must equal the Git blob at branchHeadSha before spawn. Exact pushed/read-back `TerminalBootstrapLaunchIntent={version:1,identitySha256,executionHost,manifestPath,writerId,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,invocationToken,createdAt}` precedes launch at task path `artifacts/terminal-bootstrap/launch-intent.v1.json`; ProcessRegistrationOwner terminal_bootstrap commandKind is entry|smoke. Entry prepare/spawn/ready/terminal evidence uses the ordinary create-once registered-process wait/blocker/adoption matrix, so lost launch response never duplicates the bootstrap process. Exact host root is `/Users/arimendelow/Library/Caches/spoonjoy-release/<identitySha256>/terminal-bootstrap`; bundlePath is `<root>/release-terminal.mts`, temp is `<root>/release-terminal.mts.tmp`, and smoke directory is `<root>/smoke`.

- Final no-extra-fields `TerminalBootstrapAttempt={version:1,identitySha256,executionHost,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,sourcePath,sourceSha256,sourceIdentity,bundlePath,bundleTempPath,smokeDirectory,phase,activeCommand,commandResults,bundleIdentity,startedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase prepared|source_copied|smoke_running|validated. prepared is the first pushed/read-back effect, has null activeCommand/bundleIdentity, empty results, and absent host paths. source_copied atomically copies bytes only from inherited fd 4 through mode-0600 temp/fsync/rename/parent-fsync, records exact bundleIdentity equal sourceIdentity, and creates empty mode-0700 smokeDirectory. smoke_running adds one prepared registered process envelope; ProcessRegistrationOwner adds `{kind:'terminal_bootstrap',identitySha256,commandKind:'smoke',processAttemptOrdinal}`. Exact smoke uses descriptor-backed Node plus bundle entry and argv `['--bootstrap-self-test','--bootstrap-attempt',attemptPath,'--bootstrap-attempt-sha256',attemptSha256]` in smokeDirectory. Failed/signal/timeout/warning smoke envelopes append immutably and authorize only a fresh processAttemptOrdinal after dead/reaped proof; active/lost response uses ordinary wait/blocker/adoption. The first clean terminal smoke becomes the sole commandResult and validated. Every copy/temp/directory/process/result/checkpoint crash resumes the longest exact prefix without duplicate live execution.

- Final `TerminalBootstrapReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,executionHost,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,sourcePath,sourceSha256,sourceIdentity,bundlePath,bundleIdentity,node:TrustedExecutable,commandResults,validatedAt}` replaces the attempt and is remote-readable before effect launch. Work Merger opens trusted Node and bundle descriptors and launches exact normal/repaired/successor flags plus ordered `--bootstrap-receipt P --bootstrap-receipt-sha256 H --lineage-json L`, where P/H name that receipt and L is compact canonical no-LF `{rootIdentitySha256,rootBootstrapReceiptPath,rootBootstrapReceiptSha256,predecessorSuccessorKey}` with predecessor null for the root and exact immediate predecessor for a recursive successor. The entry descriptor, receipt identity/hash, and lineage validate in the parent before launch; no in-bundle self-check is execution authority. The retained bundle survives every worktree/validator cleanup and is deleted only after the final root terminal result and all recursive journals are pushed/read back. The Round 66 esbuild/path build and mutable bundlePath execution are invalid.

- Grouped red evidence has one coordinator action. Successor action domain adds `run-doer-red`, legal only for implementation_running and the one current group's test unit before its red_green checkpoint. Exact CLI is `pnpm release:terminal -- --mode successor --journal J --action run-doer-red --doer-attempt D --group G --unit U --command-ordinal O`; D/G/U/O must select the next expected-red command in SuccessorImplementationRegistry. Exact mutable `SuccessorDoerRedAttempt={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,groupId,unitId,commandOrdinal,planEntrySha256,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,runtimeDoingSha256,productStatusBeforeSha256,phase,activeCommand,commandEvidencePath,commandEvidenceSha256,redTreePath,redTreeSha256,redEvidencePath,redEvidenceSha256,startedAt,updatedAt}` lives under the attempt-qualified unit command root and uses phase prepared|command_running|command_terminal|tree_frozen|evidence_published|complete. prepared is pushed/read back before the coordinator runs the exact registered plan command in the product worktree; only the coordinator writes Desk artifacts.

- run-doer-red captures sanitized stdout/stderr logs, exact nonzero expected-red command evidence, a temporary-index red tree containing only the plan-owned test/runtime paths, and SuccessorRedEvidence with failing IDs/codes derived by the finite test adapter. Live/lost commands use the ordinary registered-process wait/adoption matrix; terminal failure, unexpected green, warning, unowned dirt, index/worktree ambiguity, or artifact write failure preserves the attempt and authorizes no implementation. Complete atomically replaces the attempt with immutable evidence and returns exact `{mode:'successor',action:'run-doer-red',journalPath,journalSha256,phase:'implementation_running',doerAttemptOrdinal,groupId,unitId,commandOrdinal,commandEvidencePath,commandEvidenceSha256,redTreePath,redTreeSha256,redEvidencePath,redEvidenceSha256}`. Response loss readbacks that receipt byte-for-byte. record-doer-checkpoint accepts only the complete matching red producer prefix; Work Doer cannot start implementation before it. Unit29.2.5a/b own every producer/action/flag/result/crash test.

- Planner/doer runtime outputs are exact JSON, not locator prose. Terminal adapter bytes are compact canonical UTF-8 with no LF. Exact `SuccessorPlannerRuntimeOutput={version:1,successorKey,plannerAttemptOrdinal,planningPath,planningSha256,doingPath,doingSha256}` names the two frozen snapshot outputs and hashes exact bytes before publication. Exact `SuccessorDoerRuntimeOutput={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,runtimeDoingPath,runtimeDoingSha256,completedCheckpoints,checkpointSetSha256}` uses registry-order `completedCheckpoints:{groupId,checkpointKind,unitReceiptPaths,unitReceiptSetSha256,sourceHeadSha,runtimeDoingAfterSha256}[]`; every group has red_green then verification, every receipt/result cross-validates, and checkpointSetSha256 hashes compact canonical bytes. Extra/missing/reordered/duplicate fields/checkpoints, wrong paths/hashes/head/runtime, raw prose, or noncanonical bytes are invalid_output.

- Final SuccessorSuiteAttempt terminalOutcome domains are planner `approved|findings|snapshot_conflict|promotion_conflict|runtime_errored|runtime_cancelled|invalid_output` and doer `implemented|review_findings|runtime_errored|runtime_cancelled|invalid_output`. Completed valid adapter bytes project only through the matching runtime-output schema; invalid bytes require runtime_invalid_output plus SuccessorRuntimeTermination and interrupted status, never completed. Planner/doer suite artifact sets add the immutable runtime-output copy for valid completion or runtime-invalid-output for invalid completion, not both. Retry prior-outcome sets and exact planner v2/doer v3 prompts consume the corresponding suite_attempt/termination/checkpoint matrix already declared.

- Rollover namespace observation is a closed absent/collision union. ProcessRegistrationOwner adds `{kind:'successor_rollover_observe',predecessorSuccessorKey,rolloverOrdinal,commandKind:'remote_ref'|'local_ref',processAttemptOrdinal}`. Exact `RolloverRefState={presence:'absent'}|{presence:'present',sha}` uses null-free variants; exact `RolloverFilesystemState={presence:'absent'}|{presence:'present',kind,device,inode}` uses kind file|directory|symlink|other from no-follow lstat; exact `RolloverDeskTaskState={presence:'absent'}|{presence:'present',deskCommitSha,blobSha256}` reads the fetched Desk main tree only. Remote/local registered commands have immutable failed-attempt arrays plus one clean terminal command: remote uses exact ls-remote exit 2/empty for absent or exit zero/one exact ref row for present; local uses rev-parse exit one/empty for absent or zero/one exact SHA for present.

- Exact task path is `artifacts/post-merge-successor/rollover/observation-<rolloverOrdinal>.v1.json`; final no-extra-fields `SuccessorRolloverNamespaceObservation={version:1,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictReceiptPath,conflictReceiptSha256,rolloverOrdinal,priorObservationPaths,priorObservationSetSha256,rolloverSeedSha256,branch,worktreePath,taskPath,priorRemoteCommands,remoteCommand,priorLocalCommands,localCommand,remoteBranchState,localBranchState,worktreeState,taskState,outcome,observedAt}` uses outcome absent iff all four states absent, otherwise collision. Every command/history/path/state is pushed/read back before classification. collision is registered as rollover_namespace_collision, appended to the next ordinal's complete BINARY path/hash prior set, and cannot derive a rollover key or mutate any namespace. absent is registered as rollover_namespace_absent and alone enters the final rollover key preimage. Read-command response loss reuses its processAttemptOrdinal; filesystem/Desk reads rerun only before final observation publication. Unknown/malformed/changed state blocks without an observation or ordinal advance. This replaces the Round 66 one-branch observation and every untyped state.

- Rollover naming binds collision history without a cycle. Final rolloverSeedSha256 hashes compact canonical `{version:1,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictKind,conflictReceiptPath,conflictReceiptSha256,acceptedBaseSha,observedRemoteSha,rolloverOrdinal,priorObservationSetSha256}` before deriving slug/branch/worktree/task names. Final no-extra-fields `SuccessorBranchConflictRollover={version:1,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictKind,conflictReceiptPath,conflictReceiptSha256,acceptedBaseSha,observedRemoteSha,rolloverOrdinal,priorObservationPaths,priorObservationSetSha256,rolloverSeedSha256,absenceObservationPath,absenceObservationSha256,rolloverKeySha256,successorKey,branch,worktreePath,taskPath,createdAt}` requires absenceObservationPath/hash name the current absent observation and rolloverKeySha256 hash exact `{rolloverSeedSha256,absenceObservationPath,absenceObservationSha256}`. Every prior path is a collision observation for ordinals 1..N-1 in order; no absent observation appears there. This replaces the shorter Round 66 rollover schema.

- Canonical main is attached, clean, and CAS-reconciled. The old `git merge --ff-only origin/main` command is invalid. Exact registered canonical sequence in C is symbolic-ref `['git','symbolic-ref','--quiet','HEAD']`, HEAD/main/origin refs through separate `rev-parse --verify` commands, index tree through `write-tree`, and status through `status --porcelain=v2 -z --untracked-files=all`. Before mutation symbolic-ref must be `refs/heads/main`, HEAD equals refs/heads/main, index tree equals HEAD tree, status is empty, and local main is equal to or an ancestor of origin/main. Equal needs no mutation. Behind uses exact CAS `update-ref refs/heads/main <origin> <local>` followed by `read-tree --reset -u <origin>`; a crash after ref update is recoverable only from the frozen clean old index/worktree and reruns read-tree. Post-probes require symbolic-ref main, HEAD=local main=origin/main, index tree=HEAD tree, empty status, and mergeSha ancestor.

- Exact no-extra-fields `CanonicalMainReconciliation={version:1,canonicalRepoPath,oldMainSha,originMainSha,disposition:'already_current'|'fast_forwarded',commandResults,headSha,mainRefSha,indexTreeSha,statusSha256,completedAt}` owns its complete branch-specific command prefix. Final replacement `TerminalCleanupAttempt={version:1,identitySha256,attemptOrdinal,priorFailedAttemptPaths,priorFailedAttemptSetSha256,mergeSha,validatorMergeSha,branchHeadSha,executionHost,canonicalRepoPath,validatorRuntimePath,validatorRuntimeTempPath,validatorArchivePath,validatorArchiveTempPath,validatorArchiveSha256,validatorSourceTreeSha256,worktreePath,worktreeDeletePath,worktreeCleanupSha256,branch,phase,activeCommand,commandResults,originMainContainsMerge,canonicalMainReconciliation,canonicalMainContainsMerge,validatorReady,worktreeAbsent,registeredWorktreeAbsent,localBranchAbsent,remoteBranchAbsent,pruned,startedAt,updatedAt}` has reconciliation null before origin_verified and nonnull from canonical_main_ready. Final `TerminalCleanup={version:1,attemptOrdinal,priorFailedAttemptPaths,priorFailedAttemptSetSha256,mergeSha,validatorMergeSha,branchHeadSha,executionHost,canonicalRepoPath,validatorRuntimePath,validatorArchivePath,validatorArchiveSha256,validatorSourceTreeSha256,worktreePath,worktreeDeletePath,worktreeCleanupSha256,branch,commandResults,originMainContainsMerge:true,canonicalMainReconciliation,canonicalMainContainsMerge:true,validatorReady:true,worktreeAbsent:true,registeredWorktreeAbsent:true,localBranchAbsent:true,remoteBranchAbsent:true,pruned:true,completedAt}` preserves it byte-identically. A detached HEAD, alternate branch, missing/drifted ref, local-ahead/diverged state, dirt/untracked bytes, wrong index, CAS loss, or post-probe mismatch fails before validator creation. TerminalCleanup phase/count validation uses the reconciliation's exact internal results instead of one canonical-fast-forward slot; no conditional command is omitted from its declared disposition. Unit29.2.6a tests equal, behind, each update/read-tree crash, detached/alternate branches, dirt/index drift, ref race, and postcondition drift.

- Track fence schemas are complete and concurrency-safe. Final no-extra-fields `TrackArchiveLinearizationAttempt={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,predecessorPath,predecessorSha256,trackArchiveAttemptPath,trackArchiveAttemptSha256,track,triggerTask,scanDeskSha,trackGeneration,sourceCommitSha,sourceTreeSha,targetPath,movePayloadSetSha256,commitMessage,authorName,authorEmail,commitAt,phase,checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,fenceDeskSha,observedDeskRemoteSha,candidateCommitSha,candidateTreeSha,commandResults,commandResultSetSha256,outcome,startedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase prepared|push_inflight|fence_published|observed. prepared has all checkpoint/fence/observed/candidate/outcome fields null and empty commands/hash; push_inflight remains so until external checkpoint C is fetched/read back; fence_published has C plus fence path/hash, fenceDeskSha null, and all move fields null; observed additionally has nonnull fenceDeskSha, observed/candidate fields, six commands, and moved|absent|conflict outcome.

- Exact `TrackLinearizationFence={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,trackGeneration,scanDeskSha,pushInflightAttemptPath,pushInflightAttemptSha256,checkpointDeskSha,publishedAt}` and host-control `TrackFencePublicationJournal={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,publicationOrdinal,baseDeskSha,candidateCommitSha,activePush,priorPushes,fetchObservation,phase,outcome,startedAt,updatedAt}` own fence publication. Each ordinal fetches a base descendant of C preserving exact push_inflight bytes, builds the same two-path tree transform atop that base, pushes with lease on base, and observes through registered fetch. Rejection/remote advancement records the terminal push/fetch, increments publicationOrdinal, and rebases on the new preserving descendant; no candidate is reissued. The first accepted candidate F sets outcome accepted and permits fence_published readback. After eight races exact immutable `TrackFenceConflictReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,trackArchiveAttemptPath,trackArchiveAttemptSha256,checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,publicationAttempts,publicationAttemptSetSha256,code:'fence_race_exhausted',completedAt}` at sibling `attempt-<N>-fence-conflict.v1.json` records the complete ordinal-ordered publication path/hash set, atomically replaces the live linearization attempt, and is accepted by the next TrackArchiveAttempt's priorLinearizationReceipt pair for scanOrdinal+1; it is never a human blocker. Publishing that conflict uses the ordinary Desk descendant-preserving checkpoint matrix and cannot recreate or delete a winner fence.

- Final no-extra-fields `TrackLinearizationCommandJournal={version:1,identitySha256,linearizationOrdinal,executionHost,bootIdSha256,checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,fenceDeskSha,trackArchiveAttemptPath,trackArchiveAttemptSha256,privateIndexPath,indexInfoStdinSha256,candidateTreeSha,candidateCommitSha,phase,nextCommandOrdinal,commands,priorCommandProcesses,activeCommand,pushObservation,startedAt,updatedAt}` can be created only after accepted F graph/readback proof. Exact final `TrackArchiveLinearizationReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,predecessorPath,predecessorSha256,trackArchiveAttemptPath,trackArchiveAttemptSha256,track,triggerTask,scanDeskSha,trackGeneration,sourceCommitSha,sourceTreeSha,targetPath,movePayloadSetSha256,checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,fenceDeskSha,observedDeskRemoteSha,candidateCommitSha,candidateTreeSha,commandResults,commandResultSetSha256,outcome,completedAt}` projects observed fields byte-identically. read-tree/commit-tree parent and lease are F. Every phase/null matrix, fence publication retry, conflict receipt, graph lookup, host-journal handoff, move command, and receipt replacement is fault-tested; the incremental Round 66 field-addition clauses are invalid.

### Round 68 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final authority for all successor command production, descriptor transport/bootstrap identities, terminal launch/archive/cleanup ownership, rollover namespace completeness, and immutable fence-publication evidence.

- One coordinator action produces every successor command artifact. `run-doer-command` replaces run-doer-red and is invoked only through the retained descriptor-backed terminal bundle, never logical `pnpm`. Exact successor effect argv after the bundle entry is `--mode successor --journal J --action run-doer-command --doer-attempt D --group G --unit U --phase test|implementation|verification --command-ordinal O --bootstrap-receipt P --bootstrap-receipt-sha256 H --lineage-json L`; all values bind the current TerminalEffectLaunchIntent. D/G/U/phase/O select exactly the next SuccessorCommandPlan member. test/red commands run before implementation against the uncommitted plan-owned test tree; implementation/green commands run after Work Doer writes code; test/final-verification green replays and verification/green commands run after implementation in registry order. Work Doer writes product/runtime bytes only; release-terminal alone runs commands and writes Desk logs/evidence. `ProcessRegistrationOwner` adds exact `{kind:'successor_doer_command',successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,groupId,unitId,phase,commandOrdinal}`; its owner journal is the attempt below, so no command may start from a Work Doer shell or without prepared, pushed, and read-back command authority.

- Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerCommandAttempt={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,groupId,unitId,phase,commandOrdinal,planEntrySha256,executionWindow,expected,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,runtimeDoingSha256,productStatusBeforeSha256,state,activeCommand,stdoutPath,stdoutSha256,stderrPath,stderrSha256,commandEvidencePath,commandEvidenceSha256,redTreePath,redTreeSha256,redEvidencePath,redEvidenceSha256,startedAt,updatedAt}` lives at exact task-relative `artifacts/post-merge-successor/runtime/doer/attempt-<D>/commands/<unitId>/command-<O>.attempt.v1.json` and uses state prepared|command_running|command_terminal|tree_frozen|evidence_published|complete. prepared has only identity/input fields and all active/output fields null; command_running has activeCommand nonnull and every output null; command_terminal clears activeCommand and freezes both log path/hash pairs plus command evidence; expected red next freezes `redTreePath=artifacts/post-merge-successor/units/<unitId>/red-command-<O>-tree.v1.json` at tree_frozen and `redEvidencePath=artifacts/post-merge-successor/units/<unitId>/red-command-<O>.v1.json` at evidence_published, while expected green moves directly to evidence_published with all red fields null; complete preserves every field byte-identically. Every command uses registered DescriptorProcessAdapter ownership, durable wait/adoption/cancellation, sanitized immutable logs, exact plan-bound evidence, response-loss receipt readback, warning/exit matrix, and product status/index/worktree validation. Exact success is `{mode:'successor',action:'run-doer-command',journalPath,journalSha256,phase:'implementation_running',doerAttemptOrdinal,groupId,unitId,commandOrdinal,commandEvidencePath,commandEvidenceSha256,redTreePath,redTreeSha256,redEvidencePath,redEvidenceSha256}` with red fields both nonnull only for expected red. The complete attempt atomically freezes and registers as doer_command_receipt before return; PreReleaseArtifact.kind and doer SuiteArtifactRef.kind add `doer_command_receipt`, and its final path is the basename above without `.attempt`.

- Multiple red commands are first-class. Final `SuccessorImplementationUnitReceipt={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,unitId,phase,status:'completed',checkpointKind,checkpointUnitIds,checkpointUnitSetSha256,sourceBaseSha,sourceHeadSha,treeSha,redEvidencePaths,redEvidenceSetSha256,commandEvidencePaths,commandEvidenceSetSha256,artifactPaths,artifactSetSha256,runtimeDoingPath,runtimeDoingBeforeSha256,runtimeDoingAfterSha256,completedAt}` replaces the scalar-red form. Test phase redEvidencePaths is the commandOrdinal-ordered nonempty path array for every expected-red plan member and its set hash covers compact canonical `{path,sha256}[]` in that order; implementation/verification use exact `[]` and its hash. Each red evidence selects its matching red tree/command and all precede every implementation command. record-doer-checkpoint requires the complete test-red, implementation-green, test-green-replay, and verification-green producer prefixes applicable to its checkpoint. Missing/extra/reordered/duplicate evidence or any Work Doer-authored Desk file rejects.

- Descriptor entry and channel grammar is closed. Final exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorEntry={identity,fd,transport}` uses identity:SpawnSourceIdentity; stdin requires fd exactly 0 and alias requires fd exactly 4 or 5. Final exact `DescriptorExtra={role,identity,fd}` is only bootstrap_source_copy at fd 4 or child_source at fd 5 and uses identity:SpawnSourceIdentity. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorSecretChannel={role,childFd,maxBytes}` uses role cloudflare_oauth|cloudflare_d1, childFd 6|7 respectively, and positive maxBytes <=4096. Final exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorLaunchSpec={version:1,purpose,platform,executable,executableFd,entry,extras,secretChannels,argv,cwd,envNames}` uses purpose version_probe|effect, platform darwin|linux, executable:DescriptorExecutableIdentity, executableFd exact 3, entry null only for native binaries, extras BINARY-role-sorted unique, secretChannels BINARY-role-sorted unique, absolute cwd, and BINARY-sorted unique envNames. Every declared fd is unique except the sole stdin fd 0; no undeclared fd >=3 is inherited. SecretChannels contain no parent descriptor number, secret, hash, length, or value beyond the public cap. This complete schema replaces the Round 67 DescriptorLaunchSpec and DescriptorEntry/Extra domains rather than extending them incrementally.

- For each secret channel, the parent creates a private nonblocking close-on-exec pipe, maps only the read end to its declared childFd, retains the write end, and spawns the paused registered gate/child with no secret acquired. After DescriptorSpawnReceipt plus gate/child ready/recorded state is pushed/read back, the parent acquires the one secret in memory, writes exactly its bytes with bounded complete-write handling, closes/zeroizes the write side, and only then publishes run authorization. Child reads once to EOF after authorization, validates its in-memory grammar, closes the read side before provider spawn, and never forks/inherits it further. Spawn failure/abort closes both ends before secret acquisition; write failure closes/zeroizes and terminalizes without release. Tests inspect every process fd table, partial write, wrong fd/role/cap, inherited extra, early read/release, duplicate write, crash/adoption, EOF, close, and memory/log/artifact leak boundary. The Round 67 source-only extras and undeclared secret descriptors are invalid.

- Executable version identity is acyclic. Exact no-extra-fields `TrustedExecutableBase={name,absolutePath,realPath,device,inode,mode,uid,size,sha256}` is frozen before version execution. Exact `DescriptorExecutableIdentity={base,version}` has version null only when DescriptorLaunchSpec purpose is version_probe and nonnull otherwise; the final DescriptorLaunchSpec uses that identity and its purpose field before platform. A version probe descriptor-launches the base inode, parses the finite sanitized version, and projects exact `TrustedExecutable={name,absolutePath,realPath,device,inode,mode,uid,size,sha256,version}` with every base field byte-identical. Later effect specs require that final version. No version probe depends on its own result.

- Spawn identity is a closed union. Exact `SpawnedProcessIdentity={kind:'registered',processIdentity:ProcessIdentity}|{kind:'native',pid,pgid,executionHost,startToken,launchSpecSha256}`; registered is legal only for gate/child members and native only for direct native/version/helper processes after the trusted OS start probe. Final exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorSpawnReceipt={version:1,owner,launchSpecSha256,executableIdentity,entryIdentity,extraIdentities,spawnedIdentity,spawnedAt}` uses entryIdentity null or the exact entry identity and extraIdentities as the DescriptorLaunchSpec extras projection in its exact order; secret channels never appear as identities. spawn_error has no receipt; successful spawn requires one union member and cross-validates its kind to owner/purpose. pid/pgid/start token/hash drift, registered identity for native, native identity for a generation member, or missing probe rejects. This complete schema replaces the Round 67 pid/pgid/processIdentity receipt.

- Terminal bootstrap and effect evidence are registered archive members. Exact task paths are `artifacts/terminal-bootstrap/launch-intent.v1.json`, `artifacts/terminal-bootstrap/bootstrap-receipt.v1.json`, and `artifacts/terminal-bootstrap/effects/effect-<positive>-intent.v1.json` plus `effect-<positive>-spawn.v1.json`. Final exact `TerminalArtifact.kind` union is `worktree_cleanup|failure_snapshot|failure_evidence|failure_disposition|successful_result|wait_checkpoint|wait_probe|wait_resolution_evidence|wait_resolution|blocker_probe|blocker_authorization|blocker_resolution_evidence|blocker_resolution|desk_archive_cancellation|archive_validation_provisional|terminal_bootstrap_launch_intent|terminal_bootstrap_receipt|terminal_effect_launch_intent|terminal_effect_spawn`; each new kind has exactly the corresponding path grammar above, attempt/intents never alias receipts/spawns, and all retain the existing exact `{kind,terminalPhase,path,sha256}` field order. Bootstrap kinds use terminalPhase prepared; each effect intent uses the owning manifest phase at creation and its spawn repeats that phase. Mutable TerminalBootstrapAttempt and all host process/control journals live only under `/Users/arimendelow/desk/_meta/release-terminal/<identitySha256>/bootstrap/`, never task/archive roots. The root task closed set requires launch intent and bootstrap receipt from TerminalCleanup prepared onward; each task or recursive successor closed set requires every effect intent/spawn pair that touched that task before its final ArchiveValidationAttempt. They relocate with DeskArchive/TrackArchive as ordinary registered files, and archive validation resolves/hashes each through the task locator. Unknown/missing/live-at-final bootstrap/effect artifact is an extra/failure. Every earlier logical `pnpm release:terminal` normal/repaired/successor clause defines parser grammar only; it is never effect-launch authority.

- Recursive effect launch ownership is exact. `TerminalEffectLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,rootBootstrapReceiptPath,rootBootstrapReceiptSha256,effectOrdinal,mode,manifestPath,journalPath,action,lineage,invocationToken,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` has normal/repaired manifest nonnull and journal/action null, successor journal/action nonnull and manifest null; lineage byte-equals the effect argv. It is pushed/read back before DescriptorProcessAdapter prepare. `ProcessRegistrationOwner={kind:'terminal_effect',rootIdentitySha256,effectOrdinal,mode,action}` owns prepare/spawn/ready/waits/blocker/terminal. Exact `TerminalEffectSpawn={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,effectOrdinal,agentProcessId,descriptorSpawnReceipt,authorizedAt}` is pushed/read back while the effect process is paused; only then release starts that exact process. Intent-only probes absent/prepared once; spawn/response loss adopts by invocationToken and process identity; running/terminal never relaunches. Recursive successor journals bind the immediate predecessor key and same root bootstrap receipt; no sibling/ancestor action may replay an ordinal.

- Retained bootstrap-root cleanup is host-control and crash-complete. After final root ReleaseTerminalResult, terminal Desk/archive state, effect process death/reap, and Slugger terminal proof are pushed/read back, exact no-extra-fields `TerminalBootstrapCleanupAttempt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,bootstrapRoot,deletePath,members,memberSetSha256,phase,nextDeleteIndex,rootDetached,rootAbsent,startedAt,updatedAt}` lives under Desk `_meta/release-terminal/<rootIdentitySha256>/bootstrap-cleanup/attempt.v1.json`. members is the complete no-follow BINARY-relative-path-sorted file/directory/symlink manifest with hashes; deletePath is an absent same-filesystem token sibling. phase prepared|detached|deleting|complete uses atomic rename, parent fsync, reverse-depth/BINARY deletion, per-member fsync, and exact nextDeleteIndex recovery. Final `TerminalBootstrapCleanupReceipt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,bootstrapRoot,memberSetSha256,rootDetached:true,rootAbsent:true,completedAt}` replaces the attempt after both paths are absent. Overall terminal completion requires its pushed/read-back Desk-control receipt; it is not a task archive member. Unknown member, live descriptor/process, source/delete mixture, partial deletion drift, wrong inode/hash, or foreign host blocks without second notification/effect.

- Fence publication attempts are immutable. Exact Desk-control path is `_meta/release-terminal/<identitySha256>/track-generations/<trackGeneration>/<scanDeskSha>/linearization/attempt-<N>-fence-publication-<P>.v1.json`. Exact no-extra-fields `TrackFenceFetchObservation={fetchAttemptOrdinal,priorFetchCommands,fetchCommand,observedDeskSha,outcome,updatedAt}` uses outcome accepted|advanced and the final registered fetch; exact `TrackFencePublicationReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,publicationOrdinal,checkpointDeskSha,baseDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,candidateCommitSha,pushCommand,fetchObservation,outcome,completedAt}` uses outcome accepted iff observedDeskSha=candidateCommitSha, otherwise advanced with a preserving descendant. Each P has one prepared push envelope, no push retry, append-only fetch retries, dead/reaped proof, and immutable receipt before P+1. P+1 prior set is the complete ordinal-ordered `{path,sha256}[]` receipts 1..P and its compact-canonical hash. Before building P+1, the coordinator fetches a remote base that is a descendant of both checkpointDeskSha and P's observedDeskSha and proves every prior receipt blob plus the push_inflight attempt blob byte-identical; the private-index transform preserves every other remote tree entry, including concurrent Desk-control receipts. The new receipt is committed/pushed/read back before its path/hash may enter a later prior set.

- Final `TrackFencePublicationJournal={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,checkpointDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,publicationOrdinal,priorPublicationReceipts,priorPublicationReceiptSetSha256,baseDeskSha,candidateCommitSha,phase,activePush,pushCommand,fetchObservation,outcome,startedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase prepared|push_running|observing|complete. prepared has null active/push/fetch/outcome; push_running has activePush only; observing clears activePush, freezes terminal pushCommand, and has nullable in-progress fetch observation; complete has terminal fetch and accepted|advanced outcome. The journal atomically becomes TrackFencePublicationReceipt. TrackFenceConflictReceipt.publicationAttempts is exact receipts P=1..8 in order and publicationAttemptSetSha256 hashes their compact canonical path/hash bytes; accepted receipt forbids conflict, eight advanced receipts require it. All nested fields, path grammars, transitions, retries, response-loss, cleanup, and hash preimages are now closed.

- Rollover absence includes Git registration and the full Desk prefix. Exact `RolloverWorktreeRegistrationState={presence:'absent'}|{presence:'present',records}` uses records as the nonempty normalized path/HEAD/branch projection from exact registered `git worktree list --porcelain -z`; ProcessRegistrationOwner successor_rollover_observe adds commandKind worktree_list. Exact `RolloverDeskPrefixState={presence:'absent'}|{presence:'present',deskCommitSha,members}` uses nonempty BINARY path-sorted `{path,blobSha256}[]` for every blob under `spoonjoy-v2/<successorSlug>/`, not only task.md. SuccessorRolloverNamespaceObservation inserts priorWorktreeListCommands/worktreeListCommand/worktreeRegistrationState after local ref state and replaces taskState with deskPrefixState. outcome absent requires remote/local refs absent, registration absent, no-follow filesystem worktree absent, and Desk prefix absent; any occupied domain is collision. Malformed/partial/changed registrations or prefix scans block before publication.

### Round 69 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final authority for cooperative Work Doer execution, attempt-qualified command evidence, terminal artifact phases, secret-channel crash recovery, terminal process handles, rollover-ancestor cleanup, fence descendant acceptance, and externally completed bootstrap cleanup.

- Work Doer and the coordinator use bounded cooperative activations, never one autonomous interleaved runtime. Final exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerActivationIntent={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,invocationToken,priorExchangePath,priorExchangeSha256,executionBaseSha,runtimeDoingPath,runtimeDoingSha256,promptPath,promptSha256,createdAt}` lives at `artifacts/post-merge-successor/suite/doer/attempt-<D>/activations/activation-<A>-intent.v1.json`; A is positive contiguous, A=1 has null prior-exchange pair, and A>1 names the immediately preceding exchange result. Final prompt bytes are the authenticated Round 63 doer prompt with exact lines `activation_ordinal: <A>`, `prior_exchange_path: <P|null>`, and `prior_exchange_sha256: <H|null>` inserted immediately before `worktree`, and with its rules line replaced by exact `rules: treat canonical planning and doing as immutable read-only authority; execute and update only the authenticated runtime doing path; consume only the bound immediately-prior outcome and exchange; return exactly one canonical command_request, checkpoint_request, or complete object; never execute a plan command or write Desk; stop after returning the object\n`; there is one final LF. Exact `SuccessorDoerActivationDispatchIntent={version:1,activationIntentPath,activationIntentSha256,invocationToken,promptPath,promptSha256,cwd,createdAt}` and `SuccessorDoerActivationDispatchReceipt={version:1,dispatchIntentPath,dispatchIntentSha256,activationIntentPath,activationIntentSha256,invocationToken,agentId,preparedAt,releasedAt}` live at sibling `activation-<A>-dispatch-intent.v1.json` and `activation-<A>-dispatch.v1.json`; they use the paused create-once/push/readback/release protocol. The coordinator observes that invocation through the existing typed probe/cancellation protocol and permits no product read or coordinator effect while that agent is live.

- Exact compact-canonical `SuccessorDoerActivationOutput` is one of `{kind:'command_request',groupId,unitId,phase,commandOrdinal,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,runtimeDoingSha256,productStatusSha256}`, `{kind:'checkpoint_request',groupId,checkpointKind,unitIds,sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,runtimeDoingSha256,productStatusSha256}`, or `{kind:'complete',sourceHeadSha,sourceTreeSha,runtimeDoingSha256,productStatusSha256,completedCheckpointSetSha256}`. Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerActivationReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,dispatchPath,dispatchSha256,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,invocationToken,agentId,terminalProbePath,terminalProbeSha256,outputPath,outputSha256,output,terminalHeadSha,terminalTreeSha,terminalIndexTreeSha,terminalStatusSha256,changedEntries,changedEntrySetSha256,completedAt}` is pushed/read back only after the completed adapter process is dead/reaped. changedEntries is the complete BINARY path-sorted SuccessorDoerChangedEntry set for HEAD/index/worktree and its set hash covers compact canonical complete entries; every dispatch/product/runtime field validates against the activation intent, dispatch receipt, output, doer ledger, and fresh Git/status/blob probes. Its path is the intent basename with `-receipt` replacing `-intent`; the sanitized raw output is the sibling `-output.json`. This terminal receipt is the pause proof: no coordinator command, checkpoint, product read, next activation, or retry runs against a live Work Doer, and every command re-probes the exact terminal head/index/status/changed-entry bytes before launch.

- The coordinator consumes one activation receipt and writes one exact `SuccessorDoerExchangeResult={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,activationReceiptPath,activationReceiptSha256,kind,resultPath,resultSha256,productStatusAfterSha256,completedAt}` at sibling `activation-<A>-exchange.v1.json`. command_request invokes the exact `run-doer-command` producer below and binds its accepted or failed immutable outcome; checkpoint_request invokes `record-doer-checkpoint`; complete validates the closed checkpoint/runtime/product set and alone has a null result pair. The exchange is pushed/read back before A+1. revise_and_retry authorizes the next activation to edit only the current unit's declared product/runtime paths and request the same plan member; retry_unchanged authorizes no product edit; advance authorizes only the next registry member or checkpoint. Agent error/cancellation freezes the existing doer ledger/checkpoint and ends D through the ordinary interrupted-suite path. PreReleaseArtifact and doer SuiteArtifactRef add `doer_activation_intent|doer_activation_dispatch_intent|doer_activation_dispatch|doer_activation_output|doer_activation_receipt|doer_exchange`; final doer SuccessorSuiteAttempt replaces singular agentId authority with the complete contiguous activation/dispatch/output/receipt/exchange path-hash set, allows at most one nonterminal activation, and completes as implemented only on the valid complete output. The coordinator projects the existing final SuccessorDoerRuntimeOutput from that complete output plus the closed checkpoint receipts; it is not raw agent output. No request file is authored by Work Doer and Work Doer never writes Desk.

- Doer runtime evidence is activation-qualified. Final SuccessorAgentRuntimeProbe adds `activationOrdinal` after attemptOrdinal: it is nonnull positive only for doer_suite and null for every other runtimeKind. Doer paths are `suite/doer/attempt-<D>/activations/activation-<A>-runtime-<lifecycle|cancellation>-probe-<P>.v1.json`; each probe agentId/invocationToken matches that activation dispatch and cancellation ordinals remain globally capped for that activation. SuccessorRuntimeTermination likewise adds nullable activationOrdinal with the same matrix; a failed activation terminates D and names its exact output/ledger checkpoint. Final SuccessorDoerAttemptLedger adds nullable `activeActivationOrdinal` after phase: authorized/running names exactly one A, terminal_observed/classified/complete clears it only after that activation's terminal probe/receipt or termination is durable. No singular doer agentId/probe path remains final authority.

- Command execution is attempt-qualified and retryable. `run-doer-command` adds required positive `--execution-attempt E`; E is contiguous per `(D,unitId,commandOrdinal)`. Final mutable command-attempt path is `artifacts/post-merge-successor/doer-attempts/<D>/commands/<unitId>/command-<O>/execution-<E>.attempt.v1.json`. Final `SuccessorDoerCommandAttempt` inserts `executionAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256` after commandOrdinal and `descriptorSpawnReceipt,terminalCommand` after activeCommand. E=1 has exact empty prior set; E>1 has the complete ordinal-ordered immutable outcome path/hash set 1..E-1. prepared has activeCommand/descriptorSpawnReceipt/terminalCommand and all outputs null; command_running has activeCommand plus its matching DescriptorSpawnReceipt and null terminalCommand/outputs; command_terminal clears activeCommand, preserves the receipt, and freezes one terminal RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope whose owner/intent/result/stream hashes cross-bind the descriptor receipt and later SuccessorCommandEvidence. Later states preserve both byte-identically. A missing spawn receipt, cleared terminal envelope, stream/result drift, or live member rejects.

- Every terminal execution freezes exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerCommandOutcome={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,groupId,unitId,phase,commandOrdinal,executionAttemptOrdinal,planEntrySha256,expected,terminalCommand,commandEvidencePath,commandEvidenceSha256,redTreePath,redTreeSha256,redEvidencePath,redEvidenceSha256,outcome,retryDisposition,completedAt}` at the mutable basename with `-outcome.v1.json` replacing `.attempt.v1.json`. outcome is accepted_red|accepted_green|unexpected_red|unexpected_green|warning|process_failure|cancelled. accepted_red and accepted_green alone use retryDisposition advance; unexpected_red|unexpected_green|warning use revise_and_retry; retryable process_failure uses retry_unchanged, permanent process_failure and cancelled use interrupt. Every expected-red execution has a nonnull redTree pair; accepted_red alone also has redEvidence nonnull, while unexpected_green|process_failure|cancelled keep redEvidence null. Every expected-green execution has all red fields null. accepted_red requires the finite expected failure; accepted_green requires clean zero/zero-warning. Expected-green failure, unexpected green during red, warnings, transient process failures, and cancellation therefore retain immutable evidence and receive a fresh E or terminal interruption rather than rewriting or dead-ending. Exact action success adds executionAttemptOrdinal,outcomePath,outcomeSha256,retryDisposition; only advance may enter a unit receipt/checkpoint producer prefix.

- All successor implementation evidence is qualified by D. Final command evidence/log paths are under `artifacts/post-merge-successor/doer-attempts/<D>/commands/<unitId>/command-<O>/execution-<E>-{command.v1.json,stdout.log,stderr.log}`. Final red tree/evidence paths are `artifacts/post-merge-successor/doer-attempts/<D>/units/<unitId>/command-<O>-execution-<E>-{tree.v1.json,red.v1.json}`; final unit receipt path is `artifacts/post-merge-successor/doer-attempts/<D>/units/<unitId>/receipt.v1.json`. PreReleaseArtifact and doer SuiteArtifactRef add `doer_command_outcome`; command, command_log, red_tree, unit_red, and unit_receipt path grammars use this D-qualified registry only. SuccessorImplementationUnitReceipt redEvidencePaths and commandEvidencePaths select only the accepted outcomes in plan order, while the closed implementation artifact set retains every prior failed outcome/log/evidence from every D/E. Review-findings D+1 can never alias D; final PostMergeImplementationEvidence identifies the latest accepted receipt per registry unit and proves all superseded attempts immutable.

- Secret-channel state is nonsecret, journaled, and generation-scoped. Exact `DescriptorSecretChannelState={role,childFd,generation,state,executionHost,ownerStartToken,updatedAt}` uses state pending|sealed|aborted and persists no bytes, byte count, length, hash, secret-derived value, or parent fd. pending is pushed/read back before acquisition and is legal only while the same owner process and paused registered gate/child generation are live. The gate receives the declared read ends, duplicates each only to the child fd during the registered child spawn, closes its copy immediately after the child DescriptorSpawnReceipt, and proves the gate fd table no longer contains them. The child remains paused and performs no read.

- The live parent acquires each secret once, performs the bounded complete write, closes and zeroizes its write side, atomically projects sealed, and pushes/readbacks the complete all-channel sealed set before run authorization. Authorization releases only that generation; the child reads each fd once to EOF, validates in memory, closes every channel before provider spawn, and the provider fd-table proof rejects inheritance. A crash or owner-identity loss while any channel is pending treats the bytes as unknowable: recovery publishes aborted, cancels/reaps the entire unreleased generation, and starts a fresh processAttemptOrdinal/generation that reacquires every secret. A crash after sealed uses only same-generation liveness plus the durable sealed set to authorize; it never rewrites. Partial write, child/gate death, early EOF/read, mixed generation, or write error follows aborted and never releases. Unit29.2.6a fault-tests every state/owner/fd transition and artifact/log/memory leak boundary.

- Terminal effect process identity is exact. `TerminalEffectSpawn` replaces agentProcessId with `invocationToken,processIdentity,releaseHandleSha256`; processIdentity byte-equals the registered DescriptorSpawnReceipt.spawnedIdentity.processIdentity. releaseHandleSha256 hashes compact canonical `{owner,invocationToken,processIdentity,launchSpecSha256}` with no LF and is the sole key accepted by DescriptorProcessAdapter prepare/probe/release. The intent invocationToken, registration owner, receipt launch hash, ready records, spawn processIdentity, every probe, release record, and terminal envelope must all repeat those values. Intent-only response loss probes/adopts that handle; any mismatch, native identity, duplicate token, alternate generation, or unbound release rejects.

- TerminalArtifact has one final phase domain and mapping. Exact `TerminalArtifactPhase` is `terminal_bootstrap|successor|terminal_cleanup|desk_archive|track_archive|archive_validation|repair_index`; it is used only by TerminalArtifact. Failure/history `TerminalPhase` remains the existing eight-member union without terminal_bootstrap/successor. Bootstrap launch/receipt and bootstrap-cleanup launch/spawn kinds require terminal_bootstrap. A successor-mode effect intent/spawn requires successor. A normal/repaired effect intent/spawn and worktree_cleanup require terminal_cleanup. failure_snapshot|failure_evidence|failure_disposition|successful_result|wait_checkpoint|wait_probe|wait_resolution_evidence|wait_resolution|blocker_probe|blocker_authorization|blocker_resolution_evidence|blocker_resolution repeat the exact task-artifact phase encoded by their path and payload, limited to terminal_cleanup|desk_archive|track_archive|archive_validation|repair_index. desk_archive_cancellation requires desk_archive; archive_validation_provisional requires archive_validation. Desk-control archive_validation_final|resource_cleanup|slugger paths are never TerminalArtifacts. Final TerminalArtifact kinds additionally add `terminal_bootstrap_cleanup_launch_intent|terminal_bootstrap_cleanup_spawn` at exact task paths `artifacts/terminal-bootstrap/cleanup-launch-intent.v1.json` and `artifacts/terminal-bootstrap/cleanup-spawn.v1.json`. `append-terminal-artifact` requires explicit `--terminal-phase` and derives the sole legal kind/path/phase tuple before hashing; no caller-selected mismatch or generic owning-manifest phase exists.

- Bootstrap cleanup members and recovery are closed. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalBootstrapCleanupMember` is `{kind:'file',path,device,inode,mode,size,sha256}` for complete regular-file bytes, `{kind:'symlink',path,device,inode,mode,targetBase64,targetSha256}` for raw link-target bytes without following, or `{kind:'directory',path,device,inode,mode}`. path is a normalized nonempty root-relative no-dot/no-parent UTF-8 path. members contains every descendant exactly once in reverse-depth then BINARY-path deletion order; memberSetSha256 hashes compact canonical complete member objects in that order. `TerminalBootstrapRootIdentity={device,inode,mode}` binds the root directory separately. Prepared requires source root present at that identity, delete path absent, nextDeleteIndex zero, and every member present/matching. Detached requires source absent, delete root at the same identity, and zero. Deleting requires members `[0,nextDeleteIndex)` absent and every later member matching its type-specific identity/bytes; directories validate identity while the prefix matrix accounts for removed children. Complete requires both roots absent and nextDeleteIndex=members.length. No-follow lstat, rename/parent fsync, unlink/rmdir, and per-parent fsync are the only mutations; unknown/changed/mixed members block.

- Bootstrap-root deletion is executed by a preloaded external cleaner and ends in a new observable completion. Before final archive validation, the root effect descriptor-launches a paused registered cleaner from the retained bundle with exact argv `--mode bootstrap-cleanup --root-identity-sha256 I --cleanup-attempt P`; ProcessRegistrationOwner adds `{kind:'terminal_bootstrap_cleanup',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}`. Spawn failures retain contiguous host-journal outcomes and may prepare the next ordinal only after dead/reaped proof; the first successful spawn is create-once and no later ordinal is legal. Exact task-registered `TerminalBootstrapCleanupLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,bootstrapReceiptPath,bootstrapReceiptSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,invocationToken,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` and `TerminalBootstrapCleanupSpawn={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,invocationToken,processIdentity,descriptorSpawnReceipt,releaseHandleSha256,preparedAt}` are pushed/read back and included in final archive validation while the cleaner is paused with source already loaded. Before preparedAt, the cleaner's ready/fd-table attestation proves every entry/source descriptor under bootstrapRoot is closed; its only remaining executable/control/log descriptors resolve outside bootstrapRoot/deletePath. This one paused cleaner is the sole exception to the Round 68 live-at-final rejection: ArchiveValidation requires its exact intent/spawn pair, registered liveness, closed-root-descriptor proof, no release file, and no other live bootstrap/effect process; ReleaseTerminalCompletion later requires it terminal/dead/reaped.

- An ordinary-normal or repaired-root ReleaseTerminalResult is the preliminary root result; repair-successor and repaired-intermediate results remain nonroot continuation results and never authorize bootstrap cleanup. After a preliminary root result, terminal Desk/archive state, Slugger terminal proof, and root-effect death/reap are pushed/read back, Work Merger writes exact Desk-control `TerminalBootstrapCleanupReleaseIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupLaunchIntentPath,cleanupLaunchIntentSha256,cleanupSpawnPath,cleanupSpawnSha256,releaseHandleSha256,createdAt}` at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/release-intent.v1.json`. It then no-follow scans the quiescent root, writes prepared exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupAttempt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,bootstrapRoot,deletePath,rootIdentity,members,memberSetSha256,phase,nextDeleteIndex,rootDetached,rootAbsent,startedAt,updatedAt}` at exact `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/attempt.v1.json`, and pushes/readbacks both files before releasing/adopting only that preloaded cleaner. The cleaner refuses release unless the attempt is prepared, both paths/hash-bind the preliminary result, and the root/member scan still matches. Final exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupReceipt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,bootstrapRoot,rootIdentity,memberSetSha256,rootDetached:true,rootAbsent:true,completedAt}` replaces the attempt at exact `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/receipt.v1.json`. Cleaner gate/release/control files plus sanitized stdout/stderr and its command envelope live only under `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/cleaner/`, outside bootstrapRoot/deletePath, and survive root deletion. Cleaner response loss uses the registered process wait/adoption/blocker matrix and cannot spawn a second cleaner.

- Exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupReleaseIntentPath,cleanupReleaseIntentSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,cleanerTerminalCommand,completedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-completion.v1.json`. It requires the cleaner terminal envelope clean, both bootstrap paths absent, cleanup receipt read back, and every preliminary terminal/Slugger/archive proof still valid. A clean cleaner terminal with response loss reconciles the receipt/completion from absence plus the immutable attempt prefix; any ambiguous member state blocks. Unit37 and Work Merger treat only ReleaseTerminalCompletion as terminal success and never exit, clean coordinator state, or notify completion on the preliminary result.

- Fence observation distinguishes accepted descendants from lost races. Exact `TrackFenceOwnedPathExpectation={path,presence,sha256}` has presence present with nonnull complete-file hash or absent with null hash. Exact `TrackFenceOwnedPathProof={expectations,expectationSetSha256,observedTreeSha}` uses BINARY-unique expectations and hashes their compact canonical bytes; it proves exactly those owned paths and imposes no restriction on unrelated concurrent Desk entries. Final `TrackFenceFetchObservation={fetchAttemptOrdinal,priorFetchCommands,fetchCommand,baseAncestorCommand,candidateAncestorCommand,treeProof,observedDeskSha,candidateRelation,outcome,updatedAt}` uses candidateRelation candidate|preserving_descendant|not_ancestor. candidate requires observedDeskSha=candidateCommitSha. preserving_descendant requires trusted graph proof candidateCommitSha is an ancestor of observedDeskSha and a treeProof whose expectations are the exact fence/fence-published-attempt/prior-publication-receipt present path/hash set; both map outcome accepted. not_ancestor requires observedDeskSha be a preserving descendant of baseDeskSha and a treeProof whose expectations require the push_inflight attempt plus all prior receipts exact and the fence absent; it alone maps outcome advanced. A candidate descendant with changed/deleted owned bytes is invalid, never advanced; unrelated paths may change freely.

- Final TrackFencePublicationReceipt adds observedDeskSha,candidateRelation before outcome and accepted includes candidate or preserving_descendant. candidateCommitSha always names the earlier fence-transform commit and never the later commit containing this receipt. After observation, a descendant-preserving Desk CAS publishes the immutable receipt atop observedDeskSha; response loss/races fetch and rebase the same receipt bytes without reissuing the fence push. The receipt contains no containing-commit/remote-head field or post-receipt command, so its hash preimage is acyclic. Registered external fetch/graph/tree proof resolves the first current remote descendant containing the exact receipt and all owned observation bytes; the enclosing TrackArchiveLinearizationAttempt alone records that prior commit as fenceDeskSha on its later fence_published transition. An accepted receipt ends publication and forbids P+1 even when unrelated commits landed after the candidate. Only an advanced receipt authorizes P+1, whose baseDeskSha is a freshly fetched preserving descendant containing the complete prior receipt set. TrackFenceConflictReceipt requires exactly eight advanced receipts. Every fetch/graph/tree/receipt-publication command is a registered terminal envelope retained by the host publication journal, and crash/response-loss tests cover candidate, preserving descendant, rejected push with remote advance, receipt-publication races, postaccept fence drift, and all eight races.

- Rollover ancestors are cleaned after replacement authority is durable.
