Final replacement bootstrap cannot release planner/Work Doer until exact `SuccessorRolloverAncestorCleanupReceipt={version:1,replacementSuccessorKey,predecessorSuccessorKey,rolloverPath,rolloverSha256,conflictPath,conflictSha256,relocatedPayloadPath,relocatedPayloadSha256,worktreePath,worktreeHeadSha,branch,localRefSha,remoteRef,remoteObservedSha,worktreeAbsent:true,registeredWorktreeAbsent:true,localRefAbsent:true,predecessorDeskPrefixAbsent:true,sharedRemoteUntouched:true,commandResults,completedAt}` is pushed/read back at replacement-task-relative `artifacts/post-merge-successor/rollover-ancestors/<predecessorSuccessorKey>/cleanup-receipt.v1.json`. PreReleaseArtifact and doer SuiteArtifactRef add `rollover_ancestor_payload|rollover_ancestor_cleanup`; every copied blob is registered as payload through the relocated manifest's exact BINARY path/hash set and the receipt is the sole cleanup kind, so replacement/archive closed sets admit no unregistered copied byte.

- Its mutable sibling attempt first copies the predecessor's complete BINARY blob set, conflict/rollover receipts, doer ledger/runtime checkpoint, and path/hash manifest into the replacement `relocated-payload/` subtree and commits/pushes/readbacks that copy. Registered local cleanup then atomically detaches/removes only the recorded agent-owned worktree and deletes the local ref only with exact old-value CAS after all processes are dead/reaped; response loss is reconciled to worktree/registration/ref absence. Only after those local proofs are durable does a descendant-preserving Desk CAS remove the old active predecessor prefix in the same commit that freezes the final cleanup receipt under the replacement task. The observed shared remote ref is read-only and never deleted, reset, or force-pushed. Every mutation has prepared intent, terminal result, response-loss readback, and fresh-state proof. Missing payload, local SHA drift, foreign registration, Desk race, or live process blocks before deletion.

- ProcessRegistrationOwner adds `{kind:'successor_rollover_cleanup',replacementSuccessorKey,predecessorSuccessorKey,commandKind:'worktree_remove'|'worktree_list'|'local_ref_delete'|'desk_fetch'|'desk_publish',processAttemptOrdinal}`. Each replacement task carries the transitive BINARY path/hash set of ancestor cleanup receipts; PostMergeImplementationEvidence adds `rolloverAncestorCleanupPaths,rolloverAncestorCleanupSetSha256`, and final TerminalCleanup/ArchiveValidation/ReleaseTerminalCompletion validate every listed local worktree/registration/ref/old Desk prefix absent while each recorded shared remote ref remains merely observed. TerminalCleanup.remoteBranchAbsent continues to govern only the ultimately successful owned branch. Thus rollover preserves immutable conflict evidence without stranding local worktrees, refs, or active Desk tasks.

### Round 70 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final authority for activation-scoped cancellation, the complete cooperative doer suite, checkpoint outcomes, activation release timing, secret-channel journals, rollover payload/cleanup attempts, and track-wide fence scans.

- Doer cancellation is activation-qualified everywhere. Final `SuccessorAgentCancellationIntent={version:1,successorKey,runtimeKind,attemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,reviewOrdinal,reviewRole,invocationToken,agentId,dispatchPath,dispatchSha256,deadlineAt,reason:'deadline_exceeded',cancellationId,createdAt}`, and final Wait/Receipt/Blocker insert activationOrdinal after attemptOrdinal in their existing exact schemas. runtimeKind doer requires positive activationOrdinal and null review fields; planner and both review kinds require activationOrdinal null and retain their final review matrices. cancellationId hashes compact canonical `{runtimeKind,attemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,reviewOrdinal,reviewRole,invocationToken,agentId,dispatchSha256,deadlineAt}`. Doer exact paths are `artifacts/post-merge-successor/suite/doer/attempt-<D>/activations/activation-<A>-cancel-intent.v1.json`, `activation-<A>-cancel-wait-<W>.v1.json`, `activation-<A>-cancel-blocker.v1.json`, and `activation-<A>-cancel-receipt.v1.json`; lifecycle/cancellation probes remain the Round 69 activation paths. The global three-probe budget, null matrices, artifact kinds, SuccessorRuntimeTermination, and no-new-runtime blocker all key on `(D,A,cancellationId)`, so no activation can alias or resolve another's cancellation.

- Activation dispatch authorization is immutable before release. Final `SuccessorDoerActivationDispatchReceipt={version:1,dispatchIntentPath,dispatchIntentSha256,activationIntentPath,activationIntentSha256,invocationToken,agentId,authorizedAt}` replaces releasedAt. Work Merger freezes authorizedAt after paused prepare, pushes/readbacks the receipt, then calls AgentRuntimeAdapter.release against its exact path/hash. Runtime start/release observation exists only in activation-qualified probes; the dispatch receipt is never rewritten. Null authorizedAt, releasedAt, pre-readback release, or changed bytes rejects.

- The cooperative doer suite has one literal final schema. Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerActivationRef={activationOrdinal,state,intentPath,intentSha256,dispatchIntentPath,dispatchIntentSha256,dispatchPath,dispatchSha256,agentId,outputPath,outputSha256,receiptPath,receiptSha256,exchangePath,exchangeSha256,terminationPath,terminationSha256}` uses state prepared|authorized|running|terminal|exchanged|terminated. prepared has intent/dispatch-intent pairs only; authorized|running add dispatch pair and agentId; terminal adds output/receipt pairs; exchanged additionally adds exchange pair; terminated instead adds only termination pair after dispatch/agent and keeps output/receipt/exchange null. Every path is the exact D/A root, repeated fields cross-hash, ordinals are contiguous, and the activationSetSha256 preimage is the compact canonical complete array in ordinal order.

- Final exact doer `SuccessorSuiteAttempt={kind:'doer',ordinal,status,terminalOutcome,skill,cwd,documentBindingKind,planningPath,planningSha256,doingPath,doingSha256,executionBaseSha,runtimeDoingPath,runtimeDoingInitialSha256,runtimeDoingFinalSha256,runtimeDoingArchivePath,runtimeDoingArchiveSha256,doerTransitionPath,doerTransitionSha256,doerLedgerPath,doerLedgerSha256,activations,activationSetSha256,artifacts,artifactSetSha256,startedAt,deadlineAt,endedAt}` replaces every singular prompt/invocationToken/dispatch/agentId doer form; planner retains its final schema. status prepared has terminalOutcome/start/end/final-runtime/archive/ledger-hash null and activations exact `[]`; running has null outcome/end/final-runtime/archive/ledger-hash, nonnull startedAt, zero or more exchanged activations plus at most one final prepared|authorized|running|terminal member, and no terminated member; completed has terminalOutcome implemented|review_findings, all activations exchanged, final runtime/archive/ledger hashes and endedAt nonnull, and a final complete exchange; interrupted has terminalOutcome runtime_errored|runtime_cancelled|invalid_output, all prior members exchanged plus exactly one final terminated member, final checkpoint/archive/ledger hashes and endedAt nonnull. deadlineAt and doer transition pair are always nonnull. artifacts is the complete legal suite set and activationSetSha256/artifactSetSha256 hash their respective compact canonical path/hash preimages. Any singular authority field, two live activations, gap, terminal without exchange, or status/null mismatch rejects.

- Exchange branches and checkpoint output are exact. Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerCheckpointOutcome={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,activationReceiptPath,activationReceiptSha256,groupId,checkpointKind,result,unitReceiptPaths,unitReceiptSetSha256,sourceHeadSha,treeSha,runtimeDoingAfterSha256,completedAt}` lives at `artifacts/post-merge-successor/suite/doer/attempt-<D>/activations/activation-<A>-checkpoint-outcome.v1.json`; result is the complete exact record-doer-checkpoint success object, unitReceiptPaths is its checkpoint-order receipt path projection, and the set hash covers compact canonical `{path,sha256}[]`. It is registered as doer_checkpoint_outcome before exchange publication.

- Final `SuccessorDoerExchangeResult.kind` is command|checkpoint|complete. command requires activation output command_request and a nonnull result pair naming the one D/O/E SuccessorDoerCommandOutcome; checkpoint requires checkpoint_request and a nonnull pair naming the SuccessorDoerCheckpointOutcome above; complete requires complete output and both result fields null. Every branch repeats D/A, binds the activation receipt, and requires productStatusAfterSha256 equal a post-action re-probe of the activation's exact captured product bytes because coordinator actions mutate only Desk. PreReleaseArtifact and doer SuiteArtifactRef add doer_checkpoint_outcome. Missing inline-result projection, multiple result artifacts, wrong request/result kind, nullable mismatch, or A+1 before exchange readback rejects.

- Secret channels have one containing journal. Exact `DescriptorSecretOwnerIdentity={executionHost,bootIdSha256,pid,pgid,startToken}` uses positive pid/pgid and startToken as lowercase SHA-256 of the complete trusted same-host process-start probe bytes. Final `DescriptorSecretChannelState={role,childFd,generation,state,updatedAt}` removes ownerStartToken and uses state pending|sealed|aborted. ownerKeySha256 is lowercase SHA-256 of the compact canonical complete ProcessRegistrationOwner bytes. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorSecretChannelJournal={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,owner,ownerKeySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,launchSpecSha256,generation,ownerIdentity,phase,states,stateSetSha256,authorizedAt,consumedAt,updatedAt}` lives only at Desk-control path `_meta/release-terminal/<rootIdentitySha256>/descriptor-secret-channels/<ownerKeySha256>/attempt-<processAttemptOrdinal>.v1.json`; rootIdentitySha256 is the owning release root identity and every owner/key/path component cross-validates. It is never a task/archive artifact.

- states is the exact BINARY-role-ordered projection of DescriptorLaunchSpec.secretChannels with matching fd/generation and its set hash covers compact canonical complete states. pending|sealed|aborted have both time fields null and require every state equal the phase; authorized keeps every state sealed, has authorizedAt nonnull/consumedAt null; consumed keeps sealed states and has both ordered times nonnull after child EOF/close/provider-fd proof. The pending journal is pushed/read back before acquisition; sealed and aborted atomically replace it; authorized is pushed/read back before release; consumed freezes its final hash. RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope adds `secretChannelJournalPath,secretChannelJournalSha256`, both null iff the launch has no channels and otherwise nonnull from prepared onward with the hash of the current journal version; terminal preserves the consumed or aborted hash. Owner identity/generation drift, partial role set, mixed states, skipped phase, mutable consumed bytes, or missing envelope pair rejects without secret persistence.

- Rollover source and relocated bytes are exact. `RolloverDeskSourceState={kind:'absent',deskCommitSha,prefix}|{kind:'present',deskCommitSha,prefix,members,memberSetSha256}`; present members are the complete BINARY path-sorted `{path,blobSha256}` set under the predecessor prefix and absent forbids members/hash. bootstrap_pre_task conflicts require absent; doer_transition conflicts require present. Exact `RolloverGitObjectClosure={sourceCommitSha,sourceTreeSha,objectIds,objectIdSetSha256,packPath,packSha256}` uses the complete BINARY object-id set reachable from sourceCommitSha and a trusted Git pack written under the relocated payload; an empty temporary object database must index-pack it and reproduce the commit/tree/every object before local deletion.

- Exact `RolloverPayloadMember={sourcePath,sourceSha256,relocatedPath,relocatedSha256}` requires relocatedPath equal `artifacts/post-merge-successor/rollover-ancestors/<predecessorSuccessorKey>/relocated-payload/desk/<sourcePath>` and equal hashes. Exact no-extra-fields `RolloverRelocatedPayloadManifest={version:1,replacementSuccessorKey,predecessorSuccessorKey,sourceKind,sourceCommitSha,sourceTreeSha,deskSourceState,members,memberSetSha256,gitObjectClosure,createdAt}` lives at the relocated payload root's `manifest.v1.json`; members exactly map the source state's members and the Git closure uses sibling `source-objects.pack`. sourceKind is bootstrap_pre_task|desk_task_present; the former has no Desk members but still requires the verified full Git closure. The manifest and every payload byte are pushed/read back before any destructive command.

- Final exact mutable `SuccessorRolloverAncestorCleanupAttempt={version:1,replacementSuccessorKey,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictKind,conflictPath,conflictSha256,rolloverPath,rolloverSha256,sourceKind,sourceCommitSha,sourceTreeSha,acceptedBaseSha,deskSourceState,worktreePath,worktreeHeadSha,branch,localRefSha,remoteRef,remoteObservedSha,phase,payloadManifestPath,payloadManifestSha256,payloadDeskSha,activeCommand,commandResults,worktreeAbsent,registeredWorktreeAbsent,localRefAbsent,predecessorDeskPrefixAbsent,sharedRemoteUntouched,startedAt,updatedAt}` lives at replacement-task-relative `artifacts/post-merge-successor/rollover-ancestors/<predecessorSuccessorKey>/cleanup.attempt.v1.json` with phase prepared|payload_materialized|payload_published|local_cleanup_running|local_clean|desk_cleanup_running|complete.

- prepared freezes all source/pre-state fields, has null payload/active command, empty results, all absence/untouched booleans false; payload_materialized adds manifest pair with payloadDeskSha null; payload_published adds its prior containing Desk commit SHA after remote readback and proves the Git pack in a fresh object DB; local_cleanup_running has one prepared registered command and preserves false local booleans; local_clean clears active, freezes terminal command results, sets all three local absence booleans and sharedRemoteUntouched true, while predecessorDeskPrefixAbsent equals sourceKind bootstrap_pre_task; desk_cleanup_running is legal only for desk_task_present and has its prepared descendant-preserving Desk CAS command; complete has null activeCommand and every boolean true. For bootstrap_pre_task, complete follows local_clean without Desk deletion. For desk_task_present, the final CAS removes exactly the frozen source prefix and replaces the attempt with the cleanup receipt while preserving every unrelated remote entry. Response loss re-probes each exact pre/post state; no mutation reruns after its postcondition.

- Final exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorRolloverAncestorCleanupReceipt={version:1,replacementSuccessorKey,predecessorSuccessorKey,conflictKind,conflictPath,conflictSha256,rolloverPath,rolloverSha256,sourceKind,sourceCommitSha,sourceTreeSha,acceptedBaseSha,deskSourceState,worktreePath,worktreeHeadSha,branch,localRefSha,remoteRef,remoteObservedSha,payloadManifestPath,payloadManifestSha256,payloadDeskSha,commandResults,worktreeAbsent:true,registeredWorktreeAbsent:true,localRefAbsent:true,predecessorDeskPrefixAbsent:true,sharedRemoteUntouched:true,completedAt}` replaces the Round 69 shorter receipt and is the exact complete attempt projection without phase/activeCommand/startedAt/updatedAt. Its bytes contain no containing-commit SHA; external fetch/readback locates the descendant containing it. Worktree/ref deletion is illegal until manifest/payload/pack remote readback and verification; predecessor Desk deletion is illegal until local cleanup. Local-ref CAS loss, unreachable/unpacked source commit, mismatched mapping, foreign worktree, changed Desk member, or shared-remote mutation blocks. Unit29.2.5a fault-tests every phase and both source variants.

- Fence acceptance binds the whole scanned track. Exact `TrackSourceMember={path,blobSha256}` and exact `TrackSourceTask={path,status,blobSha256}` are BINARY-path-sorted unique sets of every blob and every parsed task.md under the active source-track prefix at scanDeskSha. Exact no-extra-fields `TrackFenceTrackScanProof={track,sourcePrefix,scanDeskSha,expectedMembers,expectedMemberSetSha256,observedMembers,observedMemberSetSha256,expectedTasks,expectedTaskSetSha256,observedTasks,observedTaskSetSha256}` carries complete compact-canonical set hashes. Final exact no-extra-fields `TrackFenceOwnedPathProof={expectations,expectationSetSha256,observedTreeSha,trackScanProof}` replaces the Round 69 shorter form; trackScanProof is the complete proof above, its expected sets are read from scanDeskSha, its observed sets are read from observedTreeSha, and sourcePrefix is the exact active source-track prefix from the owning TrackArchiveAttempt. Because final TrackFenceFetchObservation contains this treeProof, every observation and publication receipt carries the same scan proof transitively without a duplicate alias field.

- candidate or preserving_descendant maps accepted only when both observed track sets byte-equal their expected scan sets; unrelated commits outside sourcePrefix remain allowed. Any blob/task/status addition, removal, or change inside sourcePrefix maps outcome rescan regardless of candidate ancestry. not_ancestor with equal track sets maps advanced as before. TrackFenceFetchObservation/TrackFencePublicationReceipt outcome becomes accepted|advanced|rescan; rescan freezes the immutable P receipt, forbids P+1 and conflict counting, terminalizes the current TrackArchiveAttempt as scan_stale, and authorizes only scanOrdinal+1 with a fresh scanDeskSha/source/task/move-payload set. TrackFenceConflictReceipt still requires exactly eight advanced receipts. Unit29.2.6a races new tasks, task status changes, non-task track blobs, outside-track commits, fence-owned bytes, and all ancestry combinations.

### Round 71 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for cooperative command interruption, rollover closed-set registration, track-scan projection and stale lineage, the live archive-validation owner, preliminary root-result persistence, and bootstrap-cleaner generation recovery. They replace every conflicting Round 69-70 sentence for those subjects.

- A coordinator command interruption terminalizes its already-completed activation without inventing agent failure. Exact no-extra-fields `SuccessorDoerCommandInterruption={version:1,successorKey,doerAttemptOrdinal,activationOrdinal,activationReceiptPath,activationReceiptSha256,exchangePath,exchangeSha256,commandOutcomePath,commandOutcomeSha256,reason,doerLedgerPath,doerLedgerSha256,runtimeCheckpointPath,runtimeCheckpointSha256,completedAt}` lives at `artifacts/post-merge-successor/suite/doer/attempt-<D>/activations/activation-<A>-command-interruption.v1.json`; reason is permanent_process_failure iff the command outcome is process_failure/retryDisposition interrupt, and command_cancelled iff it is cancelled/interrupt. The command exchange is pushed/read back first and names that outcome; the interruption then freezes the complete ledger and runtime checkpoint. PreReleaseArtifact and doer SuiteArtifactRef add doer_command_interruption for only this path.

- Final doer SuccessorSuiteAttempt interrupted terminalOutcome adds command_interrupted. That branch has all activations exchanged, with the final activation's exchange kind command naming the same interrupt outcome; it has no SuccessorRuntimeTermination or terminated activation, has one matching doer_command_interruption, and has nonnull final runtime/archive/ledger hashes and endedAt. Runtime_errored|runtime_cancelled|invalid_output retain the Round 70 final terminated-activation matrix. Exact D+1 priorDoerOutcomes for command_interrupted is the BINARY-sorted path/hash set of D's suite_attempt, command-interruption, command-outcome, doer_runtime_checkpoint, and doer_ledger; the runtime-transition adoption and v3 prompt consume that complete immediate set. PostMergeSuccessorAttempt.implementation_running permits contiguous command_interrupted attempts exactly as it permits other interrupted attempts, while implementation_complete still requires implemented. Unit29.2.5a/b/c run permanent failure and cancellation through exchange, suite interruption, D+1 transition, and eventual completion.

- Rollover payload bytes have four disjoint closed-set kinds. Final PreReleaseArtifact and doer SuiteArtifactRef additions are `rollover_ancestor_manifest|rollover_ancestor_git_pack|rollover_ancestor_desk_member|rollover_ancestor_cleanup`; the Round 69 generic rollover_ancestor_payload kind is invalid. Their sole path grammars under `artifacts/post-merge-successor/rollover-ancestors/<predecessorSuccessorKey>/` are respectively `relocated-payload/manifest.v1.json`, `relocated-payload/source-objects.pack`, each exact manifest member's `relocated-payload/desk/<sourcePath>`, and `cleanup-receipt.v1.json`. Materialization writes/hash-verifies the pack, then BINARY-ordered Desk members, then the non-self-referential manifest; registration/push/readback follows that same order before payload_published. bootstrap_pre_task registers exactly pack+manifest+cleanup; desk_task_present additionally registers every manifest member. The manifest's members and Git closure must biject to those records, and every replacement/archive closed set rejects a missing, extra, aliased, or generic payload record.

- Track scans use one symmetric projection that cannot see protocol-owned writes as user drift. Exact `TrackScanProtocolPath={path,expectedPresence,expectedSha256,observedPresence,observedSha256,transition}` uses presence absent with null hash or present with a complete-file hash, and transition materialized_current_attempt|unchanged. Its BINARY-sorted four-row registry is exactly track-root `track-archive.attempt.v1.json`, track-root `track-archive.v1.json`, the triggering task's `archive-validation.attempt.v1.json`, and the triggering task's `archive-validation.v1.json`. The current-attempt row alone uses materialized_current_attempt: expected may be absent or the exact prior prepared attempt, observed is present and hash-equals the TrackArchiveLinearizationAttempt.trackArchiveAttemptSha256. Every other row uses unchanged and requires byte-equal presence/hash. Those four paths are excluded from both member sets and from task parsing, but are fully proved by this registry; no basename-wide or caller-selected exclusion is legal.

- Final exact no-extra-fields `TrackFenceTrackScanProof={track,sourcePrefix,scanDeskSha,protocolPaths,protocolPathSetSha256,expectedMembers,expectedMemberSetSha256,observedMembers,observedMemberSetSha256,expectedTasks,expectedTaskSetSha256,observedTasks,observedTaskSetSha256}` replaces the Round 70 form. Expected sets are the complete scanDeskSha projection and observed sets the complete observedTreeSha projection after applying the exact four-row registry. Each set hash covers compact canonical complete rows. Final `TrackFenceOwnedPathProof={expectations,expectationSetSha256,observedTreeSha,trackScanProof}` remains as Round 70, with the active attempt's exact path/hash also required among its owned expectations. candidate or preserving_descendant is accepted only when both projected member/task sets equal; a genuine included in-track change is rescan; an outside-track change is irrelevant; and a protocol-row mismatch is invalid, never rescan.

- Fence rescan has exact serializable state. Final TrackFencePublicationJournal complete outcome and final TrackFenceFetchObservation/TrackFencePublicationReceipt outcome are accepted|advanced|rescan. Exact final no-extra-fields `TrackFencePublicationReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,publicationOrdinal,checkpointDeskSha,baseDeskSha,fencePath,fenceSha256,candidateCommitSha,pushCommand,fetchObservation,observedDeskSha,candidateRelation,outcome,completedAt}` preserves the terminal observation. accepted requires equal projected sets and candidateRelation candidate|preserving_descendant; advanced requires equal sets and not_ancestor; rescan requires unequal included track sets under any valid relation. Only advanced authorizes P+1 or counts toward the eight-receipt conflict. accepted and rescan both forbid P+1.

- Exact immutable `TrackScanStaleReceipt={version:1,identitySha256,ordinal,scanOrdinal,trackArchiveAttemptPath,trackArchiveAttemptSha256,fencePublicationReceiptPath,fencePublicationReceiptSha256,track,trackGeneration,scanDeskSha,observedDeskSha,sourcePrefix,expectedMemberSetSha256,observedMemberSetSha256,expectedTaskSetSha256,observedTaskSetSha256,code:'track_scan_stale',completedAt}` lives at Desk-control sibling `_meta/release-terminal/<identitySha256>/track-generations/<trackGeneration>/<scanDeskSha>/linearization/attempt-<N>-scan-stale.v1.json`. After a rescan publication receipt is pushed/read back, one descendant-preserving Desk checkpoint atomically replaces the live TrackArchiveLinearizationAttempt with this receipt; it creates no move command journal and mutates no source-track byte. Response loss accepts only absent, exact candidate, or a preserving descendant containing exact bytes. Any changed old attempt, scan proof, or publication receipt blocks.

- TrackArchiveAttempt phase remains prepared|root_moved|archive_validated; scan_stale is not an invented phase. Initial prepared keeps both prior pairs null. A rescan successor fetches a remote descendant containing the exact TrackScanStaleReceipt, scans that fetched commit, and atomically replaces the old active prepared attempt with scanOrdinal+1; priorLinearizationReceiptPath/hash name the stale receipt and priorTrackArchiveAttemptPath/hash name the exact old attempt frozen by it. The existing prior-lineage pair therefore accepts exactly TrackArchiveLinearizationReceipt, TrackFenceConflictReceipt, or TrackScanStaleReceipt according to branch. Recovery before stale publication resumes it; after stale publication but before successor creation performs the fresh scan; after successor push adopts its exact bytes. Unit29.2.6a/b/c prove absent/present initial attempt materialization, resumed self-writes, genuine task/status/blob drift, outside-track commits, stale-publication races, and eventual accepted move.

- Final archive validation authenticates exactly its one live owner. Exact no-extra-fields `ArchiveValidationRootEffectProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,effectIntentPath,effectIntentSha256,effectSpawnPath,effectSpawnSha256,invocationToken,processIdentity,releaseHandleSha256,lifecycleProbePath,lifecycleProbeSha256,otherLiveOwners,observedAt}` lives at task path `artifacts/archive-validation/attempt-<A>-root-effect-live-owner.v1.json`; otherLiveOwners is exact `[]`. It is a TerminalArtifact of kind archive_validation_live_owner and terminalPhase archive_validation. The intent/spawn/handle/identity equal the currently executing ordinary-normal or repaired-root terminal effect, and the immutable lifecycle probe proves that registered process running on the same boot while every bootstrap process, prior effect, successor effect, and cleaner is dead/reaped or absent.

- Final ArchiveValidationAttempt inserts `rootEffectProofPath,rootEffectProofSha256` immediately before phase and final ArchiveValidationReceipt inserts that pair immediately before commandResult; every other field/order/null matrix remains the Round 68 final schema. The pair is always nonnull/hash-valid and the proof ordinal equals attemptOrdinal. The root effect writes/registers/pushes/readbacks the proof before the prepared validation attempt advances, then performs validation itself. This one exact owner is the sole live-at-final exception; no cleaner exists yet. The root effect must later be terminal/dead/reaped with matching identity before any cleanup cleaner generation is prepared.

- A root result is an immutable Desk-control artifact, not an unlocated return value. Exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalPreliminaryResult={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,effectIntentPath,effectIntentSha256,effectSpawnPath,effectSpawnSha256,invocationToken,processIdentity,archiveValidationPath,archiveValidationSha256,result,producedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<rootIdentitySha256>/preliminary-result.v1.json`; result is exactly ordinary-normal or repaired-root ReleaseTerminalResult and its archiveValidationPath equals the repeated pair. After every result member, final archive receipt, repair/Slugger proof, and control checkpoint is valid, the root effect writes/pushes/readbacks this create-once file before exit. Its only sanitized stdout is compact canonical no-LF `ReleaseTerminalPreliminaryResultLocator={preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256}`, and the registered terminal command stdout hash must equal those bytes. Response loss adopts the artifact/terminal envelope by intent, invocation token, and process identity and never relaunches the effect. Nonroot results do not create this artifact.

- Bootstrap cleaners are created only after the preliminary artifact and root effect terminal/dead/reaped proof; the Round 69 pre-validation task-registered cleanup launch/spawn kinds and sole paused-cleaner live exception are invalid. Cleaner control lives only under `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/generations/`. Exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,bootstrapReceiptPath,bootstrapReceiptSha256,archiveValidationPath,archiveValidationSha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,invocationToken,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` and exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupSpawn={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,invocationToken,processIdentity,descriptorSpawnReceipt,releaseHandleSha256,preparedAt}` live at `attempt-<C>-launch-intent.v1.json` and `attempt-<C>-spawn.v1.json`. C is contiguous; C=1 has the empty prior set and later intents bind every prior outcome in ordinal path order.

- Cleaner priorOutcomePaths is the complete ordinal-ordered path list for attempts 1..C-1 and priorOutcomeSetSha256 hashes compact canonical no-LF `{path,sha256}[]` bytes in that same order; each path resolves one exact immutable TerminalBootstrapCleanupPreReleaseOutcome. ReleaseTerminalCompletion repeats the selected intent's complete prior path list and hash byte-for-byte. Missing, extra, reordered, duplicate, changed, skipped-ordinal, or post-selection outcomes reject.

- A pre-release dead cleaner is replaceable without effect ambiguity. Exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupPreReleaseOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommand,releaseAbsent:true,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,processDead:true,processReaped:true,outcome,completedAt}` lives at `attempt-<C>-pre-release-outcome.v1.json`; outcome spawn_failed requires null spawn and cleanup-attempt pairs, while died_paused requires its exact spawn pair and permits the cleanup-attempt pair either both null or both nonnull naming the still-prepared immutable attempt. It is pushed/read back before C+1. Every generation is descriptor-backed from the retained validated bundle, executes no cleanup while paused, closes every bootstrap-root source/entry descriptor after preload, and proves no provider/effect fd inheritance. Only absence of every release intent plus terminal/dead/reaped/no-effect proof permits replacement; a prepared attempt is retained for C+1 only when a fresh no-follow scan reproduces every root/member byte and its exact hash. Once any generation has a release intent, no later ordinal is legal.

- Work Merger selects a live paused cleaner, no-follow scans the unchanged root, and creates or byte-validates/reuses the exact prepared TerminalBootstrapCleanupAttempt before pushing/readback. Final exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupReleaseIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupLaunchIntentPath,cleanupLaunchIntentSha256,cleanupSpawnPath,cleanupSpawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,releaseHandleSha256,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/release-intent.v1.json`; it is then pushed/read back before release. The cleaner accepts only its own ordinal/handle and exact preliminary/archive/attempt chain. Release response loss probes/adopts that generation; it never creates C+1. Cleanup attempt/receipt members and deletion phases retain Round 69's exact schema/matrix, except that their preliminary pair names the immutable artifact above.

- Final exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,priorCleanerOutcomePaths,priorCleanerOutcomeSetSha256,cleanupLaunchIntentPath,cleanupLaunchIntentSha256,cleanupSpawnPath,cleanupSpawnSha256,cleanupReleaseIntentPath,cleanupReleaseIntentSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,cleanerTerminalCommand,completedAt}` replaces the Round 69 form at the same Desk-control path. It requires the complete contiguous failed pre-release generation history, selected generation terminal/dead/reaped, both bootstrap paths absent, and every preliminary result/archive/Slugger proof still valid. Unit29.2.6a/b/c fault every pre-release cleaner death, host reboot, generation response loss, release boundary, root-effect locator publication, and final completion; only this completion is ship success.

### Round 72 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for archive-validation process proof, terminal-effect post-state reconciliation, transferable cleanup ownership, external cleaner source retention, Slugger ordering, and final root result/completion. They replace every conflicting Round 69-71 clause for those subjects.

- Archive-validation owner evidence is Desk-control-only and branch-aware. Exact `TerminalOwnerLifecycleProbe={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,ownerKeySha256,owner,state,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,executionHost,bootIdSha256,processIdentity,registrationPath,registrationSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,observedAt}` lives under `_meta/release-terminal/<rootIdentitySha256>/archive-validation/<validationIdentitySha256>/attempt-<A>/owner-<ownerKeySha256>-probe.v1.json`. owner is the complete exact ProcessRegistrationOwner and ownerKeySha256 hashes its compact canonical bytes. state prepared_absent has an intent pair and all spawn/executionHost/boot/process/registration/terminal fields null; selected_running has exact spawn/executionHost/boot/process/registration fields, null terminal pair, same-boot trusted liveness, and owner equal the current effect; terminal_dead_reaped has every spawn/executionHost/boot/process/registration/terminal field nonnull plus trusted dead/reaped proof. Every null mismatch rejects.

- Exact `ArchiveValidationEffectOwnerMember={ownerKeySha256,owner,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,probePath,probeSha256,state}` projects each probe. Exact no-extra-fields `ArchiveValidationEffectProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,mode,terminalKind,selectedOwnerKeySha256,inventoryDeskSha,registrationRootIdentity,members,memberSetSha256,observedAt}` lives at the same Desk-control attempt root's `effect-proof.v1.json`. mode/terminalKind is exactly normal/ordinary, normal/repair_successor, repaired/repair_intermediate, or repaired/repair_root and selects the current task's exact TerminalEffectLaunchIntent/Spawn owner; ancestor, child, sibling, or root substitution rejects. members is BINARY-ownerKey order and its set hash covers compact canonical complete members.

- The inventory is a bijection over every root-bootstrap, terminal-effect, successor-effect, cleanup-cleaner, and Slugger intent/spawn reachable from the root bootstrap receipt in fetched task/archive and Desk-control trees, plus every no-follow registration directory under the exact root host-control identity. The selected member alone is selected_running; every other discovered member is prepared_absent or terminal_dead_reaped. An unregistered host directory, registered owner without artifact, artifact without member, duplicate key, omitted generation, foreign lineage, changed boot/process identity, or second live owner blocks. The current effect writes/pushes/readbacks every immutable probe and then the proof; response loss adopts the exact longest prefix and no probe byte is rewritten.

- The Round 71 task TerminalArtifact archive_validation_live_owner kind/path is invalid. Final ArchiveValidationAttempt replaces rootEffectProofPath/rootEffectProofSha256 with `effectProofPath,effectProofSha256` immediately before phase; final ArchiveValidationReceipt uses that pair immediately before commandResult. The pair names the Desk-control proof above and is nonnull in every prepared|failed|validated branch for all four terminal kinds. Only the already-excluded ArchiveValidationAttempt/receipt task path changes, so targetMemberSetSha256 remains byte-identical across proof creation, retries, and receipt replacement. The final control envelope repeats the pair. Unit29.2.6a/b/c cover complete inventory omission/injection, concurrent owner, boot change, response loss, both TrackDisposition branches, all four terminal kinds, and depth-two unwind.

- The preliminary artifact is the sole effect success postcondition. Exact `ReleaseTerminalRootPreliminaryResult` is either `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'ordinary',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,sluggerPath:null}` or `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_root',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,consumedRepairIndexPath,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath,repairLinkPath,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,sluggerPath:null}`. Exact ReleaseTerminalPreliminaryResult retains its Round 71 wrapper but result selects this union and no Slugger proof is a prerequisite. Nonroot terminal results remain their existing returned continuation values and never create a root preliminary artifact.

- The terminal effect ProcessSpec expected post state is SHA-256 of compact canonical no-LF `TerminalEffectPreliminaryPostState={kind:'release_preliminary_result',preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256}`. A clean child exit may emit exact ReleaseTerminalPreliminaryResultLocator bytes and has ordinary succeeded streams. If the child dies after the preliminary file is pushed/read back but before locator write/close/clean exit, Work Merger proves every member dead/reaped, fetches and validates the fixed-path artifact, and terminalizes the RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope with outcome reconciled, disposition reconciled, empty-stream hashes, and postStateSha256 equal that expected post-state hash. It then derives the locator from the artifact; it never fabricates child stdout. Artifact absent remains failed and cannot reconcile or relaunch. Unit29.2.6 faults artifact prepare/commit/push/readback, locator prefix/close, process exit, parent response loss, and both exact terminal branches.

- Cleanup generations execute from a descriptor-authenticated image outside both deletion roots. After preliminary artifact validation and root-effect terminal/dead/reaped proof, but before the first cleaner generation, Work Merger creates exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,sourceIdentity,imagePath,imageTempPath,imageIdentity,executionHost,createdAt}` and pushes/readbacks it at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/image.v1.json`; imagePath is exact `/Users/arimendelow/Library/Caches/spoonjoy-release-cleaner/<I>/release-terminal.mts`, mode 0600, and bytes/identity equal the still-present validated retained bundle. Temp/fsync/rename/parent-fsync recovery is exact, so effect death before or after preliminary publication cannot strand image preparation. Every cleaner and final Slugger gate opens this image no-follow through a verified descriptor, preloads it, closes all image/source descriptors before run authorization, and has no executable/control/log path inside bootstrapRoot/deletePath. Changed/missing image blocks a new generation.

- Cleanup ownership is transferable only after the prior owner is terminal/dead/reaped. Final TerminalBootstrapCleanupAttempt adds `ownerProcessAttemptOrdinal,ownershipTransferPath,ownershipTransferSha256,generationOutcomePaths,generationOutcomeSetSha256` immediately after executionHost. Before first ownership these are null/null/null/[]/hash([]); every effect phase requires nonnull owner/transfer and the complete prior outcome list/hash. Exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransfer={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorProcessAttemptOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomePath,priorGenerationOutcomeSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,createdAt}` lives at generation path `attempt-<C>-ownership-transfer.v1.json`; C=1 has null prior fields, and C>1 names C-1's immutable outcome. Work Merger pushes/readbacks the transfer, then one CAS rewrite of the cleanup attempt changes only owner/transfer/outcome fields and preserves root identity, members, memberSetSha256, phase, nextDeleteIndex, booleans, and every completed deletion byte.

- Final exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupGenerationOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,ownershipTransferPath,ownershipTransferSha256,releaseIntentPath,releaseIntentSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,terminalCommand,stage,processDead:true,processReaped:true,completedAt}` replaces TerminalBootstrapCleanupPreReleaseOutcome at `attempt-<C>-outcome.v1.json`. stage pre_release_failed has null spawn/ownership/release pairs; pre_release_died has spawn nonnull and release null; released_interrupted has all pairs nonnull and freezes the longest exact cleanup-attempt prefix after the process is dead/reaped. Every nonterminal generation outcome is pushed/read back before C+1. A live/foreign/indeterminate prior owner forbids transfer; a dead prior owner cannot mutate after transfer. Each cleaner revalidates attempt ownership before every rename/unlink/rmdir/fsync checkpoint and resumes exactly nextDeleteIndex, so no completed mutation repeats.

- Cleaner launch intents, priorOutcomePaths, and ReleaseTerminalCompletion use the complete contiguous path/hash set of all prior generation outcomes. Release intents are generation-qualified at `generations/attempt-<C>-release-intent.v1.json`, bind the current ownership transfer and attempt hash, and authorize only C. Intent response loss with a live process adopts C; dead/reaped before or after observed release creates the matching outcome and C+1 transfer. The final successful generation alone replaces the cleanup attempt with TerminalBootstrapCleanupReceipt. Unit29.2.6a/b/c fault reboot/death before release observation and after every detach/delete/fsync boundary through eventual receipt with at most one live owner.

- Bootstrap cleanup completes before notification. After the cleanup receipt and successful cleaner terminal/dead/reaped proof, Work Merger descriptor-prepares the one existing Slugger generation from the external image and keeps it paused; no message is authorized yet. It then removes image/temp/parent directories through a registered response-loss-safe command and freezes exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemoval={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,imagePath,imageIdentity,command,rootAbsent:true,tempAbsent:true,parentAbsent:true,completedAt}` at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/image-removal.v1.json`. Exact `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,generationOutcomePaths,generationOutcomeSetSha256,successfulCleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,cleanerTerminalCommand,imageRemovalPath,imageRemovalSha256,sluggerSpawnPath,sluggerSpawnSha256,completedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/cleanup-completion.v1.json`; the Slugger pair names its immutable prepared unreleased spawn receipt, not the mutable notification journal. It requires bootstrapRoot/deletePath/image/temp absent and every cleaner dead/reaped.

- Only after ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion is pushed/read back may Work Merger authorize the exact existing Slugger generation and message `Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end`. Any pre-cleanup release/message is invalid. Slugger's existing one-spawn observed|uncertain terminal matrix remains final; an unreleased death records uncertainty without a second spawn, which is acceptable because cleanup and deployment are already complete. After its terminal/dead/reaped proof, Work Merger writes create-once final root ReleaseTerminalResult bytes at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-result.v1.json`, using the matching ordinary-normal or repaired-root final schema with sluggerPath nonnull and hash-valid.

- Final exact `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,sluggerTerminalCommand,completedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-completion.v1.json`. It requires exact final result bytes, cleanup completion, Slugger terminal projection, all bootstrap/image/helper paths absent, all process owners terminal/dead/reaped, and every task/archive/deploy proof still valid. Failure after the message but before these two create-once final files recovers from cleanup+Slugger postconditions without another message. Only this completion is ship success.

### Round 73 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for root-wide process inventory, all four terminal-effect results, reconciled streams, cleanup transfer and mutation recovery, helper-image lifecycle, Slugger replacement before authorization, generation outcomes, and terminal residue proof. They replace every conflicting Round 69-72 sentence for those subjects.

- All release-terminal processes use one constructible root registry. On Darwin, exact `terminalRegistrationRoot` is `/Users/arimendelow/Library/Caches/spoonjoy-release/process-control/roots/<I>`; on Linux it is `<hostControlRoot>/roots/<I>`, where `<hostControlRoot>` retains the Round 57 platform selection. `<I>` is rootIdentitySha256. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalRegistrationRootIdentity={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,path,device,inode,mode,uid,markerPath,markerSha256}` requires a real same-uid mode-0700 directory, a regular same-uid mode-0600 `root-identity.v1.json` marker whose compact canonical no-LF bytes are `{version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost}`, and identities obtained by no-follow platform lstat. No terminal process uses the legacy unscoped `<hostControlRoot>/<spawnToken>` path; its bundle, registry, startup, ready, child-run, sanitized streams, and temporary files live under `<terminalRegistrationRoot>/owners/<ownerKeySha256>/attempt-<processAttemptOrdinal>/`, with that attempt directory real same-uid mode 0700 and files regular same-uid mode 0600.

- Final `ProcessRegistrationOwner` is the existing closed union plus exact variants `{kind:'root_bootstrap',rootIdentitySha256,bootstrapOrdinal}`, `{kind:'terminal_effect',rootIdentitySha256,effectOrdinal,mode,action}`, `{kind:'successor_effect',rootIdentitySha256,effectOrdinal,successorKey,action}`, `{kind:'bootstrap_cleanup',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}`, `{kind:'terminal_helper_image',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,action:'prepare'|'remove'}`, and `{kind:'slugger_external',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}`; obsolete cleanup-cleaner, unqualified Slugger, or owner values without rootIdentitySha256 are invalid in release-terminal. ownerKeySha256 is SHA-256 of compact canonical complete owner bytes. Exact immutable `TerminalProcessAuthority={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,registrationDirectory,registrationDirectoryIdentity,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/process-owners/<ownerKeySha256>/attempt-<P>-authority.v1.json` and is pushed/read back before the registration directory or any OS process may exist. Exact immutable `TerminalProcessSpawn={version:1,authorityPath,authoritySha256,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,spawnToken,registrationBundlePath,registrationBundleSha256,descriptorSpawnReceipt,processIdentity,spawnedAt}` lives beside it as `attempt-<P>-spawn.v1.json`; prepared owners have no spawn file, and every spawned owner has this one fixed path. Thus every owner has one immutable authority path and every actual spawn has one immutable, mechanically derived spawn path.

- Exact mutable `TerminalRootOwnerRegistry={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,registrationRootIdentity,revision,entries,entrySetSha256,selectionPath,selectionSha256,updatedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/process-owner-registry.v1.json`. `entries` is the complete BINARY-ownerKey/processAttemptOrdinal-sorted array of exact `TerminalRootOwnerEntry={ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,state}`. State `prepared_absent` has a null spawn pair and terminal pair; `spawned_paused|running_unselected|selected_running` has a nonnull spawn pair and null terminal pair; `terminal_dead_reaped` has both pairs nonnull; `cancelled_absent` has both pairs null and is permitted only from prepared_absent. running_unselected is legal only for the one terminal effect currently executing archive validation; every other running owner blocks selection. Every authority creation, spawn publication, run-authorization, terminal command publication, or cancellation is one force-with-lease Desk CAS from the exact fetched containing commit, increments revision by one, rewrites only the owning entry and updatedAt, then fetches and byte-validates the remote registry. `entrySetSha256` hashes compact canonical complete entries. A process may spawn only after its authority entry's prepared registry CAS is read back. The registry is a bijection with every authority/spawn/terminal artifact reachable below `_meta/release-terminal/<I>`, every root/successor task artifact reachable from the frozen root bootstrap receipt, and every no-follow owner attempt directory below terminalRegistrationRoot; a path or process outside that bijection rejects.

- Archive validation freezes ownership with a root-global CAS, not an observational guess. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelection={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,inventoryDeskSha,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,selectedOwnerKeySha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,cancelledOwners,cancelledOwnerSetSha256,registryAfterSha256,registryAfterRevision,registrationRootIdentity,selectedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/archive-validation/<validationIdentitySha256>/attempt-<A>/owner-selection.v1.json`. `inventoryDeskSha` is the fetched remote Desk commit containing registryBeforeSha256 and every referenced authority/spawn/terminal byte. `cancelledOwners` is the complete BINARY-sorted key/ordinal set of every nonselected prepared_absent entry; the selection CAS changes the exact selected running_unselected entry to selected_running, every listed entry to cancelled_absent, sets the selection pair, increments revision once, and leaves terminal entries byte-identical. Any other live/spawned entry blocks. While selectionPath is nonnull, registry CAS rejects every new authority or spawn. After readback, the validator repeats the complete artifact/registry/no-follow-host scan and accepts only exact registryAfterSha256/revision, exact registrationRootIdentity, no unregistered directory/process, and no owner-domain addition; response loss either adopts that exact CAS or proves registryBefore and retries it.

- Final `ArchiveValidationEffectOwnerMember={ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,probePath,probeSha256,state}` and final no-extra-fields `ArchiveValidationEffectProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,mode,terminalKind,inventoryDeskSha,registrationRootIdentity,ownerSelectionPath,ownerSelectionSha256,registryPath,registrySha256,registryRevision,selectedOwnerKeySha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,members,memberSetSha256,rescanPath,rescanSha256,observedAt}` replace the Round 72 forms. Members biject to the selected registry snapshot; selected alone is selected_running, cancelled entries have immutable prepared_absent probes plus selection cancellation, and all others are terminal_dead_reaped. Exact `ArchiveValidationOwnerRescan={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,ownerSelectionPath,ownerSelectionSha256,inventoryDeskSha,registryPath,registrySha256,registryRevision,registrationRootIdentity,artifactOwnerSetSha256,hostOwnerSetSha256,ownerDomainUnchanged:true,observedAt}` is written after the repeated scan and before the proof. Every proof member, registry entry, selection member, rescan set, host directory, and reachable owner must biject; a changed Desk head is accepted only when fetch proves every byte and owner set under this root unchanged.

- Final archive-validation support bytes use exactly `FinalArchiveValidationControlEnvelope={version:1,identitySha256,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,executionHost,storageDomain:'desk_control',terminalPhase:'archive_validation_final',trackGeneration,scanDeskSha,trackArchiveLocator,trackArchiveSha256,ownerSelectionPath,ownerSelectionSha256,effectProofPath,effectProofSha256,kind,attemptOrdinal,historyKeySha256,payload,payloadSha256,recordedAt}`. kind is exactly `successful_result|failure_snapshot|failure_evidence|failure_disposition|wait_checkpoint|wait_probe|wait_resolution_evidence|wait_resolution|blocker_probe|operator_authorization|blocker_resolution_evidence|blocker_resolution`; payload is the corresponding existing exact no-extra-fields schema, payloadSha256 hashes its compact canonical bytes, attemptOrdinal/historyKeySha256 obey that payload's owner/null matrix, and the selection/proof pairs equal the bound excluded ArchiveValidationAttempt/receipt. The TrackArchive pair is null/null for kept_active and nonnull/hash-valid for archived. All identity, generation, scan, selection, proof, and TrackDisposition fields are non-nested envelope context and cross-equal every envelope for the ordinal. No omitted field, extra field, naked payload, recursive envelope, control_attempt kind, or alternate field order is legal.

- Every terminal effect has one immutable result artifact and one discriminated postcondition. Exact `TerminalEffectContinuationArtifact={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,effectOrdinal,effectIntentPath,effectIntentSha256,effectSpawnPath,effectSpawnSha256,invocationToken,processIdentity,resultKind,result,producedAt}` uses resultKind `repair_successor|repair_intermediate`; result is the exact existing ReleaseTerminalResult narrowed respectively to `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'repair_successor'}` or `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_intermediate'}` and cross-equals its intent lineage. It lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-effects/effect-<E>-repair-successor-result.v1.json` or `effect-<E>-repair-intermediate-result.v1.json`. Root ordinary and repair-root use the existing exact ReleaseTerminalPreliminaryResult at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/preliminary-result.v1.json`, narrowed respectively to ordinary or repair_root. `TerminalEffectResultArtifact` is exactly that four-branch union; a path is fixed by branch and effect ordinal, create-once, pushed/read back before child exit, and never aliases another branch.

- Exact `TerminalEffectPostState` is `{kind:'ordinary_root',artifactPath,artifactSha256}|{kind:'repair_root',artifactPath,artifactSha256}|{kind:'repair_successor',artifactPath,artifactSha256}|{kind:'repair_intermediate',artifactPath,artifactSha256}`. ProcessSpec.expectedPostStateSha256 hashes compact canonical no-LF bytes of the branch selected by its frozen mode/action/lineage. A terminal-effect command is succeeded only on clean exit zero with its exact artifact and a complete locator that resolves/hash-equals it; it is reconciled only after all members are terminal/dead/reaped and the exact artifact is remotely readable and schema-valid. Artifact absence, branch/path drift, a second artifact, or an artifact not naming the frozen intent/spawn/process identity is failed and never relaunches that ordinal. Both succeeded and reconciled derive the returned ReleaseTerminalResult solely from the artifact; no root-only special case remains.

- Reconciled streams preserve what was actually observed. Exact `TerminalEffectStreamProjection={version:1,stdoutPath,stdoutSha256,stdoutByteLength,stdoutState,stderrPath,stderrSha256,stderrByteLength,stderrState,capturedAt}` is stored in the RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope terminal projection for terminal-effect owners. Each state is `empty|prefix|complete`; empty requires byte length zero and the SHA-256 of empty bytes, prefix requires positive safely captured sanitized bytes ending before a proven EOF, and complete requires a proven EOF. Hashes cover the exact immutable sanitized files and byte lengths are safe integers. Clean success requires stdout complete and byte-equal the compact canonical no-LF branch locator plus stderr empty; reconciled accepts empty, prefix, or complete independently and never changes their hashes. Work Merger obtains the locator from the result artifact on reconciliation, never from partial stdout. Locator-prefix write, full-write-before-close, stderr-prefix, capture response loss, child death, envelope publication, and remote-artifact readback are faulted separately; no trustworthy prefix is represented as empty.

- Cleanup ownership transfer is a recoverable three-state operation. Exact mutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransferAttempt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorProcessAttemptOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomePath,priorGenerationOutcomeSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,cleanupAttemptAfterSha256,receiptPath,receiptSha256,appliedAt,phase,cancelReason,preparedAt,updatedAt}` lives at `generations/attempt-<C>-ownership-transfer.attempt.v1.json`. receiptPath is the fixed immutable transfer path, appliedAt is frozen before prepared publication, and receiptSha256 hashes the deterministic receipt bytes below. prepared has null cleanupAttemptAfterSha256/cancelReason and is pushed/read back before the cleanup-attempt CAS; applied has the deterministic nonnull after-hash/null cancelReason after observation of an attempt owned by C; cancelled has null after-hash and cancelReason `owner_dead_before_apply|attempt_changed_before_apply`. Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransfer={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorProcessAttemptOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomePath,priorGenerationOutcomeSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,cleanupAttemptAfterSha256,appliedAt}` atomically replaces only applied state at receiptPath. Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransferCancellation={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorProcessAttemptOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomePath,priorGenerationOutcomeSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,cancelReason,completedAt}` replaces only cancelled state at `attempt-<C>-ownership-transfer-cancellation.v1.json`.

- Transfer recovery first fetches the cleanup attempt and both transfer basenames. If the attempt still hash-equals cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256, a dead/reaped C cancels and a live paused C may retry the one CAS. If the attempt's owner/transfer fields select C and every other byte equals the deterministic after-image, recovery records applied and materializes the receipt even when the CAS response or new owner died; that dead owner then creates died_after_transfer and C+1 names this receipt. Any third hash/owner, both terminal artifacts, or a prior owner not terminal/dead/reaped blocks. A cleaner cannot receive release, mutate the root, or create an outcome requiring ownership until the immutable transfer receipt is pushed/read back. The attempt stores only receipt path/hash, never the mutable transfer-attempt path.

- Each bootstrap filesystem mutation has a durable intent before host effects. Final TerminalBootstrapCleanupAttempt additionally has `activeMutationPath,activeMutationSha256,mutationReceiptPaths,mutationReceiptSetSha256` immediately after nextDeleteIndex. Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupMutationIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerProcessAttemptOrdinal,mutationOrdinal,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,kind,targetMemberIndex,sourcePath,targetPath,sourceBefore,targetBefore,sourceAfter,targetAfter,parentPath,parentIdentity,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/mutations/mutation-<M>-intent.v1.json`; the activeMutation pair always names/hashes these immutable bytes. Exact mutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupMutationProgress={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,phase,observedAt,fsyncedAt,updatedAt}` lives at sibling `mutation-<M>.attempt.v1.json`. kind is detach_root|unlink_member|rmdir_member: detach has null targetMemberIndex, source bootstrapRoot, target deletePath, present root sourceBefore/absent targetBefore and the reversed poststate; member mutations have targetMemberIndex exactly nextDeleteIndex, sourcePath equal the manifest member under deletePath, null targetPath/target states, matching present sourceBefore and absent sourceAfter. `sourceBefore|sourceAfter|targetBefore|targetAfter` is exact `{state:'absent'}|{state:'present',identity:TerminalBootstrapRootIdentity|TerminalBootstrapCleanupMember}` selected by kind. parentPath/identity names the one real containing directory fsynced after the mutation.

- The immutable intent and prepared progress are pushed/read back, then the cleanup attempt CAS installs the intent path/hash as the sole active mutation before rename/unlink/rmdir. prepared has null observedAt/fsyncedAt; effect_observed has nonnull observedAt/null fsyncedAt; parent_fsynced has both ordered times nonnull. In prepared, recovery accepts exactly either the declared prestate or declared poststate; prestate reruns the one no-follow typed adapter effect, poststate adopts response loss. Any mixture or identity drift blocks. effect_observed requires exact poststate and is pushed/read back before parent fsync; parent_fsynced requires a successful fsync plus immediate exact poststate recheck. Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupMutationReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,ownerProcessAttemptOrdinal,mutationOrdinal,cleanupAttemptPath,kind,targetMemberIndex,sourcePath,targetPath,sourceBefore,targetBefore,sourceAfter,targetAfter,parentPath,parentIdentity,completedAt}` then replaces the mutable progress file. One cleanup-attempt CAS clears activeMutation, appends the receipt path/hash, and advances detach booleans for detach or nextDeleteIndex by exactly one for a member. Response loss adopts that after-image. Thus a host-ahead absent member is legal only while named by the one immutable intent plus prepared/effect_observed/parent_fsynced progress; the ordinary prefix matrix resumes only after its receipt CAS. Every owner revalidates its immutable ownership receipt before each adapter call and Desk CAS.

- Generation terminalization has four exact unsuccessful branches and successful-receipt precedence. The root scan creates or validates the unowned prepared cleanup attempt before each generation intent, so every intent and outcome binds that same nonnull cleanup pair. Final no-extra-fields `TerminalBootstrapCleanupGenerationOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,ownershipTransferPath,ownershipTransferSha256,ownershipTransferCancellationPath,ownershipTransferCancellationSha256,releaseIntentPath,releaseIntentSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,stage,processDead:true,processReaped:true,completedAt}` lives at `generations/attempt-<C>-outcome.v1.json`. Every pair is both null or both nonnull, cleanup and terminalCommand are always nonnull/hash-valid, and intent binds cleanupAttemptSha256. spawn_failed has null spawn/transfer/cancellation/release pairs. died_before_transfer has spawn nonnull, exactly one transfer-cancellation nonnull, and transfer/release null. died_after_transfer has spawn/transfer nonnull and cancellation/release null. released_interrupted has spawn/transfer/release nonnull and cancellation null. The last three require a terminal/dead/reaped exact spawn; spawn_failed requires the exact spawn_error terminal command with no OS member.

- Before writing any outcome, recovery checks the fixed TerminalBootstrapCleanupReceipt path. A hash-valid receipt naming C, its ownership transfer, complete mutation receipt set, exact memberSetSha256, and absent roots wins regardless of a missing/failed process response: Work Merger reconciles or records the terminal command, selects C as successful, writes no outcome, and forbids C+1. Without that receipt, a stage is selected only by the longest immutable spawn/transfer/release prefix above. Outcomes are contiguous and immutable; C+1's intent and transfer name every C outcome. No nullable permutation, outcome plus matching success receipt, missing terminal command, or successor before outcome readback is legal.

- Helper-image preparation and removal are registered, generation-qualified protocols. Exact `TerminalFileIdentity={path,device,inode,mode,uid,size,sha256}` describes one complete no-follow regular file; exact `TerminalCleanupImageIdentity={path,device,inode,mode:384,uid,size,sha256}` is its mode-0600 image specialization. Exact identity root is `/Users/arimendelow/Library/Caches/spoonjoy-release-cleaner/<I>` on Darwin and `<hostControlRoot>/helper-images/<I>` on Linux; imagePath is `<identityRoot>/release-terminal.mjs`, imageTempPath is `<identityRoot>/.release-terminal.mjs.tmp`, and no operation removes or chmods the shared parent or any sibling identity root. sourceIdentity is TerminalFileIdentity for the validated retained release-terminal bundle; nodeRuntimeIdentity and ouroExecutableIdentity are TerminalFileIdentity values with frozen absolute paths, same-uid regular files, and executable owner bits.

- Exact mutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImagePrepareAttempt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,owner,sourceIdentity,nodeRuntimeIdentity,ouroExecutableIdentity,imageRootPath,imageRootIdentity,imagePath,imageTempPath,imageIdentity,commandEnvelopePath,commandEnvelopeSha256,phase,startedAt,updatedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/image-prepare.attempt.v1.json`; owner is `{kind:'terminal_helper_image',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,action:'prepare'}`. Its exact registered effect argv is `[nodeRuntimeIdentity.path,'--experimental-strip-types',sourceIdentity.path,'helper-image','prepare','--root-identity',rootIdentitySha256,'--source',sourceIdentity.path,'--temp',imageTempPath,'--image',imagePath]`, cwd is the frozen product worktree, envNames are exactly `CI|HOME|LANG|LC_ALL|NO_COLOR|PATH|TMPDIR`, timeoutMs is 120000, prestate is absent imageRoot/image/temp and imageRootIdentity/imageIdentity null, while temp_written onward has one real same-uid mode-0700 imageRootIdentity and published|recorded has absent temp plus nonnull imageIdentity. phase prepared|temp_written|published|recorded persists before spawn, after complete temp fsync, after no-replace rename+identity-root fsync, and after command terminal/push/readback. Response loss rechecks only those exact states; an existing byte-identical image under the frozen root identity is adopted, while any foreign path/type/identity blocks. Exact immutable TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage is projected only from recorded, names source/node/ouro/image/root identities plus command envelope, and atomically replaces the attempt at `image.v1.json`.

- Image removal is executed by a preloaded descriptor-backed image process and has its own journal. Exact mutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemovalAttempt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,owner,imagePath,imageIdentity,imageTempPath,imageRootPath,imageRootIdentity,sluggerRunAuthorizationPath,sluggerRunAuthorizationSha256,commandEnvelopePath,commandEnvelopeSha256,phase,startedAt,updatedAt}` lives at `bootstrap-cleanup/image-removal.attempt.v1.json`; owner action is remove. Its exact registered effect argv is `[nodeRuntimeIdentity.path,'/dev/fd/<imageFd>','helper-image','remove','--root-identity',rootIdentitySha256,'--image',imagePath,'--temp',imageTempPath,'--identity-root',imageRootPath]`, cwd is `/`, envNames are the same closed seven-name set, timeoutMs is 120000, and `<imageFd>` is the decimal inherited O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW descriptor whose fstat equals imageIdentity. The process preloads and hash-verifies the complete module, closes the image fd, publishes its paused child record, and cannot run until the exact Slugger run-authorization receipt below is pushed/read back. phase prepared|files_absent|root_absent|recorded checkpoints before authorization, after no-follow unlink/adopt plus containing-directory fsync, after identity-root rmdir/adopt plus shared-parent fsync, and after terminal command readback. Prestate is exact image identity, absent temp, exact empty identity root; poststate is image/temp/identity-root absent while the shared parent and every sibling are unchanged. Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemoval={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,imagePath,imageIdentity,imageTempPath,imageRootPath,imageRootIdentity,sluggerRunAuthorizationPath,sluggerRunAuthorizationSha256,commandEnvelopePath,commandEnvelopeSha256,imageAbsent:true,tempAbsent:true,identityRootAbsent:true,sharedParentUntouched:true,completedAt}` replaces only recorded state. Spawn failure/death before authorization is replaceable by the generic owner registry/outcome protocol and cannot remove a byte; after authorization, response loss reconciles exact poststate and no second remover may run.

- Slugger has an exact external-image authority and replaceable pre-authorization attempts. Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerExternalLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,imagePath,imageIdentity,nodeRuntimeIdentity,ouroExecutableIdentity,message,owner,invocationToken,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,notificationPath,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-launch-intent.v1.json`; message is exactly `Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end`, owner is `{kind:'slugger_external',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}`, notificationPath is the sole mutable SluggerNotification path, and launchSpec/cwd/env/timeout are closed below. Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerExternalSpawn={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,invocationToken,imagePath,imageIdentity,authorityPath,authoritySha256,descriptorSpawnReceipt,processIdentity,spawnToken,preparedAt}` lives at `attempt-<S>-spawn.v1.json`. The mutable SluggerNotification adds `selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,externalLaunchIntentPath,externalLaunchIntentSha256,externalSpawnPath,externalSpawnSha256`; before selection all five are null, and the one selection CAS fills all five from the live paused attempt before any release.

- Slugger's exact gate effect argv is `[ouroExecutableIdentity.path,'msg','--to','slugger','Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end']`. Its exact external gate launch argv is `[nodeRuntimeIdentity.path,'/dev/fd/<imageFd>','process-gate','--gate',gatePath,'--release',releasePath,'--startup',gateStartupPath,'--child-ready',childReadyPath,'--registration-bundle',actualBundlePath,'--registration-bundle-sha256',actualBundleSha256,'--spawn-token',spawnToken,'--',...effectArgv]`; normalized ProcessLaunchSpec replaces only the image fd with `@image-fd@`, bundle path with `@registration-bundle-path@`, and bundle hash with `@registration-bundle-sha256@` at their exact value indices. cwd is `/`, envNames are exactly the seven-name closed set above, timeoutMs is 120000, and the descriptor fstat/hash must equal imageIdentity. Gate and child preload the image and close every image/source descriptor before their paused ready checkpoints. Exact command envelope, registration bundle, member registry, sanitized stream paths, wait/blocker artifacts, and terminal command live below terminalRegistrationRoot for the slugger_external owner and cross-bind its authority/spawn.

- Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerAttemptOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,runAuthorizationPath,runAuthorizationSha256,outcome,processDead:true,processReaped:true,completedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-outcome.v1.json`. spawn_failed has null spawn and authorization pairs; died_unreleased has spawn nonnull and authorization null. Either is pushed/read back before S+1, and S+1 priorOutcomePaths is the complete contiguous ordinal path/hash set. A live/indeterminate attempt blocks. Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerRunAuthorization={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,notificationPath,notificationSha256,releasePath,releaseSha256,runAuthorizedGenerationSha256,authorizedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/run-authorization.v1.json`; it is created/pushed/read back only after the selected external attempt reaches child_recorded, notification/release bytes are read back, and cleanup receipt is valid. Only then is child-run publication legal. Once this file exists, S+1 is forbidden; running/terminal/uncertain recovery belongs to the selected attempt and a non-exit terminal result is final without another message. Before it exists, a provably terminal/dead/reaped spawn is died_unreleased and replacement is mandatory, not accepted as final uncertainty.

- The helper image remains present through Slugger run authorization. After TerminalBootstrapCleanupReceipt and cleaner terminal/dead/reaped proof, Work Merger prepares/selects Slugger attempts until one TerminalSluggerRunAuthorization is remotely readable. It then prepares/releases the registered image remover; Slugger no longer needs the image because both members preloaded it before authorization. Final exact `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,generationOutcomePaths,generationOutcomeSetSha256,successfulCleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,cleanerTerminalCommandPath,cleanerTerminalCommandSha256,sluggerPriorOutcomePaths,sluggerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,sluggerLaunchIntentPath,sluggerLaunchIntentSha256,sluggerSpawnPath,sluggerSpawnSha256,sluggerRunAuthorizationPath,sluggerRunAuthorizationSha256,imageRemovalPath,imageRemovalSha256,completedAt}` replaces Round 72. It requires absent bootstrapRoot/deletePath/image/temp/identityRoot, retained shared parent/siblings, a run-authorized exact Slugger attempt, no unsuccessful outcome for the successful cleaner, and every process except the selected authorized Slugger terminal/dead/reaped. The selected Slugger registration directory is the sole permitted host residue at this checkpoint.

- Final residue is a closed proof, not the phrase "helper paths absent." Exact `TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember={path,sha256,kind}` has kind `root_owner_registry|owner_selection|owner_probe|owner_rescan|effect_proof|effect_result|process_authority|process_spawn|process_terminal|cleaner_image|cleaner_intent|cleaner_transfer|cleaner_transfer_cancellation|cleaner_mutation|cleaner_generation_outcome|cleaner_receipt|slugger_intent|slugger_spawn|slugger_attempt_outcome|slugger_run_authorization|slugger_notification|image_removal|cleanup_completion`; exact `TerminalAbsentHostMember={path,kind}` has kind `bootstrap_root|bootstrap_delete_root|image|image_temp|image_identity_root|owner_registration_directory|registration_temp|sanitized_stream_temp`. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalFinalResidueProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,inventoryDeskSha,retainedMembers,retainedMemberSetSha256,absentHostMembers,absentHostMemberSetSha256,registrationRootIdentity,registrationRootEmpty:true,sharedImageParentPath,sharedImageParentIdentity,sharedImageSiblingSetSha256,observedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-residue-proof.v1.json`.

- retainedMembers is the complete BINARY-path-sorted set of every immutable Desk-control byte for this root in the listed kinds that exists before the proof, including every failed/replaced generation and mutation receipt; each resolves/hash-validates at inventoryDeskSha, and no mutable `.attempt.v1.json` remains. absentHostMembers is the complete BINARY-path-sorted set of bootstrapRoot, deletePath, imagePath, imageTempPath, identityRoot, and every owner registration/temp path obtained from the final registry; each is no-follow absent. Registration root itself is retained only as its exact identity and must contain only root-identity.v1.json, so registrationRootEmpty means no owner or temp child. The image shared parent must retain its frozen identity and exact sibling set/hash. Set hashes cover compact canonical complete arrays. An unclassified path below the Desk root, terminalRegistrationRoot, bootstrap parents, or image identity root, a retained hash mismatch, or an absent path present in any type rejects.

- Final root publication order is cleanup receipt, Slugger run authorization, image removal, cleanup completion, Slugger terminal observation, final reverse host cleanup, residue proof, terminal result, then terminal completion. The Slugger message can run only after cleanup receipt and cannot be duplicated; image/root cleanup can recover after message authorization without replacing Slugger. Final exact `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,residueProofPath,residueProofSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,sluggerTerminalCommand,completedAt}` requires a final owner-registry CAS making the selected Slugger terminal_dead_reaped, completion of the existing registered-process reverse host cleanup for every owner attempt directory, no mutable attempt, every retained hash still valid, and every absent-host proof freshly reproduced.
