A run-authorized Slugger non-exit result may yield uncertain notification but still permits finality because authorization is immutable and no replacement/message is legal; an unreleased death never does. Unit29.2.6a/b/c fault every registry CAS, prepare/spawn/selection race, four result branches, locator prefix/EOF, transfer CAS/receipt boundary, rename/unlink/rmdir/fsync/advance boundary, all four cleaner outcome branches, valid-receipt precedence, image temp/publish/remove boundaries, Slugger preauthorization deaths and run authorization, residue scans, shared-sibling preservation, and terminal result/completion response loss.

### Round 74 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for process-owner storage, archive-validation selection, registered terminal streams, cleanup launch/transfer/receipt bytes, helper-image production/removal, Slugger attempt selection, residue closure, and checkpoint continuity. They replace every conflicting Round 69-73 sentence for those subjects.

- Process authority no longer predicts a filesystem inode. Final exact `TerminalProcessAuthority={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,registrationDirectory,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` is pushed/read back while registrationDirectory is absent. Exact immutable `TerminalRegistrationDirectoryReceipt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,registrationDirectory,identity,createdAt}` uses `identity:{device,inode,mode:448,uid}` from a no-follow lstat after journaled mkdir/fsync and lives beside authority as `attempt-<P>-registration-directory.v1.json`. Final TerminalProcessSpawn adds `registrationDirectoryReceiptPath,registrationDirectoryReceiptSha256` immediately before spawnToken and repeats the receipt identity through its registration bundle. A synchronous spawn failure has authority plus directory receipt but no spawn artifact. Partial mkdir response loss adopts only the exact empty same-uid mode-0700 path; a foreign member/type/identity blocks.

- The owner registry is constructible from absence. Exact revision-zero bytes are `TerminalRootOwnerRegistry={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,registrationRootIdentity,revision:0,entries:[],entrySetSha256:SHA256(compactCanonical([])),activeSelectionIntentPath:null,activeSelectionIntentSha256:null,selectionReceiptPaths:[],selectionReceiptSetSha256:SHA256(compactCanonical([])),updatedAt}` and are create-once force-with-lease pushed/read back before the first authority. Response loss adopts only those exact bytes. Later revisions are positive safe integers incremented by exactly one CAS; no update assumes a preexisting entry.

- Final exact `TerminalRootOwnerEntry={ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,registrationDirectoryReceiptPath,registrationDirectoryReceiptSha256,spawnDisposition,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,state}` uses spawnDisposition `not_attempted|spawned|spawn_failed` and state `authority_prepared|directory_ready|paused|running|validation_selected|spawn_failed|terminal_dead_reaped|cancelled_absent|host_cleaned`. authority_prepared has not_attempted and null directory/spawn/terminal pairs; directory_ready has not_attempted, nonnull directory, null spawn/terminal; paused|running|validation_selected have spawned, nonnull directory/spawn, null terminal; spawn_failed has spawn_failed, nonnull directory/terminal and null spawn; terminal_dead_reaped has spawned and all three pairs nonnull; cancelled_absent has not_attempted and all three pairs null. host_cleaned requires registrationDirectory absent and preserves exactly one legal terminal prefix: not_attempted with all three pairs null after cancelled_absent, spawn_failed with directory/terminal nonnull and spawn null, or spawned with all three pairs nonnull. No other combination is legal. running is generic for root bootstrap, terminal/successor effects, cleaners, helpers, and Slugger; validation_selected is only the current validator. Every state change is one registry CAS after its referenced immutable bytes are remotely readable.

- Archive selection is acyclic intent -> CAS -> receipt. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,inventoryDeskSha,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,selectedOwnerKeySha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,cancelledOwners,cancelledOwnerSetSha256,registrationRootIdentity,createdAt}` lives at the existing attempt root's `owner-selection-intent.v1.json` and is pushed/read back before the CAS. The CAS points activeSelectionIntentPath/hash to those intent bytes, changes the selected running entry to validation_selected, changes the complete BINARY key/ordinal cancelledOwners set from authority_prepared to cancelled_absent, increments revision, and changes no other entry. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelection={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,inventoryDeskSha,registryPath,registryAfterSha256,registryAfterRevision,selectedOwnerKeySha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,cancelledOwners,cancelledOwnerSetSha256,registrationRootIdentity,selectedAt}` is written only after fetched readback of that CAS. The registry points only to the intent, so no hash preimage contains its own after-state hash. Response loss observes registry-before or the one deterministic after-image.

- An active selection is finite. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionReleaseIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,archiveValidationTerminalPath,archiveValidationTerminalSha256,selectedTerminalCommandPath,selectedTerminalCommandSha256,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` is pushed/read back only after the attempt has one terminal success/failure record and the selected owner is terminal_dead_reaped. Its CAS clears the active intent pair, appends the selection receipt path/hash to the contiguous selectionReceiptPaths set, increments revision, and otherwise preserves entries. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionRelease={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,selectionPath,selectionSha256,registryPath,registryAfterSha256,registryAfterRevision,releasedAt}` is written after readback. Failed validation retry, repair-successor continuation, cleaner preparation, and every later authority require this release receipt; while an active pair exists, only state/terminal transitions for already registered owners are legal. A release response loss adopts only the deterministic after-image. Thus selection blocks concurrent launch during proof but never latches the root.

- Final ArchiveValidationEffectProof replaces ownerSelectionPath/hash with the selection receipt pair and adds `ownerSelectionIntentPath,ownerSelectionIntentSha256` immediately before it; registryPath/hash/revision name the selected after-image. Final ArchiveValidationAttempt and ArchiveValidationReceipt repeat the intent pair immediately before the selection pair. Final exact `FinalArchiveValidationControlEnvelope={version:1,identitySha256,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,executionHost,storageDomain:'desk_control',terminalPhase:'archive_validation_final',trackGeneration,scanDeskSha,trackArchiveLocator,trackArchiveSha256,ownerSelectionIntentPath,ownerSelectionIntentSha256,ownerSelectionPath,ownerSelectionSha256,effectProofPath,effectProofSha256,kind,attemptOrdinal,historyKeySha256,payload,payloadSha256,recordedAt}` and every attempt support envelope repeat both pairs in that order. The rescan occurs after selection receipt and before validation effects; the release receipt is a later terminal prerequisite and is not retroactively inserted into immutable validation bytes.

- Final TerminalOwnerLifecycleProbe state domain for archive selection is `validation_selected|cancelled_absent|terminal_dead_reaped`. validation_selected has authority/directory/spawn/process-registration pairs nonnull and terminal null; cancelled_absent has authority nonnull and every directory/spawn/process/terminal pair null plus the matching selection-intent cancellation row; terminal_dead_reaped has authority/directory/terminal nonnull and spawn nonnull except for spawnDisposition spawn_failed. Final ArchiveValidationEffectOwnerMember uses that same state domain. The selected member alone is validation_selected, every selection-cancelled entry is cancelled_absent, and every remaining entry is terminal_dead_reaped; the obsolete prepared_absent member wording is invalid.

- Terminal effect streams have one legal envelope field. Final exact no-extra-fields `RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope={version:1,owner,commandOrdinal,logicalName,intent,stdinSha256,executionHost,bootIdSha256,status,timeoutMs,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt,generation,waits,waitSetSha256,blocker,stdoutPath,stderrPath,secretChannelJournalPath,secretChannelJournalSha256,terminalEffectStreams,result,updatedAt}` replaces every earlier form. The secret pair follows the existing channel null matrix. terminalEffectStreams is null for every non-terminal-effect owner and for a terminal-effect envelope before terminal; it is one complete TerminalEffectStreamProjection for every terminal terminal-effect envelope and its paths/hashes equal stdoutPath/stderrPath and result stream hashes. All prior status/generation/wait/blocker/result rules remain. Reconciled terminal-effect results may therefore preserve exact prefix hashes without adding an undeclared field; the old universal reconciled-empty-stream sentence is invalid only for terminal-effect owners.

- Cleanup transfer uses an immutable intent as the after-state pointer and has no hash cycle. Exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransferIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorProcessAttemptOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomePath,priorGenerationOutcomeSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,createdAt}` lives at `generations/attempt-<C>-ownership-transfer-intent.v1.json`. Exact mutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransferAttempt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,phase,cleanupAttemptAfterSha256,cancelReason,updatedAt}` uses prepared|applied|cancelled: prepared has null after-hash/cancelReason, applied has nonnull deterministic after-hash/null cancelReason, and cancelled has null after-hash plus `owner_dead_before_apply|attempt_changed_before_apply`. The cleanup CAS sets ownerProcessAttemptOrdinal C and ownershipTransferPath/hash to the immutable intent, never to its eventual receipt. Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransfer={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,cleanupAttemptAfterSha256,appliedAt}` is materialized after after-image readback; cancellation has exact `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransferCancellation={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,cancelReason,completedAt}`. One atomic Desk O/C commit deletes the mutable attempt while creating exactly one receipt or cancellation; transfer receipt hashing never depends on a cleanup attempt that points back to the receipt.

- Final cleaner generation schemas are literal. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalBootstrapCleanupLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,bootstrapReceiptPath,bootstrapReceiptSha256,archiveValidationPath,archiveValidationSha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,imagePath,imageSha256,invocationToken,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` replaces Round 71; each priorOutcomePaths member is an exact `TerminalOutcomeRef={path,sha256}` in contiguous ordinal order and the set hash covers compact canonical complete refs in that order. Exact final `TerminalBootstrapCleanupReceipt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,successfulProcessAttemptOrdinal,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeReceiptSha256,ownershipTransferIntentPath,ownershipTransferIntentSha256,ownershipTransferReceiptPath,ownershipTransferReceiptSha256,bootstrapRoot,deletePath,rootIdentity,memberSetSha256,mutationReceiptPaths,mutationReceiptSetSha256,nextDeleteIndex,rootDetached:true,rootAbsent:true,completedAt}` requires nextDeleteIndex equal manifest length, the complete contiguous mutation receipt set including detach, and both roots absent. It atomically replaces the cleanup attempt only after transfer receipt readback; valid receipt precedence uses these exact generation/transfer/mutation fields.

- For C=1 the cleanup attempt is unowned before transfer; for C>1 it retains C-1 ownership and the exact completed mutation prefix until C's transfer CAS. The Round 73 phrase unowned before each generation is invalid. Generation intent is created only after the current cleanup-attempt hash and complete prior-outcome set are frozen. spawn_failed still binds that nonnull current cleanup pair. A C+1 transfer names C's immutable outcome and changes only owner/transfer/outcome fields; no scan, member, phase, active-mutation, receipt-prefix, or nextDeleteIndex byte changes.

- The external image is produced before worktree cleanup by a measured self-contained JavaScript build, not copied from TypeScript afterward. During root bootstrap, while identity.repoCwd and the retained source exist, exact immutable `TerminalHelperLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,action,processAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,sourceIdentity,pnpmIdentity,esbuildIdentity,nodeRuntimeIdentity,ouroExecutableIdentity,imageRootPath,imagePath,imageTempPath,inlineProgram,invocationToken,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` owns both prepare and remove attempts. action prepare requires source/pnpm/esbuild/node/ouro identities, null inlineProgram, and exact cwd identity.repoCwd; action remove requires null source/pnpm/esbuild/ouro, nonnull node plus exact `TerminalInlineProgram={sourceBase64,sha256}`, and cwd `/`. Prior refs use contiguous TerminalOutcomeRef bytes. Authority.intentPath/hash always names this immutable intent.

- Prepare's exact registered effect argv is `[pnpmIdentity.path,'exec','esbuild',sourceIdentity.path,'--bundle','--platform=node','--format=cjs','--target=node22','--log-level=error','--outfile='+imageTempPath]`; the pinned lockfile package/version, pnpmIdentity, esbuild entry identity, source identity, argv, cwd, env, and output cap are frozen in intent. The result is a self-contained CommonJS `release-terminal.cjs`; imagePath is `<identityRoot>/release-terminal.cjs`. TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage records source/pnpm/esbuild/argv/output image hashes, so every cleaner and Slugger executes measured CJS bytes without a TypeScript loader or deleted dependency tree.

- Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,successfulProcessAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,sourceIdentity,pnpmIdentity,esbuildIdentity,nodeRuntimeIdentity,ouroExecutableIdentity,imageRemovalProgram,buildArgvSha256,imageRootPath,imageRootIdentity,imagePath,imageIdentity,imageTempPath,commandEnvelopePath,commandEnvelopeSha256,createdAt}` atomically replaces only recorded prepare state at `bootstrap-cleanup/image.v1.json`; prior outcomes are the complete contiguous prepare-attempt TerminalOutcomeRef set. imageRemovalProgram is exact TerminalInlineProgram whose decoded source byte-equals exported `IMAGE_REMOVER_PROGRAM_V1` in sourceIdentity at sourceHeadSha; Work Merger reads/freezes it before worktree cleanup and every remove intent copies it byte-for-byte. buildArgvSha256 hashes compact canonical exact argv. The receipt requires absent temp and root containing only the exact image.

- Final TerminalBootstrapReceipt adds `helperImageReceiptPath,helperImageReceiptSha256` immediately before commandResults, and bootstrap success repeats that pair. Root bootstrap cannot publish its receipt, terminal worktree cleanup cannot begin, and no terminal effect can launch until the exact image receipt is pushed/read back. Every terminal effect launch intent and recursive lineage therefore transitively binds the measured image without adding mutable image fields to task archives. A later image mismatch blocks cleaner/Slugger launch; it never rebuilds from a deleted worktree.

- Image preparation journals every filesystem prefix. Final mutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImagePrepareAttempt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,ownerProcessAttemptOrdinal,imageRootPath,imageRootIdentity,imagePath,imageTempPath,imageIdentity,phase,commandEnvelopePath,commandEnvelopeSha256,startedAt,updatedAt}` uses `prepared|root_created|temp_writing|temp_fsynced|published|recorded`. prepared has all identities null and all three paths absent. root_created has exact empty same-uid mode-0700 root. temp_writing is persisted before create/write and permits absent, zero-length, or partial same-uid mode-0600 temp up to the frozen output cap; dead-owner recovery no-follow unlinks/fsyncs that temp then resumes the same deterministic build under a fresh owner ordinal. temp_fsynced requires complete temp identity/hash; published requires exact image identity, absent temp, and root containing only image; recorded has terminal command readback. Each mkdir/create/write/fsync/rename/parent-fsync boundary is separately persisted or legally adopted. An immutable image receipt wins over a failed process response.

- Helper attempts have explicit replacement lineage. Exact `TerminalHelperAttemptOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,action,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,journalPath,journalSha256,stage,processDead:true,processReaped:true,completedAt}` lives at `bootstrap-cleanup/helper/<action>/attempt-<H>-outcome.v1.json`; stage is spawn_failed with null spawn, died_pre_effect with nonnull spawn and unchanged declared prestate, or died_recoverable_prefix with nonnull spawn and one legal journal prefix. The mutable journal's owner changes only by a CAS that names the complete prior outcome set. A live/foreign/indeterminate prior owner blocks. One atomic Desk O/C transition removes each terminal mutable helper command/transfer attempt when its immutable outcome or success receipt is created.

- Image removal uses the retained Node runtime plus a frozen inline CommonJS program, not the image it removes. Its launch argv is `[nodeRuntimeIdentity.path,'--input-type=commonjs','--eval',decodedInlineProgram,'--',rootIdentitySha256,imagePath,imageTempPath,imageRootPath]`, cwd `/`, closed seven-name env, timeout 120000; sourceBase64 decodes to exact NUL-free UTF-8 and sha256 validates before spawn. The program performs only lstat/fstat identity checks, unlink, rmdir, and parent fsync through the typed adapter. Final removal journal phases are `prepared|image_absent|root_absent|recorded`; prepared requires identity root containing exactly the image and no temp/extra member. Each atomic effect is preceded by the phase intent and its response loss adopts exact poststate. Because Node/inline bytes survive image deletion, terminal/dead/reaped death at any declared prefix creates died_recoverable_prefix and a fresh ordinal may resume; a valid TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemoval receipt wins and forbids another effect. Shared parent identity and sibling set are frozen before attempt one and rechecked after every ordinal.

- Exact immutable `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemoval={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,successfulProcessAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,inlineProgram,nodeRuntimeIdentity,imagePath,imageIdentity,imageTempPath,imageRootPath,imageRootIdentity,sharedParentPath,sharedParentIdentity,sharedSiblingSetSha256,commandEnvelopePath,commandEnvelopeSha256,imageAbsent:true,tempAbsent:true,identityRootAbsent:true,sharedParentUntouched:true,completedAt}` atomically replaces only recorded removal state; prior outcomes are the complete contiguous remove-attempt TerminalOutcomeRef set. A receipt plus same-ordinal outcome is illegal and receipt precedence is checked before outcome publication.

- Slugger attempts do not mutate notification until one can be authorized. Final exact no-extra-fields `SluggerNotification={version:1,task,mergeSha,message,ownershipGeneration:1,writerId,phase,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,externalLaunchIntentPath,externalLaunchIntentSha256,externalSpawnPath,externalSpawnSha256,generation,resultPath,resultSha256,completedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase `prepared|released|observed|uncertain`. prepared has any contiguous prior TerminalOutcomeRef prefix, null selected/launch/spawn/generation/result/completed fields. released has selected/launch/spawn/generation nonnull, generation run_authorized|running, and null result/completed. observed|uncertain preserve them, require terminal|aborted generation and matching result/completed; observed is clean exit, uncertain every non-exit after authorization. A dead unreleased attempt writes its immutable outcome, then one notification CAS appends that outcome ref while all selection fields remain null; S+1 therefore needs no clear/replace operation.

- Final `TerminalSluggerAttemptOutcome` outcome domain is exactly spawn_failed|died_unreleased; its spawn pair is null/non-null respectively, runAuthorization pair is always null, and terminal command is nonnull. Exact `SluggerPriorOutcomeSet` is the contiguous processAttemptOrdinal-ordered `TerminalOutcomeRef[]`, never a string path array or BINARY reorder. Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerRunAuthorizationIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,notificationPath,notificationBeforeSha256,releasePath,releaseSha256,generationCheckpointPath,generationCheckpointSha256,createdAt}` is pushed/read back after the external generation's exact child_recorded checkpoint; generationCheckpointPath/hash names that immutable child_recorded state. One CAS changes notification prepared to released, selects that attempt, and projects the same generation with only status/runAuthorizedAt advanced to run_authorized. Exact `TerminalSluggerRunAuthorization={version:1,authorizationIntentPath,authorizationIntentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,notificationPath,notificationBeforeSha256,notificationAfterSha256,releasePath,releaseSha256,generationCheckpointPath,generationCheckpointSha256,authorizedAt}` is written after readback; child-run publication and the owner-registry paused -> running CAS wait for this receipt. The notification points to the intent/generation, not the authorization receipt, so no hash cycle exists. Once receipt exists, no S+1 is legal.

- Slugger's external CJS vectors are literal. Gate actual argv is `[nodeRuntimeIdentity.path,'/dev/fd/'+gateImageFd,'process-gate','--gate',gatePath,'--release',releasePath,'--startup',gateStartupPath,'--child-ready',childReadyPath,'--registration-bundle',actualBundlePath,'--registration-bundle-sha256',actualBundleSha256,'--spawn-token',spawnToken,'--',...effectArgv]`; normalized sentinel value indices are exactly image 1, bundle path 12, bundle hash 14. The gate preloads/verifies CommonJS bytes and closes gateImageFd before gate ready. At the later child-spawn point, while the image receipt is still required present, the gate opens a new O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW childImageFd, fstat/hash-validates it against the frozen image identity, and launches child actual argv `[nodeRuntimeIdentity.path,'/dev/fd/'+childImageFd,'process-gate','--child','--child-ready',childReadyPath,'--registration-bundle',actualBundlePath,'--registration-bundle-sha256',actualBundleSha256,'--spawn-token',spawnToken,'--',...effectArgv]`; normalized indices are image 1, bundle path 7, bundle hash 9. The child preloads/verifies the bytes and closes childImageFd before child ready. effectArgv is the exact absolute ouro executable vector already declared; both wrappers use `/` cwd. Any index/count/flag/order or image/runtime/ouro identity drift rejects before spawn.

- Bootstrap cleanup uses the same gate/child vectors and descriptor lifecycle with exact internal effectArgv `['bootstrap-cleanup','--launch-intent',intentPath,'--launch-intent-sha256',intentSha256,'--cleanup-attempt',cleanupAttemptPath,'--cleanup-attempt-sha256',cleanupAttemptSha256,'--process-attempt',String(processAttemptOrdinal)]`. The loaded CommonJS child dispatches this internal verb directly and never resolves it through PATH or spawns another script. Slugger alone uses external effectArgv `[ouroExecutableIdentity.path,'msg','--to','slugger','Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end']`. Any other effect verb/member is invalid.

- Terminal residue scans an exact transitive closure rather than all of Desk. Final TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind is exactly `root_owner_registry|owner_selection_intent|owner_selection|owner_selection_release|owner_probe|owner_rescan|effect_proof|effect_result|archive_validation_control|track_linearization|track_fence|track_scan_stale|track_conflict|process_authority|process_directory|process_spawn|process_terminal|cleaner_image_intent|cleaner_image|cleaner_intent|cleaner_transfer_intent|cleaner_transfer|cleaner_transfer_cancellation|cleaner_mutation_intent|cleaner_mutation|cleaner_generation_outcome|cleaner_receipt|helper_intent|helper_outcome|slugger_intent|slugger_spawn|slugger_attempt_outcome|slugger_run_authorization|slugger_notification|image_removal|cleanup_completion`. owner_selection_release classifies both exact release-intent and release-receipt basenames for each validation attempt. archive_validation_control uses the exact envelope basenames under each validation attempt; track kinds use the previously exact linearization/fence/stale/conflict paths reachable from final ArchiveValidationReceipt -> TrackDisposition -> TrackArchive receipts. Other kinds use the exact `_meta/release-terminal/<I>` paths declared here.

- Final TerminalFinalResidueProof adds `closureRoots,closureRootSetSha256` before retainedMembers. closureRoots is exactly the one release-terminal Desk-control root `_meta/release-terminal/<I>` and its hash covers compact canonical bytes for that singleton path/identity. Before proof creation, retainedMembers bijects to every immutable file recursively under that root except the fixed post-proof basenames `terminal-residue-proof.v1.json`, `terminal-result.v1.json`, and `terminal-completion.v1.json`; those three are absent at observation and are later validated directly by ReleaseTerminalCompletion, avoiding self-reference. It additionally contains each individually resolved task/archive/track transitive receipt ref named by final ArchiveValidationReceipt -> TrackDisposition -> TrackArchive, classified by the exact track kinds above; siblings of those individual refs are not scanned or implied. It does not inspect unrelated Desk paths or whole task/archive directories. Every mutable `.attempt.v1.json` under the release-terminal root is absent; final process registry entries are host_cleaned; terminalRegistrationRoot contains only its marker; bootstrap/delete/image identity roots and every owner attempt directory are absent; shared image parent/sibling identity is unchanged. Missing kinds, extra release-terminal-root files, broken transitive refs, or path aliases reject.

- Final cleanup order remains cleanup receipt -> one Slugger authorization -> image removal -> cleanup completion -> Slugger terminal -> owner host cleanup -> residue proof -> terminal result -> terminal completion. Final exact `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,generationOutcomePaths,generationOutcomeSetSha256,successfulCleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,cleanerTerminalCommandPath,cleanerTerminalCommandSha256,helperPrepareIntentPaths,helperPrepareIntentSetSha256,helperPrepareOutcomePaths,helperPrepareOutcomeSetSha256,imageReceiptPath,imageReceiptSha256,sluggerPriorOutcomePaths,sluggerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,sluggerLaunchIntentPath,sluggerLaunchIntentSha256,sluggerSpawnPath,sluggerSpawnSha256,sluggerRunAuthorizationPath,sluggerRunAuthorizationSha256,helperRemovalIntentPaths,helperRemovalIntentSetSha256,helperRemovalOutcomePaths,helperRemovalOutcomeSetSha256,imageRemovalPath,imageRemovalSha256,completedAt}` uses contiguous ordinal TerminalOutcomeRef preimages for every intent and outcome set. Final exact `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,ownerRegistryPath,ownerRegistrySha256,selectionReceiptPaths,selectionReceiptSetSha256,selectionReleasePaths,selectionReleaseSetSha256,residueProofPath,residueProofSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,sluggerTerminalCommand,completedAt}` requires the complete ordinal selection and selection-release histories, final owner-registry host_cleaned set, exact residue closure, and one authorized Slugger terminal command. Unit29.2.6a/b/c now additionally fault registry creation, directory creation, selection intent/CAS/receipt/release, generic running/spawn-failure states, stream-projection insertion, transfer intent/CAS/O-C, exact cleaner schemas, every image build prefix, helper replacement, inline removal prefixes, Slugger outcome append/authorization intent/CAS/receipt, both literal launch vectors, and every residue kind/root.

### Round 75 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for registration-root creation, validation probes and paths, actual cleanup ownership, measured helper execution, Slugger lineage/completion, terminal-control residue, and Unit 37 terminal ordering. They replace every earlier conflicting sentence for those subjects.

- Registration-root creation is create-once and crash-complete. Exact immutable `TerminalRegistrationRootIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,registrationRootPath,markerPath,markerTempPath,markerBytesSha256,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/process-registration-root-intent.v1.json`; markerTempPath is exact `<terminalRegistrationRoot>/.root-identity.v1.json.tmp`. Exact mutable `TerminalRegistrationRootAttempt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,phase,rootIdentity,markerIdentity,updatedAt}` lives beside it as `process-registration-root.attempt.v1.json` with phase `prepared|root_created|marker_writing|marker_fsynced|marker_published|recorded`. prepared has root/marker/temp absent and null identities; root_created has exact empty same-uid mode-0700 root; marker_writing is persisted before create/write and permits absent, zero-length, or a bounded prefix of the exact mode-0600 marker temp; marker_fsynced requires complete temp bytes; marker_published requires absent temp plus exact final marker and parent fsync. Recovery unlinks/fsyncs only a verified partial temp and otherwise adopts exact poststate. Exact immutable `TerminalRegistrationRootReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,registrationRootIdentity,markerPath,markerSha256,completedAt}` is written/fsynced/renamed to `process-registration-root.v1.json` and parent-fsynced before the recorded attempt is unlinked and the parent fsynced again. Recovery treats an exact receipt as winning, verifies its complete poststate, and removes a matching stale attempt; a mismatching receipt or poststate blocks without mutation. Registry revision zero cannot be created until receipt publication and attempt removal are both proved. Final TerminalRootOwnerRegistry adds `registrationRootReceiptPath,registrationRootReceiptSha256` immediately after registrationRootIdentity; revision zero and every later revision require the exact receipt pair and byte-equal registrationRootIdentity. Final residue retains intent/receipt and requires the mutable attempt absent.

- Final TerminalRootOwnerEntry adds `hostCleanupDisposition` immediately after state. It is null in every non-host_cleaned state. host_cleaned uses exactly `cancelled_absent` with not_attempted/null directory-spawn-terminal pairs, `directory_ready_no_spawn` with not_attempted/non-null historical directory pair/null spawn-terminal, `spawn_failed` with the existing spawn-failed matrix, or `spawned_terminal` with the existing spawned-terminal matrix. Preselection settles every nonselected owner into the proof domain. Before selection intent publication, authority_prepared owners may be selected for cancellation; directory_ready owners must no-follow verify and remove their exact empty registration directory, fsync its parent, and CAS directly to host_cleaned/directory_ready_no_spawn through exact immutable `TerminalOwnerPreselectionSettlement={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,registryBeforeSha256,registryAfterSha256,reason:'directory_ready_no_spawn',completedAt}` at `process-owners/<ownerKey>/attempt-<P>-preselection-settlement.v1.json`. paused|running owners must be terminated/reaped and terminalized through their existing command protocol before retry. spawn_failed|terminal_dead_reaped owners remain terminal; host_cleaned remains absent. Only after no nonselected directory_ready|paused|running entry exists may selection freeze. Selection members are therefore exactly validation_selected, cancelled_absent, terminal_dead_reaped, or host_cleaned; final host cleanup later maps cancelled/terminal states to the matching host_cleaned disposition.

- Archive owner probe bytes are complete. Final exact no-extra-fields `TerminalOwnerLifecycleProbe={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,state,hostCleanupDisposition,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,executionHost,bootIdSha256,processIdentity,processRegistrationPath,processRegistrationSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,selectionIntentPath,selectionIntentSha256,observedAt}` lives at the previously fixed owner-probe path. validation_selected has null cleanup disposition, authority/directory/spawn/host/boot/process/registration/selection pairs nonnull, and terminal null; cancelled_absent has null disposition, authority/selection nonnull, and every directory/spawn/host/boot/process/registration/terminal member null; terminal_dead_reaped has null disposition, authority/directory/host/boot/terminal nonnull, spawn/process/registration nonnull for spawned and null only for spawn_failed, and selection null. host_cleaned has the registry's exact nonnull cleanup disposition, preserves its historical authority/directory/spawn/terminal pairs, has host/boot nonnull except cancelled_absent may keep both null, and has process identity/registration plus selection pairs null. Final exact `ArchiveValidationEffectOwnerMember={ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,probePath,probeSha256,state,hostCleanupDisposition}` projects those bytes. No older intentPath/registrationPath shorthand remains legal.

- Round 74 immutable path grammars are literal. Selection release intent/receipt live at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/archive-validation/<validationIdentitySha256>/attempt-<A>/owner-selection-release-intent.v1.json` and `owner-selection-release.v1.json`. Helper launch intent and outcome live at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/helper/<prepare|remove>/attempt-<H>-launch-intent.v1.json` and `attempt-<H>-outcome.v1.json`; helper mutable journal lives only at `attempt.v1.json` in that same attempt directory and is absent after outcome/receipt. Slugger authorization intent/receipt live at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/run-authorization-intent.v1.json` and `run-authorization.v1.json`. Registration-root files use the basenames above. Every path component cross-equals its schema identities/ordinals; alternate root, generic basename, alias, duplicate kind, or unqualified path rejects.

- Cleanup replacement binds the actual prior owner, not the previous failed ordinal. Final `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransferIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomeOrdinal,priorGenerationOutcomePath,priorGenerationOutcomeSha256,cleanupOwnerBeforeOrdinal,cleanupOwnerBeforeTransferIntentPath,cleanupOwnerBeforeTransferIntentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256,createdAt}` replaces Round 74. C=1 has all three prior-generation members null; C>1 has priorGenerationOutcomeOrdinal exactly C-1 and a nonnull matching outcome pair. cleanupOwnerBeforeOrdinal is null while the attempt is unowned or the positive ordinal of the latest generation whose transfer CAS actually applied, which may be less than C-1. Its transfer-intent pair is null iff owner is null and otherwise names that owner's immutable intent. The cleanup CAS compares this exact owner/pair plus cleanupAttemptBeforeSha256, then sets owner C and the current intent pair. spawn_failed or died_before_transfer never changes cleanup ownership. Thus C=2 after C=1 spawn failure legally transfers null -> 2, and later failures transfer from the last actual owner while preserving complete outcome history.

- The helper image build executes authenticated esbuild bytes directly. Final prepare TerminalHelperLaunchIntent removes pnpmIdentity. DescriptorExtra adds role `helper_image_source`. Exact DescriptorLaunchSpec uses trusted Node as executable fd 3, esbuildIdentity as alias entry fd 4, sourceIdentity as helper_image_source fd 5, and argv `['<node-fd-3>','<esbuild-entry-fd-4>','<source-fd-5>','--bundle','--platform=node','--format=cjs','--target=node22','--log-level=error','--outfile='+imageTempPath]`, normalized only to the declared descriptor sentinels. cwd is identity.repoCwd; env remains closed. Final TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage removes pnpmIdentity and preserves esbuildIdentity, sourceIdentity, descriptor launch spec/hash, and DescriptorSpawnReceipt. Pathname/package-name resolution, `pnpm exec`, changed entry/source bytes, undeclared fd, or output not produced by this receipt rejects.

- Slugger retains dual merge lineage. Final exact no-extra-fields `SluggerNotification={version:1,task,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,message,ownershipGeneration:1,writerId,phase,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,externalLaunchIntentPath,externalLaunchIntentSha256,externalSpawnPath,externalSpawnSha256,generation,resultPath,resultSha256,completedAt,updatedAt}` replaces Round 74's singular mergeSha form. rootMergeSha equals the root identity/preliminary result; deployedValidatorMergeSha equals the exact deployed/validated leaf SHA and may differ in repaired chains. Both equal the earlier Round 61 SluggerGate/ReleaseTerminalResult fields and every launch/run-authorization intent. Ordinary mode requires equality; repaired root A -> ... -> C preserves A/C respectively. No inferred singular alias is legal.

- Slugger terminal evidence is always a path/hash pair. Final TerminalFinalResidueProof adds `sluggerPath,sluggerSha256` immediately before retainedMembers, naming the terminal observed|uncertain canonical external notification at `_meta/notifications/spoonjoy-v2/<identity.repairRootTask>/slugger-notification.v1.json`; the proof is created only after that terminal hash is frozen. Final ReleaseTerminalCompletion replaces sluggerTerminalCommand with adjacent `sluggerTerminalCommandPath,sluggerTerminalCommandSha256`; that pair equals the selected slugger_external owner registry terminal pair and resolves one terminal RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope, while its existing sluggerPath/hash equals the residue pair. The canonical notification pair is the one explicit external `slugger_notification` retained member and is permitted despite living outside closureRoots. Cleanup completion contains only the earlier released notification authority and never freezes the later terminal notification hash. No path string, inline envelope, duplicate notification, or alternate external ref is legal.

- Residue root hashing is exact. `TerminalResidueClosureRoot={path,rootIdentitySha256}` is no-extra-fields; closureRoots is exactly `[ {path:'_meta/release-terminal/<I>',rootIdentitySha256:I} ]` and closureRootSetSha256 hashes the compact canonical no-LF singleton array in that field order. Exact external retained refs are the canonical Slugger notification pair plus individually named task/archive/track refs; they are part of retainedMembers but not closureRoots. No string-only or filesystem-inode interpretation is legal.

- Terminal retained kinds cover the complete control history. Final TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind additionally includes `registration_root_intent|registration_root_receipt|owner_preselection_settlement|terminal_control_attempt|terminal_successful_result|terminal_failure_snapshot|terminal_failure_evidence|terminal_failure_disposition|terminal_progress|terminal_wait|terminal_blocker|terminal_resolution|descriptor_secret_channel`. terminal_control_attempt/successful_result/failure/progress/wait/blocker/resolution classify every existing exact resource_cleanup, archive_validation_final, repair, track, Slugger, and recursive identity wrapper/support basename under its declared `_meta/release-terminal/<root I>` path, including every failed ordinal retained by history. descriptor_secret_channel classifies only exact terminal consumed|aborted DescriptorSecretChannelJournal paths; pending|sealed|authorized at finality reject. Recursive identity roots individually contribute their immutable refs through terminal result lineage. Every release-root immutable byte now maps to exactly one kind/path grammar; duplicate mapping, omitted failed history, or unknown kind rejects.

- Final residue and completion ordering remain Round 74, with these additions: registration-root mutable attempt, helper/transfer mutable attempts, and every nonterminal secret journal are absent; the canonical external Slugger notification is hash-valid; actual-owner transfer histories are complete; all selection release paths use their literal basenames; and final completion's Slugger terminal pair is path/hash. Unit29.2.6a/b/c fault registration-root mkdir/marker prefixes, directory_ready settlement, every complete probe branch, pre-transfer failures with null/older owners, direct esbuild descriptor replacement races, dual-SHA repaired depth two, external notification inclusion, closure-root hash bytes, every terminal-control/secret-channel kind, literal path rejection, and Unit37's full cleanup-to-completion order.

### Round 76 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for owner-transition exclusion, two-member archive validation, selection history, descriptor identity projection, Slugger executable and merge authority, authenticated live feature proof, and request-bound deployment identity. They replace every earlier conflicting sentence for those subjects.

- Owner host effects are registry-reserved before they begin. Final exact no-extra-fields `TerminalRootOwnerEntry={ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,registrationDirectoryReceiptPath,registrationDirectoryReceiptSha256,spawnDisposition,spawnPath,spawnSha256,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,state,hostCleanupDisposition,transitionIntentPath,transitionIntentSha256}` uses state exactly `authority_prepared|directory_preparing|directory_ready|spawn_preparing|paused|running|validation_effect_selected|validation_child_selected|validation_child_running|spawn_failed|terminal_dead_reaped|cancelled_absent|settlement_prepared|host_cleaned`; singular validation_selected is invalid. authority_prepared and directory_preparing use not_attempted with null directory/spawn/terminal pairs; directory_ready, spawn_preparing, and settlement_prepared use not_attempted with nonnull directory and null spawn/terminal; paused, running, and all three validation states use spawned with nonnull directory/spawn and null terminal; spawn_failed uses spawn_failed with nonnull directory/terminal and null spawn; terminal_dead_reaped uses spawned with all three pairs; cancelled_absent uses not_attempted with all three null; host_cleaned retains the final Round 75 disposition matrix. The transition pair is nonnull exactly in directory_preparing, spawn_preparing, or settlement_prepared and null in every other state. hostCleanupDisposition remains nonnull exactly in host_cleaned. Exact immutable `TerminalRegistrationDirectoryIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,registrationDirectory,parentPath,parentIdentity,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/process-owners/<ownerKey>/attempt-<P>-registration-directory-intent.v1.json`. Its CAS changes authority_prepared to directory_preparing and installs its pair before mkdir; only then may the no-follow mkdir/fsync occur. The existing exact directory receipt is published before a CAS changes directory_preparing to directory_ready and clears the pair. Response loss adopts only the exact receipt/poststate/CAS image.

- Spawn and preselection removal use the same exclusion protocol. Exact immutable `TerminalProcessSpawnPreparationIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,registrationBundlePath,registrationBundleSha256,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` lives at sibling `attempt-<P>-spawn-intent.v1.json`; its CAS changes directory_ready to spawn_preparing before registration-bundle creation or the OS spawn. In spawn_preparing, recovery accepts the bundle path absent, a verified bounded prefix of the exact mode-0600 bytes, or the complete fsynced bytes; it removes/fsyncs only a verified prefix and otherwise finishes/fsyncs the deterministic bundle before spawning. Exact registered-title/process observation adopts a host-ahead spawn and materializes TerminalProcessSpawn before the CAS changes spawn_preparing to paused; synchronous spawn failure materializes the exact terminal command before changing it to spawn_failed. Exact immutable `TerminalOwnerPreselectionSettlementIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,registrationDirectory,parentPath,parentIdentity,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` lives at `attempt-<P>-preselection-settlement-intent.v1.json`; its CAS changes directory_ready to settlement_prepared before rmdir/fsync. Final exact `TerminalOwnerPreselectionSettlement={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,ownerKeySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,registryBeforeSha256,registryAfterSha256,reason:'directory_ready_no_spawn',completedAt}` is published after exact absence/parent-fsync proof and before the CAS changes settlement_prepared to host_cleaned/directory_ready_no_spawn and clears the pair. Selection and every competing transition block on a preparing state. The registry CAS decides every authority-prepared cancellation versus directory reservation and every directory-ready spawn versus settlement race before any host effect; no empty-directory observation alone authorizes removal.

- Archive validation selects two already registered members. ProcessRegistrationOwner adds exact `{kind:'archive_validator',rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,processAttemptOrdinal}`. Before selection, the current terminal-effect owner is running, the matching archive-validator owner is paused, every nonselected owner is terminal/dead/reaped, host_cleaned, or authority_prepared, and no preparing/directory_ready/other paused/running owner exists. Exact no-extra-fields `ArchiveValidationSelectedOwnerRef={role,ownerKeySha256,owner,processAttemptOrdinal,authorityPath,authoritySha256,directoryReceiptPath,directoryReceiptSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256}` uses role `terminal_effect|archive_validator`; selectedOwners is exactly those two members in that role order and selectedOwnerSetSha256 hashes their compact canonical no-LF array. Final `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionIntent` replaces singular selectedOwnerKeySha256/selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal with adjacent `selectedOwners,selectedOwnerSetSha256`; its CAS changes the terminal effect running -> validation_effect_selected and validator paused -> validation_child_selected, cancels the complete authority_prepared set, installs the active intent pair, increments once, and changes no other entry. Final `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelection` repeats both selected fields and the deterministic after revision/hash. Response loss adopts only that image.

- The validator runs only after selection readback and the frozen host/artifact rescan. Final probes and ArchiveValidationEffectOwnerMember use state `validation_effect_selected|validation_child_selected|cancelled_absent|terminal_dead_reaped|host_cleaned`; the two selected roles have their complete authority/directory/spawn/process-registration pairs, null terminal pair, null cleanup disposition, and exact selected role. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationChildRunAuthorizationIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,effectProofPath,effectProofSha256,rescanPath,rescanSha256,validatorOwnerKeySha256,validatorProcessAttemptOrdinal,childRunPath,childRunSha256,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` lives at the selection attempt root as `owner-validator-run-intent.v1.json`. Its CAS changes only validation_child_selected -> validation_child_running. Exact immutable `ArchiveValidationChildRunAuthorization={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,registryPath,registryAfterSha256,registryAfterRevision,authorizedAt}` at `owner-validator-run.v1.json` is published after readback and alone permits the paused child-run token. The validator terminalizes to terminal_dead_reaped; the outer effect writes the final validation record/result, exits, and terminalizes separately.

- Selection release waits for both selected processes. Exact `TerminalSelectedOwnerTerminalRef={role,ownerKeySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256}` uses the same two-role order. Final `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionReleaseIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,childRunAuthorizationPath,childRunAuthorizationSha256,archiveValidationTerminalPath,archiveValidationTerminalSha256,selectedTerminals,selectedTerminalSetSha256,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` requires both selected entries terminal_dead_reaped and hashes the two complete terminal refs. Its CAS clears the active intent and appends one release-intent reservation below; the immutable release receipt repeats selection, child-authorization, selected-terminal, and deterministic after-state pairs. The parent coordinator, not the exited effect, owns this release. No retry, cleaner, helper, successor, or later authority begins before release receipt plus the cumulative history index below are read back. Tests interleave selection against authority reservation, mkdir, spawn, settlement, rescan, child authorization, both process exits, and release for all four terminal branches.

- Selection history has one acyclic constructible representation. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalArchiveSelectionReservation={validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,releaseIntentPath,releaseIntentSha256}` is sorted by BINARY validationIdentitySha256 then increasing attempt ordinal; duplicate identity/ordinal or path is invalid. Final exact `TerminalRootOwnerRegistry={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,executionHost,registrationRootIdentity,registrationRootReceiptPath,registrationRootReceiptSha256,revision,entries,entrySetSha256,activeSelectionIntentPath,activeSelectionIntentSha256,selectionReservations,selectionReservationSetSha256,updatedAt}` uses exact empty array/hash at revision zero. Selection CAS changes only entries/active pair; release CAS clears the active pair and appends exactly one reservation naming the already immutable release intent. Every later registry preserves the array byte-identically. After release-receipt readback, exact no-extra-fields `TerminalArchiveSelectionHistoryMember={validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,releasePath,releaseSha256}` projects that reservation plus its final receipt. Exact immutable `TerminalArchiveSelectionHistoryIndex={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,registryPath,registrySha256,members,memberSetSha256,completedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/archive-validation/selection-history/revision-<positive history length>.v1.json`; members are the complete reservation-order history, the set hash covers their compact canonical no-LF array, and completedAt byte-equals the final member's release receipt releasedAt. It is materialized only after the registry CAS and all selected release receipts resolve, so it may hash the registry without a reverse pointer. Recovery derives its unique deterministic bytes from the final registry and receipts. Final ReleaseTerminalCompletion replaces all selectionReceiptPaths/selectionReleasePaths fields with adjacent `selectionHistoryIndexPath,selectionHistoryIndexSha256` and validates its members against the final registry plus every referenced intent/selection/child-authorization/release/terminal byte.

- Descriptor file and executable identities are typed and project mechanically. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFileIdentity={path,sourceIdentity}` has an absolute NUL-free path and sourceIdentity:SpawnSourceIdentity obtained from the same opened regular no-follow file. Exact `descriptorExecutableIdentityOf(trusted)` returns `{base:{name,absolutePath,realPath,device,inode,mode,uid,size,sha256},version}` by copying the corresponding TrustedExecutable fields byte-for-byte; no other projection is legal. DescriptorEntry and DescriptorExtra carry only SpawnSourceIdentity, never a path-bearing wrapper. DescriptorExtra's final role domain is `bootstrap_source_copy|child_source|helper_image_source`. Exact `DescriptorInheritedExecutable={role:'external_effect',identity:DescriptorExecutableIdentity,fd:6}` is a separate type, and exact `DescriptorExecutableTransfer={role:'external_effect',identity:DescriptorExecutableIdentity,parentFd:6,childFd:3}` defines the sole Slugger child-to-Ouro remap. Final DescriptorLaunchSpec inserts BINARY-role-sorted unique `inheritedExecutables` between extras and secretChannels; all descriptor fds across executable, entry, extras, inheritedExecutables, and secretChannels are distinct. Only Slugger gate/child launch specs contain external_effect; they contain no secret channel. The separate Ouro effect launch spec has no inheritedExecutables and executes the transferred descriptor at executableFd 3, preserving the final executable-fd invariant.

- Final helper intents are a closed discriminated union. `TerminalHelperPrepareLaunchIntent` retains the common version/root/action:'prepare'/ordinal/prior-outcome/image/invocation/time fields and replaces sourceIdentity/esbuildIdentity/nodeRuntimeIdentity with `sourceFile:DescriptorFileIdentity,esbuildEntryFile:DescriptorFileIdentity,nodeExecutableIdentity:DescriptorExecutableIdentity`, plus one exact launchSpec/hash. `TerminalHelperRemoveLaunchIntent` retains action:'remove', prior/image/inline fields and contains only `nodeExecutableIdentity` plus launchSpec/hash. TerminalHelperLaunchIntent is exactly that union. Final TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage repeats the prepare intent's three typed identities and launch spec/hash plus DescriptorSpawnReceipt; Final TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemoval repeats the remove intent's node identity and launch spec/hash plus DescriptorSpawnReceipt. Prepare uses Node executable fd 3, esbuild alias entry fd 4, helper_image_source fd 5, and argv `['<node-fd-3>','<esbuild-entry-fd-4>','<source-fd-5>','--bundle','--platform=node','--format=cjs','--target=node22','--log-level=error','--outfile='+imageTempPath]`. Removal uses descriptor-backed Node fd 3, null entry/extras/inherited/secret arrays, and argv `['<node-fd-3>','--input-type=commonjs','--eval',decodedInlineProgram,'--',rootIdentitySha256,imagePath,imageTempPath,imageRootPath]`. Reopening a path is permitted only to obtain a descriptor whose complete frozen identity matches; every spawn executes the held descriptor alias.

- Slugger uses descriptor aliases through the external message process. Final TerminalSluggerExternalLaunchIntent adds adjacent `rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha` after rootIdentitySha256 and replaces nodeRuntimeIdentity/imageIdentity/ouroExecutableIdentity plus one ambiguous launchSpec with `nodeExecutableIdentity,gateImageFile,childImageFile,ouroExecutableIdentity,effectExecutableTransfer,gateLaunchSpec,gateLaunchSpecSha256,childLaunchSpec,childLaunchSpecSha256,effectLaunchSpec,effectLaunchSpecSha256`. effectExecutableTransfer is the exact DescriptorExecutableTransfer above. Gate uses Node fd 3, gate image entry fd 4, child image extra fd 5, and inherited Ouro fd 6; it preserves fd 5/6 into the child. Child uses Node fd 3, child image entry fd 5, inherited Ouro fd 6, and no pathname execution. Immediately before the one effect spawn, the child revalidates held fd 6, dup-maps it to effect child fd 3, and closes every unrelated inherited fd. Exact effect launch spec uses the frozen Ouro identity at executableFd 3 and argv `['<ouro-fd-3>','msg','--to','slugger','Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end']`. ProcessMemberRegistration.role adds `effect`; regular generations remain exact gate/child, while Slugger ProcessRegistrationBundle.memberRegistrations is exact `[gate,child,effect]` and predeclares the effect title/launch hash. On successful Ouro spawn the child sends the DescriptorSpawnReceipt/native process identity to the parent, which atomically extends the same Slugger member registry to gate/child/effect and publishes exact immutable `TerminalSluggerExternalEffectSpawn={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,processAttemptOrdinal,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,effectExecutableTransfer,effectLaunchSpecSha256,descriptorSpawnReceipt,spawnedAt}` at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-external-effect-spawn.v1.json`; DescriptorSpawnReceipt.owner is exact `{kind:'slugger_message_effect',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}`. Synchronous spawn failure publishes no effect member/receipt and terminalizes uncertain/spawn_error; an effect that exits before trustworthy identity projection terminalizes uncertain/observation_error and never respawns. The slugger_external owner reaches terminal_dead_reaped only after every actually spawned gate/child/effect member is dead/reaped and its terminal envelope records the nullable effect-spawn pair. Replacement after launch intent, child_recorded, run authorization, descriptor validation, or immediately before spawn either executes the held inode or blocks/terminalizes uncertain without a second message.

- Slugger merge lineage is derived, not asserted. rootMergeSha is exact root TerminalCleanup.mergeSha and deployedValidatorMergeSha is exact deployed leaf ArchiveValidationReceipt.validatorMergeSha; both also equal the root preliminary result's adjacent fields. Eligible ordinary delivery requires equality, while repaired root preserves root A/deployed leaf C. Final SluggerGate adds both adjacent fields after notificationPath. Final TerminalSluggerRunAuthorizationIntent and TerminalSluggerRunAuthorization add both immediately after rootIdentitySha256 and require equality to launch intent, gate, root result, and notification. Eligible ordinary-normal and repaired-root ReleaseTerminalResult branches add the same adjacent fields after validatorMergeSha; ineligible repair-successor/intermediate branches cannot own notification bytes. Final SluggerNotification remains the Round 75 dual-SHA schema. Its resultPath/resultSha256 are exactly the selected slugger_external RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope terminal path/hash, whose terminal payload cross-links the one TerminalSluggerExternalEffectSpawn; ReleaseTerminalCompletion.sluggerTerminalCommandPath/hash and TerminalFinalResidueProof.sluggerPath/hash resolve the same command and notification authorities respectively. No separate ambiguous Slugger result file or singular merge alias remains.

- Round 76 immutable control bytes are residue-classified. TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind additionally includes `process_directory_intent|process_spawn_intent|owner_preselection_settlement_intent|owner_validator_run_authorization|owner_selection_history|slugger_external_effect_spawn`, each mapping only to the exact Round 76 path/schema. The prior owner_preselection_settlement kind remains receipt-only. Every transition pair is null and every preparing state absent at finality; every cumulative selection index through the final history length is retained and hash-valid. Unknown or multiply classified intent, authorization, index, transfer, or external-effect bytes reject.

- Every live response is request-bound to the expected deployment. Exact `LiveBuildIdentity={sourceSha,workerVersionId}` uses the expected merge/branch SHA and provider version UUID. The application emits `x-spoonjoy-build-sha` from the same immutable authority as the declared health body and `x-spoonjoy-worker-version` from CF_VERSION_METADATA on every HTML, API, OAuth, MCP, error, redirect, 204, and WebSocket-101 response; missing, duplicate, or mismatched identity fails the request before semantic acceptance. Exact `LiveIdentityObservation={sequence,kind,status,sourceSha,workerVersionId}` uses kind `health|http|websocket_upgrade|main_document`, safe HTTP status, contiguous sequence, and observed header values rather than caller projections. Final ApiSmokeReport and LifecycleSmokeReport add `identityObservations,identityObservationSetSha256` immediately after workerVersionId; final VisualReport adds the same pair before captures. FeatureSmokeReport already contains them. Every set hash covers compact canonical complete observation bytes in sequence order. Each API, lifecycle, feature, and visual runner receives explicit expected identity and begins/ends with an exact no-store health observation. Lifecycle socket frames are accepted only from a CDP-captured 101 observation for that socket generation. Every visual capture begins with a full navigation/reload, names its exact main-document observation, and VisualCapture adds `identityObservationSequence`. A deployment change at any point fails that runner.

- Lifecycle screenshots have their own request binding. Exact no-extra-fields `LifecycleScreenshotIdentity={screenshot,identityObservationSequence}` uses each of the six literal lifecycle screenshot basenames in its existing order and selects a unique main_document LiveIdentityObservation. Immediately before each capture the runner performs a full navigation/reload, validates that response's exact headers, then performs the existing DOM privacy preflight; no observation is reused by another screenshot. Final LifecycleSmokeReport adds `screenshotIdentities` immediately before artifactPaths. Missing, non-main-document, wrong-status, reused, or expected-only identity rejects the report and screenshot set.

- Unit37 visual reviewer identity is serialized exactly. Its final prompt adds `worker_version_id: <candidateVersionId>` immediately after source_sha and changes visual_rule to require exact no-store health before and after all live inspection. This historical Round76 CONVERGED report replaced the earlier eight-line form with thirteen LF-terminated lines in order, but Round77/Round97 supersedes it: `# Spoonjoy Production Visual Review`, `version: 1`, `role: <visual_qa|design_visual>`, `agent_id: <uuid>`, `verdict: clean`, `source_sha: <mergeSha>`, `worker_version_id: <uuid>`, `health_before_source_sha: <mergeSha>`, `health_before_worker_version_id: <uuid>`, `health_after_source_sha: <mergeSha>`, `health_after_worker_version_id: <uuid>`, `visual_artifact_set_sha256: <sha256>`, and `open_findings: 0`. Exact final `VisualReviewer={role,agentId,verdict:'clean',artifactPath,reportSha256,sourceSha,workerVersionId,healthBefore,healthAfter,visualArtifactSetSha256}` uses both health members as exact LiveBuildIdentity. FINDINGS runtime JSON adds the same four observed health values and fails if either mismatches. Unit34 design_visual retains the generic format. Missing/expected-only/changed health rejects before a reviewer artifact is accepted.

- Authenticated feature smoke is a separate live oracle. Exact `FeatureSmokeCheck` is the no-extra-fields union: `{name:'anonymous_adoption',anonymousNetworkMutationCount:0,storedVersion:2,adoptionOutcome:'adopted',serverRevision:0,localClearedAfterPersistence:true}`; `{name:'shopping_restore',webReloadRestored:true,restItemCount,checkedItemCount,nativeSyncObserved:true}`; `{name:'saved_privacy',ownerSaved:true,ownerBlankSearchCount,ownerFilteredSearchCount,otherPrincipalVisibleCount:0,ownerUnsaved:true}`; `{name:'tag_discovery',course:'dinner',customTags:['quick','weeknight'],ownerFilterCount,searchCount}`; `{name:'rest_metadata_scale',stepCount:2,sourceDisplayNamePresent:true,course:'dinner',tags:['quick','weeknight'],scaleFactor:2,neutralProjectionSha256}`; or `{name:'mcp_metadata_scale',searchStepCount:2,getStepCount:2,sourceDisplayNamePresent:true,course:'dinner',tags:['quick','weeknight'],scaleFactor:2,neutralProjectionSha256}`. Counts are positive safe integers except the explicit zero; REST and MCP neutral hashes must be equal and cover the shared metadata/scaled-ingredient projection with private isSaved omitted. Existing MCP search_recipes/get_recipe are exercised through a real in-memory-only OAuth token; no new MCP shopping or import tool is added. Shopping is exercised through web restore, v1 read/write, and native sync.

- Exact `FeatureSmokeReport={version:1,targetBaseUrl,sourceSha,workerVersionId,identityObservations,identityObservationSetSha256,checks,userACleanupOutcomePath,userACleanupOutcomeSha256,userBCleanupOutcomePath,userBCleanupOutcomeSha256,artifactPaths,artifactSetSha256,passed:true,completedAt}` contains the six checks in the order above and two disposable principals. User A owns the fixture recipe/tags/save/shopping/adopted cook state; User B proves cross-principal privacy. Both use the shared primary cleanup state machine and must end terminal-204/relational zero/content-bearing DO zero before pass. Its fresh output set is exactly `feature-smoke-results.json` plus the six primary cleanup basenames under each of `user-a/` and `user-b/`; artifactPaths contains the twelve subordinate cleanup refs and excludes the report. Any recovery file, raw token/email, residual row/state, extra file, or feature mismatch rejects.

- Feature and final-health ProcessSpecs are literal. Exact feature argv is `['pnpm','run','feature:smoke','--','--base-url',targetBaseUrl,'--source-sha',sourceSha,'--worker-version-id',workerVersionId,'--release-identity-sha256',identitySha256,'--release-attempt-path',attemptPath,'--out','@release-out@']`, with logical name qa-feature-smoke or production-feature-smoke, REPO_CWD cwd, the existing closed smoke environment plus no raw credential variables, 600000ms total timeout, clean exit/zero warnings, and exact 13-file output. QA now requires a nonnull version-metadata UUID before any live runner; absence is a failed QA identity preflight, not a nullable accepted report. qa-final-health and production-final-health use the existing exact `pnpm release:provenance -- verify-health --base-url <target> --source-sha <sourceSha> --worker-version-id <workerVersionId> --out @release-out@` ProcessSpec after their preceding live work; they produce a fresh exact HealthSeries and accept only all-success identity equality. API smoke's final argv adds ordered `--source-sha <sourceSha> --worker-version-id <workerVersionId>` before `--out`. No runner reads expected identity only from environment or report fields.

- Final stage evidence projects the new terminal health. Replacement Unit33 evidence adds `finalHealthSamples:Health[]` immediately after smokeArtifactPaths; replacement Unit37 evidence adds it immediately after visualReviewers. In both, healthSamples is the initial deployed-target series and finalHealthSamples byte-equals the separately registered qa-final-health or production-final-health HealthSeries.samples. smokeArtifactPaths is exactly the 27-file aggregate and resolves the one FeatureSmokeReport whose two cleanup pairs and twelve subordinate paths validate. No caller boolean, expected-only identity, or unregistered feature/final-health output can satisfy either passed-stage schema.

- Release command order and closed sets include the new proof. Unit33's final order replaces its tail with `qa-health,qa-api-smoke,qa-lifecycle-smoke,qa-feature-smoke,qa-final-health,qa-cleanup-assert-after`. Unit37's final order replaces its live tail with `production-health,production-readiness,production-cleanup-reconcile,production-cleanup-assert-before,production-api-smoke,production-lifecycle-smoke,production-feature-smoke,production-visual-dogfood,production-visual-qa-review,production-visual-design-review,production-final-health,production-cleanup-assert-after,continuation-scan`. API smoke now also receives source/version operands. Every live runner report's before/after health pair and every request observation match the same identity; qa-final-health or production-final-health is a separately registered exact verify-health result after all smoke/reviewer work. The primary smoke aggregate is the BINARY-sorted union of one API file, thirteen lifecycle files, and thirteen feature files: exactly 27 files. feature-smoke-live is the fifteenth pre-visual task script and production-visual-dogfood is the sixteenth/final task script; the coverage/typecheck registry validates literal ordered membership, not only counts. Unit30 owns red/green feature runner behavior and the pre-cleanup nine-file API/lifecycle/feature functional set; Unit31 owns both feature-principal cleanups and the final 27-file primary aggregate; Units33 and 37 execute it in QA and production. Unit29.2 owns schemas, process registry/order, identity projection, and exact aggregate validation. Fault tests change deployment identity before, during, and after every runner, socket, capture, reviewer, and final health boundary.

### Round 77 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for descriptor-relative filesystem containment, helper-image construction, archive-selection history, complete terminal lineage/result schemas, the Slugger native effect, live ProcessSpecs and identity bindings, the authenticated feature oracle, Unit30/31 ownership, and terminal health ordering. They replace every earlier conflicting sentence for those subjects. A Round 77 type or field list declared exact or no-extra-fields is a complete replacement, not a patch against an earlier schema.

- Every release-terminal filesystem effect is descriptor-relative. Exact no-extra-fields `DirectoryComponentIdentity={name,device,inode,mode,uid}` uses one nonempty NUL-free path component with no slash, dot, or dot-dot and the no-follow `fstat` identity of the held directory descriptor. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorRelativePathAuthority={absolutePath,components,authoritySha256}` starts at the real filesystem root descriptor, contains every absolute-path component in order, and hashes compact canonical no-LF `{absolutePath,components}` bytes. Production uses one descriptor-executed trusted Python 3 identity and the measured fixed inline program `release-terminal-fs-v1`: with `-I -S`, a closed environment, and no imports beyond the Python standard library, it opens `/`, walks each component only with `os.open(name,O_RDONLY|O_DIRECTORY|O_NOFOLLOW,dir_fd=parentFd)`, verifies every stored identity, and performs only `os.mkdir`, `os.rename`, `os.unlink`, `os.rmdir`, `os.open`, and `os.fsync` with explicit `dir_fd`/`src_dir_fd`/`dst_dir_fd` arguments while the complete ancestor chain remains open. Its exact DescriptorLaunchSpec uses trusted Python at executable fd 3, null entry/extras/inherited/secret/stdin members, cwd `/`, the closed release-terminal environment, and argv `['<python-fd-3>','-I','-S','-c',decodedInlineProgram,'--',compactCanonicalMutationJson]`; decodedInlineProgram has a frozen source SHA in the registry and compactCanonicalMutationJson is the complete mutation intent projection. Darwin uses that component walk; Linux may additionally require `openat2` `RESOLVE_BENEATH|RESOLVE_NO_SYMLINKS`, but never weakens it. Exact immutable `DescriptorFilesystemMutationIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,operation,sourceAuthority,targetAuthority,expectedBefore,expectedAfter,pythonExecutableIdentity,inlineProgramSha256,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` is pushed/read back before execution; operation is `mkdir|rename|unlink|rmdir|write_publish|fsync`, only the path authorities needed by that operation are nonnull, and BlobState before/after bytes are complete. Exact immutable `DescriptorFilesystemMutationReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,sourceAuthority,targetAuthority,observedBefore,observedAfter,descriptorSpawnReceipt,completedAt}` is written only after poststate and every required directory fsync validate through the still-held descriptors. Response loss reopens from `/` component-by-component, validates identities, and adopts only exact before or after state; an intermediate or changed identity blocks without mutation. No release-terminal mkdir, temp publication, rename, unlink, rmdir, owner-directory preparation/settlement, registration-root mutation, image preparation/removal, or residue cleanup may call a pathname effect directly or resolve its absolute path again after helper start. `TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind` additionally includes `descriptor_filesystem_intent|descriptor_filesystem_receipt`; finality requires every intent paired with one receipt and no mutable helper journal.

- Shared helper parents are concurrency-neutral. A release freezes only the descriptor chain through the shared parent and its own `<rootIdentitySha256>` child authority; it never records or compares the shared parent's complete sibling-name or sibling-identity set. Mutation intents prove their final target component equals the current release's exact identity root and descriptor-relative operations cannot escape that root. Concurrent release roots may appear or disappear without invalidating another release. Final residue verifies the shared parent identity and this release's own root absence/presence contract only. Every earlier whole-sibling-set equality, frozen sibling inventory, or cleanup requirement that an unrelated release root remain present is invalid.

- Helper-image preparation executes the measured native esbuild binary directly. Final `TerminalHelperPrepareLaunchIntent` replaces `esbuildEntryFile,nodeExecutableIdentity` with `esbuildExecutableIdentity:DescriptorExecutableIdentity` and retains `sourceFile:DescriptorFileIdentity`; its DescriptorLaunchSpec adds exact `stdinFile:sourceFile`, uses the esbuild executable at fd 3, has no entry/extras/inherited/secret descriptors, and uses argv `['<esbuild-fd-3>','--bundle','--platform=node','--format=cjs','--target=node22','--log-level=error','--loader=ts','--sourcefile=release-terminal.ts','--outfile='+imageTempPath]`. The executor streams the complete held source descriptor bytes to stdin, closes stdin only after the exact byte count/hash, and accepts only zero exit, empty sanitized stderr, and the expected output produced under the descriptor-relative write-publish intent. Final `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage` repeats sourceFile, esbuildExecutableIdentity, launchSpec/hash, source-byte count/hash, and DescriptorSpawnReceipt. Node, an extensionless fd alias, a package-relative esbuild shim, service discovery, PATH lookup, or an unmeasured dependency is invalid. Helper removal uses the Round 77 descriptor-relative filesystem helper and no Node inline pathname program.

- Archive-selection history is append-only by release ordinal. Final exact `TerminalArchiveSelectionReservation={selectionOrdinal,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,releaseIntentPath,releaseIntentSha256}` uses selectionOrdinal equal to the prior reservation-array length plus one. The registry stores reservations in strictly increasing contiguous selectionOrdinal order; validation identity is data, never an ordering key. Final exact no-extra-fields `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionReleaseIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,selectionOrdinal,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,childRunAuthorizationPath,childRunAuthorizationSha256,archiveValidationTerminalPath,archiveValidationTerminalSha256,selectedTerminals,selectedTerminalSetSha256,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,createdAt}` requires selectionOrdinal=`selectionReservations.length+1`. Final exact no-extra-fields `ArchiveValidationOwnerSelectionRelease={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,selectionOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,childRunAuthorizationPath,childRunAuthorizationSha256,archiveValidationTerminalPath,archiveValidationTerminalSha256,selectedTerminals,selectedTerminalSetSha256,registryPath,registryBeforeSha256,registryBeforeRevision,registryAfterSha256,registryAfterRevision,releasedAt}` is the sole release receipt; its selectedTerminals bytes and set hash equal the intent, registryAfterRevision is before+1, and registryAfterSha256 is the deterministic CAS after-image containing that exact reservation. Final exact `TerminalArchiveSelectionHistoryMember={selectionOrdinal,validationIdentitySha256,archiveValidationAttemptOrdinal,selectionPath,selectionSha256,releaseIntentPath,releaseIntentSha256,releasePath,releaseSha256}` and `TerminalArchiveSelectionHistoryIndex={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,registryPath,registrySha256,members,memberSetSha256,completedAt}` use the same contiguous ordinal order; completedAt equals the highest ordinal receipt's releasedAt. No completedAt sort, BINARY identity sort, sorted insertion, reverse index pointer, shorthand repeated fields, or extra field is legal.

- Root terminal merge lineage and external-effect evidence have complete branch schemas.
