Final `ReleaseTerminalRootPreliminaryResult` is exactly the union `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'ordinary',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath:null,externalEffectSpawnPath:null,externalEffectSpawnSha256:null}` or `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_root',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,consumedRepairIndexPath,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath,repairLinkPath,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath:null,externalEffectSpawnPath:null,externalEffectSpawnSha256:null}`. Both SHAs are nonnull lowercase Git SHAs derived by the existing root/leaf authorities; ordinary requires equality and repaired-root permits inequality. The preliminary wrapper/result hash contains these bytes before any Slugger authority is created.

- Final `ReleaseTerminalResult` is exactly a four-branch no-extra-fields union. Ordinary is `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'ordinary',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath,externalEffectSpawnPath,externalEffectSpawnSha256}`. Repair-successor is `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'repair_successor',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha:null,deployedValidatorMergeSha:null,sluggerPath:null,externalEffectSpawnPath:null,externalEffectSpawnSha256:null}`. Repair-intermediate is `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_intermediate',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath,postMergeRepairRelayPath,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,consumedRepairIndexPath,createdRepairIndexPath,predecessorSupersedePath,repairLinkPath,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha:null,deployedValidatorMergeSha:null,sluggerPath:null,externalEffectSpawnPath:null,externalEffectSpawnSha256:null}`. Repair-root is `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_root',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,consumedRepairIndexPath,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath,repairLinkPath,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath,externalEffectSpawnPath,externalEffectSpawnSha256}`. Existing conditional DeskArchive/postMergeRepair/trackArchive nullability still applies inside the two repaired path-bearing branches. Eligible branches require nonnull lineage and Slugger path; the external-effect pair is both null for a pre-spawn uncertain terminal and both nonnull/hash-valid iff the native effect reached the persisted spawned checkpoint. Ineligible branches have every notification/effect member null. The same exact result union is used by preliminary-to-final derivation, terminal effect artifacts, final result files, doing output, residue validation, and Slugger authorization; there is no absent field anchor or alternate display schema.

- Ouro is not a ProcessMemberRegistration and never extends the gate/child registry. Slugger's ProcessRegistrationBundle is exact `[gate,child]`; `ProcessMemberRegistration.role` remains `gate|child`, and every earlier `effect` role or three-member equality is invalid. Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerNativeEffectIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,processAttemptOrdinal,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,effectExecutableTransfer,effectLaunchSpec,effectLaunchSpecSha256,operationKeySha256,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-native-effect-intent.v1.json` and is pushed/read back before the child may fork. Exact local no-extra-fields `TerminalSluggerNativeEffectStartup={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,spawnToken,descriptorSpawnReceipt,processIdentity,processGroupIdentitySha256,phase:'paused',createdAt}` is atomically fsynced as `native-effect-startup.v1.json` inside the already registered Slugger attempt directory while Ouro is paused in the same process group. The parent publishes exact immutable `TerminalSluggerExternalEffectSpawn={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,effectExecutableTransfer,effectLaunchSpecSha256,startupSha256,descriptorSpawnReceipt,processIdentity,processGroupIdentitySha256,spawnedAt}` at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-external-effect-spawn.v1.json`. Exact immutable `TerminalSluggerNativeEffectRunRelease={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,externalEffectSpawnPath,externalEffectSpawnSha256,operationKeySha256,releasedAt}` lives beside it as `attempt-<S>-native-effect-run.v1.json`; after both Desk bytes are remotely read back, the parent sends those exact release bytes/hash over the existing authenticated gate-child control channel and the child releases Ouro to execute the held fd-3 descriptor. Response loss resends only the same release bytes and never forks again. `operationKeySha256` is unique for the root notification and forbids a second native attempt after intent publication. Lost fork response adopts the exact paused startup record and publishes the same spawn receipt; synchronous fork failure has no startup/spawn/run file, leaves the final spawn pair null, and terminalizes uncertain/spawn_error; corrupt or unobservable startup terminalizes uncertain/observation_error; post-release uncertainty terminalizes uncertain and never retries. The helper child waits/reaps Ouro when possible; parent recovery proves the frozen process group absent before terminal_dead_reaped. Final exact slugger RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope adds adjacent nullable `externalEffectSpawnPath,externalEffectSpawnSha256` and `externalEffectRunPath,externalEffectRunSha256` before its terminal payload; the run pair is nonnull iff the spawn pair is nonnull, and the result cross-links the spawn pair. Local startup is removed descriptor-relatively only after terminal/dead/reaped proof and is absent at final residue. Gate/child member finality and the separately journaled native effect finality are both required, but their identity unions never overlap.

- Round 77 live ProcessSpecs are complete literal registry rows. Define output policy `feature-smoke-provisional` as the release-ineligible nine-file Unit30 fixture below and output policy `feature-smoke` as the exact thirteen-file final set. QA lifecycle is `qa-lifecycle-smoke[cloudflare-d1,lifecycle-smoke]=['node','scripts/smoke-live.mjs','--target-env','qa','--base-url',QB,'--source-sha',H,'--worker-version-id',V,'--release-identity-sha256',X,'--release-attempt-path',Y,'--out',O]`; production lifecycle is the same with logical name `production-lifecycle-smoke`, target environment `production`, base PB, and source S. QA feature is `qa-feature-smoke[cloudflare-d1,feature-smoke]=['pnpm','run','feature:smoke','--','--target-env','qa','--base-url',QB,'--source-sha',H,'--worker-version-id',V,'--release-identity-sha256',X,'--release-attempt-path',Y,'--out',O]`; production feature replaces qa/QB/H with production/PB/S. Both feature rows use REPO_CWD, the exact existing closed smoke environment, operationPhaseCapMs 600000, totalTimeoutMs 1380000, and clean exit/zero warnings; 600000 is never a total timeout. QA final health is `qa-final-health[base,json:health.json:HealthSeries]=['pnpm','release:provenance','--','verify-health','--base-url',QB,'--source-sha',H,'--worker-version-id',V,'--out',O]`; production final health substitutes production/PB/S. Both use operationPhaseCapMs 300000 and totalTimeoutMs 1080000. Every operand index/domain is declared by the existing sentinel rules, and every row rejects omitted, reordered, extra, environment-only, or nullable expected identity.

- Live identity evidence has an operation-to-observation bijection. Final exact `LiveIdentityObservation={sequence,operationId,kind,status,sourceSha,workerVersionId}` requires contiguous positive sequence, operationId matching `^[a-z0-9]+(?:[._-][a-z0-9]+)*$`, kind `health|http|websocket_upgrade|main_document`, and observed response headers/status. Exact `LiveOperationReference={operationId,identityObservationSequences}` uses a unique operationId and a nonempty strictly increasing sequence list. ApiSmokeReport, LifecycleSmokeReport, FeatureSmokeReport, and VisualReport each add `boundaryHealthObservationSequences:[number,number],operationReferences:LiveOperationReference[]`; the first/last sequences select distinct health observations, every accepted HTTP exchange, 101 upgrade, socket generation, main-document navigation, capture, and semantic check has exactly one operation reference, and the report observation array is the exact numeric-order union of all referenced sequences with no omission, reuse, or unreferenced observation. A WebSocket frame inherits its generation's unique upgrade operation reference. Every LifecycleScreenshotIdentity and VisualCapture sequence resolves an observation whose operationId is its exact screenshot/capture basename. Unit37 `VisualReviewer` adds `identityObservations:[LiveIdentityObservation,LiveIdentityObservation],healthBeforeObservationSequence:1,healthAfterObservationSequence:2`; the two observations use operationIds `<role>.health-before|<role>.health-after` and are the actual no-store reviewer requests rather than copied expected values. Its exact replacement canonical report is seventeen LF-terminated lines: title, version, role, agent_id, verdict, source_sha, worker_version_id, `health_before_operation_id: <role>.health-before`, `health_before_observation_sequence: 1`, health_before_source_sha, health_before_worker_version_id, `health_after_operation_id: <role>.health-after`, `health_after_observation_sequence: 2`, health_after_source_sha, health_after_worker_version_id, visual_artifact_set_sha256, and open_findings. Values and field spellings otherwise retain Round 76. Repeated health values without the two observed records reject. Faults replace identity between every adjacent referenced operation, including after the first health and before the second.

- Authenticated feature evidence is typed and recomputable. Exact `NeutralMetadataProjection={stepCount:2,sourceDisplayName:'example.com',course:'dinner',tags:['quick','weeknight']}` and `NeutralScaleProjection={scaleFactor:2,ingredients:[{stepNum:1,ingredientOrdinal:1,quantity:2,unit:'cup'},{stepNum:2,ingredientOrdinal:1,quantity:4,unit:'tablespoon'}]}` are fixture-exact no-extra-fields values. Exact `NeutralRecipeProjection={metadata,scale}` hashes its own compact canonical bytes; private `isSaved` is absent. Exact `ShoppingTransportProjection={transport,operation,itemCount,checkedItemCount,restoredStableId}` uses transports/operations exactly `[web,restore],[rest_v1,restore],[mcp_add_shopping_list_item,restore],[native_sync,read]` in that order, itemCount 2, checkedItemCount 1, and the same sanitized stable item id; each restore is preceded by the typed delete projection for that transport and proves deleted-to-active restoration, while native sync observes the final active row. Exact `ObservedFeatureSmokeCheck={operationId,identityObservationSequences,check}` wraps one check and references the report bijection.

- Final FeatureSmokeCheck is the exact seven-member union: the existing anonymous_adoption check with fixed values; shopping_restore with `webReloadRestored:true,restItemCount:2,checkedItemCount:1,transportProjections` equal to the four exact projections above; saved_privacy with `ownerSaved:true,ownerBlankSearchCount:1,ownerFilteredSearchCount:1,otherPrincipalVisibleCount:0,ownerUnsaved:true`; tag_discovery with dinner/quick/weeknight and `ownerFilterCount:1,searchCount:1`; rest_metadata_scale with `summaryMetadata,detailMetadata,detailScale,neutralProjection,neutralProjectionSha256`; mcp_metadata_scale with `searchMetadata,getMetadata,getScale,neutralProjection,neutralProjectionSha256`; and `{name:'cook_history_privacy',ownerMyKitchenAttemptCount:1,otherPrincipalMyKitchenAttemptCount:0,anonymousMyKitchenAttemptCount:0,ownerSnapshotVisible:true,otherPrincipalSnapshotStatus:404,anonymousSnapshotStatus:401}`. REST summary/detail and MCP search/get projections must byte-equal the exact metadata; REST detail and MCP get scale must byte-equal the exact scale; each stored hash must recompute from the complete stored neutralProjection, and the REST/MCP hashes must equal. The MCP shopping projection invokes the existing `add_shopping_list_item` mutation with a memory-only OAuth token; no new MCP tool is added. User B and anonymous contexts attempt both My Kitchen/history and canonical snapshot reads, while User A's card/snapshot remain visible. Counts, subsets, check order, route/tool names, and projection preimages are exact; positive-only placeholders or hash-only semantics reject.

- Unit30 and Unit31 have a hard provisional/final boundary. Unit30 uses an injected `FeatureCleanupPort` and performs no live D1/user cleanup. Its exact release-ineligible `FeatureSemanticFixture={version:1,checks,identityObservations,identityObservationSetSha256,operationReferences,artifactPaths,artifactSetSha256,passed:true}` writes `feature-semantic-results.json` plus eight typed sanitized operation-projection JSON files, exactly nine files; it contains no cleanup fields and cannot parse as FeatureSmokeReport or satisfy any Unit33/37 output policy. Unit30 tests all seven semantic checks, request bindings, and both success/failure cleanup-port calls in memory. Unit31 first supplies the production cleanup port, owns both users' six-file primary cleanup subtrees, and alone creates final exact `FeatureSmokeReport={version:1,targetBaseUrl,sourceSha,workerVersionId,identityObservations,identityObservationSetSha256,boundaryHealthObservationSequences,operationReferences,checks,userACleanupOutcomePath,userACleanupOutcomeSha256,userBCleanupOutcomePath,userBCleanupOutcomeSha256,artifactPaths,artifactSetSha256,passed:true,completedAt}` plus the twelve subordinate files. The final thirteen-file set and 27-file API/lifecycle/feature aggregate are Unit31 acceptance only. Unit30 acceptance never names two-user cleanup, FeatureSmokeReport, thirteen files, or the final aggregate.

- Terminal target health is literally last. Unit33's final order is `qa-health,qa-api-smoke,qa-lifecycle-smoke,qa-feature-smoke,qa-cleanup-assert-after,qa-final-health`; Unit37's final live/order tail is `production-health,production-readiness,production-cleanup-reconcile,production-cleanup-assert-before,production-api-smoke,production-lifecycle-smoke,production-feature-smoke,production-visual-dogfood,production-visual-qa-review,production-visual-design-review,production-cleanup-assert-after,continuation-scan,production-final-health`. No QA/production HTTP, WebSocket, D1, R2, cleanup, visual, readiness, continuation, or provider query follows the corresponding final-health ProcessCommand before finish-stage. Final stage evidence uses only that last HealthSeries as finalHealthSamples. Fault tests flip deployment after every prior target operation and immediately before final health; once final health passes, only local schema/hash/Desk stage-finalization work remains.

### Round 78 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final authority for feature-fixture values, shopping restore evidence, the Unit30/31 handoff, descriptor-relative filesystem effects, Slugger execution, terminal result nullability, cleanup completion, and residue classification. They replace every earlier conflicting sentence or schema for those subjects.

- The controlled course fixture is valid. Final exact no-extra-fields `NeutralMetadataProjection={stepCount:2,sourceDisplayName:'example.com',course:'main',tags:['quick','weeknight']}` replaces every `course:'dinner'` projection. The `tag_discovery` check is exactly `{name:'tag_discovery',course:'main',tags:['quick','weeknight'],ownerFilterCount:1,searchCount:1}`. REST summary/detail, MCP search/get, the neutral projection, saved/tag discovery, fixtures, hashes, reports, and tests all use `main`; `dinner` in any Round 77 feature artifact is invalid.

- Shopping restore evidence is an ordered operation transcript, not an asserted aggregate. Exact no-extra-fields `ShoppingItemStateProjection={stableId,active,checked,itemCount,checkedItemCount}` uses one sanitized stableId throughout. Exact no-extra-fields `ShoppingMutationProjection={sequence,operationId,transport,operation,status,before,after}` has sequence 1 through 7 and the following literal matrix: `(1,'shopping.web.delete','web','delete',200,active,inactive)`, `(2,'shopping.web.restore','web','restore',200,inactive,active)`, `(3,'shopping.rest-v1.delete','rest_v1','DELETE /api/v1/shopping-list/items/{itemId}',200,active,inactive)`, `(4,'shopping.rest-v1.restore','rest_v1','POST /api/v1/shopping-list/items',200,inactive,active)`, `(5,'shopping.mcp.delete','mcp','remove_shopping_list_item',200,active,inactive)`, `(6,'shopping.mcp.restore','mcp','add_shopping_list_item',200,inactive,active)`, and `(7,'shopping.native-sync.read','native_sync','GET /api/v1/shopping-list/sync',200,active,active)`. Every active state is exactly `{stableId,active:true,checked:false,itemCount:2,checkedItemCount:1}` and every inactive state is exactly `{stableId,active:false,checked:false,itemCount:1,checkedItemCount:1}`; the one checked count belongs to the untouched companion item, and the native read's before and after both equal the active state. Each operationId has exactly one `LiveOperationReference`, the referenced observations prove that exchange's source SHA and Worker version, and the MCP calls use one memory-only OAuth token with `shopping_list:write`. Final `ShoppingTransportProjection={stableId,mutations}` contains exactly the seven rows above in sequence order. The `shopping_restore` check is exactly `{name:'shopping_restore',webReloadRestored:true,restItemCount:2,checkedItemCount:1,transportProjection}` and recomputes every count from the transcript. Aggregate-only restore evidence, an undefined delete projection, omission of `remove_shopping_list_item`, a changed ID, or a read not bound to its own live observation rejects.

- The seven semantic projection files are literal and typed. `anonymous-adoption.json`, `shopping-restore.json`, `saved-privacy.json`, `tag-discovery.json`, `rest-metadata-scale.json`, `mcp-metadata-scale.json`, and `cook-history-privacy.json` each contain exact no-extra-fields `FeatureCheckProjectionFile={version:1,operationId,identityObservationSequences,check}` where check is the correspondingly named member of final `FeatureSmokeCheck`; filenames and check names are one-to-one in the order shown. `operation-identity-index.json` contains exact no-extra-fields `FeatureOperationIdentityIndex={version:1,boundaryHealthObservationSequences:[number,number],identityObservations:LiveIdentityObservation[],identityObservationSetSha256,operationReferences:LiveOperationReference[]}`. Its observations are contiguous by sequence, its references are BINARY-unique by operationId, every reference is nonempty/strictly increasing, and the existing operation-to-observation bijection covers every exchange represented by all seven checks. These are the only eight subordinate provisional basenames; aliases, extras, missing files, mismatched check names, or a second semantic representation reject.

- Unit30's exact release-ineligible fixture is constructible. Exact `FeatureArtifactRef={path,sha256}` uses a basename-only path from the eight-file list and a lowercase SHA-256. `artifactPaths` is exactly eight `FeatureArtifactRef` rows BINARY-sorted by path; `artifactSetSha256` hashes compact canonical no-LF bytes of that complete ordered array. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureSemanticFixture={version:1,checks:ObservedFeatureSmokeCheck[],identityObservations:LiveIdentityObservation[],identityObservationSetSha256,operationReferences:LiveOperationReference[],artifactPaths:FeatureArtifactRef[],artifactSetSha256,passed:true}` lives only in `feature-semantic-results.json`; the report itself is deliberately excluded from artifactPaths and its own hash preimage. `checks` has exactly seven rows in `anonymous_adoption,shopping_restore,saved_privacy,tag_discovery,rest_metadata_scale,mcp_metadata_scale,cook_history_privacy` order, and every row byte-equals the check in its named projection file while retaining its operation reference. The fixture directory contains exactly that report plus the eight named subordinate files. Unit30's output policy rejects cleanup fields, final-report fields, directories, symlinks, aliases, and any ninth subordinate file.

- `FeatureCleanupPort` is exact and ordered. It exposes only `cleanup(request:FeatureCleanupRequest):Promise<FeatureCleanupResult>`. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureCleanupRequest={attemptId,targetEnvironment,principal,chefId,recipeIds,cookAttemptIds,shoppingItemIds,expectedSourceSha,expectedWorkerVersionId}` uses principal `user_a|user_b`, a unique attemptId, BINARY-sorted unique sanitized identifier arrays, and the live identity. User A has exactly one recipeId, one cookAttemptId, and two shoppingItemIds; User B has all three arrays empty because it is the hostile privacy principal, not a fabricated product-data owner. Success is exact `FeatureCleanupSuccess={ok:true,principal,deletedCookAttemptCount,deletedSavedRecipeCount,deletedRecipeTagCount,deletedShoppingItemCount,deletedRecipeCount,deletedChefCount,remainingDisposableRowCount:0,completedAt}`; failure is exact `FeatureCleanupFailure={ok:false,principal,stage,code,retryable,observedDisposableRowCount,message}` with stage `cook_attempts|saved_recipe|recipe_tags|shopping_items|recipe|chef|absence_assertion` and sanitized message. `FeatureCleanupResult` is that union. Unit30 injects a fake and tests success plus every failure stage without D1 effects. Unit31 validates the nine-file provisional fixture, calls `cleanup(user_a)` exactly once, then `cleanup(user_b)` exactly once even when A fails, and publishes nothing unless both succeed with zero remaining rows. A rejected/timeout call is normalized to a failure at its active stage; retries belong to the outer process-attempt policy and never occur inside the port.

- Unit31 owns a crash-safe provisional-to-final transition. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureFinalizationAttempt={version:1,attemptId,provisionalRoot,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,stagingRoot,deleteRoot,phase,userAResult,userBResult,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,updatedAt}` lives at the output parent's `.feature-finalization-<attemptId>.attempt.v1.json`; phase is `prepared|cleanup_a_complete|cleanup_b_complete|final_staged|final_published|provisional_detached|provisional_removed|complete`. `userAResult` is null only in prepared, `userBResult` is null through cleanup_a_complete, final hashes are null through cleanup_b_complete, and all later fields are nonnull; deleteRoot is always the unique same-filesystem operation-keyed path. Before prepared, Unit31 no-follow validates the exact nine-file provisional root and requires finalRoot/stagingRoot/deleteRoot absent. It creates a same-filesystem stagingRoot containing the exact final thirteen-file set, fsyncs every file and directory, atomically renames stagingRoot to absent finalRoot, fsyncs the parent, then atomically renames provisionalRoot to deleteRoot, fsyncs the parent, removes only its validated nine-file manifest, fsyncs again, and marks complete. Recovery adopts only the exact next-state hash at each phase; source-and-target coexistence, missing source before publication, foreign member, changed hash, final drift, or ambiguous rename blocks without a second cleanup call. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureFinalizationReceipt={version:1,attemptId,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,userAResult,userBResult,provisionalRootAbsent:true,stagingRootAbsent:true,deleteRootAbsent:true,completedAt}` atomically replaces the attempt at `.feature-finalization-<attemptId>.receipt.v1.json` only after complete and is retained/hash-listed as Unit31 stage evidence outside finalRoot. Release eligibility requires that receipt plus exact finalRoot; no mutable attempt exists. The final thirteen-file `FeatureSmokeReport` remains Unit31-only: its twelve subordinate files are exactly `user-a/cleanup-outcome.json`, `user-a/cleanup-phase-primary.json`, `user-a/cleanup-pre-delete-rows.json`, `user-a/cleanup-user-delete.json`, `user-a/cleanup-post-delete-rows.json`, `user-a/cleanup-retained-do-allowlist.json` and the same six under `user-b/`; artifactPaths excludes the report and BINARY-hashes those twelve, while checks is the same seven observed checks. No Unit30 test artifact is accepted as live evidence.

- Descriptor-relative filesystem authority ends at an existing held parent. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorRelativeParentAuthority={rootIdentitySha256,absoluteParentPath,components,componentIdentities,authoritySha256}` has BINARY path components with neither empty, dot, dot-dot, slash, nor NUL; componentIdentities has equal length and each row proves the no-follow directory identity opened relative to the preceding held descriptor. `absoluteParentPath` is display-only and never reopened. Exact `DescriptorFilesystemLeaf={parentAuthoritySha256,name}` names one direct leaf; `name` obeys the same single-component grammar and may be absent. Exact `DescriptorFilesystemLeafState` is `{kind:'absent'}|{kind:'regular',mode,uid,size,sha256}|{kind:'directory',mode,uid,empty,entrySetSha256}|{kind:'symlink',mode,uid,targetByteLength,targetSha256}`. Symlink is observation-only and illegal in every expected-after state; encountering it where absent/file/directory was expected is conflict, never traversal.

- The mutation request is acyclic, bounded, and complete. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFilesystemMutationRequest={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,operation,sourceLeaf,targetLeaf,tempLeaf,expectedBefore,expectedAfter,payloadByteLength,payloadSha256,operationKeySha256}` uses operation `mkdir|write_publish|rename|unlink_file|rmdir_empty|fsync_directory|barrier`. `expectedBefore` and `expectedAfter` are exact arrays of `DescriptorFilesystemExpectedState={role:'source'|'target'|'temp',leaf,state}` in source/target/temp order with one row for every nonnull leaf. mkdir/unlink/rmdir use target only; rename uses source+target; write_publish uses target+temp, requires temp absent before/after and target absent-before/regular-after; fsync_directory uses target directory with equal before/after; barrier has every leaf null, both arrays empty, and no payload. Only write_publish has positive payload bytes/hash and it writes an operation-keyed exclusive temp, fsyncs it, renames to target, and fsyncs the held parent; every other operation has zero/null payload. `operationKeySha256` hashes compact canonical bytes of every preceding request field and therefore never includes itself, launch metadata, intent metadata, or a receipt. Requests are at most 64 KiB; payload is at most 16 MiB and never enters argv or an environment variable.

- Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFilesystemMutationPlan={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,lockBootstrapIntentPath,lockBootstrapIntentSha256,lockBootstrapReceiptPath,lockBootstrapReceiptSha256,parentAuthorities,parentAuthoritySetSha256,operationIntents,operationIntentSetSha256,finalBarrierOrdinal,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/mutation-plan.v1.json` before operation 1. `parentAuthorities` is the BINARY-by-authoritySha256 unique complete set, and every leaf authority hash in every request resolves exactly one member. `operationIntents` is exact contiguous `TerminalOutcomeRef[]` in operationOrdinal order, its set hash covers compact canonical complete bytes, and finalBarrierOrdinal equals its length and selects the sole barrier request. No authority/request/operation may be discovered or appended after plan readback; a necessary unplanned mutation blocks and enters the existing terminal repair path.

- Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFilesystemMutationIntent={version:1,request,requestSha256,pythonExecutableIdentity,inlineProgram,inlineProgramSha256,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,lockIdentity,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/operation-<F>-intent.v1.json`; F is the contiguous positive operationOrdinal. The measured `/usr/bin/python3`-family executable is transferred at fd 3, compact canonical request bytes at authenticated read-only fd 4, and write payload at read-only fd 5 or the declared null descriptor. Exact argv is `['<python-fd-3>','-I','-S','-c',decodedInlineProgram,'--',rootIdentitySha256,String(operationOrdinal),operationKeySha256]`; the closed environment, cwd, descriptor map, request byte count/hash, and payload byte count/hash are all in DescriptorLaunchSpec. `inlineProgram` is UTF-8, at most 48 KiB, contains only standard-library imports, and its hash is measured before launch. No request, payload, path, package lookup, PATH value, HOME value, `--outfile`, or undeclared descriptor is legal in argv/env.

- The descriptor helper has one explicit process exception and one global serialization authority. It is the only process outside TerminalRootOwnerRegistry; the exception is closed to a measured Python executable whose exact spawn receipt, process identity, request hash, lock identity, stdout/stderr bounds, exit, and dead/reaped proof live in immutable intent/receipt evidence. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFilesystemLockBootstrapIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,hostId,parentAuthority,name,pythonExecutableIdentity,inlineProgram,inlineProgramSha256,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` is pushed/read back at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/lock-bootstrap-intent.v1.json` before the sole bootstrap helper spawn. That measured helper opens the held host-control parent, performs no-follow `openat(O_CREAT|O_EXCL)` for the one lock leaf, or on EEXIST opens and validates the existing regular leaf, then fsyncs the leaf and parent. Exact `DescriptorFilesystemLockIdentity={hostId,parentAuthoritySha256,name,device,inode,mode,uid}` is immutable for the root release. Exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFilesystemLockBootstrapReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,lockIdentity,disposition:'created'|'adopted_existing',descriptorSpawnReceipt,processIdentity,exitCode:0,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` lives beside the intent and is read back before mutation operation 1 may spawn. Concurrent creators converge on the same exact identity; replacement, symlink, ownership/mode drift, or a different inode blocks. Every mutation helper opens that leaf through the held parent, takes an exclusive advisory lock before state inspection, retains it through effect-result emission, and releases it only on exit; the controller publishes the later dead/reaped receipt. The lock is shared host-control infrastructure, not task residue, and final validation proves the same identity rather than deleting it.

- Mutation recovery is deterministic. Under the lock the helper classifies the complete observed leaf tuple as `before|owned_partial|after|conflict`. `owned_partial` is legal only for write_publish when target is still expected-before and the exact temp leaf is a regular file owned by operationKeySha256; the helper unlinks/fsyncs that temp and restarts the write. `before` executes once, `after` adopts without mutation, and conflict performs no effect. While holding the lock, the helper emits a bounded canonical `DescriptorFilesystemEffectResult={requestSha256,operationOrdinal,operationKeySha256,lockIdentity,effectDisposition:'executed'|'adopted_after_state'|'recovered_owned_partial',observedBefore,observedAfter}` to its held stdout and then exits; it never claims its own death or reap. The unique registered terminal controller waits and reaps that exact process, validates stdout plus expectedAfter, then publishes exact no-extra-fields `DescriptorFilesystemMutationReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,requestSha256,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,operationKeySha256,lockIdentity,effectDisposition,observedBefore,observedAfter,descriptorSpawnReceipt,processIdentity,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,exitCode:0,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/operation-<F>-receipt.v1.json` through the existing registered Desk evidence writer. No later operation launches before receipt remote readback. Synchronous spawn failure creates existing terminal failure evidence with no receipt; response loss first proves the exact helper dead/reaped, then the unique controller relaunches the same request, which can only adopt after-state. The barrier request itself is a locked no-op; after its helper is dead/reaped, the controller separately validates the mutation plan, every lower intent/receipt pair, contiguous ordinals, exact after-states, and absence of lower live helpers before publishing the barrier receipt and then residue proof.

- Native esbuild never owns a pathname output. Helper-image preparation and every `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImage`, `TerminalBootstrapCleanupImageRemoval`, helper-image temp, and helper-image removal schema are invalid and absent. When a bundle is required before terminal cleanup, the measured native esbuild executable receives held source bytes on stdin with argv `['<esbuild-fd-3>','--bundle','--platform=node','--format=cjs','--target=node22','--log-level=error','--loader=ts','--sourcefile=release-terminal.ts']`; omission of `--outfile` makes stdout the sole bundle byte stream. The registered command captures bounded raw stdout and empty sanitized stderr, hashes both, and a later write_publish request transfers the captured bytes through fd 5 to the exact temp/final leaves. Esbuild and the filesystem helper never share a writable pathname. `--outfile`, `--outdir`, shell redirection, package shim, PATH lookup, or direct filesystem publication rejects.

- Slugger has exactly the already registered gate and child; the child is the effect. Final `ProcessRegistrationBundle` for Slugger is exact `[gate,child]`, both are prepared/registered before spawn, and no native Ouro, shell wrapper, `effect` role, fork, third member, external-effect startup, external-effect spawn, or native-effect run file exists. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalSluggerNotifierProgram={version:1,socketPath:'/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock',recipient:'slugger',message:'Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end',sourceByteLength,sourceSha256,protocolVersion:1}` freezes one UTF-8 CommonJS program of at most 24 KiB using only `node:net`; the complete source bytes are held and hash-verified before spawn. The child DescriptorLaunchSpec transfers trusted Node at fd 3, the program at read-only fd 4, and the existing authenticated duplex gate-child control socket at fd 5; it has closed env/cwd and uses exact argv `['<node-fd-3>','<notifier-program-fd-4>',rootIdentitySha256,operationKeySha256]`. The source treats fd 5 as its sole pre-effect ready/authorization channel and never opens another control path. No HOME, PATH, Ouro install path, package import, mutable code, or filesystem fallback is consulted.

- The notifier program implements the live-daemon `ouro msg --to slugger` wire path after authorization; it deliberately records daemon-unavailable as uncertain instead of consulting Ouro's mutable filesystem fallback. Before release it sends only its authenticated ready frame and blocks on the existing gate-child control channel. After the remotely read-back TerminalSluggerRunAuthorization, it opens the literal socket once and sends compact JSON plus LF for `{kind:'message.send',from:'ouro-cli',to:'slugger',content:'Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end'}`. It requires one valid `ok:true` response with a nonempty `data.id`, then best-effort opens the socket once more and sends `{kind:'private.wake',agent:'slugger',reason:'operator CLI message',triggerSource:'operator-cli',budgetClass:'interactive',idempotencyKey:'cli:message:slugger:'+receiptId,originRefs:[{kind:'cli-command',id:'ouro msg'},{kind:'daemon-receipt',id:receiptId}]}`. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalSluggerNotifierResult={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationKeySha256,messageReceiptId,messageResponseSha256,wakeDisposition:'accepted'|'failed',wakeResponseSha256,outcome:'observed',completedAt}` is its sole success stdout; wakeResponseSha256 is nonnull exactly for a valid `ok:true` wake response and null for sanitized failure, matching the CLI's ignored wake failure. A message connect/error/timeout/empty/invalid/non-ok response is sanitized terminal uncertainty. Because the registered child itself owns the only effect, recovery waits/reaps it and never respawns or resends after run authorization. This is the sole terminal notification operation.

- Final Slugger records have no external-effect members. Exact no-extra-fields `SluggerNotification={version:1,task,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,message,ownershipGeneration:1,writerId,phase,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,notifierProgramPath,notifierProgramSha256,generation,resultPath,resultSha256,completedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase `observed|uncertain`; resultPath/hash name the selected child RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope, and notifierProgramPath/hash name the immutable TerminalSluggerNotifierProgram. Final Slugger RegisteredProcessCommandEnvelope is the existing exact gate/child terminal envelope with no `externalEffectSpawnPath`, `externalEffectSpawnSha256`, `externalEffectRunPath`, or `externalEffectRunSha256`. Every Round 77 native-effect intent/startup/spawn/run schema and every corresponding retained kind/path grammar is invalid. `ReleaseTerminalCompletion.sluggerTerminalCommandPath/hash`, `TerminalFinalResidueProof.sluggerPath/hash`, and ReleaseTerminalResult.sluggerPath all resolve the same terminal child/notification authorities under their existing pair meanings.

- Root preliminary and final result schemas are complete and have no external-effect fields. Final `ReleaseTerminalRootPreliminaryResult` is exactly `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'ordinary',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath:null}` or `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_root',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,consumedRepairIndexPath,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath,repairLinkPath,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath:null}`. Ordinary deskArchivePath is nonnull. Repair-root's predecessor/consumed/supersede/link fields are nonnull; its deskArchivePath is null iff predecessorArchiveState is `validating_active` and nonnull otherwise. Both lineages are nonnull lowercase Git SHAs; ordinary requires equality and repair-root preserves root/deployed lineage. In either branch trackArchivePath is null iff the referenced TrackDisposition decision is `kept_active` and nonnull/hash-valid iff `archived`.

- Final `ReleaseTerminalResult` is exactly a four-branch no-extra-fields union. Ordinary is `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'ordinary',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath:null,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath:null,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,sluggerPath}` with desk/archive/lineage/slugger nonnull and equal lineage. Repair-successor is `{mode:'normal',terminalKind:'repair_successor',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath,postMergeRepairRelayPath:null,predecessorArchiveState:null,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint:null,consumedRepairIndexPath:null,createdRepairIndexPath,predecessorSupersedePath:null,repairLinkPath:null,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha:null,deployedValidatorMergeSha:null,sluggerPath:null}` with deskArchivePath, postMergeRepairPath, and createdRepairIndexPath nonnull. Repair-intermediate is `{mode:'repaired',terminalKind:'repair_intermediate',terminalCleanupPath,deskArchivePath,postMergeRepairPath,postMergeRepairRelayPath,predecessorArchiveState,predecessorArchiveCheckpoint,consumedRepairIndexPath,createdRepairIndexPath,predecessorSupersedePath,repairLinkPath,trackDispositionPath,trackArchivePath,archiveValidationPath,rootMergeSha:null,deployedValidatorMergeSha:null,sluggerPath:null}` with every predecessor/index/supersede/link/relay member nonnull; deskArchivePath is null iff predecessorArchiveState is `validating_active`, while postMergeRepairPath independently is nonnull iff the consumed repair dispositionKind is `terminal` and null iff `stage`. Repair-root is the preliminary repair-root shape with nonnull sluggerPath and the same validating_active desk nullability. Every branch has nonnull cleanup/disposition/validation paths, and trackArchivePath is null iff `kept_active` and nonnull iff `archived`. There is no omitted field, inferred null, external-effect field, or alternate result display.

- Cleanup completion has one latest complete schema with no helper image. Exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,generationOutcomePaths,generationOutcomeSetSha256,successfulCleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,cleanupReleaseIntentPath,cleanupReleaseIntentSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,descriptorMutationPlanPath,descriptorMutationPlanSha256,finalFilesystemBarrierPath,finalFilesystemBarrierSha256,cleanerTerminalCommandPath,cleanerTerminalCommandSha256,sluggerPriorOutcomePaths,sluggerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,sluggerLaunchIntentPath,sluggerLaunchIntentSha256,sluggerSpawnPath,sluggerSpawnSha256,sluggerNotifierProgramPath,sluggerNotifierProgramSha256,sluggerReadyPath,sluggerReadySha256,completedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/cleanup-completion.v1.json`. Both outcome arrays are complete contiguous `TerminalOutcomeRef` histories; the mutation plan is the contiguous exact lock-bootstrap plus intent/request set; finalFilesystemBarrier is the highest ordinal DescriptorFilesystemMutationReceipt. Completion requires the cleaner terminal envelope clean, bootstrap root/delete/temp/helper-image paths absent, every request receipt present/hash-valid, lock identity unchanged, all helpers/cleaners dead/reaped, cleanup receipt read back, the selected Slugger gate/child prepared and ready but not run-authorized, and the preliminary terminal/archive proofs still valid. Final exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,ownerRegistryPath,ownerRegistrySha256,selectionReceiptPaths,selectionReceiptSetSha256,selectionReleasePaths,selectionReleaseSetSha256,residueProofPath,residueProofSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,sluggerTerminalCommandPath,sluggerTerminalCommandSha256,completedAt}` is the sole terminal completion. It has no bootstrap image, inline-program removal, Node-runtime identity, native effect, external-effect, or sibling-set member.

- Final residue proof is likewise complete. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalFinalResidueProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,descriptorMutationPlanPath,descriptorMutationPlanSha256,finalFilesystemBarrierPath,finalFilesystemBarrierSha256,filesystemLockIdentity,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,closureRoots,closureRootSetSha256,retainedMembers,retainedMemberSetSha256,absenceProofs,absenceProofSetSha256,completedAt}` requires the same immutable lock identity and highest barrier receipt, the canonical Slugger notification, and all exact result/cleanup refs. `absenceProofs` includes bootstrap root/delete root, every operation temp leaf, every helper-image/temp/removal path, every native-effect startup/intent/spawn/run path, mutable terminal attempts, pending secret channels, and every unclassified release-owned path. Unit31's separate FeatureFinalizationReceipt, not terminal residue, proves its provisional/staging/delete roots absent. No sibling inventory is present or required.

- Retained classification is closed under the replacement design. `TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind` additionally admits exactly `descriptor_filesystem_lock_bootstrap_intent|descriptor_filesystem_lock_bootstrap_receipt|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_intent|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_receipt|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_plan|slugger_notifier_program`; their literal path grammars are respectively `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/lock-bootstrap-intent.v1.json`, `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/lock-bootstrap-receipt.v1.json`, `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/operation-<F>-intent.v1.json`, `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/operation-<F>-receipt.v1.json`, `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/mutation-plan.v1.json`, and `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-notifier-program.v1.json`. FeatureFinalizationReceipt is ordinary Unit31 stage evidence outside terminal closure. The registry removes helper-image intent/outcome/removal and native-effect intent/startup/spawn/run kinds because those files are forbidden. Final closure scans require each retained byte to match exactly one live grammar and require every mutable attempt, provisional path, local startup, unknown file, alias, and obsolete kind absent.

- Unit29.2.6a/b/c and Unit37 test the replacement protocol end to end: absent-leaf creation; file/directory/symlink states; held-ancestor replacement attempts; request/hash/launch acyclicity; 64 KiB request and 16 MiB payload boundaries; partial temp recovery; before/after adoption; conflict; lock creation/replacement/concurrency; response loss; barrier ordering; stdout-only esbuild; forbidden outfile/path lookup; exact two-member Slugger readiness/release; direct message/wake bytes; receipt loss and no resend; every result nullability branch; all eight provisional basenames and schemas; cleanup-port success and every failure stage; finalization crashes at every phase; exact nine/thirteen/27-file sets; retained-kind bijection; and final residue with no stale sibling, helper-image, or native-effect field. Any prior test fixture requiring `dinner`, an undefined typed delete, a pathname output, a third Slugger process, or a removed schema is invalid.

### Round 79 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for captured feature semantics, feature cleanup/finalization, terminal descriptor transport, filesystem helper generations, cleaner/notifier programs, Slugger socket execution, completion/residue schemas, publication order, and the Round 78 continuity correction. They replace every earlier conflicting clause for those subjects.

- Feature checks derive only from captured exchanges. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureTransportCapture={operationId,transport,principal,request,status,sourceSha,workerVersionId,response,requestSha256,responseSha256,captureSha256}` stores no credential, cookie, email, raw header, or free-form error. `requestSha256` and `responseSha256` hash compact canonical no-LF bytes of their complete typed objects. `captureSha256` hashes compact canonical `{operationId,transport,principal,requestSha256,status,sourceSha,workerVersionId,responseSha256}`. Exact `FeatureExchangeEvidence={ordinal,kind,operationId,identityObservationSequences,capture}` uses contiguous ordinal within its owning file; operationId/capture.operationId match; every network/main-document capture's nonempty strictly increasing observation sequence resolves the status/sourceSha/workerVersionId observed in that same transport callback; local-state capture uses the unique main-document observation that loaded the executing page. The runner writes the capture directly from the instrumented browser/fetch/MCP/native result before deriving a check. Caller-supplied expected values never populate response.

- `FeatureExchangeRequest` and `FeatureExchangeResponse` are a closed kind-discriminated registry. The exact rows are: `anonymous_local_start` / `adoption.anonymous.start` / browser_local / anonymous / request `{recipeId,action:'start'}` / response `{localAttemptKey,stepIndex:1,checkedIngredientOrdinals:[1],scaleFactor:2,persistedLocally:true}`; `anonymous_adopt` / `adoption.owner.adopt` / web / user_a / request `{recipeId,localAttemptKey,stepIndex:1,checkedIngredientOrdinals:[1],scaleFactor:2}` / response `{attemptId,revision:1,stepIndex:1,checkedIngredientOrdinals:[1],scaleFactor:2,adoptionDisposition:'created'}`; `anonymous_resume` / `adoption.owner.resume` / web / user_a / request `{recipeId,attemptId}` / the same canonical state plus `{resumed:true}`. Dynamic IDs use their existing sanitized grammars and cross-equal later rows.

- The shopping rows are exact ordinals and bodies: `shopping_web_delete` / `shopping.web.delete` / web / user_a / `{stableId,action:'delete'}` -> status 200 plus inactive `ShoppingItemStateProjection`; `shopping_web_restore` / `shopping.web.restore` / web / user_a / `{stableId,action:'restore'}` -> 200 plus active state; `shopping_web_reload` / `shopping.web.reload` / web / user_a / `{route:'/shopping-list',action:'reload'}` -> 200 plus `{items:[activeTarget,checkedCompanion],itemCount:2,checkedItemCount:1}` from the post-reload DOM/state loader; `shopping_rest_delete` / `shopping.rest-v1.delete` / rest_v1 / user_a / `{method:'DELETE',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items/{itemId}',stableId,clientMutationId}` -> 200 inactive; `shopping_rest_restore` / `shopping.rest-v1.restore` / rest_v1 / user_a / `{method:'POST',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items',name,quantity,unit,clientMutationId}` -> 200 `{created:false,updated:true,item:activeTarget}`; `shopping_mcp_delete` / `shopping.mcp.delete` / mcp / user_a / `{tool:'remove_shopping_list_item',arguments:{itemId:stableId}}` -> 200 `{items:[checkedCompanion]}`; `shopping_mcp_restore` / `shopping.mcp.restore` / mcp / user_a / `{tool:'add_shopping_list_item',arguments:{name,quantity,unit}}` -> 200 `{created:0,updated:1,items:[activeTarget,checkedCompanion]}`; and `shopping_native_read` / `shopping.native-sync.read` / native_sync / user_a / `{method:'GET',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/sync'}` -> 200 `{items:[activeTarget,checkedCompanion],itemCount:2,checkedItemCount:1}`. activeTarget is exact `{stableId,active:true,checked:false,itemCount:2,checkedItemCount:1}`; inactive target is `{stableId,active:false,checked:false,itemCount:1,checkedItemCount:1}`; checkedCompanion has a different stable ID and checked true. The derived shopping check requires all eight rows and derives webReloadRestored only from shopping_web_reload.

- The remaining exact exchange registry is literal. saved privacy rows are `saved.owner.save`, `saved.owner.blank-search`, `saved.owner.filtered-search`, `saved.other.list`, `saved.owner.unsave` with respectively request/response `{recipeId}->{isSaved:true}`, `{query:''}->{recipeIds:[recipeId]}`, `{query:fixtureTitle}->{recipeIds:[recipeId]}`, `{query:''}->{recipeIds:[]}`, and `{recipeId}->{isSaved:false}`. Tag rows are `tags.owner.update`, `tags.owner.filter`, `tags.owner.search` with `{recipeId,course:'main',tags:['quick','weeknight']}->{metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection}`, `{course:'main',tags:['quick']}->{recipeIds:[recipeId]}`, and `{query:'weeknight'}->{recipeIds:[recipeId]}`. REST rows are `rest.recipe.summary` and `rest.recipe.detail-scale` with exact v1 GET requests and responses `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection}` and `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection,scale:NeutralScaleProjection}`. MCP rows are `mcp.recipe.search` and `mcp.recipe.get-scale` with exact `search_recipes` and `get_recipe` tool/argument requests and the same neutral summary/detail projections. History rows are `history.owner.list`, `history.other.list`, `history.anonymous.list`, `history.owner.snapshot`, `history.other.snapshot`, `history.anonymous.snapshot`; list responses are respectively `{attemptIds:[attemptId]}`, `{attemptIds:[]}`, `{attemptIds:[]}`, and snapshot responses are status/body `200/{attemptId,visible:true}`, `404/{code:'not_found'}`, `401/{code:'authentication_required'}`. Every list/snapshot request names its literal My Kitchen route or canonical snapshot route and its principal. The derived saved/tag/REST/MCP/history checks are pure functions of these bodies; REST/MCP neutral hashes recompute from captured projections, never from a caller-authored check.

- Projection files bind exact exchange sets. Final `FeatureCheckProjectionFile={version:1,checkName,exchanges:FeatureExchangeEvidence[],exchangeSetSha256,derivedCheck}` replaces FeatureCheckProjectionFile and ObservedFeatureSmokeCheck. `exchangeSetSha256` hashes the compact canonical complete exchange array in ordinal order; derivedCheck is recomputed from it. The literal file-to-operation arrays are: anonymous-adoption `[adoption.anonymous.start,adoption.owner.adopt,adoption.owner.resume]`; shopping-restore the eight shopping IDs above; saved-privacy the five saved IDs; tag-discovery the three tag IDs; rest-metadata-scale the two REST IDs; mcp-metadata-scale the two MCP IDs; cook-history-privacy the six history IDs. `FeatureSemanticFixture.checks` is the seven exact derived checks in existing order and byte-equals each file's derivedCheck. `FeatureOperationIdentityIndex.operationReferences` is the BINARY-operationId-sorted exact union of all 29 exchange references plus the two boundary-health references; each exchange's sequence list byte-equals its reference. Missing, duplicated, unreferenced, expected-only, or capture/check-tampered bytes reject even if all stored hashes are self-consistent. Unit30/31 tests mutate every request, response, identity header, status, operation ID, exchange hash, and derived check independently.

- Final exact no-extra-fields `FeatureSemanticFixture={version:1,checks:FeatureSmokeCheck[],identityObservations:LiveIdentityObservation[],identityObservationSetSha256,operationReferences:LiveOperationReference[],artifactPaths:FeatureArtifactRef[],artifactSetSha256,passed:true}` replaces the Round 78 ObservedFeatureSmokeCheck form. checks has exactly the seven derived values; artifactPaths remains the exact eight projection/index refs. Final FeatureSmokeReport uses the same `checks:FeatureSmokeCheck[]` and operationReferences, adds the selected cleanup receipt path/hash pairs and existing twelve cleanup artifact refs, and accepts only byte-equal derived checks. No singular check-level operationId survives Round 79.

- Feature cleanup is resumable by operation key; call count is not used as an exactly-once claim. Final `FeatureCleanupRequest={version:1,attemptId,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',targetBaseUrl,principal,chefId,recipeIds,cookAttemptIds,shoppingItemIds,expectedSourceSha,expectedWorkerVersionId,operationKeySha256}` hashes compact canonical bytes of every preceding field. `FeatureCleanupPort` exposes only `cleanup(request):Promise<FeatureCleanupPortResult>`; `FeatureCleanupPortResult` is exactly FeatureCleanupSuccess|FeatureCleanupFailure. It must resume the existing six-file primary cleanup state machine at the operation-keyed principal staging root. Every irreversible mutation retains its existing write-ahead CleanupMutationJournal and returned-or-read-only-reconciled receipt; repeated calls with the same request may only resume/read back those bytes and cannot issue a duplicate logical deletion. A different request for one operation key rejects.

- Per-principal invocation evidence closes every response-loss window. Exact immutable `FeatureCleanupInvocationIntent={version:1,finalizationAttemptId,principal,invocationOrdinal,priorInvocationReceipts,priorInvocationReceiptSetSha256,request,requestSha256,stagingRoot,createdAt}` is fsynced before the call. Exact mutable `FeatureCleanupInvocationAttempt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,phase,activeStage,portCallOrdinal,result,startedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase `prepared|running|terminal`; prepared has null activeStage/result and call ordinal zero, running has the exact cleanup stage plus positive call ordinal and null result, terminal clears activeStage and has one result. After a crash in running, recovery invokes the same idempotent request and the port reconciles/resumes its own stage journals. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureCleanupArtifactRef={path,sha256}` uses the six literal principal-relative primary cleanup basenames in their existing order. Success is `FeatureCleanupSuccess={ok:true,principal,operationKeySha256,disposition:'executed'|'reconciled'|'resumed',deletedCookAttemptCount,deletedSavedRecipeCount,deletedRecipeTagCount,deletedShoppingItemCount,deletedRecipeCount,deletedChefCount,remainingDisposableRowCount:0,artifactRefs,artifactSetSha256,completedAt}` with exactly six hash-valid refs; failure is `FeatureCleanupFailure={ok:false,principal,operationKeySha256,stage,code,retryable,observedDisposableRowCount,diagnosticArtifactRefs,diagnosticArtifactSetSha256,message,completedAt}`. Exact immutable `FeatureCleanupInvocationReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,result,resultSha256,completedAt}` atomically replaces terminal attempt. A retryable failure receipt alone authorizes invocationOrdinal+1 with the same request/operation key and complete prior receipt history; permanent failure terminalizes Unit31. User B still runs after User A terminalizes so both principals are cleaned, but final publication requires one successful selected receipt for each.

- Final FeatureFinalizationAttempt is exact `{version:1,attemptId,provisionalRoot,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,stagingRoot,deleteRoot,phase,userAInvocationReceipts,userAInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userBInvocationReceipts,userBInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,updatedAt}`. Phase is `prepared|cleanup_a|cleanup_b|cleanups_complete|final_staged|final_published|provisional_detached|provisional_removed|complete|failed`. cleanup_b begins after any terminal A receipt so B cleanup still runs; either selected success may remain null through cleanup_b. cleanups_complete requires both selected success pairs nonnull, and final hashes become nonnull at final_staged. If both principals terminalize but either lacks success, exact `FeatureFinalizationFailureReceipt={version:1,attemptId,userAInvocationReceipts,userAInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userBInvocationReceipts,userBInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,code:'cleanup_failed',completedAt}` atomically replaces failed attempt, publishes no finalRoot, and preserves diagnostic evidence. The staging thirteen-file set is constructed only by copying/readback-verifying the exact six artifact refs from each selected success receipt and deriving the report from captured projection files. Exact no-extra-fields `FeatureFinalizationReceipt={version:1,attemptId,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userAArtifactSetSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,userBArtifactSetSha256,provisionalRootAbsent:true,stagingRootAbsent:true,deleteRootAbsent:true,completedAt}` atomically replaces the complete attempt. Every crash before/after cleanup effect, response, receipt, staging write, rename, detach, and deletion resumes from these bytes.

- Terminal descriptor transport has one complete replacement schema. Exact `DescriptorNullDeviceIdentity={path:'/dev/null',device,inode,mode,uid}` and `DescriptorDuplexChannelIdentity={channelTokenSha256,parentFd,childFd}` contain no payload/control bytes. Exact `TerminalDescriptorInput` is `{kind:'bytes',role:'request'|'payload'|'program',fd,access:'read_only',fileIdentity:DescriptorFileIdentity,byteLength,sha256}|{kind:'null',role:'payload',fd,access:'read_only',deviceIdentity:DescriptorNullDeviceIdentity}|{kind:'duplex_socket',role:'run_control',fd,access:'duplex',channelIdentity:DescriptorDuplexChannelIdentity,maxFrameBytes}`. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalDescriptorLaunchSpec={version:1,purpose:'filesystem_helper'|'cleaner'|'slugger_notifier',platform,executable:DescriptorExecutableIdentity,executableFd:3,inputs,argv,cwd,envNames}` requires inputs in fd order with unique fds, closed undeclared descriptors, BINARY-unique closed envNames, and no secrets. Filesystem helper uses request fd4, payload-or-null fd5, run-control fd6; cleaner uses program fd4 and run-control fd5; notifier uses program fd4 and run-control fd5. `maxFrameBytes` is 16384. This schema, not DescriptorLaunchSpec, is binding for these three purposes.

- Filesystem roots and state hashes are constructible after controller loss. Exact `DescriptorRootAuthority={path:'/',device,inode,mode,uid,identitySha256}` hashes compact canonical preceding fields. Exact `DescriptorDirectoryComponentIdentity={name,device,inode,mode,uid}` and `DescriptorRelativeParentAuthority={rootIdentitySha256,rootAuthority,absoluteParentPath,components,componentIdentities,authoritySha256}` require components equal the identity names and hash compact canonical bytes of every preceding field. On every attempt the measured Python child opens literal `/` once with directory/no-follow flags, validates rootAuthority, then walks every component with openat directory/no-follow while retaining ancestors and validating identities; absoluteParentPath is recomputed display only. Replacement, symlink, missing component, device/inode/mode/uid drift, or post-open fstat drift conflicts before effect. Exact `DescriptorDirectoryEntry` is `{name,kind:'regular',mode,uid,size,sha256}|{name,kind:'directory',mode,uid}|{name,kind:'symlink',mode,uid,targetByteLength,targetSha256}`; entries are BINARY-name sorted and `entrySetSha256` hashes their compact canonical array. Directory states use that exact set.

- Final exact `DescriptorFilesystemMutationRequest={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,operation,sourceLeaf,targetLeaf,tempLeaf,expectedBefore,expectedAfter,payloadRef,payloadByteLength,payloadSha256,operationKeySha256}` uses ordinals 1..N and operation `mkdir|write_publish|rename|unlink_file|rmdir_empty|fsync_directory|barrier`. mkdir is target absent -> exact empty directory; write_publish is target/temp absent -> target exact regular/temp absent; rename is source exact regular-or-directory plus target absent -> source absent plus target byte-identical state; unlink_file is exact regular -> absent; rmdir_empty is exact empty directory -> absent; fsync_directory is exact directory -> byte-identical; barrier has no leaves/states. payloadRef/length/hash are nonnull exactly for write_publish. `operationKeySha256` hashes all preceding fields. Symlink or any tuple outside these matrices is conflict.

- Every payload is durable before the plan. Exact `DescriptorPayloadManifest={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,payloadOrdinal,sourceArtifactPath,sourceArtifactSha256,blobPath,byteLength,sha256,createdAt}` names raw immutable `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/payloads/payload-<P>.bin`; its bytes are at most 16 MiB and hash/length exact. Registered Desk publication fsyncs/pushes/readbacks blob and manifest before any request references it. Final exact `DescriptorFilesystemMutationPlan={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,globalLockDirectoryAuthority,parentAuthorities,parentAuthoritySetSha256,payloadManifests,payloadManifestSetSha256,operationRequests,operationRequestSetSha256,finalBarrierOrdinal,createdAt}` freezes the one shared Desk `_meta/release-terminal` directory authority, BINARY authority/payload sets, and contiguous operation request refs before operation 1. Every write_publish payloadRef resolves one manifest, and recovery no-follow opens/revalidates that blob into fd5. Captured esbuild stdout is one such source artifact. Missing blob or changed identity/hash blocks; no controller-memory-only payload exists. Helper launch intents and outcomes are created later and never appear in the pre-execution plan.

- Filesystem helpers are ordinary manifest-first paused generations, never process exceptions. Exact `DescriptorFilesystemRequestFile={version:1,request:DescriptorFilesystemMutationRequest}` lives at the operation request path and its file hash is requestSha256. Operation F attempt A owns exact immutable `DescriptorFilesystemHelperLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,attemptOrdinal,priorOutcomeRefs,priorOutcomeSetSha256,requestPath,requestSha256,launchSpec,launchSpecSha256,invocationToken,createdAt}`, `DescriptorFilesystemHelperSpawn={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,registrationBundlePath,registrationBundleSha256,gateProcessIdentity,childProcessIdentity,descriptorSpawnReceipts,readyPath,readySha256,preparedAt}`, and `DescriptorFilesystemHelperRunAuthorization={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationToken,authorizedAt}` at literal operation/attempt-qualified paths. ProcessRegistrationBundle is exact `[gate,child]` and remotely registered before spawn; child sends authenticated ready while paused and cannot inspect/mutate before authorization.

- Final `DescriptorFilesystemHelperOutcome` is a no-extra-fields five-branch union sharing `{version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}`. succeeded and conflict have nonnull spawn/authorization/terminal pairs; succeeded result is the exact DescriptorFilesystemMutationReceipt, while conflict result is exact `DescriptorFilesystemConflictResult={observedStates,code:'state_conflict'|'authority_drift'|'symlink'}` with exit 40 and no mutation receipt. spawn_failed has all spawn/authorization pairs null, result the sanitized spawn failure, and nonnull terminal pair. unreleased_dead has nonnull spawn, null authorization, and sanitized result. uncertain has nonnull spawn, authorization nullable exactly by whether release occurred, and sanitized result. A+1 is legal only after prior outcome readback and both prior members dead/reaped. Authorized uncertainty never assumes no effect: A+1 reacquires the global lock, observes before/owned-partial/after/conflict, and adopts or blocks. All attempts remain retained.

- Serialization uses no created lock leaf. Unit0 ensures the shared Desk `_meta/release-terminal` directory exists before any release identity and freezes its DescriptorRelativeParentAuthority as `globalLockDirectoryAuthority`; every helper reconstructs/validates that directory and takes exclusive `flock` on the held directory fd before state inspection, retaining it through effect-result emission. Thus concurrent release roots share one existing identity and there is no create/adopt race or bootstrap process. Final barrier is highest F and is accepted only after the controller validates the complete plan, all earlier attempt histories, one success receipt per operation, exact after-states, no live helper, and unchanged global lock directory identity. Every earlier DescriptorFilesystemLockBootstrapIntent/Receipt, lock_bootstrap operation, or helper-exception schema is invalid.

- The cleaner has an immutable retained program, not a helper image. Exact `TerminalCleanerProgram={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,sourceFile,esbuildExecutableIdentity,buildCommandPath,buildCommandSha256,programPayloadManifestPath,programPayloadManifestSha256,programSha256,createdAt}` is created from stdout-only measured esbuild and points to a DescriptorPayloadManifest/raw program blob. Final `TerminalBootstrapCleanupLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,bootstrapReceiptPath,bootstrapReceiptSha256,archiveValidationPath,archiveValidationSha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,cleanerProgramPath,cleanerProgramSha256,invocationToken,launchSpec:TerminalDescriptorLaunchSpec,launchSpecSha256,createdAt}` replaces every image-bearing cleaner intent. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalCleanerSpawn={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,processAttemptOrdinal,registrationBundlePath,registrationBundleSha256,gateProcessIdentity,childProcessIdentity,descriptorSpawnReceipts,preparedAt}`, `TerminalCleanerReady={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyFrameSha256,phase:'paused',readyAt}`, and `TerminalCleanerRunAuthorization={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,cleanupAttemptPath,cleanupAttemptSha256,authorizationToken,authorizedAt}` use literal attempt-qualified paths. Final TerminalCleanerOutcome is a four-branch no-extra-fields union: succeeded has nonnull spawn/authorization/cleanup-receipt/terminal pairs; spawn_failed has all spawn/authorization/cleanup-receipt pairs null and nonnull terminal pair; unreleased_dead has nonnull spawn, null authorization/cleanup receipt, and nonnull terminal pair; uncertain has nonnull spawn, authorization nullable exactly by release, null cleanup receipt, and nonnull terminal pair. Every branch has exact dead:true/reaped:true/completedAt. ProcessRegistrationBundle is exact `[gate,child]`, remotely registered before spawn. The child coordinates the frozen filesystem plan and registered helpers, then terminalizes. Program payload remains hash-valid retained Desk evidence through final residue; there is no image path, temp, remover, or removal receipt.

- Final exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanerProgramPath,cleanerProgramSha256,cleanerPriorOutcomePaths,cleanerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,successfulCleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,cleanerOutcomePath,cleanerOutcomeSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,descriptorMutationPlanPath,descriptorMutationPlanSha256,finalFilesystemBarrierPath,finalFilesystemBarrierSha256,cleanerTerminalCommandPath,cleanerTerminalCommandSha256,sluggerPriorOutcomePaths,sluggerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,sluggerLaunchIntentPath,sluggerLaunchIntentSha256,sluggerSpawnPath,sluggerSpawnSha256,sluggerNotifierProgramPath,sluggerNotifierProgramSha256,sluggerReadyPath,sluggerReadySha256,completedAt}` replaces every image/helper-removal cleanup completion. Both histories are complete contiguous TerminalOutcomeRef arrays. It requires successful cleaner outcome/receipt, every helper terminal/dead/reaped, the prepared/ready unreleased Slugger pair, and no run authorization.

- Slugger has a complete byte and state authority. Exact raw program path is `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/notifier-program.v1.cjs` and manifest path is `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/notifier-program.v1.json`; exact `TerminalSluggerNotifierProgram={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,programPath,sourceByteLength,sourceSha256,socketPath:'/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock',recipient:'slugger',message:'Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end',protocolVersion:1,createdAt}` hashes/locates those immutable bytes. `operationKeySha256` hashes compact canonical `{rootIdentitySha256,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,recipient,message,ownershipGeneration:1}`. Exact `TerminalSluggerLaunchIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,processAttemptOrdinal,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,notificationPath,notificationBeforeSha256,notifierProgramPath,notifierProgramSha256,operationKeySha256,processBundlePath,processBundleSha256,gateLaunchSpec,gateLaunchSpecSha256,childLaunchSpec:TerminalDescriptorLaunchSpec,childLaunchSpecSha256,invocationToken,createdAt}` replaces every image/Ouro/native-effect form. Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalSluggerSpawn={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,registrationBundlePath,registrationBundleSha256,gateProcessIdentity,childProcessIdentity,gateDescriptorSpawnReceipt,childDescriptorSpawnReceipt,spawnedAt}` lives at `attempt-<S>-spawn.v1.json`; exact `TerminalSluggerReady={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,processAttemptOrdinal,readyFrameSha256,phase:'paused',readyAt}` lives at `attempt-<S>-ready.v1.json`.

- Slugger notification CAS is serializable and acyclic. Final `SluggerNotification` phase is `prepared|released|observed|uncertain`. Common fields are `{version:1,task,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,message,ownershipGeneration:1,writerId,phase,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,notifierProgramPath,notifierProgramSha256,generation,runAuthorizationIntentPath,runAuthorizationIntentSha256,resultPath,resultSha256,completedAt,updatedAt}`. prepared has authorization-intent/result/completed null; released has the immutable authorization-intent pair nonnull and result/completed null; observed|uncertain preserve that pair and add nonnull result/completed. Exact `TerminalSluggerRunAuthorizationIntent={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,processAttemptOrdinal,launchIntentPath,launchIntentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,notifierProgramPath,notifierProgramSha256,operationKeySha256,notificationPath,notificationBeforeSha256,authorizationToken,createdAt}` is remotely read back before CAS. Exact `TerminalSluggerRunAuthorization={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,notificationPath,notificationBeforeSha256,notificationAfterSha256,authorizationToken,authorizedAt}` records the sole prepared -> released CAS; notificationAfter points only to the intent, never this receipt, so hashes are acyclic. Child control frame is 4-byte big-endian length plus compact canonical authorization JSON/hash. Identical duplicate frames before local effect start are acknowledged once; any mismatch, oversized frame, duplicate after start, or pre-readback frame terminalizes uncertain without message. Terminal envelope/result and final notification CAS occur only after both registered members are dead/reaped. No third process or external-effect field exists.

- Slugger preparation order is literal: publish/readback notifier program; freeze prior outcomes; CAS canonical notification to prepared with selected ordinal S; prepare/readback the exact `[gate,child]` registration bundle; publish/readback launch intent against the prepared notification hash; spawn both members; publish/readback spawn and ready; publish/readback authorization intent; CAS prepared -> released; publish/readback authorization receipt; send the exact control frame; wait/reap; publish outcome/terminal command; CAS released -> observed|uncertain. A crash adopts only the exact next prefix. No record points to a later record except the mutable notification CAS after-image, whose only authorization pointer is the already immutable intent.

- Exact no-extra-fields `TerminalSluggerAttemptOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationIntentPath,authorizationIntentSha256,authorizationReceiptPath,authorizationReceiptSha256,outcome:'observed'|'uncertain'|'spawn_failed'|'unreleased_dead',notifierResult,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` lives at the attempt outcome path. observed/uncertain have all launch/spawn/ready/authorization pairs nonnull and notifierResult respectively TerminalSluggerNotifierResult or sanitized uncertainty; spawn_failed has every post-intent pair null; unreleased_dead has spawn/ready nonnull and authorization pairs null. Only spawn_failed/unreleased_dead while notification remains prepared authorize S+1. An authorized observed/uncertain outcome is final and the notification CAS points resultPath/hash to its terminal command.

- Final `ProcessRegistrationOwner` removes terminal_helper_image and every native Slugger effect variant, retains `{kind:'bootstrap_cleanup',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}` and `{kind:'slugger_external',rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal}`, and adds exact `{kind:'descriptor_filesystem_helper',rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,attemptOrdinal}`. Each owner uses the existing manifest-first authority/directory/registration rules; for these owners ProcessRegistrationBundle is exact `[gate,child]` and no native SpawnedProcessIdentity is legal.

- Socket I/O is bounded. Each message/wake socket has 5000 ms connect deadline, 30000 ms total deadline, and 65536-byte response cap. Client writes exactly one compact JSON request plus LF and does not half-close; server EOF is the sole response terminator. Bytes decode with fatal UTF-8, trim only JSON whitespace, and must parse as exactly one object with no trailing value; empty, partial, extra JSON, cap overflow, timeout, error, or non-ok message response is uncertainty. The socket is destroyed and listeners removed on every settlement. A valid message response with nonempty `data.id` is observed message effect. Wake uses the same bounds but remains best-effort: failure yields wakeDisposition failed/null hash without changing observed. stdout is exactly one bounded TerminalSluggerNotifierResult; stderr is empty for observed and one sanitized finite failure record for uncertainty.

- Selection history and absence proof shapes are final. ReleaseTerminalCompletion contains adjacent `selectionHistoryIndexPath,selectionHistoryIndexSha256` and no selection receipt/release arrays. Exact `TerminalAbsenceRegistryMember={kind,path,parentAuthoritySha256,leafName}` uses kind `bootstrap_root|bootstrap_delete_root|filesystem_temp|legacy_helper_image|legacy_native_effect|mutable_terminal_attempt|pending_secret_channel|local_process_startup`; members are BINARY `(kind,path)` sorted and derive finitely from the mutation plan plus literal legacy/mutable grammars. Exact `TerminalAbsenceRegistry={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,closureRoots,members,memberSetSha256,createdAt}` freezes that complete set before scan; memberSetSha256 hashes compact canonical no-LF bytes of the complete ordered members.
