Exact `TerminalAbsentHostMember={kind,path,parentAuthoritySha256,leafName,observation:'fstatat_no_follow_absent',observedAt}` byte-matches one registry member; absenceProofs is the complete registry order and absenceProofSetSha256 hashes its compact canonical array. Separately, a no-follow recursive scan of closureRoots must classify every present byte in retainedMembers exactly once; an unknown present path rejects and is not represented as an impossible absent wildcard.

- Final exact no-extra-fields `TerminalFinalResidueProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,ownerRegistryPath,ownerRegistrySha256,selectionHistoryIndexPath,selectionHistoryIndexSha256,descriptorMutationPlanPath,descriptorMutationPlanSha256,finalFilesystemBarrierPath,finalFilesystemBarrierSha256,globalLockDirectoryAuthority,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,closureRoots,closureRootSetSha256,retainedMembers,retainedMemberSetSha256,absenceRegistryPath,absenceRegistrySha256,absenceProofs,absenceProofSetSha256,completedAt}` uses the exact registry/order/hash rules above. Every path/hash is nonnull and read back before proof creation. closureRoots remains the exact release-root singleton; external task/archive/track/notification refs remain explicit retained members.

- The complete final `TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind` union is exactly `root_owner_registry|owner_selection_intent|owner_selection|owner_selection_release|owner_probe|owner_rescan|effect_proof|effect_result|archive_validation_control|track_linearization|track_fence|track_scan_stale|track_conflict|process_authority|process_directory_intent|process_directory|process_spawn_intent|process_spawn|process_terminal|registration_root_intent|registration_root_receipt|owner_preselection_settlement_intent|owner_preselection_settlement|owner_validator_run_authorization|owner_selection_history|terminal_control_attempt|terminal_successful_result|terminal_failure_snapshot|terminal_failure_evidence|terminal_failure_disposition|terminal_progress|terminal_wait|terminal_blocker|terminal_resolution|descriptor_secret_channel|cleaner_intent|cleaner_spawn|cleaner_ready|cleaner_authorization|cleaner_transfer_intent|cleaner_transfer|cleaner_transfer_cancellation|cleaner_generation_outcome|cleaner_receipt|terminal_cleaner_program|descriptor_payload_manifest|descriptor_payload_blob|descriptor_filesystem_request|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_plan|descriptor_filesystem_helper_intent|descriptor_filesystem_helper_spawn|descriptor_filesystem_helper_authorization|descriptor_filesystem_helper_outcome|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_receipt|slugger_notifier_program|slugger_intent|slugger_spawn|slugger_ready|slugger_attempt_outcome|slugger_run_authorization|slugger_notification|terminal_absence_registry|cleanup_completion`. Every earlier cleaner_image/helper_intent/helper_outcome/image_removal/slugger_external_effect_spawn/descriptor_filesystem_intent/lock-bootstrap kind is removed.

- New literal grammars are closed: payload manifest/blob at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/payloads/payload-<P>.{v1.json,bin}`; plan at `filesystem/mutation-plan.v1.json`; request at `filesystem/operation-<F>/request.v1.json`; helper records at `filesystem/operation-<F>/attempt-<A>/{launch-intent,spawn,run-authorization,outcome}.v1.json`; successful mutation receipt at `filesystem/operation-<F>/receipt.v1.json`; cleaner program at `bootstrap-cleanup/cleaner-program.v1.json`; cleaner attempt records at `bootstrap-cleanup/generations/attempt-<C>-{launch-intent,spawn,ready,run-authorization,outcome}.v1.json`; notifier manifest/raw at `slugger/notifier-program.v1.{json,cjs}`; Slugger attempt records at `slugger/attempt-<S>-{launch-intent,spawn,ready,outcome}.v1.json`; Slugger authorization intent/receipt at the existing singular literal paths; absence registry at `terminal-absence-registry.v1.json`; cleanup completion at `cleanup-completion.v1.json`. Each path maps to one kind above. All older kinds retain their latest pre-Round79 literal grammars. Raw `.bin` maps only descriptor_payload_blob and raw `.cjs` only slugger_notifier_program. Duplicate/unknown classification rejects.

- Authoritative final order is `cleanup receipt -> cleaner terminal/dead/reaped -> cleanup completion with Slugger prepared/ready -> Slugger run authorization -> Slugger terminal/dead/reaped -> owner registry host_cleaned -> terminal result -> final residue proof -> ReleaseTerminalCompletion`. Terminal result therefore exists before TerminalFinalResidueProof names it. Final exact no-extra-fields `ReleaseTerminalCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanupCompletionPath,cleanupCompletionSha256,ownerRegistryPath,ownerRegistrySha256,selectionHistoryIndexPath,selectionHistoryIndexSha256,residueProofPath,residueProofSha256,terminalResultPath,terminalResultSha256,sluggerPath,sluggerSha256,sluggerTerminalCommandPath,sluggerTerminalCommandSha256,completedAt}` is the sole terminal completion. No target/provider/product/D1/R2/HTTP operation follows final health; these remaining steps are local/Desk control only. Unit37's older residue-before-result, helper-image, native-effect, selection-array, and nullable external-effect output sentences are invalid.

- Unit29.2.6a/b/c tests the full Round 79 launch schemas, root reconstruction, directory entries/hashes, every operation matrix, durable payload loss/reopen, helper registration/ready/release/attempt replacement, conflict, cleaner program lifecycle, Slugger prepared/released/terminal phases, all control/socket framing limits, selection index, finite absence registry, classification, and final order. Units30/31 test all 29 literal captures, capture/check tampering, web reload, per-principal cleanup stage crashes, response-loss reconciliation, retry/permanent failure receipts, six-file handoff, and thirteen-file publication. The actual Round 78 repair commit is `cd6d1cf666dd10afeee188cd019e12d0144dbf09`; any other full expansion of `cd6d1cf6` is invalid.

### Round 80 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for feature transport captures, shopping restore semantics, feature finalization, descriptor programs and control frames, cleanup/notifier generation states, global-lock provisioning, resource bounds, and terminal residue classification. They replace every earlier conflicting clause for those subjects. A schema declared exact is no-extra-fields, compact-canonical JSON in the displayed field order when hashed, and uses no final LF unless this section says otherwise.

- Feature capture values are closed and sanitized. `FeatureOpaqueId` is ASCII matching `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,128}$`; `FeatureMutationId` is a lowercase UUID-v4; `FeatureTitleToken` is literal `codex-smoke-feature-recipe`; `FeatureItemName` is literal `codex-smoke-target`; `FeatureUnit` is literal `cup`; `FeatureTag` is `quick|weeknight`; `FeatureCourse` is literal `dinner`; `FeatureRoute` is one of the literal routes below with only the named `{recipeId}`, `{attemptId}`, or `{stableId}` substitution. IDs are copied only after their raw values pass the owning live schema, and no title, ingredient, user, email, token, header, or free-form response string is copied. Exact `FeatureTransportCapture={operationId,kind,transport,principal,statusSource,status,request,sourceSha,workerVersionId,response,requestSha256,responseSha256,captureSha256}` uses transport `browser_local|web|rest_v1|mcp|native_sync`, principal `anonymous|user_a|user_b`, statusSource `local_state|http_response|mcp_http_response`, and status null exactly for local_state and an integer 100..599 otherwise. Local state binds the page-load identity observation; every other capture binds the exact response's observed build headers. `requestSha256` and `responseSha256` hash their complete typed values; captureSha256 hashes `{operationId,kind,transport,principal,statusSource,status,requestSha256,sourceSha,workerVersionId,responseSha256}`. `FeatureExchangeEvidence={ordinal,operationId,identityObservationSequences,capture}` has a positive contiguous ordinal in its projection file, capture.operationId equality, and the existing nonempty observation-reference equality; local state references the page load and every network exchange references its response callback.

- Shared feature response projections are exact. `CookFeatureState={attemptId,recipeId,revision,status:'ACTIVE',activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[]}` uses FeatureOpaqueId identifiers; recipeFingerprint uses the canonical 64-lowercase-hex cook grammar. `ShoppingItemProjection={stableId,name:'codex-smoke-target'|'codex-smoke-companion',quantity:1,unit:'cup',checked,active,sortIndex,updatedAt}` uses a nonnegative safe sortIndex and canonical updatedAt; target and companion IDs differ. `ShoppingListProjection={items,itemCount,checkedItemCount}` has items in ascending `(sortIndex,updatedAt,stableId)` order and both counts exactly derived from active items. Every check response target is active/checked true with itemCount 2 and checkedItemCount 1; every delete response target is inactive/checked true and returns active items `[companion]` with itemCount 1 and checkedItemCount 0; every restore response preserves the target stableId, sets active true/checked false, returns `[companion,target]` with itemCount 2/checkedItemCount 0, and has target.sortIndex strictly greater than both its captured pre-delete sortIndex and every prior target restore sortIndex. Companion remains active and unchecked. Web reload and native sync byte-equal the final MCP-restored list. These inequalities are recomputed from stored captures, not asserted booleans.

- The exact 32-row capture registry is below. A row's request and response are its complete typed values; braces contain literal field order. `status` is the displayed value and every omitted dynamic token is a previously introduced FeatureOpaqueId that must cross-equal all later uses. MCP request means the exact JSON-RPC tool-call projection after stripping request id and OAuth bytes; MCP response means the exact successful tool result projection after parsing its JSON text. Web response means the named fetch/action response plus deterministic DOM/loader projection where stated.

| # | kind / operationId | transport / principal / status | exact request -> exact response |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `anonymous_local_start` / `adoption.anonymous.start` | `browser_local` / `anonymous` / null | `{route:'/recipes/{recipeId}/cook',action:'set_progress',recipeId,activeStepIndex:1,ingredientId,scaleFactor:2}` -> `{storageKey:'spoonjoy-cook-progress:'+recipeId,storedVersion:2,recipeId,activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[],networkMutationCount:0,persistedLocally:true}` |
| 2 | `anonymous_adopt` / `adoption.owner.adopt` | `web` / `user_a` / 201 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/cook-sessions',body:{recipeId,mutationId,adoption:{version:1,recipeFingerprint,activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[]}}}` -> `{data:{snapshot:CookFeatureState,adoptionOutcome:'adopted'},localStorageClearedAfterAuthenticatedPersistence:true}` where snapshot revision is 0 |
| 3 | `anonymous_resume` / `adoption.owner.resume` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{data:{snapshot:CookFeatureState},resumed:true}` byte-equal to row 2 snapshot |
| 4 | `shopping_web_check` / `shopping.web.check` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{route:'/shopping-list',action:'set_checked',stableId,checked:true}` -> `{item:ShoppingItemProjection,list:ShoppingListProjection}` with target checked |
| 5 | `shopping_web_delete` / `shopping.web.delete` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{route:'/shopping-list',action:'delete',stableId}` -> `{removed:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,list:ShoppingListProjection}` with inactive target and target pre-delete sortIndex copied from row 4 |
| 6 | `shopping_web_restore` / `shopping.web.restore` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{route:'/shopping-list',action:'add',name:'codex-smoke-target',quantity:1,unit:'cup'}` -> `{created:false,updated:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,list:ShoppingListProjection}` with unchecked target at a newer end |
| 7 | `shopping_web_reload` / `shopping.web.reload` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/shopping-list',action:'reload'}` -> `{list:ShoppingListProjection,domStableIds:[companionStableId,stableId]}` byte-equal to row 6 list |
| 8 | `shopping_rest_check` / `shopping.rest-v1.check` | `rest_v1` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'PATCH',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items/{stableId}',body:{clientMutationId,checked:true}}` -> `{data:{item:ShoppingItemProjection,mutation:{clientMutationId,replayed:false}},list:ShoppingListProjection}` with target checked |
| 9 | `shopping_rest_delete` / `shopping.rest-v1.delete` | `rest_v1` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'DELETE',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items/{stableId}',body:{clientMutationId}}` -> `{data:{removed:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,mutation:{clientMutationId,replayed:false}},list:ShoppingListProjection}` with inactive target |
| 10 | `shopping_rest_restore` / `shopping.rest-v1.restore` | `rest_v1` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items',body:{clientMutationId,name:'codex-smoke-target',quantity:1,unit:'cup'}}` -> `{data:{created:false,updated:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,mutation:{clientMutationId,replayed:false}},list:ShoppingListProjection}` with unchecked target at a newer end |
| 11 | `shopping_mcp_check` / `shopping.mcp.check` | `mcp` / `user_a` / 200 | `{tool:'set_shopping_list_item_checked',arguments:{itemId:stableId,checked:true}}` -> `{shoppingList:ShoppingListProjection}` with target checked |
| 12 | `shopping_mcp_delete` / `shopping.mcp.delete` | `mcp` / `user_a` / 200 | `{tool:'remove_shopping_list_item',arguments:{itemId:stableId}}` -> `{shoppingList:ShoppingListProjection}` with inactive target captured separately and active items `[companion]` |
| 13 | `shopping_mcp_restore` / `shopping.mcp.restore` | `mcp` / `user_a` / 200 | `{tool:'add_shopping_list_item',arguments:{name:'codex-smoke-target',quantity:1,unit:'cup'}}` -> `{created:0,updated:1,shoppingList:ShoppingListProjection}` with unchecked target at the newest end |
| 14 | `shopping_native_read` / `shopping.native-sync.read` | `native_sync` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/sync'}` -> `{items:ShoppingListProjection['items'],itemCount:2,checkedItemCount:0,nextCursorPresent:true}` byte-equal to row 13 list |
| 15 | `saved_owner_save` / `saved.owner.save` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{route:'/recipes/{recipeId}',action:'save',recipeId}` -> `{recipeId,isSaved:true}` |
| 16 | `saved_owner_blank_search` / `saved.owner.blank-search` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?tab=saved&q='}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 17 | `saved_owner_filtered_search` / `saved.owner.filtered-search` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?tab=saved&q=codex-smoke-feature-recipe'}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 18 | `saved_other_list` / `saved.other.list` | `web` / `user_b` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?tab=saved&q='}` -> `{recipeIds:[]}` |
| 19 | `saved_owner_unsave` / `saved.owner.unsave` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{route:'/recipes/{recipeId}',action:'unsave',recipeId}` -> `{recipeId,isSaved:false}` |
| 20 | `tags_owner_update` / `tags.owner.update` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{route:'/recipes/{recipeId}/edit',action:'set_taxonomy',recipeId,course:'dinner',tags:['quick','weeknight']}` -> `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection}` |
| 21 | `tags_owner_filter` / `tags.owner.filter` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?course=dinner&tag=quick'}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 22 | `tags_owner_search` / `tags.owner.search` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?q=weeknight'}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 23 | `rest_recipe_summary` / `rest.recipe.summary` | `rest_v1` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/v1/recipes?query=codex-smoke-feature-recipe'}` -> `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection}` |
| 24 | `rest_recipe_detail_scale` / `rest.recipe.detail-scale` | `rest_v1` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/v1/recipes/{recipeId}?scale=2'}` -> `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection,scale:NeutralScaleProjection}` |
| 25 | `mcp_recipe_search` / `mcp.recipe.search` | `mcp` / `user_a` / 200 | `{tool:'search_recipes',arguments:{query:'codex-smoke-feature-recipe'}}` -> `{recipes:[{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection}]}` |
| 26 | `mcp_recipe_get_scale` / `mcp.recipe.get-scale` | `mcp` / `user_a` / 200 | `{tool:'get_recipe',arguments:{recipeId,scaleFactor:2}}` -> `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection,scale:NeutralScaleProjection}` |
| 27 | `history_owner_list` / `history.owner.list` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?tab=cooks'}` -> `{attemptIds:[attemptId]}` |
| 28 | `history_other_list` / `history.other.list` | `web` / `user_b` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?tab=cooks'}` -> `{attemptIds:[]}` |
| 29 | `history_anonymous_list` / `history.anonymous.list` | `web` / `anonymous` / 302 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-kitchen?tab=cooks'}` -> `{location:'/login?returnTo=%2Fmy-kitchen%3Ftab%3Dcooks',attemptIds:[]}` |
| 30 | `history_owner_snapshot` / `history.owner.snapshot` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/attempts/{attemptId}'}` -> `{attemptId,visible:true}` |
| 31 | `history_other_snapshot` / `history.other.snapshot` | `web` / `user_b` / 404 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/attempts/{attemptId}'}` -> `{code:'not_found'}` |
| 32 | `history_anonymous_snapshot` / `history.anonymous.snapshot` | `web` / `anonymous` / 401 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/attempts/{attemptId}'}` -> `{code:'authentication_required'}` |

- The final derived check registry is exact. `NeutralMetadataProjection={stepCount:2,sourceDisplayName:'example.com',course:'dinner',tags:['quick','weeknight']}` and the existing NeutralScaleProjection remain exact. The seven FeatureSmokeCheck members and order remain anonymous_adoption, shopping_restore, saved_privacy, tag_discovery, rest_metadata_scale, mcp_metadata_scale, cook_history_privacy. The shopping member is now exactly `{name:'shopping_restore',stableId,webReloadRestored:true,restItemCount:2,checkedItemCount:0,nativeSyncObserved:true,restoreSortIndexes:[webSortIndex,restSortIndex,mcpSortIndex],finalOrderedStableIds:[companionStableId,stableId]}` with strictly increasing restoreSortIndexes. Projection exchange arrays are adoption rows 1-3, shopping 4-14, saved 15-19, tags 20-22, REST 23-24, MCP 25-26, and history 27-32. `FeatureOperationIdentityIndex.operationReferences` is the BINARY-operationId-sorted exact union of all 32 exchange references plus the two boundary-health references. Unit30/31 independently mutate every row discriminant, status source/status, request field, response field, dynamic cross-equality, hash, observation identity, and derived check.

- Semantic source bytes survive release finalization. The eight subordinate files retain their literal basenames; final copies live under `semantic/` with byte-identical contents and hashes. `FeatureSemanticFixture` remains the release-ineligible nine-file provisional set: its report plus seven projection files and the identity index. Final `FeatureSmokeReport={version:1,targetBaseUrl,sourceSha,workerVersionId,identityObservations,identityObservationSetSha256,checks,operationReferences,semanticArtifactPaths,semanticArtifactSetSha256,userACleanupReceiptPath,userACleanupReceiptSha256,userBCleanupReceiptPath,userBCleanupReceiptSha256,artifactPaths,artifactSetSha256,passed:true,completedAt}` is exact. semanticArtifactPaths is the BINARY-path-sorted eight `{path,sha256}` refs under `semantic/`; artifactPaths is the BINARY-path-sorted exact twenty-member union of those eight plus the six primary cleanup files under each of `user-a/` and `user-b/`, and excludes the report. Every check and operation reference is rederived from the retained semantic bytes. The final feature output is exactly 21 files, and the primary API+lifecycle+feature aggregate is exactly 35 files: one API, thirteen lifecycle, and twenty-one feature. The standalone production visual runner remains its own exact 38-file output and is not arithmetically merged into that primary aggregate.

- Feature cleanup control paths are constructible and outside every published output root. For finalization attempt FeatureOpaqueId `T`, controlRoot is exact sibling `<parent(finalRoot)>/.feature-finalization/<T>`; provisionalRoot, finalRoot, stagingRoot=`<controlRoot>/publish`, and deleteRoot=`<controlRoot>/detached-provisional` are distinct no-symlink descendants of one held parent. Principal paths use `principal-user-a|principal-user-b`; invocation N uses `invocation-<N>-intent.v1.json`, mutable `invocation-<N>-attempt.v1.json`, and immutable `invocation-<N>-receipt.v1.json`. Finalization uses `finalization-attempt.v1.json`, `finalization-failure.v1.json`, and `finalization-receipt.v1.json`. `FeatureCleanupReceiptRef={invocationOrdinal,path,sha256,resultSha256}` is sorted by contiguous invocationOrdinal and cross-links the receipt. `FeatureCleanupArtifactRef={path,sha256}` uses exactly the six existing primary cleanup basenames, principal-relative and in their existing literal order. `FeatureCleanupDiagnosticRef` uses the same shape and one of those six basenames, BINARY-path sorted, length 0..6.

- Cleanup failure domains and retries are finite. `FeatureCleanupStage` is `validate_request|purge_cooks|retire_projection|delete_user|verify_relational_zero|verify_retained_do|materialize_artifacts`; `FeatureCleanupFailureCode` is `request_invalid|operation_key_conflict|deadline_exceeded|attempt_limit_exhausted|network|rate_limited|server|protocol|identity|residue|projection|user_delete|artifact_mismatch|unknown`. `FeatureCleanupFailureDiagnostic` is the same finite code, not free-form text. Exact failure is `{ok:false,principal,operationKeySha256,stage,code,retryable,observedDisposableRowCount,diagnosticArtifactRefs,diagnosticArtifactSetSha256,diagnostic,completedAt}`; retryable is true only for network/rate_limited/server and false otherwise. Exact success remains the Round 79 success shape and requires six refs, exact deletion counts from immutable traces, and remainingDisposableRowCount zero. Every port call has a 60000 ms deadline; a finalization has absolute `deadlineAt=preparedAt+540000ms`; each principal has at most three invocations. Scheduling is fixed round-robin `A1,B1,A2,B2,A3,B3`, skipping a principal only after principal-terminal success, permanent failure, attempt exhaustion, or deadline. Invocation-terminal does not mean principal-terminal. A retryable receipt before the bound schedules that principal's next slot; a third retryable receipt becomes the unique synthetic nonretryable attempt_limit_exhausted receipt without a fourth port call. Reaching deadline first creates the unique nonretryable deadline_exceeded receipt for each nonterminal principal without another call. Final failure is legal only after both principals are principal-terminal and either lacks a selected success.

- Invocation and finalization schemas are total. `FeatureCleanupInvocationIntent={version:1,finalizationAttemptId,principal,invocationOrdinal,priorInvocationReceipts,priorInvocationReceiptSetSha256,request,requestSha256,stagingRoot,callDeadlineAt,createdAt}` uses the path grammar above. `FeatureCleanupInvocationAttempt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,phase,activeStage,portCallOrdinal,result,startedAt,updatedAt}` has matrices prepared/null/0/null, running/non-null/1/null, or terminal/null/1/result; synthetic exhaustion/deadline receipts skip the mutable attempt and their receipt has portCallOrdinal zero. `FeatureCleanupInvocationReceipt={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,portCallOrdinal,result,resultSha256,completedAt}` is immutable and its prior history is complete. A real terminal receipt is write/fsync/rename/read back before the mutable attempt is unlink/fsynced; if both survive a crash, the valid immutable receipt wins and the attempt is removed, while a running attempt without receipt resumes the same operation-keyed call. Exact `FeatureFinalizationAttempt={version:1,attemptId,provisionalRoot,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,stagingRoot,deleteRoot,preparedAt,deadlineAt,phase,nextScheduleSlot,userAInvocationReceipts,userAInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userAPrincipalTerminal,userBInvocationReceipts,userBInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,userBPrincipalTerminal,semanticArtifactSetSha256,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,updatedAt}` uses phase `prepared|cleaning|cleanups_complete|final_staged|final_published|provisional_detached|provisional_removed|complete|failed`; nextScheduleSlot is safe integer 0..6 during cleaning and null otherwise. Histories are empty at prepared, append only in schedule order during cleaning, and are complete thereafter. Selected pairs are null until that principal succeeds and immutable afterward. cleanups_complete and every later success phase require both principal-terminal true and both selected pairs nonnull; semanticArtifactSetSha256 becomes nonnull at final_staged with the eight retained copies, and final hashes become nonnull there. Failed requires both terminal, at least one selected pair null, and all three final hashes null.

- Finalization root states are exact. prepared/cleaning/cleanups_complete require provisionalRoot present/hash-valid and finalRoot/stagingRoot/deleteRoot absent. final_staged requires provisionalRoot and a complete 21-file stagingRoot present, finalRoot/deleteRoot absent, and all three final hashes nonnull. final_published requires provisionalRoot and the 21-file finalRoot present, stagingRoot/deleteRoot absent. provisional_detached requires finalRoot/deleteRoot present and provisionalRoot/stagingRoot absent, with deleteRoot bytes equal the former nine-file provisional root. provisional_removed/complete require only finalRoot present and the other three absent. failed requires provisionalRoot present and the other three absent. Every phase transition fsyncs and readbacks its exact after-state; no phase can be inferred from an unverified path.

- Exact `FeatureFinalizationFailureReceipt={version:1,attemptId,userAInvocationReceipts,userAInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userBInvocationReceipts,userBInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,code:'cleanup_failed',provisionalRoot,finalRootAbsent:true,stagingRootAbsent:true,deleteRootAbsent:true,completedAt}` replaces failed state and preserves the provisional semantic bytes plus diagnostics. Exact `FeatureFinalizationReceipt={version:1,attemptId,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,semanticArtifactSetSha256,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userAArtifactSetSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,userBArtifactSetSha256,provisionalRootAbsent:true,stagingRootAbsent:true,deleteRootAbsent:true,completedAt}` replaces complete state. Final staging contains only the twenty copied subordinate bytes before the report is derived and added; publication atomically renames the 21-file root. Every selected receipt must match principal, operation key, six files, deletion counts, and zero residue before copying. Crashes at every journal/effect/copy/hash/rename/detach/delete boundary resume only from these bytes.

- Descriptor programs and plan members are measurable and bounded. Exact `DescriptorProgramManifest={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,purpose:'filesystem_helper'|'cleaner'|'slugger_notifier',programPath,programByteLength,programSha256,sourcePath,sourceSha256,builderExecutableIdentity,builderCommandPath,builderCommandSha256,createdAt}` points to one immutable raw payload. For filesystem_helper, the builder triple is null, source/program bytes and hashes are equal, the UTF-8 Python lives at `filesystem/helper-program.v1.py`, is at most 64 KiB, and imports only `os,sys,json,hashlib,hmac,struct,time,fcntl`. For cleaner and slugger_notifier, all builder members are nonnull and cross-link the measured stdout-only esbuild command; their UTF-8 CommonJS raw payloads are at most 64 KiB and use only declared Node built-ins. This exact manifest replaces TerminalCleanerProgram and TerminalSluggerNotifierProgram; old manifests cannot classify. `DescriptorAuthorityRef={authoritySha256,path}` is BINARY-authoritySha256 sorted and resolves one complete parent authority. `DescriptorPayloadRef={payloadOrdinal,manifestPath,manifestSha256,blobPath,blobSha256,byteLength}` is contiguous by ordinal. `DescriptorOperationRef={operationOrdinal,requestPath,requestSha256,operationKeySha256}` is contiguous by ordinal. The mutation plan's arrays use exactly these types and set hashes over their complete arrays.

- Plan limits are exact: at most 256 operation refs including one final barrier, 512 parent authorities, 128 payloads, 16 MiB per payload, 64 MiB aggregate payload bytes, and 4 MiB compact canonical plan bytes. There are at most three attempts per filesystem operation, three cleaner attempts, and three preauthorization Slugger attempts; retainedMembers is at most 8192, absence-registry members at most 4096, and every control/history array at most 8192. Equality with a limit is accepted; one over rejects before publication or spawn. Exhaustion files are exact `DescriptorAttemptExhaustion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,priorOutcomeRefs,priorOutcomeSetSha256,code:'attempt_limit_exhausted',terminal:true,completedAt}`, `CleanerAttemptExhaustion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,priorOutcomeRefs,priorOutcomeSetSha256,code:'attempt_limit_exhausted',terminal:true,completedAt}`, and `SluggerAttemptExhaustion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,notificationPath,notificationSha256,priorOutcomeRefs,priorOutcomeSetSha256,code:'attempt_limit_exhausted',terminal:true,completedAt}` at the domain's `attempt-exhaustion.v1.json`. Each is unique, forbids another attempt, and enters the existing terminal failure/repair path; Slugger exhaustion CASes prepared notification to uncertain with the exhaustion result and sends no message.

- The helper launch is literal. `TerminalDescriptorInput` adds exact `{kind:'secret_pipe',role:'channel_token',fd,access:'read_only',byteLength:32,sha256}`; raw token bytes are generated with the OS CSPRNG, written exactly once by the parent to the inherited pipe, then zeroed and never persisted. Filesystem-helper inputs are program fd4, request fd5, payload-or-null fd6, channel-token fd7, run-control fd8; cleaner and notifier use program fd4, channel-token fd5, run-control fd6. Helper executable is the pinned trusted Python at fd3, cwd `/`, closed envNames `['LANG','LC_ALL','NO_COLOR','PATH']` with exact values `C,C,1,/usr/bin:/bin`, and argv `['/dev/fd/3','-I','-S','/dev/fd/4','--request-fd','5','--payload-fd','6','--token-fd','7','--control-fd','8']`. Cleaner and notifier use trusted Node at fd3, cwd `/`, the same closed locale/color environment plus `PATH=/usr/bin:/bin`, and argv respectively `['/dev/fd/3','/dev/fd/4','--token-fd','5','--control-fd','6']`; notifier has no filesystem or Ouro executable path.

- Descriptor control frames are exact and common to helper, cleaner, and notifier. Every frame is four-byte big-endian length followed by compact canonical JSON, maximum 16384 bytes, and contains exact `version:1`. `DescriptorReadyFrame={version:1,type:'ready',purpose,rootIdentitySha256,attemptKey,programSha256,nonce,mac}` uses a 32-byte lowercase-hex nonce and `mac=HMAC-SHA256(channelToken,compactCanonical(all preceding fields))`. Parent replies `DescriptorReadyAck={version:1,type:'ready_ack',readySha256,mac}`. After the immutable authorization receipt is remote-readable, parent sends `DescriptorRunFrame={version:1,type:'run',authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,readySha256,authorizationToken,mac}` and child replies `DescriptorRunAck={version:1,type:'run_ack',authorizationSha256,mac}` before effect. `DescriptorControlError={version:1,type:'error',phase:'token'|'ready'|'authorization'|'effect',code:'invalid_frame'|'invalid_mac'|'unexpected_frame'|'frame_too_large'|'timeout'|'eof'|'internal',mac}` is the sole error frame. attemptKey is `filesystem:<F>:<A>|cleaner:<C>|slugger:<S>`, authorizationToken is a lowercase UUID-v4, and every mac excludes only its own mac field. Exact duplicate ready or run frames before effect start receive the same acknowledgement; mismatched or duplicate run after effect start causes sanitized uncertainty and no second effect. Partial prefix, invalid length/UTF-8/JSON/fields/mac, timeout, or EOF before ready terminalizes pre_ready_dead; after ready and before run it terminalizes pre_authorization_dead; after a valid run frame it terminalizes authorized_uncertain. The child closes the token fd after exactly 32 bytes and the control fd after its terminal result; no token, frame bytes, or free-form error is persisted, only frame hashes and finite diagnostics.

- Filesystem-helper records no longer contain forward references. `DescriptorFilesystemHelperSpawn={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,registrationBundlePath,registrationBundleSha256,gateProcessIdentity,childProcessIdentity,descriptorSpawnReceipts,spawnedAt}` is published after both registered members are identified. `DescriptorFilesystemHelperReady={version:1,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,operationOrdinal,attemptOrdinal,readyFrameSha256,phase:'paused',readyAt}` is then published at `filesystem/operation-<F>/attempt-<A>/ready.v1.json`. Run authorization names the already-published ready pair. Exact replacement `DescriptorFilesystemMutationReceipt={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,attemptOrdinal,requestPath,requestSha256,operationKeySha256,planPath,planSha256,globalLockDirectoryAuthority,helperProgramPath,helperProgramSha256,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,effectDisposition:'executed'|'adopted_after_state'|'recovered_owned_partial',observedBefore,observedAfter,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,exitCode:0,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` uses the held directory flock authority and no created-lock or native process fields. Barrier receipt uses the same schema, effectDisposition adopted_after_state, and observed before/after equal. Every lower receipt and plan ref is validated before it.

- `DescriptorFilesystemHelperOutcome` is exact `{version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,attemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}`. Branches are succeeded with every pair nonnull and result DescriptorFilesystemMutationReceipt; conflict with every pair nonnull and result DescriptorFilesystemConflictResult; spawn_failed with all post-intent pairs null and a finite spawn diagnostic; pre_ready_dead with spawn nonnull, ready/authorization null and finite control diagnostic; pre_authorization_dead with spawn/ready nonnull, authorization null and finite control diagnostic; authorized_uncertain with every pair nonnull and finite uncertainty diagnostic. Only the first four non-success branches authorize A+1 within the three-attempt bound; authorized_uncertain requires locked state reconciliation by A+1 only when the effect boundary can still be classified, otherwise it enters terminal failure. Retained kinds add `descriptor_filesystem_helper_ready|descriptor_filesystem_attempt_exhaustion`; literal paths add ready and exhaustion files.

- The shared lock parent is provisioned before any release identity. Unit0 owns tracked exact anchor `/Users/arimendelow/desk/_meta/release-terminal/lock-root-anchor.v1.json` with compact bytes `{"version":1,"purpose":"global_release_lock_parent"}` and no LF. Provisioning performs concurrent-safe mkdir with mode 0755, verifies effective uid ownership and no group/world write, creates the anchor O_EXCL or adopts byte-identical existing bytes, fsyncs anchor and directory, pushes/readbacks the Desk commit, then records DescriptorRelativeParentAuthority. Concurrent creators converge only on the same directory inode and anchor bytes; symlink, wrong owner/mode, changed inode, nonregular anchor, or different bytes fails closed. Runtime helpers open this existing directory no-follow and flock its held fd. Unit0a red-tests absent, concurrent, partial, replacement, symlink, mode, uid, fsync, push, and readback cases; Unit0b implements provisioning; Unit0c records the frozen authority. No release-root helper provisions this prerequisite.

- Cleaner state is total. `TerminalCleanerSpawn` has no ready forward reference. Exact `TerminalCleanerOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` has branches succeeded (all pairs nonnull; result exact cleanup receipt), spawn_failed (all post-intent and cleanup pairs null; finite spawn diagnostic), pre_ready_dead (spawn nonnull; later pairs null), pre_authorization_dead (spawn/ready nonnull; authorization/cleanup null), and authorized_uncertain (spawn/ready/authorization nonnull; cleanup null). The latter three use the matching finite control/uncertainty result. Only preauthorization branches may advance to C+1 within the bound. Cleaner program manifest, literal launch vector, ready frame, authorization frame, outcome, and exhaustion each have the retained kind and attempt-qualified path already implied by their schema; parser classification is one-to-one.

- Slugger state is total and binds cleanup completion after authorization. `generation` in SluggerNotification is a positive safe integer equal to selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal. `TerminalSluggerSpawn` has no ready forward reference. The launch intent's processBundlePath/hash equals the already registered exact `[gate,child]` bundle; that bundle contains member identities and launch specs but no intent path/hash, so publication is acyclic. Exact `TerminalSluggerAttemptOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationIntentPath,authorizationIntentSha256,authorizationReceiptPath,authorizationReceiptSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` has observed (all pairs nonnull, result TerminalSluggerNotifierResult), authorized_uncertain (all pairs nonnull, finite uncertainty), spawn_failed (all post-intent pairs null), pre_ready_dead (spawn nonnull and later pairs null), and pre_authorization_dead (spawn/ready nonnull and authorization pairs null). Only the three preauthorization branches allow S+1 within the bound while notification remains prepared. Spawn-present/ready-absent and every protocol prefix are therefore representable.

- Slugger's live daemon protocol matches the pinned Ouro source, not the invalid Round 79 wake protocol. The notifier uses only `node:net,node:crypto`; after a valid DescriptorRunFrame it opens `/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock` once, writes exactly compact canonical `{kind:'message.send',from:'ouro-cli',to:'slugger',content:'Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end'}` in one `socket.end(requestBytes)` call with no LF, then reads through server EOF. Exact success response is `{ok:true,message:'queued message '+id,data:{id,queuedAt}}` with no other fields, id matching `^msg-[0-9]{17}$`, and canonical queuedAt. Exact daemon error is `{ok:false,error}` where error is UTF-8 1..1024 bytes but is mapped only to finite diagnostic `daemon_error`; raw error is never persisted. Connect deadline is 5000 ms, total deadline 30000 ms, response cap 65536 bytes, fatal UTF-8 and exactly one no-extra-fields object. Empty/partial/extra/oversized/timeout/socket/error/non-ok/invalid-success responses produce authorized uncertainty. Exact `TerminalSluggerNotifierResult={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationKeySha256,messageReceiptId,queuedAt,messageResponseSha256,outcome:'observed',completedAt}` is the sole success stdout. There is no private.wake request, second socket, LF request, resend, or fallback. Response loss after daemon acceptance is uncertain and permanently forbids another message.

- Slugger publication order is now `prepare/readback program -> freeze outcomes -> prepared notification CAS -> register/spawn -> ready -> authorization intent -> released notification CAS -> authorization receipt -> cleanup completion -> DescriptorRunFrame -> terminal/dead/reaped -> terminal notification CAS`. Cleanup completion therefore binds one nonnull authorization intent/receipt and the released notification hash, and is never published for a replaceable preauthorization attempt. Its exact replacement adds `sluggerAuthorizationIntentPath,sluggerAuthorizationIntentSha256,sluggerAuthorizationReceiptPath,sluggerAuthorizationReceiptSha256,sluggerReleasedNotificationSha256,sluggerPreRunOutcomePath,sluggerPreRunOutcomeSha256` immediately after the ready pair. The pre-run outcome pair is null when the registered child is alive/paused at cleanup completion; if it dies after authorization receipt but before cleanup-completion publication, the pair instead names its authorized_uncertain outcome, cleanup completion is then published, no DescriptorRunFrame is sent, and notification terminalizes uncertain. A death after a live-path cleanup completion likewise becomes authorized_uncertain and final, never S+1. A preauthorization death can select S+1 before any cleanup completion exists.

- Residue classification includes every new byte. `TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind` additionally contains `descriptor_program_manifest|descriptor_program_blob|descriptor_filesystem_helper_ready|descriptor_filesystem_attempt_exhaustion|cleaner_attempt_exhaustion|slugger_attempt_exhaustion|terminal_result`; their unique paths are respectively each declared program manifest/raw path, helper ready/exhaustion, cleaner exhaustion, Slugger exhaustion, and `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-result.v1.json`. The terminal_result member's path/hash byte-equals TerminalFinalResidueProof.terminalResultPath/hash. Ready records and exhaustion records map to exactly one kind; raw helper `.py`, cleaner `.cjs`, and notifier `.cjs` bytes map only descriptor_program_blob. During the final scan terminal result is present and classified; only `terminal-final-residue-proof.v1.json` and `release-terminal-completion.v1.json` are not yet present. Unknown or over-limit members reject.

- Authoritative live-child terminal order is `cleanup effect receipt -> cleaner terminal/dead/reaped -> Slugger prepared/ready -> authorization intent -> released notification CAS -> authorization receipt -> cleanup completion -> notifier run frame -> Slugger terminal/dead/reaped -> terminal notification CAS -> owner registry host_cleaned -> terminal result -> final residue proof -> ReleaseTerminalCompletion`. The sole pre-run-death branch inserts authorized_uncertain outcome before cleanup completion, skips the run frame, then rejoins at terminal notification CAS. Unit29.2.6a/b/c tests every schema field, frame byte, response-loss point, generation prefix, limit equality/overflow, lock provisioning race, exact result branch, residue kind/path, and both final orders. Units30/31 test the literal 32-row registry, checked-to-unchecked fresh-end shopping cycles, retained semantic copies, bounded alternating cleanup retries, complete phase/null matrices, failure receipts, 21-file feature publication, and 35-file aggregate. Units33/37 accept only those final counts and rederive all checks from retained exchange bytes.

### Round 81 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for the feature fixture, live routes, shopping restore captures, cleanup terminality, descriptor authority/program/control schemas, helper and cleaner retry recovery, global-lock anchor publication, pinned Ouro daemon semantics, and final residue completion. They replace every conflicting Round 80 or earlier clause for those subjects. Exact schemas are no-extra-fields compact-canonical JSON in displayed field order with no final LF unless this section explicitly says otherwise.

- The feature fixture uses the canonical controlled course. `FeatureCourse` is literal `main`; `dinner` is invalid and is rejected by fixture, web action, REST, MCP, retained-projection, and derived-check tests. `NeutralMetadataProjection` is exactly `{stepCount:2,sourceDisplayName:'example.com',course:'main',tags:['quick','weeknight']}`. `FeatureMutationIds={adoptionMutationId,restCheckMutationId,restDeleteMutationId,restRestoreMutationId}` contains four named lowercase UUID-v4 values that are pairwise distinct. Every request and response uses the named value for its operation, and retained replay tests reject reuse, swapping, or a response value different from its request.

- Shopping restore never increments the existing target. Web restore request is exactly `{route:'/shopping-list',action:'add',name:'codex-smoke-target',unit:'cup'}`; REST restore body is exactly `{clientMutationId:restRestoreMutationId,name:'codex-smoke-target',unit:'cup'}`; MCP restore arguments are exactly `{name:'codex-smoke-target',unit:'cup'}`. Quantity is omitted on all three restore requests. The existing inactive row's quantity remains 1, its stableId is preserved, it becomes active and unchecked, and it moves to a strictly newer end sortIndex. Check/delete requests do not alter quantity. MCP remove returns only its actual active-list projection: its evidence is row 11's checked target prestate, row 12's absence of stableId from the active list with `[companionStableId]` remaining, and row 13's same stableId reappearing active/unchecked at the fresh end. Web and REST delete responses additionally carry their existing inactive item projection; no inactive item is invented in the MCP response.

- The 32 exchange rows remain contiguous and retain their Round 80 operationIds, ordinals, transports, principals, and expected statuses except for the literal replacements below. Row 1 route is corrected to the existing recipe-page cook anchor `/recipes/{recipeId}#cook`; its request and response are otherwise byte-identical. Rows not listed are byte-identical except for the named mutation-id substitution, course replacement, or restore quantity omission just defined. All browser page captures use paths present in `app/routes.ts` at the integrated HEAD, with the URL fragment handled client-side; no `/recipes/{recipeId}/cook`, `/my-kitchen`, or attempt-keyed cook-session route exists or may be captured.

| # | exact replacement request -> response |
|---|---|
| 1 | `{route:'/recipes/{recipeId}#cook',action:'set_progress',recipeId,activeStepIndex:1,ingredientId,scaleFactor:2}` -> the Round 80 anonymous local-state response unchanged |
| 2 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/cook-sessions',body:{recipeId,mutationId:adoptionMutationId,adoption:{version:1,recipeFingerprint,activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[]}}}` -> `{data:{snapshot:CookFeatureState,adoptionOutcome:'adopted'},localStorageClearedAfterAuthenticatedPersistence:true}` |
| 6 | `{route:'/shopping-list',action:'add',name:'codex-smoke-target',unit:'cup'}` -> `{created:false,updated:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,list:ShoppingListProjection}` |
| 8 | `{method:'PATCH',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items/{stableId}',body:{clientMutationId:restCheckMutationId,checked:true}}` -> `{data:{item:ShoppingItemProjection,mutation:{clientMutationId:restCheckMutationId,replayed:false}},list:ShoppingListProjection}` |
| 9 | `{method:'DELETE',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items/{stableId}',body:{clientMutationId:restDeleteMutationId}}` -> `{data:{removed:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,mutation:{clientMutationId:restDeleteMutationId,replayed:false}},list:ShoppingListProjection}` |
| 10 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/v1/shopping-list/items',body:{clientMutationId:restRestoreMutationId,name:'codex-smoke-target',unit:'cup'}}` -> `{data:{created:false,updated:true,item:ShoppingItemProjection,mutation:{clientMutationId:restRestoreMutationId,replayed:false}},list:ShoppingListProjection}` |
| 12 | `{tool:'remove_shopping_list_item',arguments:{itemId:stableId}}` -> `{shoppingList:ShoppingListProjection}` where items are exactly `[companionStableId]`; inactivity is derived from the row 11/12/13 transition, not a hidden response member |
| 13 | `{tool:'add_shopping_list_item',arguments:{name:'codex-smoke-target',unit:'cup'}}` -> `{created:0,updated:1,shoppingList:ShoppingListProjection}` |
| 16 | `{method:'GET',path:'/saved-recipes?q='}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 17 | `{method:'GET',path:'/saved-recipes?q=codex-smoke-feature-recipe'}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 18 | `{method:'GET',path:'/saved-recipes?q='}` -> `{recipeIds:[]}` for user_b |
| 20 | `{route:'/recipes/{recipeId}/edit',action:'set_taxonomy',recipeId,course:'main',tags:['quick','weeknight']}` -> `{recipeId,metadata:NeutralMetadataProjection}` |
| 21 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-recipes?course=main&tag=quick'}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 22 | `{method:'GET',path:'/my-recipes?q=weeknight'}` -> `{recipeIds:[recipeId]}` |
| 27 | `{method:'GET',path:'/'}` -> `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[attemptId]}` for user_a |
| 28 | `{method:'GET',path:'/'}` -> `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[]}` for user_b |
| 29 | `{method:'GET',path:'/'}` -> `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[]}` for anonymous with status 200 |
| 30 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{snapshot:CookFeatureState}` for user_a, using the canonical CookWire success projection |
| 31 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{code:'not_found'}` for user_b, sanitized from the canonical CookWire error envelope |
| 32 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{code:'authentication_required'}` for anonymous, sanitized from the canonical CookWire error envelope |

- Row 30 snapshot byte-equals row 3's canonical owner snapshot and includes attemptId. Rows 31 and 32 retain status 404 and 401. Row 29 changes only to status 200 and the stated public-home projection. SavedRecipe privacy is demonstrated by the same `/saved-recipes` loader under two principals; cook-history privacy is demonstrated by the same `/` and recipe-keyed cook-session resources under all three principals. Unit30 red tests every replaced literal and explicitly rejects `/my-kitchen`, `/api/cook-sessions/attempts/*`, course `dinner`, quantity-bearing restore requests, duplicate mutation IDs, and an MCP inactive-item projection.

- Cleanup invocation receipts are always real invocation receipts. `FeatureCleanupInvocationAttempt` matrices are prepared/null/0/null, running/non-null/1/null, and terminal/null/1/result; every immutable `FeatureCleanupInvocationReceipt` has `portCallOrdinal:1` and names one actual operation-keyed port call. The fixed schedule remains `A1,B1,A2,B2,A3,B3`; no synthetic invocation intent, attempt, receipt, ordinal zero, or fourth invocation exists.

- Principal terminality has its own immutable record at `<controlRoot>/principal-user-a/principal-terminal.v1.json` or the user-b equivalent. Exact `FeatureCleanupPrincipalTerminalReceipt={version:1,attemptId,principal,disposition,triggerReceiptPath,triggerReceiptSha256,invocationReceipts,invocationReceiptSetSha256,selectedSuccessPath,selectedSuccessSha256,terminalAt}` uses disposition `succeeded|permanent_failure|attempt_limit_exhausted|deadline_exceeded`. Histories are the complete contiguous real invocation receipts and hash over their complete refs. `succeeded` names its successful trigger and selected pair; `permanent_failure` names its nonretryable trigger and has a null selected pair; `attempt_limit_exhausted` names the third retryable trigger and has a null selected pair; `deadline_exceeded` names the latest real trigger when one exists or has a null trigger pair when the deadline preceded the principal's first slot, and has a null selected pair. Every nonnull trigger is a member of invocationReceipts. Once published/read back, the terminal receipt is immutable and causes all later slots for that principal to be skipped.

- `FeatureFinalizationAttempt` replaces the two terminal booleans with `userAPrincipalTerminalPath,userAPrincipalTerminalSha256,userBPrincipalTerminalPath,userBPrincipalTerminalSha256`; each pair is null until its immutable principal record exists and is nonnull thereafter. It retains both complete invocation histories/set hashes and both selected-success pairs. cleanups_complete and every later successful phase require both terminal pairs and both selected pairs nonnull. failed requires both terminal pairs and at least one selected pair null. Exact replacement `FeatureFinalizationFailureReceipt={version:1,attemptId,userAInvocationReceipts,userAInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userAPrincipalTerminalPath,userAPrincipalTerminalSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userBInvocationReceipts,userBInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userBPrincipalTerminalPath,userBPrincipalTerminalSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,code:'cleanup_failed',provisionalRoot,finalRootAbsent:true,stagingRootAbsent:true,deleteRootAbsent:true,completedAt}` preserves both full histories and terminal decisions. Exact replacement `FeatureFinalizationReceipt={version:1,attemptId,provisionalReportSha256,provisionalArtifactSetSha256,finalRoot,semanticArtifactSetSha256,finalReportSha256,finalArtifactSetSha256,userAInvocationReceipts,userAInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userAPrincipalTerminalPath,userAPrincipalTerminalSha256,userASelectedSuccessPath,userASelectedSuccessSha256,userAArtifactSetSha256,userBInvocationReceipts,userBInvocationReceiptSetSha256,userBPrincipalTerminalPath,userBPrincipalTerminalSha256,userBSelectedSuccessPath,userBSelectedSuccessSha256,userBArtifactSetSha256,provisionalRootAbsent:true,stagingRootAbsent:true,deleteRootAbsent:true,completedAt}` preserves the same histories and terminal records on success. During execution, each invoked slot has its immutable intent plus exactly one mutable attempt or immutable receipt; receipt publication removes/fsyncs the attempt. At either finalization terminal state, every invoked slot has exactly its intent and receipt, every mutable attempt is absent, each principal has exactly one terminal receipt, and exactly one finalization success or failure receipt exists. Unused skipped slots have no files. No extra invocation, terminal, or finalization file is legal. The 21-file feature output and 35-file aggregate counts do not change because principal/finalization control evidence remains outside finalRoot.

- Descriptor mutation plans embed complete parent authorities and have no unresolved path grammar. `DescriptorAuthorityRef` is removed. The exact plan member is the already-defined complete `DescriptorRelativeParentAuthority`; `parentAuthorities` is BINARY sorted by each authority's `authoritySha256`, contains no duplicate authority hash or `(device,inode)` alias with conflicting identity, and `parentAuthoritySetSha256` hashes the complete sorted array. Each filesystem request's parent-authority hash resolves exactly one member. There is no separate authority artifact, path, retained kind, or path-based lookup.

- Descriptor program manifests and raw programs have one literal namespace. The complete mapping is:

| purpose | manifest path | raw program path |
|---|---|---|
| `filesystem_helper` | `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/helper-program.v1.json` | `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/helper-program.v1.py` |
| `cleaner` | `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/cleaner-program.v1.json` | `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/cleaner-program.v1.cjs` |
| `slugger_notifier` | `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/notifier-program.v1.json` | `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/notifier-program.v1.cjs` |

- `DescriptorProgramManifest.programPath/programSha256` always names the raw program while every schema field named `helperProgramPath`, `cleanerProgramPath`, or `notifierProgramPath` names the JSON manifest and its SHA unless the field is explicitly suffixed `Raw`. Publication writes, fdatasyncs, renames, parent-fsyncs, pushes, and readbacks raw bytes first and manifest second. A raw-only crash validates the exact operation-keyed source/builder authority and either publishes the matching manifest or blocks changed/foreign bytes; a manifest without its exact raw program blocks. The final retained kind for every JSON file is `descriptor_program_manifest` and for every `.py`/`.cjs` file is `descriptor_program_blob`; `descriptor_payload_blob` is reserved for non-program mutation payloads.

- The exact final `TerminalRetainedEvidenceMember.kind` union is `root_owner_registry|owner_selection_intent|owner_selection|owner_selection_release|owner_probe|owner_rescan|effect_proof|effect_result|archive_validation_control|track_linearization|track_fence|track_scan_stale|track_conflict|process_authority|process_directory_intent|process_directory|process_spawn_intent|process_spawn|process_terminal|registration_root_intent|registration_root_receipt|owner_preselection_settlement_intent|owner_preselection_settlement|owner_validator_run_authorization|owner_selection_history|terminal_control_attempt|terminal_successful_result|terminal_failure_snapshot|terminal_failure_evidence|terminal_failure_disposition|terminal_progress|terminal_wait|terminal_blocker|terminal_resolution|descriptor_secret_channel|cleaner_intent|cleaner_spawn|cleaner_ready|cleaner_authorization|cleaner_run_dispatch_intent|cleaner_run_ack|cleaner_transfer_intent|cleaner_transfer|cleaner_transfer_cancellation|cleaner_generation_outcome|cleaner_receipt|descriptor_program_manifest|descriptor_program_blob|descriptor_payload_manifest|descriptor_payload_blob|descriptor_filesystem_request|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_plan|descriptor_filesystem_helper_intent|descriptor_filesystem_helper_spawn|descriptor_filesystem_helper_ready|descriptor_filesystem_helper_authorization|descriptor_filesystem_helper_run_dispatch_intent|descriptor_filesystem_helper_run_ack|descriptor_filesystem_helper_outcome|descriptor_filesystem_mutation_receipt|descriptor_filesystem_attempt_exhaustion|cleaner_attempt_exhaustion|slugger_intent|slugger_spawn|slugger_ready|slugger_attempt_outcome|slugger_run_authorization|slugger_run_dispatch_intent|slugger_run_ack|slugger_notification|slugger_attempt_exhaustion|terminal_absence_registry|cleanup_completion|terminal_result`. Every kind has one previously defined path or a literal Round 81 path; no obsolete `terminal_cleaner_program`, `slugger_notifier_program`, helper-image, or native-effect kind is legal.

- Control secrets and frames use byte-precise representations. channelToken is exactly 32 raw CSPRNG bytes; its persisted SHA is 64 lowercase hex. `DescriptorReadyFrame.nonce` is exactly 32 raw CSPRNG bytes encoded as 64 lowercase hex. A frame hash covers the complete four-byte big-endian length prefix followed by compact-canonical payload bytes. A frame MAC is lowercase-hex HMAC-SHA256 over the compact-canonical payload object with only its `mac` field omitted. readySha256 and runAckFrameSha256 use complete framed bytes. The child must read exactly 32 token bytes before parsing control; EOF after 0..31 bytes emits no frame, exits with the finite local partial-token status, and parent records `partial_token`. `DescriptorControlError.phase` is only `ready|authorization|effect`; every error frame is authenticated and therefore impossible before the token is complete.

- Exact finite diagnostics are `TerminalProcessDiagnostic={kind:'spawn',code:'spawn_error'|'registration_error'|'partial_spawn',detailSha256}|{kind:'control',phase:'token'|'ready'|'authorization'|'run_ack',code:'invalid_frame'|'invalid_mac'|'unexpected_frame'|'frame_too_large'|'timeout'|'eof'|'partial_token',detailSha256}|{kind:'uncertainty',code:'run_delivery_unknown'|'run_ack_lost'|'effect_result_lost'|'effect_state_unclassifiable'|'daemon_response_unknown',detailSha256}`. The only detail preimage is exact `TerminalProcessDiagnosticDetail={version:1,attemptKey,kind,phase,code,observedByteCount}`: spawn uses phase null and observedByteCount 0; control uses its displayed phase and a safe integer 0..16388; uncertainty uses phase `dispatch|ack|effect|daemon` and a safe integer 0..65536. kind/code cross-equal the outer diagnostic and are rejected unless the selected branch admits that pair. detailSha256 hashes these complete compact-canonical bytes. No raw OS, socket, parser, or daemon text is retained or hashed. Every outcome.result is either its exact success/conflict receipt or this exact union.

- Run delivery is explicit and immutable. After authorization readback and before writing any run frame, the parent publishes/readbacks exact `DescriptorRunDispatchIntent={version:1,purpose,rootIdentitySha256,attemptKey,intentPath,intentSha256,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,runFrameSha256,deliveryOrdinal:1,createdAt}`. Filesystem helper paths are `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/filesystem/operation-<F>/attempt-<A>/run-dispatch-intent.v1.json` and `run-ack.v1.json`; cleaner paths are `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/bootstrap-cleanup/generations/attempt-<C>-run-dispatch-intent.v1.json` and `attempt-<C>-run-ack.v1.json`; Slugger paths are `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-run-dispatch-intent.v1.json` and `attempt-<S>-run-ack.v1.json`. The parent sends no run byte before dispatch-intent readback. After a complete valid run-ack frame it publishes exact `DescriptorRunAckReceipt={version:1,runDispatchIntentPath,runDispatchIntentSha256,runAckFrameSha256,acknowledgedAt}` at the corresponding literal ack path. Response loss adopts only byte-identical fixed-path artifacts. `authorized_not_run` means authorization nonnull, dispatch and ack null, and the child is proven dead/reaped before a dispatch intent existed; it permits a fresh counted attempt. `authorized_uncertain` means dispatch nonnull and ack nullable; it never claims the effect did or did not run.

- Helper, cleaner, and Slugger outcomes add `runDispatchIntentPath,runDispatchIntentSha256,runAckPath,runAckSha256` immediately after their authorization pair. Every branch has a nonnull `terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256`, including spawn_failed; terminal command bytes classify the registered owner as terminal/dead/reaped even when no child spawned. Branch nullability is exact: spawn_failed has all spawn-through-ack pairs null; pre_ready_dead has spawn nonnull and later pairs null; pre_authorization_dead has spawn/ready nonnull and authorization-through-ack null; authorized_not_run has spawn/ready/authorization nonnull and dispatch/ack null; authorized_uncertain has spawn/ready/authorization/dispatch nonnull and ack nullable; succeeded/observed/conflict has every applicable pair nonnull. Cleanup or notification result pairs are nonnull only for their successful/observed branch. `all post-intent pairs null` never includes the terminal-command pair.

- Helper uncertainty has a closed locked-state decision. A fresh counted A+1 is permitted within the three-attempt bound after authorized_uncertain only after the prior owner is terminal/dead/reaped and the global lock is held. Classification `before` executes the same operation once; `owned_partial` completes/reverses only the operation-keyed owned partial then executes or adopts the prescribed after-state; `after` emits an adopted-after-state receipt with no effect; `conflict` emits terminal conflict; `unclassifiable` emits terminal effect_state_unclassifiable and forbids another effect. No completed after-state is repeated. The third attempt's terminal outcome creates DescriptorAttemptExhaustion only when another attempt would otherwise be needed.

- Cleaner uncertainty is resumable because the cleaner coordinates the immutable mutation plan rather than owning an indivisible side effect. C+1 is legal within three attempts after the prior cleaner is terminal/dead/reaped and the existing ownership-transfer receipt moves ownership. It revalidates the plan and resumes the longest valid contiguous helper-receipt prefix; completed filesystem operations never repeat. An existing final cleanup receipt is adopted as success. An unclassifiable helper partial follows the helper terminal rules. Existing `TerminalBootstrapCleanupOwnershipTransfer` and generation-outcome schemas bind the exact previous outcome and attempt hash; release authorization for C+1 is impossible before transfer readback. A third retryable cleaner terminal outcome creates CleanerAttemptExhaustion and enters terminal repair.

- Slugger pre-dispatch recovery is distinct from message uncertainty. An `authorized_not_run` Slugger attempt may advance to S+1 within the three-attempt bound because no dispatch intent existed. Once its run-dispatch intent exists, any unobserved terminal result is authorized_uncertain, permanently forbids another message, and terminalizes notification uncertain. observed requires dispatch and ack nonnull plus exact notifier success. This replaces every clause that allowed authorization receipt alone to imply possible message delivery.

- Lock-root anchor publication is crash recoverable. The final anchor remains `/Users/arimendelow/desk/_meta/release-terminal/lock-root-anchor.v1.json` with exact no-LF bytes `{"version":1,"purpose":"global_release_lock_parent"}`. A creator writes a unique same-directory `.lock-root-anchor.<32-lowercase-hex>.tmp` with O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW mode 0600, writes exact bytes, fdatasyncs, closes, lstat-validates, then hard-links it to the final basename with no replacement. EEXIST validates the final inode/bytes. It fsyncs the directory, unlinks exact protocol temps, fsyncs again, then pushes/readbacks. When final is absent, recovery may remove a regular same-uid mode-0600 protocol temp whose bytes are an exact prefix (including empty) of the anchor, rebuild an exact temp, and hard-link it; one or more complete exact temps converge by lexicographically smallest basename. A symlink, wrong uid/mode/type, oversized bytes, or non-prefix content blocks. When final exists exact, complete or prefix protocol temps may be removed; changed final blocks. Push/readback response loss reconciles by fetched remote ref, commit, tree entry, and exact blob hash before another commit. Unit0 tests interruption after every write/link/fsync/unlink/commit/push boundary and concurrent creators.

- The notifier pins the installed Ouro daemon success path rather than claiming the mutable filesystem fallback. Authority is package `@ouro.bot/cli@0.1.0-alpha.721` with SHA-256 package.json `abfb024a6373cf2277579ef31945418140d84d103ab8c4b70f0ffd1a77e221be`, socket-client.js `c8beafd123d5821a285f89014f8b0bc5aa25a356e1214d6fdccfdb093d3a0a96`, cli-exec.js `90822583dee67cea347cc0efaec10e0e7c4d3426b6deb437b40201c29a795e20`, and message-router.js `552e007e556e91472337c5291f9590bf52273dafbe723af3bd8a69b761e9060d`. Unit29.2.6 verifies those installed source bytes before program construction; mismatch is a capability blocker and sends nothing. Selected semantics are named `pinned_daemon_delivery`: they reproduce the successful daemon message and best-effort wake exchanges, but intentionally fail closed instead of using the CLI's mutable filesystem fallback because that fallback cannot be operation-owned by release evidence.

- For message delivery the notifier opens `/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock`, writes `JSON.stringify({kind:'message.send',from:'ouro-cli',to:'slugger',content:'Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end'})+'\n'` without half-closing, keeps the socket open, accumulates at most 65536 response bytes through server EOF, trims only leading/trailing JSON whitespace for parsing, and requires exactly one JSON object, with 5000 ms connect and 30000 ms total deadline. Success is exact `{ok:true,message:'queued message '+messageReceiptId,data:{id:messageReceiptId,queuedAt}}`, messageReceiptId matches `^msg-[0-9]{17}(?:-[2-9][0-9]*)?$`, queuedAt is canonical, and messageResponseSha256 hashes the complete untrimmed response bytes. After observed success only, a second socket best-effort writes `JSON.stringify({kind:'private.wake',agent:'slugger',reason:'operator CLI message',triggerSource:'operator-cli',budgetClass:'interactive',idempotencyKey:'cli:message:slugger:'+messageReceiptId,originRefs:[{kind:'cli-command',id:'ouro msg'},{kind:'daemon-receipt',id:messageReceiptId}]})+'\n'` without half-closing and uses the same EOF, cap, trim, and one-object parser. Wake acceptance is any parsed no-array object with `ok:true`; any wake connection, timeout, empty/oversized/invalid parse, or non-ok result is `wakeDisposition:'failed'` but does not change observed message delivery. Exact `TerminalSluggerNotifierResult={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationKeySha256,deliverySemantics:'pinned_daemon_delivery',messageReceiptId,queuedAt,messageResponseSha256,wakeDisposition:'accepted'|'failed',wakeResponseSha256,outcome:'observed',completedAt}` requires wakeResponseSha256 nonnull only for accepted and hashes the complete untrimmed accepted wake response. Message failure or response loss remains authorized uncertainty; no fallback or resend occurs.

- Final proof filenames are canonical and have one recovery state machine. The result is `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-result.v1.json`, proof is `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-residue-proof.v1.json`, and completion is `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/terminal-completion.v1.json`. `TerminalFinalResidueProof` is the existing exact proof schema at the canonical proof path. `ReleaseTerminalCompletion` is the existing exact completion schema at the canonical completion path. During an initial final scan, result is classified as terminal_result and proof/completion must be absent. If only result exists after a crash, the full scan resumes and publishes proof then completion. If proof exists and completion is absent, recovery hash-validates the proof, rescans the current closure while treating the exact proof as the sole allowed post-scan addition, rederives every retained/absence set, and publishes completion only if they byte-equal the proof. If completion exists, recovery validates its complete reference closure and returns without mutation. A changed/extra proof, alternate `terminal-final-residue-proof.v1.json`, alternate `release-terminal-completion.v1.json`, or unknown file blocks. Publication response loss adopts exact fixed-path bytes.

- Final terminal order is `cleanup effect receipt -> cleaner terminal/dead/reaped -> Slugger prepared/ready/authorization -> cleanup completion -> Slugger run-dispatch intent -> run ack or uncertainty -> Slugger terminal/dead/reaped -> terminal notification CAS -> owner registry host_cleaned -> terminal result -> terminal-residue-proof -> terminal-completion`. The authorized-not-run branches may choose a fresh counted generation before cleanup completion for Slugger or before later cleaner work; no cleanup completion binds a replaceable Slugger generation. Unit29.2.6a/b/c red/green/verify every exact schema, nullability branch, finite diagnostic, token length, frame/MAC bytes, dispatch boundary, helper classification, cleaner prefix, lock temp state, pinned Ouro request/wake byte, program path/kind, retained-union member, and proof recovery state. Units30/31 red/green/verify the literal Round 81 32-row registry, all four mutation IDs, canonical course, route existence, MCP absence proof, every one-through-six real-invocation schedule prefix, two principal-terminal receipts, retained histories, 21-file final feature output, and 35-file aggregate.

### Round 82 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for scale fixtures, terminal cook-history proof, process/control outcomes, retained diagnostic and daemon-response preimages, Slugger selection/retry ordering, Ouro peer authority, receipt IDs, and completed residue recovery. They replace every conflicting Round 81 or earlier clause for those subjects. Exact schemas are no-extra-fields compact-canonical JSON in displayed field order with no final LF unless stated otherwise.

- REST and MCP scale fixtures use their implemented names. Row 24 request is exactly `{method:'GET',path:'/api/v1/recipes/{recipeId}?scaleFactor=2'}`. Row 26 request is exactly `{tool:'get_recipe',arguments:{id:recipeId,scaleFactor:2}}`. Their responses and neutral scale equality remain unchanged. Unit30 rejects query `scale`, MCP argument `recipeId`, missing scaleFactor, aliases, and extra fields.

- Terminal history is distinct from active continuation. The authenticated `/` loader queries private `CookSessionIndex` rows for its own user only: ACTIVE rows feed `continueCookingAttemptIds`; COMPLETED/ABANDONED rows feed `cookHistoryAttemptIds`, sorted by `(updatedAt DESC,attemptId BINARY ASC)` and capped at 20 after exact owner filtering. Anonymous `/` returns both arrays empty without a D1 owner query. Other-user rows never appear. The recipe-keyed canonical DO GET remains the only snapshot route; no attempt-keyed route is added. The existing home layout renders a compact `Cook history` section below active continuation without a new top-level navigation destination.

- `FeatureMutationIds` adds `historyCompleteMutationId`; all five named UUID-v4 values are pairwise distinct and every request/response cross-equals its named value. Exact `CookFeatureTerminalState={attemptId,recipeId,revision:1,status:'COMPLETED',activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[],completedAtPresent:true}` preserves the row 2 attempt and progress. Exact `FeatureHistoryProjectionBarrier={attemptId,status:'COMPLETED',pollCount,observedProjectionSeq,observedAt}` has pollCount 1..60, nonnegative safe observedProjectionSeq, canonical observedAt, and is produced by Unit31's bounded parameterized D1 read after row 27 and before row 28; it stores no title/user/email. Polls use one operation-keyed query, 1000 ms spacing, 60000 ms deadline, and the existing deployment identity. Unit30 injects the same typed barrier. A missing, wrong-owner, ACTIVE, mismatched-attempt, duplicate, or timed-out row fails before history capture.

- The feature registry is now exactly 33 contiguous exchanges. Rows 1-26 retain their Round 81 definitions except the scale replacements above. Rows 27-33 are:

| # | kind / operationId | transport / principal / status | exact request -> exact response |
|---|---|---|---|
| 27 | `history_owner_complete` / `history.owner.complete` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}/complete',body:{attemptId,baseRevision:0,mutationId:historyCompleteMutationId}}` -> `{snapshot:CookFeatureTerminalState}` |
| 28 | `history_owner_list` / `history.owner.list` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/'}` -> `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[],cookHistoryAttemptIds:[attemptId]}` after the projection barrier |
| 29 | `history_other_list` / `history.other.list` | `web` / `user_b` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/'}` -> `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[],cookHistoryAttemptIds:[]}` |
| 30 | `history_anonymous_list` / `history.anonymous.list` | `web` / `anonymous` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/'}` -> `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[],cookHistoryAttemptIds:[]}` |
| 31 | `history_owner_snapshot` / `history.owner.snapshot` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{snapshot:CookFeatureTerminalState}` |
| 32 | `history_other_snapshot` / `history.other.snapshot` | `web` / `user_b` / 404 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{code:'not_found'}` |
| 33 | `history_anonymous_snapshot` / `history.anonymous.snapshot` | `web` / `anonymous` / 401 | `{method:'GET',path:'/api/cook-sessions/recipes/{recipeId}'}` -> `{code:'authentication_required'}` |

- `cook-history-privacy.json` retains the projection barrier and these seven exchanges. Its derived check is exactly `{name:'cook_history_privacy',attemptId,terminalStatus:'COMPLETED',ownerHistoryVisible:true,ownerTerminalSnapshotVisible:true,otherHistoryCount:0,anonymousHistoryCount:0,otherSnapshotStatus:404,anonymousSnapshotStatus:401}`. Projection exchange arrays are adoption 1-3, shopping 4-14, saved 15-19, tags 20-22, REST 23-24, MCP 25-26, history 27-33. The operation identity index contains all 33 unique exchange refs plus two boundary-health refs. The eight semantic files, 21-file feature output, and 35-file aggregate do not change. Unit30 mutates every terminal/barrier/list/snapshot field and rejects an ACTIVE-only proof mislabeled as history.

- The complete authenticated error-frame union is exact `DescriptorControlError={version:1,type:'error',phase:'ready'|'authorization',code:'invalid_frame'|'invalid_mac'|'unexpected_frame'|'frame_too_large'|'timeout'|'eof',mac}`. A token EOF/partial token emits no frame. No error frame admits phase token/run_ack/effect or code partial_token/internal. Deterministic post-run success/conflict uses its typed result; a lost/indeterminate post-run boundary uses the uncertainty outcome below.

- Every process diagnostic has a retained preimage. Exact `TerminalProcessDiagnostic={kind:'spawn',code:'spawn_error'|'registration_error'|'partial_spawn',detailPath,detailSha256}|{kind:'control',phase:'token'|'ready'|'authorization'|'run_ack',code:'invalid_frame'|'invalid_mac'|'unexpected_frame'|'frame_too_large'|'timeout'|'eof'|'partial_token',detailPath,detailSha256}|{kind:'uncertainty',code:'run_delivery_unknown'|'run_ack_lost'|'effect_result_lost'|'effect_state_unclassifiable'|'daemon_response_unknown',detailPath,detailSha256}` names exact `TerminalProcessDiagnosticDetail={version:1,attemptKey,kind,phase,code,observedByteCount}`. Spawn detail uses phase null/count 0; control uses the displayed phase/count 0..16388; uncertainty uses phase `dispatch|ack|effect|daemon`/count 0..65536. Outer discriminants cross-equal detail. Literal paths are helper `filesystem/operation-<F>/attempt-<A>/diagnostic.v1.json`, cleaner `bootstrap-cleanup/generations/attempt-<C>-diagnostic.v1.json`, and Slugger `slugger/attempt-<S>-diagnostic.v1.json`, all below `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/`. The detail is pushed/read back before its outcome and classifies as retained kind `terminal_process_diagnostic`; detailSha256 is recomputed from retained bytes. No raw diagnostic text is retained or hashed.

- Final exact helper outcome is `DescriptorFilesystemHelperOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationOrdinal,attemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,runDispatchIntentPath,runDispatchIntentSha256,runAckPath,runAckSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` with outcome `succeeded|conflict|spawn_failed|pre_ready_dead|pre_authorization_dead|authorized_not_run|authorized_uncertain`.
