Final exact cleaner outcome is `TerminalCleanerOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationPath,authorizationSha256,runDispatchIntentPath,runDispatchIntentSha256,runAckPath,runAckSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` with outcome `succeeded|spawn_failed|pre_ready_dead|pre_authorization_dead|authorized_not_run|authorized_uncertain`. Final exact Slugger outcome is `TerminalSluggerAttemptOutcome={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,intentPath,intentSha256,outcome,spawnPath,spawnSha256,readyPath,readySha256,authorizationIntentPath,authorizationIntentSha256,authorizationReceiptPath,authorizationReceiptSha256,runDispatchIntentPath,runDispatchIntentSha256,runAckPath,runAckSha256,result,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,dead:true,reaped:true,completedAt}` with outcome `observed|spawn_failed|pre_ready_dead|pre_authorization_dead|authorized_not_run|authorized_uncertain`. These displayed orders and domains are sole authority; Slugger's authorization intent and receipt pairs always precede dispatch.

- Outcome nullability and diagnostics are total. Every branch has a nonnull terminal-command pair. `spawn_failed` has spawn through ack null and spawn diagnostic. `pre_ready_dead` has spawn nonnull, later pairs null, and token/ready control diagnostic. `pre_authorization_dead` has spawn/ready nonnull, authorization through ack null, and authorization control diagnostic. `authorized_not_run` has spawn/ready/authorization nonnull, dispatch/ack null, and authorization `eof|timeout` control diagnostic after dead/reaped proof. `authorized_uncertain` has dispatch nonnull; ack null maps to `run_delivery_unknown|run_ack_lost`, ack nonnull maps to `effect_result_lost|effect_state_unclassifiable`, and Slugger daemon response ambiguity maps to `daemon_response_unknown`. Helper `succeeded|conflict`, cleaner `succeeded`, and Slugger `observed` have every prefix pair nonnull and their exact typed result; only cleaner succeeded has cleanup receipt nonnull. No other outcome, diagnostic pair, null matrix, or field order is legal.

- The Slugger notification no longer releases at authorization. Final exact `SluggerNotification={version:1,task,rootMergeSha,deployedValidatorMergeSha,message,ownershipGeneration:1,writerId,phase,terminalReason,priorOutcomePaths,priorOutcomeSetSha256,selectedProcessAttemptOrdinal,notifierProgramPath,notifierProgramSha256,generation,authorizationIntentPath,authorizationIntentSha256,authorizationReceiptPath,authorizationReceiptSha256,runDispatchIntentPath,runDispatchIntentSha256,resultPath,resultSha256,completedAt,updatedAt}` uses phase `prepared|released|observed|uncertain`. prepared has terminalReason and every selection-through-result member null. Each spawn/pre-ready/pre-authorization/authorized-not-run outcome appends to priorOutcomePaths while remaining prepared, so S+1 is legal within the three-attempt bound. After an authorization receipt and live-child proof, the immutable run-dispatch intent is pushed/read back. One CAS then changes prepared to released, copies the selected attempt, manifest, generation, authorization intent/receipt, and dispatch pairs, and leaves result/completed null. observed preserves them and adds the observed outcome terminal-command result. uncertain has terminalReason `selected_uncertain|attempt_limit_exhausted`: selected_uncertain preserves every selected pair and names the selected uncertain outcome; attempt_limit_exhausted leaves selection-through-dispatch null and names SluggerAttemptExhaustion. Every other phase has terminalReason null.

- Slugger crash recovery has one point of no replacement. Authorization-only death has no dispatch intent, writes authorized_not_run, appends it to prepared notification, and may select S+1. Once dispatch intent exists, no S+1 is legal. If the selected child is live, response-loss-safe prepared-to-released CAS precedes cleanup completion. If it is dead before that CAS, Work Merger writes authorized_uncertain and CASes prepared directly to uncertain selected_uncertain; cleanup completion records that pre-run terminal outcome and sends no frame. CAS response loss fetches and adopts only the exact expected after-image. There is no released-to-prepared transition.

- Final exact cleanup completion is `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,preliminaryResultPath,preliminaryResultSha256,cleanerProgramPath,cleanerProgramSha256,cleanerPriorOutcomePaths,cleanerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,successfulCleanerProcessAttemptOrdinal,cleanerOutcomePath,cleanerOutcomeSha256,cleanupReceiptPath,cleanupReceiptSha256,descriptorMutationPlanPath,descriptorMutationPlanSha256,finalFilesystemBarrierPath,finalFilesystemBarrierSha256,cleanerTerminalCommandPath,cleanerTerminalCommandSha256,sluggerPriorOutcomePaths,sluggerPriorOutcomeSetSha256,sluggerLaunchIntentPath,sluggerLaunchIntentSha256,sluggerSpawnPath,sluggerSpawnSha256,sluggerNotifierProgramPath,sluggerNotifierProgramSha256,sluggerReadyPath,sluggerReadySha256,sluggerAuthorizationIntentPath,sluggerAuthorizationIntentSha256,sluggerAuthorizationReceiptPath,sluggerAuthorizationReceiptSha256,sluggerRunDispatchIntentPath,sluggerRunDispatchIntentSha256,sluggerNotificationPath,sluggerNotificationSha256,sluggerPreRunOutcomePath,sluggerPreRunOutcomeSha256,completedAt}`. Released live-child state requires released notification and null pre-run outcome; uncertain pre-run state requires selected_uncertain notification, its exact outcome pair, dead/reaped child, and no run ack/message. Only released state sends the run frame after cleanup-completion readback.

- Ouro authority pins every relied-on client/router/server entry. In addition to the Round 81 hashes, daemon.js is `d72735805726612ab539597d8c4f91024107f0a18b37fb04f53d0e01b1d43dd5` and daemon-entry.js is `5b465f8b5a3d8e82659b5fec7dc491f7ab3d93f1e7b9e6cdbf6bd237964bd405`. The resolved package root is exactly `/Users/arimendelow/.ouro-cli/versions/0.1.0-alpha.721/node_modules/@ouro.bot/cli`; `CurrentVersion` is not execution authority. Exact `OuroPackageFile={path,sha256,device,inode,mode,uid,byteLength,mtimeMs}` requires lowercase hash, nonnegative identities/length/safe-integer milliseconds, regular no-follow same-uid file, and no group/world write. Exact `OuroSocketIdentity={device,inode,mode,uid,gid,type:'unix_socket'}` uses nonnegative integer identities. Exact `OuroDaemonPeerAuthority={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,packageVersion,packageRoot,packageFiles,packageFileSetSha256,socketPath,socketIdentity,daemonPid,daemonUid,daemonStartedAt,daemonEntryPath,daemonEntrySha256,processProbePath,processProbeSha256,socketProbePath,socketProbeSha256,createdAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/ouro-daemon-peer-authority.v1.json`. packageFiles is the BINARY-path-sorted six exact OuroPackageFile rows for package.json, socket-client.js, cli-exec.js, message-router.js, daemon.js, and daemon-entry.js; packageFileSetSha256 hashes its complete array. socketIdentity is exact OuroSocketIdentity. Every component remains byte/identity-equal through notification, and daemonStartedAt is later than every file mtime.

- Peer validation is OS-bound before any message. socketIdentity is exact same-uid UNIX-socket `(device,inode,mode,uid,gid)` for `/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock`, with no symlink and no group/world write. Registered trusted `/bin/ps` and `/usr/sbin/lsof` probes prove one live same-uid positive daemonPid, its exact command executes the pinned daemon-entry.js under packageRoot, and that PID alone owns the listening socket identity/path. The socket path identity and process start identity are revalidated immediately before each connect and after each server EOF; drift, extra owner, dead/reused PID, changed package byte, or probe ambiguity yields pre-send failure or post-dispatch authorized uncertainty. The same-uid local account is the explicit trust boundary; shape-compatible bytes from an unproved peer are never accepted. Peer authority classifies as retained kind `ouro_daemon_peer_authority` and is pushed/read back before Slugger dispatch intent.

- Daemon response preimages are retained inline and sanitized. Exact `TerminalDaemonMessageResponse={ok:true,message,data:{id,queuedAt}}` is accepted only when message is `queued message ` plus the same id, id matches `^msg-[0-9]{17}(?:-(?:[2-9]|[1-9][0-9]+))?$`, and queuedAt is canonical. Exact `TerminalDaemonWakeResponse={ok:true}` is projected from any parsed non-array wake object with `ok:true`; all extra wake fields are discarded before hashing. Final exact `TerminalSluggerNotifierResult={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationKeySha256,deliverySemantics:'pinned_daemon_delivery',peerAuthorityPath,peerAuthoritySha256,messageReceiptId,queuedAt,messageResponse,messageResponseSha256,wakeDisposition:'accepted'|'failed',wakeResponse,wakeResponseSha256,outcome:'observed',completedAt}` hashes compact-canonical complete inline response projections. accepted wake requires inline `{ok:true}` and its hash; failed requires both null. The result is itself retained through the observed outcome, so every response hash is recomputable without raw socket text. Raw bytes remain bounded in memory and are never persisted.

- Final residue validation is always closed-world. Initial, proof-only, and completion-present recovery all rerun the same no-follow closure-root scan and complete absence-registry checks. Proof-only permits the exact canonical proof as the sole post-scan addition; completion-present permits exactly the canonical proof and completion as the only additions. Both rederive retainedMembers, absenceProofs, set hashes, every referenced artifact hash, owner terminal/dead/reaped state, and forbidden host-path absence, then byte-compare them to proof/completion. Unknown, changed, missing, alias, symlink, alternate basename, or over-limit state blocks even after completion. Completion-present recovery is read-only after this validation; it never returns from reference validation alone.

- The exact final retained-kind union is the Round 81 union plus `terminal_process_diagnostic|ouro_daemon_peer_authority`; their only paths are the three attempt-qualified diagnostic grammars and the single peer-authority path above. Unit29.2.6a/b/c red/green/verify every complete outcome schema, frame/error mapping, diagnostic byte/hash, Slugger CAS prefix, cleanup-completion branch, peer/package/socket/process probe, response projection/hash, receipt-id boundary including `-9|-10|-100`, and completion-present unknown-file race. Units30/31 red/green/verify all 33 rows, the terminal projection barrier, private history/list/snapshot semantics, five mutation IDs, unchanged 21/35 file counts, and stale scale/MCP spellings.

### Round 83 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for visitor privacy capture, terminal projection identity, Ouro file timestamps and source authority, peer probe bytes, daemon message observation, Slugger authorization paths, pre-dispatch exhaustion, cleanup completion, and wake recovery. They replace every conflicting Round 82 or earlier clause for those subjects. Every displayed schema is exact, no-extra-fields compact-canonical JSON in displayed field order with no final LF unless stated otherwise.

- History privacy is proved in the visitor context, not from User B's own kitchen. Row 28 remains User A's authenticated `GET /` and exact response `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[],cookHistoryAttemptIds:[attemptId]}`. Rows 29 and 30 both request the existing public profile route `GET /users/{userAIdentifier}`, under authenticated User B and anonymous contexts respectively, and both capture exact sanitized response `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[],cookHistoryAttemptIds:[],privateCookQueryCount:0}`. `userAIdentifier` is a validated `FeatureOpaqueId` copied from User A's public route identity; it is not an email. The public profile loader neither reads nor receives a private cook-history projection, and the injected query observer must record zero `CookSessionIndex` private-owner queries for each visitor request. Row 29/30 use of `/`, a caller-supplied empty projection, a hidden private query, or omission of `privateCookQueryCount` rejects. Rows 31-33 and all 33 exchange/count/file totals remain unchanged.

- The terminal projection barrier identifies the one exact progress-free D1 row, not merely any terminal row. Final `FeatureHistoryProjectionBarrier={attemptId,status:'COMPLETED',pollCount,observedProjectionSeq:1,observedRevision:1,lastProjectionId,observedAt}` requires `lastProjectionId` byte-equal `attemptId+':1:UPSERT'`, pollCount 1..60, and canonical observedAt. The selected row must match User A, recipeId, terminal metadata timestamps, and row 27's attempt/status/revision only; scale, active step, and checked state are verified solely from the independently captured DO snapshot and never selected from D1. Unit31's parameterized poll returns the complete final progress-free row and rejects sequence/revision zero, sequence/revision greater than one, a different lastProjectionId, a duplicate, wrong owner/recipe, any progress column, or a value copied from the response instead of D1. Unit30's fake supplies the same exact barrier.

- Ouro package file time is lossless. Final `OuroPackageFile={path,sha256,device,inode,mode,uid,byteLength,mtimeNs}` replaces mtimeMs. `mtimeNs` is a canonical nonnegative decimal bigint string matching `^(0|[1-9][0-9]*)$`, is read from the same no-follow bigint stat as device/inode/mode/uid/byteLength, and must remain byte-equal at every boundary. Any Number conversion, fractional millisecond value, exponent, sign, leading zero, or mixed-stat record rejects. Comparisons with daemonStartedAt convert that canonical timestamp to integer nanoseconds without loss and require the daemon start strictly later than every package file mtimeNs.

- Slugger authorization is attempt-qualified and immutable. Intent path is exactly `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-run-authorization-intent.v1.json`; receipt path is exactly `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-run-authorization-receipt.v1.json`. Their retained kinds are `slugger_authorization_intent` and `slugger_authorization_receipt` respectively. TerminalSluggerAttemptOutcome authorization pairs must name its own S-qualified paths, and prior outcome S may therefore coexist with a fresh S+1 intent/receipt after `authorized_not_run`. The singular `slugger/run-authorization-{intent,receipt}.v1.json` paths and generic `slugger_run_authorization` kind are invalid. Exactly one receipt may enter a dispatch intent; all earlier receipts remain retained through final residue.

- Slugger attempt exhaustion has a complete terminal authority. Exact `TerminalSluggerExhaustionCommand={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,logicalName:'slugger-attempt-limit-exhausted',argv:[],cwd:null,stdinSha256,processStarted:false,outcome:'not_started',exitCode:null,signal:null,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,warningCount:0,failureReason:'attempt_limit_exhausted',startedAt:null,endedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-exhaustion-terminal-command.v1.json`; stdin/stdout/stderr hashes are the SHA-256 of empty bytes. Exact `SluggerAttemptExhaustion={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,notificationPath,notificationBeforeSha256,priorOutcomeRefs,priorOutcomeSetSha256,attemptLimit:3,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,code:'attempt_limit_exhausted',terminal:true,completedAt}` lives at the existing attempt-exhaustion path and names that read-back command. It is legal only after three contiguous replacement-eligible outcomes, notification still prepared, no selected/dispatch pair, and no live/indeterminate member. One CAS changes prepared directly to uncertain/attempt_limit_exhausted, points resultPath/hash to the exhaustion record, and forbids S=4 or any message/wake frame; the exhaustion record supplies the subordinate final terminal-command authority.

- Cleanup completion is an exact three-branch union. Final `ReleaseTerminalCleanupCompletion` adds `sluggerDisposition:'released_live'|'selected_uncertain'|'attempt_limit_exhausted'` before the Slugger path members and adds `sluggerAttemptExhaustionPath,sluggerAttemptExhaustionSha256,sluggerTerminalCommandPath,sluggerTerminalCommandSha256` immediately before completedAt. `released_live` requires nonnull selected launch/spawn/ready/authorization/dispatch/notification pairs, null pre-run outcome/exhaustion pairs, released notification, and a live registered child; its terminal-command pair is null because the run frame has not yet been sent. `selected_uncertain` requires the selected pairs, exact uncertain pre-run outcome and terminal-command pair, null exhaustion pair, uncertain/selected_uncertain notification, and dead/reaped members. `attempt_limit_exhausted` requires all selected launch-through-dispatch and pre-run outcome pairs null, exact exhaustion plus exhaustion-command pairs, uncertain/attempt_limit_exhausted notification, and every prior member dead/reaped. Cleanup completion is pushed/read back in all three branches. The selected Slugger run frame is legal only for released_live; the other branches proceed through final terminal result/residue/completion without a message and use their nonnull terminal command authority.

- TrustedToolchain adds nonnull `lsof:TrustedExecutable`; its frozen absolute path is `/usr/sbin/lsof` and expected SHA-256 is `53b9a14df1036dd6d0b7f04d6f212823bddde2e090aaed1e772b73d7d43b2558`. The frozen `/bin/ps` expected SHA-256 is `b337747e8db7a3443b82ec9f3685432ddf76b971878188c757ca09db1c5a0288`. Both are regular no-follow root-owned mode-0755 files, are opened and hashed through their retained descriptors, and execute with exact environment `PATH=/usr/bin:/bin,HOME=/Users/arimendelow,USER=arimendelow,LOGNAME=arimendelow,LANG=C,LC_ALL=C,TZ=UTC,NO_COLOR=1`, all other keys unset. They use the existing registered process command machinery and its finite stream/time/reap rules; PATH lookup, shell, alternate flags, inherited locale, warning, truncation, or unregistered probe rejects.

- Peer probes have literal schemas and parsers. Exact `OuroDaemonProcessProbe={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,probeOrdinal,argv,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,exitCode:0,daemonPid,daemonUid,daemonStartedAt,nodeExecutablePath,daemonEntryArgument,socketArgument,observedAt}` uses argv `[ps.absolutePath,'-p',String(daemonPid),'-o','pid=','-o','uid=','-o','lstart=','-o','command=']`; stdout is exactly one LF-terminated macOS ps row, parsed as positive pid, uid 501, fixed C-locale lstart converted to canonical daemonStartedAt, and command tokens `<absolute-node> <daemon-entry> --socket /tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock` with no extra token. `daemonEntryArgument` may contain the literal CurrentVersion path, but no-follow realpath resolution must equal the pinned daemon-entry.js package file. Exact `OuroDaemonSocketProbe={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,probeOrdinal,argv,stdoutSha256,stderrSha256,exitCode:0,daemonPid,socketPath,socketIdentity,ownerCount:1,observedAt}` uses argv `[lsof.absolutePath,'-nP','-U','-a','-Fnpt','--','/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock']`; stdout is exactly `p<daemonPid>\nf<positive-fd>\ntunix\nn/tmp/ouroboros-daemon.sock\n`, with no second process/file block. The socket probe's no-follow lstat identity equals socketIdentity immediately before and after the command. Process/socket probe paths are attempt-qualified `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-ouro-{process,socket}-probe-<P>.v1.json`, classify as `ouro_daemon_process_probe|ouro_daemon_socket_probe`, and their paths/hashes are the peer authority's exact initial P=1 pair.

- Package source pins are narrow and complete for the protocol claims they make. The six BINARY-sorted package-relative path/hash rows are exactly `package.json` / `abfb024a6373cf2277579ef31945418140d84d103ab8c4b70f0ffd1a77e221be`, `dist/heart/daemon/socket-client.js` / `c8beafd123d5821a285f89014f8b0bc5aa25a356e1214d6fdccfdb093d3a0a96`, `dist/heart/daemon/cli-exec.js` / `90822583dee67cea347cc0efaec10e0e7c4d3426b6deb437b40201c29a795e20`, `dist/heart/daemon/message-router.js` / `552e007e556e91472337c5291f9590bf52273dafbe723af3bd8a69b761e9060d`, `dist/heart/daemon/daemon.js` / `d72735805726612ab539597d8c4f91024107f0a18b37fb04f53d0e01b1d43dd5`, and `dist/heart/daemon/daemon-entry.js` / `5b465f8b5a3d8e82659b5fec7dc491f7ab3d93f1e7b9e6cdbf6bd237964bd405`. They prove socket framing, message response shape, best-effort wake shape, and daemon entry/route ownership only. They do not claim a transitive hash closure over message-router imports or prove durable inbox persistence. Durable delivery is instead proved by the operation-owned append evidence below; importing an unpinned file as persistence authority rejects.

- Message observation crosses an authenticated parent boundary. Before the child receives run authorization, the parent captures the literal inbox `/Users/arimendelow/.ouro-cli/daemon/messages/slugger-inbox.jsonl` as a regular no-follow same-uid non-group/world-writable file with stable device/inode, byteLength, and SHA-256. After one valid message response reaches server EOF, the child sends exact HMAC-authenticated `OuroMessageEofFrame={version:1,type:'ouro_message_eof',operationKeySha256,messageResponse,messageResponseSha256,socketIdentity,observedAt,mac}` over the existing control channel and performs no wake until a parent decision. Exact `OuroDaemonBoundaryProbe={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,boundary,responseSha256,processProbePath,processProbeSha256,socketProbePath,socketProbeSha256,packageFileSetSha256,socketIdentity,stable:true,observedAt}` has boundary `message_eof|wake_eof`, and responseSha256 equals the corresponding projected message/wake response hash. The parent validates the message frame, writes fresh P=2 process/socket probes, and publishes the message boundary at `slugger/attempt-<S>-ouro-boundary-message-eof.v1.json`. A missing/invalid message frame or failed P=2 boundary is authorized uncertainty and creates no wake.

- Exact `OuroMessageInboxAppendProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,inboxPath,beforeDevice,beforeInode,beforeByteLength,beforeSha256,afterDevice,afterInode,afterByteLength,afterSha256,appendedLineOffset,appendedLineByteLength,appendedLineSha256,appendedLine,messageReceiptId,queuedAt,provedAt}` lives at `_meta/release-terminal/<I>/slugger/attempt-<S>-message-inbox-append-proof.v1.json`. The after file must retain the same device/inode, byte-prefix-equal all before bytes, end on LF, and contain exactly once after beforeByteLength the UTF-8 line `JSON.stringify({id:messageReceiptId,from:'ouro-cli',to:'slugger',content:'Done: Shipped Clem feedback end to end',queuedAt,priority:'normal'})+'\n'`; appendedLine is that exact parsed no-extra-fields object, and its id/queuedAt equal the validated daemon response. Other complete concurrent suffix lines may surround it but are read only in memory, must not duplicate this receipt ID, and are not retained. Prefix drift, truncation, rotation, partial JSONL, duplicate receipt, wrong line, or absent append is uncertainty. A valid P=2 boundary plus this proof is the durable `message_observed` point; both classify as `ouro_daemon_boundary_probe|ouro_message_inbox_append_proof` and are immutable/read back before wake.

- Wake starts only after message_observed and never changes delivery. The parent writes exact HMAC-authenticated `OuroWakeDecisionFrame={version:1,type:'ouro_wake_decision',operationKeySha256,messageResponseSha256,boundaryProbePath,boundaryProbeSha256,inboxAppendProofPath,inboxAppendProofSha256,processProbePath,processProbeSha256,socketProbePath,socketProbeSha256,decision:'send'|'skip',mac}`. Decision send requires fresh pre-wake P=3 process/socket probes equal to the authority and names their read-back paths/hashes; failure to obtain them produces skip with all four P=3 members null. The child sends at most one pinned private.wake only for send. After one valid wake response reaches EOF, the child sends authenticated `OuroWakeEofFrame={version:1,type:'ouro_wake_eof',operationKeySha256,wakeResponse:{ok:true},wakeResponseSha256,socketIdentity,observedAt,mac}` and waits. Accepted requires parent validation, fresh P=4 process/socket probes, and read-back `slugger/attempt-<S>-ouro-boundary-wake-eof.v1.json` with boundary wake_eof. The parent returns one authenticated terminal wake disposition before child stdout. Connect/write/response/EOF/frame/probe/package/socket drift, timeout, invalid/non-ok bytes, or parent skip all map to `wakeDisposition:'failed'` with null wake response/hash and null wake-boundary pair; no valid wake bytes are claimed when their P=4 boundary fails. Once message_observed exists, no wake or later peer failure may change the message to uncertainty, authorize replacement, or resend it.

- Final `TerminalSluggerNotifierResult={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,operationKeySha256,deliverySemantics:'pinned_daemon_delivery',peerAuthorityPath,peerAuthoritySha256,messageBoundaryProbePath,messageBoundaryProbeSha256,inboxAppendProofPath,inboxAppendProofSha256,messageReceiptId,queuedAt,messageResponse,messageResponseSha256,wakeDisposition:'accepted'|'failed',wakeResponse,wakeResponseSha256,wakeBoundaryProbePath,wakeBoundaryProbeSha256,outcome:'observed',completedAt}` requires the valid message boundary and append proof. Accepted wake requires nonnull complete wake response/hash and wake-boundary pair; failed requires all four null. The observed Slugger outcome and notification point to this result through their terminal command; uncertainty is legal only before message_observed. Final residue retains every attempt-qualified authorization intent/receipt, process/socket/boundary probe, append proof, exhaustion authority when present, and diagnostic. The exact retained-kind union replaces `slugger_run_authorization` with `slugger_authorization_intent|slugger_authorization_receipt` and adds `slugger_attempt_exhaustion|slugger_exhaustion_command|ouro_daemon_process_probe|ouro_daemon_socket_probe|ouro_daemon_boundary_probe|ouro_message_inbox_append_proof`; all other Round 82 kinds remain.

- Unit29.2.6a/b/c red/green/verify the S then S+1 authorization paths, all three cleanup branches, exact exhaustion/no-fourth-attempt terminalization, ps/lsof tool identity and parsers, bigint mtime drift, six-path source claim, message frame/P=2 boundary/inbox append proof, concurrent suffix lines, wake decision and every post-observation failure. Units30/31 red/green/verify the public-profile User B/anonymous captures, zero private-query observer, exact sequence/revision/projection ID barrier, unchanged 33 exchanges, and unchanged 21/35 file counts.

### Round 84 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for legacy cook rollout, anonymous telemetry, owner history implementation, cross-device discovery, visitor query proof, terminal projection retention, broad QA cleanup, Ouro inbox trim behavior, and terminal wake recovery. They replace every conflicting Round 83 or earlier clause for those subjects. Every displayed schema is exact no-extra-fields compact-canonical JSON in displayed order with no final LF unless stated otherwise.

- Existing local v1 cook progress receives a safe rollout instead of becoming stranded. A raw `spoonjoy-cook-progress:<recipeId>` v1 value is never rendered or sent by an authenticated mount before a choice. If the existing legacy parser can normalize it to any nondefault progress, the UI shows exactly `Use this device's progress` and `Start without old progress`; it exposes no prior checked identifiers to a different authenticated account before that choice. Use canonicalizes the normalized current-recipe values into the existing exact v2 shape with the current recipeFingerprint and a fresh canonical updatedAt, then writes/readbacks exact user-scoped `LegacyCookProgressMigration={version:1,userId,recipeId,rawV1Sha256,candidateV2,createdAt}` at `spoonjoy-auth-cook-legacy-migration:v1.<userComponent>.<recipeComponent>` under the shared user lock. It leaves the raw v1 key untouched, persists the ordinary authenticated Start command with adoption from candidateV2, then sends. After the server snapshot and authenticated record are durably stored, it removes/rescans the raw v1 key and migration key in that order. Recovery for the same user resumes from the hash-matching migration record; another user cannot read or adopt that record and sees only the neutral original choice. Logout cleanup deletes the departing user's migration prefix under its existing generation fence. Start-without removes/rescans the raw recipe key and the current user's migration key before bootstrap. A write/remove failure preserves the last durable prefix, sends nothing new, and offers retry. Outside explicit discard, the raw v1 key is never removed before durable server/authenticated state. Anonymous mounts retain the legacy filtered v1 behavior until a choice is made after authentication. Malformed/default-only v1 remains nonadoptable and follows the existing preserve-until-local-action rule. Units15/16 test deployment over every current v1 field/boundary, conversion write loss, reload at each prefix, adoption response loss, logout, user A -> user B switch, and discard.

- Anonymous local-only means no off-device progress telemetry. `recipe_scaled`, `ingredient_toggled`, and `step_output_toggled` may be emitted only while a loader-confirmed authenticated identity owns the canonical cook controller; their payload is derived from the accepted authenticated display state. Anonymous cook interactions emit none of those events and no equivalent recipe/ingredient/output/check/count/scale progress payload through PostHog, fetch, beacon, service worker, console transport, or another analytics sink. Generic public recipe-view analytics remain unchanged and may not add progress fields. Unit16 injects every sink, performs every anonymous control, and proves zero progress egress; separate authenticated assertions preserve the three intentional events.

- Owner history is product work in Unit17, not smoke-only behavior. The authenticated owner `/` loader performs the exact private `CookSessionIndex` owner query once, partitions ACTIVE continuation from COMPLETED/ABANDONED history, orders history `(updatedAt DESC,attemptId BINARY ASC)`, caps it at 20 after owner filtering, and renders an unframed compact `Cook history` section below Continue cooking. Terminal cards link to the public recipe detail without implying a resumable attempt; active cards retain their canonical activation flow. Anonymous, `/?chef=...`, and `/users/{identifier}` visitors neither query nor receive private cook rows. Unit17a imports the production loaders with the real route-platform DB boundary instrumented and asserts the exact Prisma method/where/select/order/take call ledger: one owner-private query for owner `/`, zero CookSessionIndex access for every visitor/public route. This production-loader test, its coverage log, and source hash are the query-isolation proof; no live endpoint exposes a query counter.

- Feature visitor captures therefore contain only observable product data. Rows 29 and 30 remain User B and anonymous `GET /users/{userAIdentifier}`, but their exact response projection is `{continueCookingAttemptIds:[],cookHistoryAttemptIds:[]}` with no `privateCookQueryCount`. The live 33-exchange artifact proves empty visitor projections; Unit17's production-loader test proves zero query access. Feature finalization cross-links the Unit17 verified source SHA through the release manifest rather than inventing runtime query instrumentation. Any response/query-count field, debug header, test endpoint, or hidden live observer is invalid.

- Terminal evidence separates DO progress from D1 metadata. Final `CookFeatureTerminalState={attemptId,recipeId,recipeFingerprint,revision:1,status:'COMPLETED',activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[],updatedAt,completedAt}` is captured from the DO and requires canonical equal updatedAt/completedAt. Exact D1 `FeatureHistoryProjectionRowProof={attemptId,userId,recipeId,activeKey:null,status:'COMPLETED',recipeFingerprint,recipeTitle:'codex-smoke-feature-recipe',recipeCoverImageUrl:null,revision:1,projectionSeq:1,lastProjectionId,startedAt,updatedAt,completedAt,abandonedAt:null}` contains every and only progress-free CookSessionIndex column. Final `FeatureHistoryProjectionBarrier={pollCount,row,rowSha256,observedAt}` has pollCount 1..60, hashes that complete row, and canonical observedAt at/after row.updatedAt. D1 fields cross-equal only owner/recipe/attempt/status/fingerprint/revision/times and sanitized recipe metadata; step, scale, and checked ids are proven only by the DO capture. The parameterized query is by exact `(attemptId,userId,recipeId)` and returns zero, one, or over-one rows; only one passes. `cook-history-privacy.json` retains both independently hashed proofs. A hash-only owner, dropped field, progress-bearing D1 field, copied response value, or unretained query result rejects.

- Cross-device lifecycle smoke begins from discovery. Client 2 starts with no recipeId or attemptId in its page URL, storage, test adapter, or navigation input. After Client 1's accepted progress and bounded D1 projection barrier, Client 2 opens `/`, waits under the existing projection deadline until the owner Continue cooking card appears, activates that rendered card, and asserts navigation to `/recipes/{recipeId}#cook`, canonical GET discovery of the same attemptId/revision, and the displayed Client 1 progress before opening its socket and continuing the two-client mutation sequence. Direct residue/known-id/bootstrap GET may still verify recovery later but cannot satisfy discovery. Unit30 faults D1 enqueue/alarm, owner loader, card rendering, href/hash, click, and canonical bootstrap independently; QA and production retain screenshot/exchange evidence for the discovery.

- Broad QA cleanup freezes targets before effects. Migration `0024_qa_cleanup_coordination.sql` drops the nine legacy undeclared helper tables `disposable_users|hard_delete_recipes|soft_delete_recipes|disposable_spoons|e2e_oauth_clients|disposable_covers|disposable_cover_image_urls|disposable_credentials|cleanup_blockers` and creates the sole migration-owned `QaCleanupLease={leaseName TEXT PRIMARY KEY,operationKeySha256 TEXT NOT NULL,ownerTokenSha256 TEXT NOT NULL,expiresAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL,updatedAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL}` with safe nonnegative millisecond checks. The Prisma migration mirror uses the same bytes. The mutating QA command requires a task-relative journal root and operation key, acquires/readbacks leaseName `broad-disposable-cleanup` through one conditional insert/update transaction with exact `expiresAtMs=nowMs+900000`, and renews to `nowMs+900000` under the same operation/token before each effect while enforcing an absolute 1800000 ms cleanup deadline. Every first-party QA smoke/signup path refuses creation while an unexpired foreign lease exists. Lease loss/expiry blocks mutation; recovery reacquires only for the same manifest/operation key. A user created after manifest selection is never added to that operation and makes final assert-clean fail for a later cleanup.

- Exact `QaCleanupTargetManifest={version:1,operationKeySha256,targetEnvironment:'qa',databaseName,r2Bucket,leaseReceiptPath,leaseReceiptSha256,disposableUserIds,hardDeleteRecipeIds,softDeleteRecipeIds,disposableSpoonIds,e2eOauthClientIds,disposableCoverIds,disposableCredentialIds,r2DeleteKeys,existingSearchTables,targetSetSha256,schemaBefore,schemaBeforeSha256,createdAt}` uses BINARY-sorted unique FeatureOpaqueId/key/table-name arrays and targetSetSha256 over all target arrays. It is mode-0600, temp-write/fsync/rename/parent-fsync/readback durable before the first D1 or R2 effect. Selection evaluates the disposable predicates once under the lease; every D1 DELETE/soft-delete/search cleanup and retry uses only manifest IDs, never reevaluates a broad selector. R2 keys are captured before their identifying D1 rows change. Recovery without the exact manifest blocks rather than deriving an empty target set.

- Cleanup progress is response-loss safe. Exact `QaCleanupEffectReceipt={version:1,operationKeySha256,effectOrdinal,kind:'d1_transaction'|'r2_delete'|'r2_verify'|'schema_verify'|'final_oracle',target,requestSha256,outcome:'executed'|'reconciled',observationSha256,completedAt}` forms a contiguous immutable history. The single remote AtomicSqlPort batch is journaled before dispatch and reconciles response loss only by exact manifest-target absence plus expected soft-delete poststates. Each R2 delete has its own prepared intent, then an independent get/list absence observation; response loss never drops the next manifest key. The production first-party Cloudflare client paginates `GET /accounts/{accountId}/r2/buckets/{bucket}/objects` through every cursor under the frozen token/capability and proves every manifest key absent plus zero object under the task's QA-disposable prefix. This listing is independent of D1. Only then may the exact broad D1 zero oracle pass and the lease row be removed.

- Broad cleanup never creates schema outside migrations. The apply SQL uses CTEs and manifest-bound parameter tables in memory only; `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` for any helper name is forbidden. Exact `QaCleanupSchemaProof={version:1,operationKeySha256,before,after,beforeSha256,afterSha256,equal:true,observedAt}` stores BINARY-sorted `sqlite_schema` rows `{type,name,tblName,sqlSha256}` for every noninternal table/index/trigger/view and requires byte equality around cleanup. Baseline must equal applied migrations including QaCleanupLease and exclude all nine legacy helpers. Migration rerun, local/QA schema proof, process death after D1 commit, every R2 boundary, list pagination, lease expiry/loss, concurrent signup, and final-oracle response loss are Unit0 and Unit31 red/green/verify cases.

- Ouro inbox proof models the pinned router's cap. Exact `OuroInboxLineDigest={lineSha256,byteLength,kind:'blank'|'invalid_json'|'valid_json'}` hashes each LF-terminated non-LF line and records its nonnegative byte length. kind blank means JavaScript `line.trim().length===0`; otherwise valid_json means the pinned UTF-8 decode plus JSON.parse succeeds and invalid_json means it throws. It retains no other message content. Exact `OuroInboxTransitionAppend={ordinal,kind:'other'|'target',lineDigest}` is contiguous; target occurs once and its bytes are the exact expected message line, while other lines retain digest/length/kind only. Final `OuroMessageInboxAppendProof={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,inboxPath,beforeState,afterState,transition,transitionAppends,transitionAppendSetSha256,targetLineOffset,targetLineByteLength,targetLineSha256,targetLine,messageReceiptId,queuedAt,provedAt}` uses `OuroInboxFileState={kind:'absent'}|{kind:'file',device,inode,mode,uid,gid,byteLength,lineDigests,lineDigestSetSha256}`. lineDigestSetSha256 hashes the complete ordered digest array; byteLength is the sum of every line byteLength plus one LF per line, with the final LF represented once and no phantom trailing empty member. File states allow at most 8192 lines/16 MiB; present states are regular no-follow same-uid and non-group/world-writable. The before state contains no target receipt. After is present, contains the target exact line once, and retains the same device/inode when before was present.

- Inbox transition is `append|trim_append`. Replay starts from before lineDigests and applies transitionAppends in exact observed order. Each append adds one valid_json digest and runs the pinned trim: if valid_json count is at most 1000 it performs no rewrite; otherwise it first drops every blank member, then removes the earliest valid_json members until exactly 1000 remain while preserving every invalid_json member and relative order. Append requires no rewrite; trim_append requires that legal rewrite. The replayed digest array/set hash, derived byte length, and target offset/hash must equal afterState. An absent-before file starts with an empty array. Ambiguous reconstruction, polling/truncation, rotation, partial line, over-limit state, duplicate target receipt, target removal, or any observed line digest outside this append/trim replay yields pre-observation uncertainty and no wake/resend. Tests cover 999 and 1000 valid lines, whitespace-only and invalid-JSON retained/removed behavior, multiple concurrent before/after appends, poll/clear, response loss, and exact current 1000-line state. Prefix digest equality is required only for append, never trim_append.

- Wake completion has literal persisted wire authority. Before sending the terminal frame, the parent publishes/readbacks exact `OuroWakeTerminalDecision={version:1,rootIdentitySha256,processAttemptOrdinal,operationKeySha256,messageResponseSha256,wakeDisposition:'accepted'|'failed',wakeResponse,wakeResponseSha256,wakeBoundaryProbePath,wakeBoundaryProbeSha256,decidedAt}` at `slugger/attempt-<S>-ouro-wake-terminal-decision.v1.json`; accepted has all wake members nonnull, failed has all null. Exact HMAC frame `OuroWakeTerminalFrame={version:1,type:'ouro_wake_terminal',operationKeySha256,messageResponseSha256,decisionPath,decisionSha256,wakeDisposition,wakeResponse,wakeResponseSha256,wakeBoundaryProbePath,wakeBoundaryProbeSha256,decidedAt,mac}` byte-matches that decision. Exact ACK is `OuroWakeTerminalAckFrame={version:1,type:'ouro_wake_terminal_ack',operationKeySha256,decisionSha256,outcome:'accepted',acknowledgedAt,mac}`.

- The child accepts one valid terminal frame, emits its ACK, and then its bounded notifier stdout. Identical frame replay before stdout is acknowledged again without a wake; mismatch/oversize/invalid MAC rejects. Parent waits 5000 ms for ACK and retains exact `OuroWakeTerminalReceipt={version:1,decisionPath,decisionSha256,ackFrameSha256,disposition:'acknowledged'|'ack_lost',completedAt}`; acknowledged requires the exact nonnull ACK hash, while ack_lost requires null. Timeout/EOF/dead child after decision is ack_lost, never message uncertainty. Recovery adopts the read-back decision and may materialize the exact observed notifier result from peer/message-boundary/inbox-proof/decision bytes after dead/reaped proof, without another message or wake. Final TerminalSluggerNotifierResult adds decision and receipt path/hash pairs before outcome. New retained kinds are `ouro_wake_terminal_decision|ouro_wake_terminal_receipt`. Unit29.2.6a/b/c fault frame/MAC/nullability/ACK/stdout/crash at every byte and prove every state after message_observed remains observed.

- Unit15/16 own v1 rollout and anonymous telemetry; Unit17 owns active continuation plus terminal history/query isolation; Unit30 lifecycle owns user-facing discovery; Units30/31 own the complete retained terminal row proof; Units0/31 own migration-bound broad cleanup, manifests, D1/R2 receipts/listing, lease, and schema invariance; Unit29.2.6 owns inbox append/trim and terminal wake. Every corresponding a/b/c and visual unit carries these latest behaviors, and Units32-37 reject evidence built from a stale pre-Round84 contract.

### Round 85 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for legacy-v1 choice arbitration, explicit replacement adoption, metadata physical deletion, terminal D1 row completeness, broad-cleanup authority/order, reviewed production migrations, and overlapping-merge deployment. They replace every conflicting Round 84 or earlier clause for those subjects.

- Legacy choice is generation-fenced and first-commit-wins. Exact `LegacyCookProgressDecision=LegacyUseDecision|LegacyDiscardDecision`; `LegacyUseDecision={version:1,userId,recipeId,userGeneration,recipeGeneration,rawV1Sha256,decision:'use',candidateV2,resolution:'pending'|'keep_server'|'replace_server',replaceMutationId:UUID|null,decidedAt,updatedAt}` and `LegacyDiscardDecision={version:1,userId,recipeId,userGeneration,recipeGeneration,rawV1Sha256,decision:'discard',decidedAt}`. replaceMutationId is nonnull iff resolution is replace_server. The exact key is `spoonjoy-auth-cook-legacy-decision:v1.<userComponent>.<recipeComponent>`. Both visible choices acquire the existing user Web Lock exclusively, never nest a shared acquisition, validate no cleanup/reset marker, and use direct helpers that consume that exclusive-lock authority. They reread exact user/recipe generations and raw-v1 hash immediately before every decision write, raw removal, authenticated-record write, and network send; final decision removal instead requires the same generations, no marker, raw-key rescan zero, and the owning terminal keep/discard/adopted proof. An existing hash/generation-matching original decision is resumed and cannot be changed by another tab; pending resolution is also first-commit-wins, while a nonpending resolution is immutable. Malformed, mismatched, or stale decision blocks. Logout/reset waits for an already-held choice, then advances generation and deletes the departing scope; a delayed choice cannot write or send afterward. Units15/16 interleave use versus discard and keep versus replace, both click orders, paused writers before/after lock acquisition, storage events, logout, reset, and A-to-B switch.

- Exact `LegacyCandidateProgress={recipeFingerprint,activeStepIndex,scaleFactor,checkedIngredientIds,checkedStepOutputIds}` is derived from candidateV2 and excludes its client-only updatedAt. Ordinary adoption remains pristine-only. Initial Use persists LegacyUseDecision with pending/null, sends the existing adoption shape with `adoptionMode` omitted, and may remove raw/decision bytes only after exact outcome adopted, a server snapshot whose LegacyCandidateProgress byte-equals the candidate projection, and readback of an authenticated record whose snapshot has that same projection. `server_won`, including ACTIVE, terminal, and fully purged history, removes nothing and renders exact conflict actions `Keep server progress` and `Replace with this device's progress`. Keep-server atomically advances resolution to keep_server under the same exclusive authority, then removes/rescans raw and decision keys without a cook mutation. Replace atomically advances resolution to replace_server with one persisted fresh mutation id, then sends the exact JSON body `{recipeId,mutationId,adoption,adoptionMode:'replace'}` in that field order. Response loss retries that same body/id. Raw/decision bytes remain until outcome adopted and both server/authenticated LegacyCandidateProgress projections byte-match; every other outcome/error preserves retry/conflict state.

- `POST /api/cook-sessions` therefore accepts exact `{recipeId,mutationId,adoption?,adoptionMode?}`. adoptionMode may be omitted, or be literal replace only when adoption is present; explicit null, pristine text, replace without adoption, and unknown values are 422. Omission has all prior permanent-history behavior. Replace is an explicit owner-only destructive arbitration command: after structural validation and PURGING precedence, the DO performs the ordinary current-recipe D1 snapshot batch and validates the adoption semantically against that current fingerprint. If current is ACTIVE, replacement queues an exact old-attempt DELETE at sequence `current.revision+1` before the new ACTIVE UPSERT; this intentionally removes the replaced active projection/history and releases activeKey, while an already-terminal current keeps its terminal history row/order. In one storage transaction it creates the new ACTIVE attempt from adoption, creates/preserves permanent history arbitration, writes start replay/ordered projections/alarm, and converts every live record for the replaced current attempt to attempt_replaced tombstones. Queue/tombstone/serialized-byte capacity for the complete old-DELETE-plus-new-UPSERT transition is preflighted before writes; revision `2_147_483_646` may use reserved DELETE sequence `2_147_483_647`. It returns 201/adopted; exact replay returns 201/adopted plus the current evolved matching attempt while live. It works with ACTIVE, terminal, absent-after-purge, or expired coordinator state; closes replaced sockets only after commit; and can neither revive nor mutate another user's attempt. Start requestHash includes adoptionMode only when present. Units8-14 cover validation/hash/replay/D1 failure, current-state variants, queue/tombstone/size capacity, sockets/projection ordering, and restart; Units15/16 cover the two-step conflict UI and delete-only-after-matching-persistence rule. QA and production feature smoke retain one server_won conflict followed by explicit replace and matching cross-device state before cleanup.

- The deployed feature registry is now exactly 34 contiguous exchanges. Exact `CookFeatureDefaultState={attemptId,recipeId,revision:0,status:'ACTIVE',activeStepIndex:0,scaleFactor:1,checkedIngredientIds:[],checkedStepOutputIds:[]}` and CookFeatureState retains the existing candidate progress. Rows 1-4 replace the former adoption rows:

| # | kind / operationId | transport / principal / status | exact request -> exact response |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `anonymous_local_start` / `adoption.anonymous.start` | `browser_local` / `anonymous` / null | the existing exact local-only v2 candidate capture |
| 2 | `legacy_server_seed` / `adoption.owner.server-seed` | `web` / `user_a` / 201 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/cook-sessions',body:{recipeId,mutationId:legacySeedMutationId}}` -> `{data:{snapshot:CookFeatureDefaultState,adoptionOutcome:'not_requested'}}` |
| 3 | `legacy_use_conflict` / `adoption.owner.legacy-conflict` | `web` / `user_a` / 200 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/cook-sessions',body:{recipeId,mutationId:legacyConflictMutationId,adoption:{version:1,recipeFingerprint,activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[]}}}` -> `{data:{snapshot:CookFeatureDefaultState,adoptionOutcome:'server_won'},rawLegacyPreserved:true,decisionPreserved:true}` |
| 4 | `legacy_replace` / `adoption.owner.legacy-replace` | `web` / `user_a` / 201 | `{method:'POST',path:'/api/cook-sessions',body:{recipeId,mutationId:legacyReplaceMutationId,adoption:{version:1,recipeFingerprint,activeStepIndex:1,scaleFactor:2,checkedIngredientIds:[ingredientId],checkedStepOutputIds:[]},adoptionMode:'replace'}}` -> `{data:{snapshot:CookFeatureState,adoptionOutcome:'adopted'},priorAttemptId:seedAttemptId,localStorageClearedAfterAuthenticatedPersistence:true}` with snapshot attemptId different from priorAttemptId |

- Exact `FeatureMutationIds={legacySeedMutationId,legacyConflictMutationId,legacyReplaceMutationId,restCheckMutationId,restDeleteMutationId,restRestoreMutationId,historyCompleteMutationId}` replaces the five-member schema; all seven lowercase UUID-v4 values are pairwise distinct, and former adoptionMutationId is absent/invalid. Row 4 snapshot attempt differs from rows 2-3/seedAttemptId and becomes `attemptId` for every later cook/history use. Former rows 4-33 shift to rows 5-34 byte-for-byte except their ordinal; former history-complete row 27 is row 28, owner history is row 29, visitor history rows are 30-31, and snapshot rows are 32-34. Adoption exchange array is rows 1-4, shopping 5-15, saved 16-20, tags 21-23, REST 24-25, MCP 26-27, and history 28-34. The exact derived anonymous_adoption check adds `serverWonPreserved:true` and `explicitReplaceAdopted:true`; all seven check names remain. The identity index contains 34 exchange refs plus two boundary-health refs. The eight semantic files, 21-file feature output, and 35-file aggregate remain unchanged. Unit30/31 independently tamper every new request/response/cross-attempt/preservation field; a hidden seed, already-equal server candidate, deleted legacy bytes after row 3, non-replace row 4, same attempt, old adoptionMutationId, or stale 33-reference index rejects.

- Metadata deletion has a persisted retry concern. `retry-state:v1` is now exact `{version:1,projection:RetryChannel|null,sockets:RetryChannel|null,metadata:RetryChannel|null}`; all three null deletes the key. metadata is nonnull only after logical expiry when coordinator and/or expired replay tombstones remain while current/five live keys/sockets are absent. A failed physical metadata deletion is caught; before the alarm handler returns it transactionally writes/advances the saturating 2/4/8/16/32/60-second metadata channel, sets the replacement alarm to the minimum of all concerns, and readbacks both retry state and `getAlarm()` exactly. Successful deletion clears only metadata. If replacement persistence/readback itself fails, the handler throws for the platform retry, and every invocation repeats scheduling before returning; once any invocation can write storage, retries continue independently of Cloudflare's finite automatic retry series until deletion succeeds. Unit13 injects more than six consecutive delete failures with object restarts, proves replacement-alarm readback each time, then proves physical deletion and channel clearing.

- The complete terminal D1 proof selects every and only final progress-free `CookSessionIndex` column. Exact `FeatureHistoryProjectionRowProof={attemptId,userId,recipeId,activeKey:null,status:'COMPLETED',recipeFingerprint,recipeTitle:'codex-smoke-feature-recipe',recipeCoverImageUrl:null,revision:1,projectionSeq:1,lastProjectionId,startedAt,updatedAt,completedAt,abandonedAt:null}`. The row query is an explicit all-column select by exact `(attemptId,userId,recipeId)`, rowSha256 hashes that complete row, recipe title/cover cross-equal the sanitized fixture and row-28 pinned snapshot, and activeKey must be SQL NULL after terminal UPSERT. Units30/31/33/37 independently tamper each field, including stale activeKey and title/cover, reject a partial select/hash, and reject every progress/check/count column.

- Broad cleanup uses one stable cross-attempt operation authority. Exact `QaCleanupOperationIdentity={version:1,purpose:'broad_disposable_cleanup',targetEnvironment:'qa',databaseName,r2Bucket,releaseIdentitySha256,sourceSha,treeSha,releaseRun,stageRun,logicalName:'qa-cleanup-apply-before'}` has positive run numbers and exact release/source/tree bindings; operationKeySha256 hashes its compact bytes. The operation root is task-relative `artifacts/qa-cleanup-operations/<operationKeySha256>` outside every command-attempt directory, mode 0700, no-follow under the held Desk task-root descriptor. Exact create-once `QaCleanupOperationRegistryEntry={version:1,operationIdentity,operationKeySha256,operationRoot,createdAt}` lives at `artifacts/qa-cleanup-operations/registry/<operationKeySha256>.v1.json`, is mode 0600/temp-fsync-rename-parent-fsync-readback durable before any operation-root effect, and must rederive its filename/key/root from identity. The registry is the BINARY filename-sorted set of those files; alias, duplicate key/root, malformed entry, unknown file, or symlink fails closed. `qa-cleanup-reconcile` scans every incomplete current-identity or ancestry-authorized registered root across Command attempts, StageRuns, and superseded ReleaseRuns until its terminal receipt, while a passed prior operation remains immutable and a fresh StageRun gets its distinct identity/key.

- Exact `QaCleanupLeaseReceipt={version:1,operationKeySha256,ordinal,kind:'acquire'|'renew',priorLeaseReceiptSha256,leaseName:'broad-disposable-cleanup',ownerTokenSha256,databaseName,acquiredAtMs,expiresAtMs,rowSha256,completedAt}` is mode-0600/fsync/rename/parent-fsync/readback durable before manifest selection/effect. Acquisition is ordinal 0/kind acquire/prior null; each renewal increments exactly one, is kind renew, hashes the prior receipt, and retains owner/operation while advancing expiry. Exact `QaCleanupEffectIntent={version:1,operationKeySha256,effectOrdinal,kind:'d1_transaction'|'r2_delete'|'r2_verify'|'schema_verify'|'final_oracle'|'lease_release',target,manifestPath,manifestSha256,leaseReceiptPath,leaseReceiptSha256,preStateSha256,requestSha256,preparedAt}` is create-once and durable before dispatch and points to the latest valid lease receipt. `QaCleanupEffectReceipt` now adds `intentPath,intentSha256,manifestSha256,leaseReceiptSha256` before outcome. Exact `QaCleanupLeaseReleaseReceipt={version:1,operationKeySha256,intentPath,intentSha256,leaseReceiptPath,leaseReceiptSha256,disposition:'released'|'reconciled',rowAbsent:true,completedAt}` follows returned delete or response-loss exact owner/operation row absence. Intent ordinals are contiguous through lease_release, bind the exact manifest/current lease/pre-state/request, and recovery may reconcile only that prepared effect; no intent means no absence-derived success. Unit0/31 fault every registry/lease-receipt/intent write/fsync/rename/readback, dispatch, response, receipt, release, and fresh-command recovery at every D1/R2/list/schema/oracle boundary.

- The frozen cleanup ProcessSpec is output-bearing and literal: `pnpm cleanup:qa -- --apply --journal-root <absolute stable operation root> --operation-key <operationKeySha256> --out @release-out@`. Its typed `QaCleanupCommandResult={version:1,operationIdentity,operationKeySha256,operationRoot,manifestPath,manifestSha256,leaseReceiptPath,leaseReceiptSha256,effectReceiptRefs,schemaProofPath,schemaProofSha256,finalOraclePath,finalOracleSha256,leaseReleaseReceiptPath,leaseReleaseReceiptSha256,outcome:'executed'|'reconciled',completedAt}` requires hash-valid complete refs and terminal lease-release proof. A fresh command attempt always reuses the registered root/key and writes only its per-attempt result to out. The old log-only argv/output policy and attempt-owned journal root are invalid.

- Unit33's exact prefix is now `qa-deployment-before,qa-cleanup-reconcile,qa-deploy-ensure,qa-deployment-after,qa-health,qa-cleanup-apply-before,qa-cleanup-assert-before`. There is no standalone remote `qa-preflight` before migration. On a first run with no registered incomplete cleanup root, reconcile emits exact already_clean without D1, R2, lease, or schema access; a registered incomplete operation can exist only after its owning earlier stage passed qa-deploy-ensure, and reconciliation reuses that root. qa-deploy-ensure's sole child remains exact `pnpm run deploy:qa`, whose existing skip-remote local preflight precedes build, whose QA migration installs 0024-0028, whose remote preflight runs after migration, and whose deploy is followed by captured deployment/health. Only after the new Worker signup lease gate and QaCleanupLease schema are live may broad cleanup acquire/select/mutate; only after its assert may any smoke create a disposable identity. The registry, StageRun evidence, order tests, and cleanup ownership use this replacement sequence.

- Production migration review is closed-world. Unit29.1 adds exact hardcoded `REVIEWED_NON_ADDITIVE_MIGRATIONS` with the five and only five BINARY-ordered keys `0024_qa_cleanup_coordination.sql` through `0028_recipe_tags.sql`; each value is the lowercase SHA-256 of its final committed migration bytes and a migration-specific semantic validator identifier. `deploy-production-canary.ts` computes pending-file hashes before review. A matching key/hash must pass its named semantic validator (0024 exact IF-EXISTS legacy drops plus QaCleanupLease create; 0025 bounded reconciliation/tombstone migration; 0026 exact additive cook index; 0027 exact SavedRecipe create/backfill; 0028 exact tag/search-trigger/lease migration). Any missing map entry, extra map entry, reordered statement, byte/hash drift, validator mismatch, or unknown non-additive file rejects. The live D1 applied/pending list may select the exact order-preserving unapplied suffix of those five after a prior partial apply; a gap, reordering, applied-byte mismatch, or non-suffix subset rejects, while recovery revalidates and applies only that suffix. Every unrecognized migration still passes only the existing additive grammar; the allowlist never generalizes DROP/DML/trigger acceptance. Canary tests execute/rerun all five against adversarial fixtures, lose the apply response after each prefix and recover the remaining suffix, and preserve existing DROP rejection for an unrecognized file. ProductionRelease.reviewedMigrations lists the exact names actually reviewed/applied in that attempt.

- Overlapping main merges cannot cancel or invalidate an exact automatic release. `.github/workflows/production-deploy.yml` keeps group `production-deploy`, removes cancel-in-progress, and sets literal `queue: max`. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` adds workflow concurrency with `queue:max`; main push runs share group `ci-main-push`, while each PR uses a distinct PR-qualified group, so successful main push completions enqueue production in main-push order without pending replacement. Automatic workflow_run validation retains exact successful CI id/attempt/event/path/head SHA and `HEAD===SOURCE_SHA`, but requires `SOURCE_SHA` be an ancestor of current `origin/main` rather than equal to its tip. Manual nonrollback dispatch retains exact tip equality, and rollback retains its existing trusted-tooling rules. Unit29.1 adversarially simulates three rapid main merges while an older production run is active: no pending CI/production run is cancelled, each selected workflow remains bound to its own CI/SOURCE_SHA, and each checkout/artifact/deploy evidence is exact. Unit37 continues to require its mergeSha's own queued workflow, artifact, promoted canary, and canonical health before a later queued descendant may replace it.

- Unit8-16 own legacy decision/replace and metadata retry; Units30/31 own every terminal row column; Units0/31/33 own stable cleanup operation authority, intents, typed output, and post-deploy order; Unit29.1 owns the five-migration closed review plus CI/production queue and ancestor validation; Units33/37 rederive those authorities. Every a/b/c, release-security, and workflow test named by those units carries Round85 behavior, and Units32-37 reject evidence from a pre-Round85 contract.

### Round 86 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for explicit replacement authorization/replay, browser-local identity privacy, broad-cleanup provider identity and argv, SavedRecipe rollout, reviewed-migration attestation, and monotonic production promotion. They replace every conflicting Round 85 or earlier clause for those subjects.

- A destructive legacy replacement requires a live server-issued conflict authorization and compare-and-swap. Every ordinary adoption request that returns `server_won` creates/replays a live start entry whose mutation id is the authorization id, whose attemptId and acceptedRevision bind the returned current snapshot, and whose ordinary 24-hour expiry is the authorization deadline. Exact `LegacyConflict={ordinal:positive-safe-integer,authorizationMutationId:UUID,expectedAttemptId:UUID,expectedRevision:nonnegative-safe-integer,snapshotSha256,observedAt}`; snapshotSha256 hashes the full returned CookSessionSnapshot. Exact `LegacyReplaceIntent={mutationId:UUID,authorizationMutationId:UUID,expectedAttemptId:UUID,expectedRevision:nonnegative-safe-integer}`. The current generation-fenced use decision becomes version 2 and uses opaque device namespaces below; it stores `conflict:LegacyConflict|null`, `resolution:'pending'|'keep_server'|'replace_server'`, and `replaceIntent:LegacyReplaceIntent|null`. conflict is nonnull after server_won; replaceIntent is nonnull iff replace_server and byte-equals that conflict's authorization/expected fields. Initial Use stores the ordinary mutation id before send. Keep/replace remains first-commit-wins within one conflict ordinal.

- Replace sends exact JSON `{recipeId,mutationId,adoption,adoptionMode:'replace',authorizationMutationId,expectedAttemptId,expectedRevision}`; all three replacement-bound fields are required only with literal replace, are forbidden otherwise, and participate in the start requestHash. After replay/tombstone precedence and before any queue/socket/projection mutation, the DO requires an unexpired exact server_won start entry for authorizationMutationId with the same user/recipe/attempt/revision and requires current attemptId/revision equal expectedAttemptId/expectedRevision. Missing/expired authorization returns private `409 replacement_authorization_expired`; any current mismatch returns private `409 replacement_conflict` with exact current snapshot or explicit null when absent. Both mutate nothing. A fresh accepted replace retains Round85's atomic old-attempt DELETE/new ACTIVE UPSERT/tombstone/storage transaction and returns 201/adopted.

- Exact live replace replay is checked before authorization/CAS and returns its recorded 201/adopted plus the current evolved snapshot. It settles locally after that returned snapshot is persisted and byte-read back as the authenticated canonical record; candidate equality is not required because the accepted replay proves the candidate was applied at linearization. A matching `attempt_replaced|attempt_purged` start tombstone for the replace mutation is accepted-and-superseded proof: the client GETs canonical current/idle state, persists/readbacks it, then may remove the legacy bytes/decision. If live replay and matching tombstone evidence are both absent, the server never treats the old body as authorized: expiry/mismatch causes a fresh ordinary adoption probe with a fresh mutation id, which records the next conflict ordinal and renders Keep/Replace again. Only a new user click may create its replace intent. Units8-16 fault response loss followed by same-attempt PATCH, terminal/new start, replacement, purge, tombstone expiry, authorization expiry, and both outcomes of the fresh prompt; no stale replace automatically destroys newer progress.

- Browser-local persistent identities use opaque server-issued namespaces. Migration 0026 creates `CookStorageIdentity(userId PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES User(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,userNamespace UNIQUE NOT NULL,userSecret NOT NULL,createdAt NOT NULL)`, backfills every existing User with independent 32-byte randomness, and signup/get-or-create provisions it transactionally. userNamespace is 64 lowercase hex; userSecret is 32 bytes and never leaves server memory, logs, telemetry, evidence, or a response. Exact `recipeNamespace=hex(HMAC-SHA-256(userSecret,UTF8(JSON.stringify(recipeId))))`. Authenticated cook loaders/internal responses provide exact `CookDeviceScope={version:2,userNamespace,recipeNamespace}`; neither namespace is accepted from a caller as authority.

- Every version-2 authenticated local key, Web Lock name, generation value, cleanup/reset marker, and legacy decision uses only userNamespace/recipeNamespace, never raw ids or reversible `storageIdComponent`. Active client records may contain the authenticated snapshot's ids only while that user is current. Exact long-lived values are `{version:2,userNamespace,generation}` and `{version:2,userNamespace,recipeNamespace,generation}`; marker/decision values likewise contain namespaces only. Before deleting any reversible v1 generation/key, the one-time migration writes exact raw-free `LegacyCookRetirementFence={version:2,state:'retired',installedAtMs:nonnegative-safe-integer}` at generic key `spoonjoy-auth-cook-cleanup:v1` under the existing exclusive old user authority. Every old parser treats that unknown-version global marker as fail-closed before its final write/send check; v2 recognizes only this exact sentinel as a permanent no-association retirement fence, rewrites it on removal observation before any v2 send, and treats every other malformed v1 marker as corruption. Successful recipe reset removes every matching authenticated v1 reversible key/value before retaining the opaque recipe-generation fence. Successful logout/account switch removes every departing authenticated active record, decision, recipe-generation key, reversible v1 prefix/generation/marker value, session client id, and raw/reversible authenticated id occurrence before identity confirmation is released; it retains only the generic retirement fence plus incremented opaque v2 user-generation fence needed to block delayed authenticated writers. Tests enumerate all localStorage/sessionStorage keys and parsed/raw values after reset/logout/switch and prove no departing authenticated user id, authenticated snapshot, reversible authenticated component, authenticated recipe association, or userSecret is recoverable; the only allowed v1-named authenticated residual is the exact generic retirement fence, while account-neutral anonymous recipe-key state from a pre-auth old tab may remain without identity/send authority and requires explicit adoption. Paused old authenticated writers fail closed.

- Broad cleanup is provider-bound. Exact `QaCleanupProviderIdentity={accountId,d1DatabaseName,d1DatabaseId,r2BucketName}` uses canonical Cloudflare account id, D1 UUID/name, and the account-qualified configured R2 bucket name. It is embedded byte-for-byte in QaCleanupOperationIdentity, registry entry, immutable manifest, every lease receipt, effect intent, effect receipt, lease-release receipt, and QaCleanupCommandResult; operationKeySha256 therefore changes across accounts/databases/buckets. Exact `QaCleanupProviderReadback={version:1,providerIdentity,operationKeySha256,kind:'acquire'|'renew'|'effect'|'reconcile'|'release',effectOrdinal:nonnegative-safe-integer|null,observedAt}` is obtained side-effect-free from current Cloudflare account visibility plus exact D1 get and R2 bucket visibility immediately before each acquire, renewal, dispatch/reconciliation, and release. It is durably written before the next effect, and that effect's intent/receipt carries exact `providerReadbackPath,providerReadbackSha256`. Any credential/config/resource drift blocks before product/provider mutation; absence in a different resource can never reconcile a prepared effect.

- The QA broad-apply ProcessSpec is exactly stored as `pnpm cleanup:remote:qa:apply -- --journal-root artifacts/qa-cleanup-operations/<operationKeySha256> --operation-key <operationKeySha256> --out @release-out@`. The `--journal-root` operand is a newly declared `task_relative` StoredArgv position: storage contains only the normalized task-relative value, and execution resolves it under the held task-root descriptor to an absolute path. `--out` remains task_relative. The local-only `cleanup:qa` alias is never legal for remote apply. Unit0/29.2/31/33 reject absolute stored paths, local-target aliases, provider mismatch, missing readback, or a runtime root not equal to the descriptor-resolved stored operand.

- SavedRecipe rollout closes the live old-Worker interval. Migration 0027 creates the clock/table/backfill plus exact reviewed AFTER INSERT triggers on User and RecipeInCookbook. The User trigger creates a missing NativeSyncClock from strictly normalized NEW.updatedAt. The membership trigger atomically removes an existing savedRecipe tombstone, inserts active SavedRecipe with Cookbook.authorId, NEW.recipeId, NEW.createdAt, and syncUpdatedAt allocated by the complete normalized account-high-water SQL, and advances the clock in the originating statement. D1 migration serialization means a membership committed before migration is included by backfill and one after is captured by the trigger. The new Worker dual-writes through Unit19's helper. After canonical health, QA and production use the final direct release-provider-command SavedRecipe ProcessSpec; Unit29.1.3's provider bootstrap invokes Unit19's provider-neutral idempotent helper, excludes any membership protected by a savedRecipe tombstone, writes deployment-bound before/after/zero-compatibility-eligible-missing proof, and only then enables cleanup/smoke/release success. Unit18 faults trigger/signup races, Unit19 faults helper batch/response loss, and Unit29.1.3 faults provider boundaries; no layer duplicates another.

- Reviewed D1 migrations carry immutable database-bound historical attestations. Migration 0024 creates `SpoonjoyMigrationAttestation(migrationName PRIMARY KEY,reviewedTemplateSha256,payloadSha256,templateUtf8Base64,validatorId,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,accountId,databaseId,appliedAt)` plus aborting UPDATE/DELETE triggers. Each committed 0024-0028 SQL file is a reviewed template with exactly one terminal attestation sentinel. The migration materializer accepts exact sourceSha/sourceTreeSha/provider identity, requires that commit/tree pair at dispatch, hashes the committed template and exact SQL payload preceding the sentinel, validates its named semantics, and replaces only that sentinel with one literal INSERT containing those fields plus unpadded base64 of the original exact UTF-8 template bytes. The template contains the sentinel, not its generated INSERT, so the encoding is finite and non-self-referential. It writes no tracked file. `qa:migrate` and production canary apply only the mode-0600/fsync/readback private materialized directory through Wrangler; Wrangler's per-file migration transaction atomically applies payload, attestation INSERT, and its `d1_migrations` record or rolls all of them back. Tests prove that transaction property locally and against QA before production use.

- Unit0b creates the reusable import-safe `scripts/apply-reviewed-d1-migrations.ts` engine and package alias `d1:migrate:reviewed` while only 0024 exists; Unit0c invokes exact `pnpm d1:migrate:reviewed -- --target-env local --source-sha <HEAD_SHA> --source-tree-sha <HEAD_TREE_SHA>` twice. Local materialization uses exact nonremote provider identity `{accountId:'local',databaseId:'local:'+sha256(real persisted database path)}` and never satisfies a QA/production provider check. Later migration units add templates only. Unit29.1 extends the same engine's closed validator registry after 0024-0028 are final, adds its final exhaustive tests/coverage, and rewires `qa:migrate` plus production canary; no earlier unit duplicates the engine.

- Round86's five semantic validators are exact: 0024 removes only the named legacy cleanup helpers and creates QaCleanupLease plus immutable SpoonjoyMigrationAttestation; 0025 performs only the bounded shopping reconciliation/tombstone migration; 0026 creates the full CookSessionIndex projection and CookStorageIdentity opaque-scope table/backfill; 0027 creates/backfills SavedRecipe plus its compatibility trigger; 0028 creates the controlled course/custom tag/search-trigger/lease schema. Each also requires its sole terminal attestation sentinel. This list replaces Round85's narrower 0026/0027 descriptions; unknown migrations still use only the additive grammar.

- Recovery reads both `d1_migrations` and SpoonjoyMigrationAttestation from the exact account/database. For every applied member of 0024-0028 it requires one immutable attestation, strictly decodes templateUtf8Base64 to exact UTF-8 with canonical re-encoding, locates its sole sentinel, and rederives template/payload hashes, provider fields, validator id, and deterministic materialized SQL from those database-retained bytes. It cross-equals the reviewed closed map/current template but does not require the historical branch commit to remain reachable after squash cleanup. No name/final-schema inference is allowed. An applied name without an exact attestation, an attestation without the applied name, malformed/noncanonical/changed retained template, field/hash/provider mismatch, or non-prefix blocks before applying anything. Pending remains the exact unapplied suffix and each successful apply appends one attested row. ProductionRelease.reviewedMigrations is the exact ordered array of `{migrationName,reviewedTemplateSha256,payloadSha256,validatorId,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,accountId,databaseId,outcome:'applied'|'already_attested'}`, not names. Unit29.1 loses each apply response, mutates every attestation/template field, and proves suffix recovery without inferring bytes from filenames.

- Production promotion is monotonic even when GitHub chooses an arbitrary waiting-run order. Concurrency still prevents cancellation and permits at most one active production workflow, but queue order is not an authority. Immediately before migration mutation and again before canary promotion, the workflow reads the canonical production deployment attestation. Exact `ProductionPromotionDecision={version:1,sourceSha,currentDeployedSha:string|null,relationship:'none'|'equal'|'current_is_ancestor'|'current_is_descendant',outcome:'deploy'|'reconcile_exact'|'superseded_by_descendant',observedAttestationSha256,decidedAt}`. None/current ancestor permits deploy; equal permits only exact reconciliation; current strict descendant writes a hash-bound superseded result and performs no migration/deploy/promotion; divergent/unverifiable ancestry fails closed. The decision and post-decision canonical readback are durable workflow artifacts.

- Unit29.1 simulates three merges in every legal queue order and proves production source advances only along ancestry. Unit37 accepts its mergeSha workflow only when it either promotes exact mergeSha or records superseded_by_descendant naming a currently canonical deployed SHA for which mergeSha is a verified ancestor. In the latter case every readiness/API/lifecycle/feature/visual/cleanup/final-health observation binds that actual descendant deployment, while evidence separately binds the task mergeSha ancestry and its completed exact workflow. A later ancestor can never roll production back, and an unrelated SHA can never satisfy the task.

- Round86 QA ordering is exact `qa-deployment-before,qa-cleanup-reconcile,qa-deploy-ensure,qa-deployment-after,qa-health,qa-saved-recipe-catchup,qa-cleanup-apply-before,qa-cleanup-assert-before`; production runs `production-saved-recipe-catchup` after canonical deployed health/attestation and before any cleanup or smoke identity. Both catch-up ProcessSpecs are mutation-bearing, provider/deployment-bound, idempotent, response-loss recoverable, and output-bearing under ordinary ProcessCommand authority.

- Units8-16 own conflict authorization/CAS/replay settlement and opaque local namespaces; Units18/19 own the compatibility trigger and backfill invariant; Units0/31/33 own provider-bound cleanup and legal StoredArgv; Unit0 owns the reusable 0024-capable migration engine and Unit29.1 owns its final five-template attestation registry, post-promotion SavedRecipe catch-up, and monotonic promotion; Units30/31/33/37 rederive the latest smoke/release identities. Every a/b/c/d, release-security, migration, privacy, account-switch, and workflow test named by those units carries Round86 behavior, and Units32-37 reject evidence from a pre-Round86 contract.

### Round 87 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for release-registry closure, reviewed-migration execution order, CookStorageIdentity provisioning, production workflow-result evidence, and Unit37 deployment freezing. For those subjects they replace paragraphs 143-144, 158, 163, 173, 176, 180, 183-184, 194-203, 221, 227, 230, 341, 1197, 1199, 1201, 1221, 1231, 1652, 1656, 1684, and 1688-1694 wherever those paragraphs conflict. Any old literal order, raw-Wrangler migration command, `ProductionRelease`, `production-release.json`, mergeSha-only live binding, log-only QA broad apply, or narrative-only catch-up row is historical and rejected by source/config tests.

- CookStorageIdentity provisioning has one owned creation boundary.
