Unit5 adds import-safe `app/lib/user-provisioning.server.ts` and routes password signup in `app/lib/auth.server.ts`, OAuth creation in `app/lib/oauth-user.server.ts`, and API/agent user creation in `app/lib/spoonjoy-api.server.ts` through it. Every new User and its independent random CookStorageIdentity are inserted atomically by one transaction or one nested create; no new-user commit without the identity is legal. Existing/migrated users use one idempotent get-or-create that generates candidate randomness before `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(userId) DO NOTHING`, discards a losing candidate, rereads the winner, and never returns/logs/persists the secret outside its row. Unit5 tests all three creation surfaces, rollback between parent/identity writes, duplicate namespace retry, concurrent existing-user repair, and secret non-egress.

- Reviewed migration order is executable. Unit0's closed engine and persistent local apply know only 0024. Units1c, 5c, 18c, and 24c validate their newly added template and rerun semantics only in test-owned `mkdtemp` SQLite/D1 databases that are deleted in the same test process; those tests may materialize a test-local expected sentinel replacement but may not call the package migration alias, mutate the persistent local Wrangler database, or create a durable `d1_migrations` row. Unit29.1, after every 0024-0028 template is final, installs the five-entry production validator registry and then immediately runs exact `pnpm d1:migrate:reviewed -- --target-env local --source-sha <HEAD_SHA> --source-tree-sha <HEAD_TREE_SHA>` twice against the persistent local D1. The first call validates every historical attestation and applies only the pending suffix; the second proves byte-stable `already_attested` recovery. Unit32's `local-d1-migrate-first` and `local-d1-migrate-second` both use the same reviewed argv with frozen H/B. Both CI local-migration workflow steps, `qa:migrate`, and production canary invoke only `d1:migrate:reviewed`; raw `wrangler d1 migrations apply` is forbidden after Unit0c. Workflow/source tests reject raw apply and require exact source/tree/provider binding.

- The ProcessSpec schema now has required `recoveryPolicy:'ordinary'|'registered_operation'|'idempotent_zero_missing'|'read_only_refreeze'`; every pre-Round87 row is `ordinary`. The four Round87 rows below are complete literal registry entries, including zero-based StoredArgv operands. `qa-cleanup-apply-before` is `{environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-rest',outputPolicy:'json:qa-cleanup-result.json:QaCleanupCommandResult',operationPhaseCapMs:1200000,totalTimeoutMs:1980000,recoveryPolicy:'registered_operation',storedArgv:['pnpm','cleanup:remote:qa:apply','--','--journal-root',<task-relative operation root>,'--operation-key',<operationKeySha256>,'--out',O],operands:[{index:4,kind:'task_relative'},{index:8,kind:'task_relative'}]}`. `qa-saved-recipe-catchup` is `{environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-d1',outputPolicy:'json:saved-recipe-catchup.json:SavedRecipeCatchupResult',operationPhaseCapMs:300000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'idempotent_zero_missing',storedArgv:['pnpm','saved-recipes:catchup','--','--target-env','qa','--source-sha',H,'--source-tree-sha',B,'--out',O],operands:[{index:10,kind:'task_relative'}]}`. `production-saved-recipe-catchup` is the same row with target `production`, source/tree `DS/DT`, and the same sole operand. `production-stage-deployment-freeze` is `{environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-github',outputPolicy:'json:production-stage-deployment.json:ProductionStageDeployment',operationPhaseCapMs:310000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'read_only_refreeze',storedArgv:['pnpm','release:provenance','--','freeze-production-stage','--repo','spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2','--task-source-sha',S,'--task-tree-sha',B,'--workflow-result',K,'--script','spoonjoy-v2','--base-url',PB,'--out',O],operands:[{index:11,kind:'task_relative'},{index:17,kind:'task_relative'}]}`. Here K is the task-relative passed `production-workflow-result.json` path; DS/DT/DV are the frozen ProductionStageDeployment deployed source/tree/version. `--journal-root` and `--workflow-result` join `--manifest|--out|--intent|--arm|--dispatch` as the only task-relative flag values; no absolute stored value is legal.

- QA broad cleanup output is exact/no-extra-fields `QaCleanupCommandResult={version:1,operationIdentity,providerIdentity,operationKeySha256,operationRoot,manifestPath,manifestSha256,leaseReceiptPath,leaseReceiptSha256,providerReadbackRefs,effectReceiptRefs,schemaProofPath,schemaProofSha256,finalOraclePath,finalOracleSha256,leaseReleaseReceiptPath,leaseReleaseReceiptSha256,outcome,completedAt}`. `providerIdentity` byte-equals every nested authority; `providerReadbackRefs` and `effectReceiptRefs` are contiguous effect-order `{path,sha256}` arrays and prove fresh provider readback before acquire, every renew/effect/reconcile, and release. `outcome` is `executed|reconciled`. A new command after response loss reuses only the registered operation root/key, validates all immutable prefixes, and resumes or reconciles the next prepared effect; it never reevaluates selectors or infers success from another provider. Only atomic `qa-cleanup-result.json` may satisfy the output policy.

- SavedRecipe catch-up proof is provider/deployment readback based, not argv-echo based. Exact `CloudflareDatabaseIdentity={accountId,d1DatabaseName,d1DatabaseId}` is target-neutral and excludes R2. Exact `SavedRecipeCatchupProviderReadback={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',providerIdentity:CloudflareDatabaseIdentity,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,workerVersionId,deploymentAttestationSha256,observedAt}` and exact `SavedRecipeCatchupResult={version:1,targetEnvironment,operationKeySha256,providerBefore,providerBeforeSha256,beforeMissingCount,insertedCount,providerAfter,providerAfterSha256,afterMissingCount:0,disposition:'executed'|'observed_complete',completedAt}` are no-extra-fields. The operation key hashes target, provider identity, source/tree/version, and deployment-attestation hash. Before and after are independently fetched from current Cloudflare account/D1 identity plus canonical deployed health/attestation, byte-equal except observation time, and must match the frozen QA H/B/version or Unit37 DS/DT/DV. `executed` requires positive beforeMissingCount, insertedCount equal to it, and the one idempotent `INSERT OR IGNORE ... SELECT` transaction; `observed_complete` requires both counts zero and performs no insert. A dead/response-lost predecessor may be retried only through a fresh command/readback; observing zero is sufficient release evidence without inventing the predecessor's count or response. Provider/deployment drift, missing trigger, a nonzero after count, extra output, or a second mutation kind fails.

- Production workflow artifact evidence is the exact `production-workflow-result.json` discriminated union `ProductionWorkflowResult={version:1,outcome:'promoted'|'reconcile_exact'|'superseded_by_descendant',taskSourceSha,taskTreeSha,runAttempt,phase:'complete',triggeringCiDatabaseId,triggeringCiRunAttempt,promotionDecision,promotionDecisionSha256,postDecisionReadback,postDecisionReadbackSha256,reviewedMigrations,migrationApply,databaseRollbackSupported:false,deployedSourceSha,deployedTreeSha,deployedVersionId,previousVersionId,candidateVersionId,promotionReceiptSha256,zeroMutationProof}`. Exact `ProductionWorkflowProviderReadback={version:1,script:'spoonjoy-v2',deploymentId,sourceSha,treeSha,workerVersionId,attestationSha256,observedAt}` is the required postDecisionReadback type. Common identities equal the selected completed-success task CI/workflow and exact task merge/tree; promotionDecision/readback are embedded exact objects whose hashes validate; reviewedMigrations is Round86's ordered attestation-object array. `promoted` requires decision `deploy`, deployed source/tree equal task, `migrationApply:'succeeded'|'not_needed'`, distinct nonnull previous/candidate IDs with candidate equal deployedVersionId, nonnull promotion receipt, and null zeroMutationProof. `reconcile_exact` requires decision `reconcile_exact`, deployed source/tree equal task, `migrationApply:'not_needed'`, null previous/candidate/promotion receipt, and exact nonnull zero-mutation proof. `superseded_by_descendant` requires decision `superseded_by_descendant`, taskSourceSha a strict verified ancestor of deployedSourceSha, `migrationApply:'not_needed'`, null previous/candidate/promotion receipt, and exact nonnull zero-mutation proof. Exact `ProductionZeroMutationProof={version:1,reason:'equal'|'strict_descendant',decisionSha256,beforeAttestationSha256,afterAttestationSha256,providerMutationCount:0,observedAt}` requires equal before/after canonical provider bytes. `download-artifact` re-fetches the workflow artifact, CI/workflow identity, canonical provider readback, and Git tree/ancestry independently; it cannot trust artifact assertions alone.

- Every Unit37 StageRun freezes current production independently of the workflow's historical outcome. Exact `ProductionDeploymentReadback={version:1,script:'spoonjoy-v2',deploymentId,sourceSha,treeSha,workerVersionId,healthBodySha256,healthHeaderSha256,observedAt}`, `GitAncestryProof={repo:'spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2',ancestorSha,descendantSha,mergeBaseSha,relationship:'equal'|'ancestor'}`, and `ProductionStageDeployment={version:1,taskSourceSha,taskTreeSha,workflowResultPath,workflowResultSha256,readback,readbackSha256,ancestry,ancestrySha256,deployedSourceSha,deployedTreeSha,deployedVersionId,relationship:'equal'|'task_is_ancestor',observedAt}` are no-extra-fields. Freeze performs fresh canonical provider/health and Git reads after artifact validation; exact task or a strict descendant passes, while ancestor/divergent/unverifiable state fails. Workflow outcome does not dictate the frozen identity. DS/DT/DV and `deploymentIdentitySha256=sha256(canonical ProductionStageDeployment bytes)` remain immutable for that StageRun.

- Final ProcessCommand and AgentCommand schemas add `deploymentIdentitySha256:Sha256|null`. It is null for every command before `production-stage-deployment-freeze` and every non-Unit37 command; it byte-equals the frozen hash for every later Unit37 command and visual reviewer. Unit37 visual prompts/reports include deployed source, deployed version, and deployment-identity hash; ReviewInput.sourceSha is DS while task mergeSha remains separately in stage/workflow evidence. Every later argv that previously used live S uses DS and DV, and every output/identity observation cross-equals DS/DV. If any boundary read observes another valid descendant, the current command/stage fails with deployment drift and a fresh same-merge Unit37 StageRun refreezes; ancestor/divergent drift fails closed. Final health is the closing canonical read. A legal exact-then-descendant queue therefore cannot strand the task.

- Round87's closed Unit33/37 orders are historical. Round100's qualification-bearing orders below are the only executable orders. Only the two visual review names remain AgentCommand; old `qa-preflight`, log-only cleanup rows, omitted qualification/catch-up/freeze names, and every different order are illegal.

- `release-artifact-download` is now `github,json:production-workflow-result.json:ProductionWorkflowResult` with its existing task merge/tree/workflow argv and artifact name. Unit37 evidence replaces `productionRelease:ProductionRelease` with adjacent `workflowResult:ProductionWorkflowResult,stageDeployment:ProductionStageDeployment,deploymentIdentitySha256`; every deployed live aggregate member uses DS/DT/DV while `ciWorkflow`, selected workflow, taskSourceSha/taskTreeSha, mergeSha, and ancestry evidence retain task provenance. Unit29.2 red/green tests compare the complete new registry/order, exact basenames/schemas/operands/environment policies/deadlines/recovery policies, all three workflow-result variants, exact-then-descendant stage retry, and rejection of any pre-Round87 evidence.

### Round 88 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for User-insertion closure, production-freeze credentials, and descendant-drift disposition. They replace Round87's narrower three-surface provisioning sentence, its `cloudflare-github` freeze policy, and paragraphs 173, 176, 212-214, 495, and every later conflicting terminal-code/retry statement for these subjects.

- Every executable non-test User insertion is registry-owned. Unit5's source gate parses all tracked production/configured-seed TypeScript, JavaScript, MJS, SQL, and Prisma files. The exact direct User-insertion primitives are `app/lib/user-provisioning.server.ts` and `scripts/seed-qa.mjs`; exact shared-helper callers are `app/lib/auth.server.ts`, `app/lib/oauth-user.server.ts`, `app/lib/spoonjoy-api.server.ts`, and `prisma/seed.ts`; migration 0026 is separately authorized only to backfill CookStorageIdentity for preexisting Users. Any other Prisma `User.create|createMany|upsert`, nested User create, raw `INSERT ... User`, direct provisioning-helper caller, or dynamically assembled equivalent fails. Test/fixture-only insertions are separately enumerated and cannot enter production output. `prisma/seed.ts` calls the dependency-injected import-safe shared provisioning transaction so each seed User and CookStorageIdentity commit together, including OAuth child creation where present. `scripts/seed-qa.mjs` uses database-side `lower(hex(randomblob(32)))` and `randomblob(32)` inside one `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction containing User plus CookStorageIdentity insertion/repair; the identity INSERT uses conflict handling only for existing userId, so a user-namespace collision aborts the whole transaction. After COMMIT the same Wrangler command returns only exact scalar projection `{identityCount:1,namespaceValid:1,secretLengthValid:1}`; no namespace or secret bytes leave D1. The script makes at most three complete Wrangler transaction attempts; each retry reevaluates database randomness, while response-loss replay and ordinary rerun preserve an existing identity byte-for-byte. Units5a/c fault parent/identity/OAuth/commit/readback boundaries, all three attempts, collision/retry/exhaustion, response loss, existing-user repair, and prove no userSecret in dry-run SQL bytes, logs, responses, telemetry, or evidence.

- The eighth and final environment policy is `cloudflare-rest-github`. Its public preflight is the exact union of `github` and `cloudflare-rest`: trusted-gh auth/Actions proofs plus trusted Wrangler account proof. Its ProcessPreflightSecret has `cloudflareD1ApiToken:null` and the same one-shot nonempty `cloudflareRestBearer` as `cloudflare-rest`; the bearer uses the existing `cloudflare_oauth` DescriptorSecretChannel/fd/cap and is never placed in env, argv, output, or persisted proof. Capability preflight completes both public halves before start-command; any missing half creates zero manifest/provider mutation. `production-stage-deployment-freeze` replaces only its environmentPolicy with `cloudflare-rest-github`; argv, output, operands, deadlines, and recoveryPolicy remain Round87-exact. The child uses authenticated Cloudflare REST for canonical deployment and health plus trusted gh/GitHub reads for commit/tree ancestry. No other ProcessSpec may use this policy, and no undeclared credential resolver is legal.

- Descendant drift has a total, nonrepair retry path. `PostMergeTerminalCode` adds exact `deployment_descendant_advanced`; `PostMergeFailureDisposition.classification` adds exact `deployment_refreeze`. Exact no-extra-fields `DeploymentDriftEvidence={version:1,taskSourceSha,frozenDeploymentIdentitySha256,frozenSourceSha,frozenTreeSha,frozenVersionId,observedSourceSha,observedTreeSha,observedVersionId,ancestry:GitAncestryProof,providerReadback:ProductionDeploymentReadback,providerReadbackSha256,observedAt}` is written/hash-checked before disposition and requires observedSourceSha to be a current canonical strict descendant of frozenSourceSha and taskSourceSha, with matching tree/version/provider bytes. Between commands the tuple is `attempt:null,commandMode:'preflight'`; inside a process or agent boundary it uses that positive attempt and matching commandMode. Any equal, ancestor, divergent, stale, or unverifiable observation cannot use this code.

- The sole disposition row for `(deployment_descendant_advanced,any positive drift history,any post-freeze Unit37 logicalName,preflight|process|agent,that row's environmentPolicy)` is `{classification:'deployment_refreeze',maxRetries:0,runFailureReason:null,repairCode:null}` with null predecessor archive fields. maxRetries zero means this classification never consumes the ordinary transient-retry budget; it authorizes exactly one replacement StageRun for the immutable frozenDeploymentIdentitySha256, and the same disposition/evidence replay is byte-idempotent. After evidence and disposition are pushed/read back, recovery terminalizes the old StageRun failed if needed and appends exactly one fresh same-ReleaseRun/same-merge Unit37 StageRun beginning at production workflow discovery and ending in a new freeze. A crash before disposition push leaves the old stage recoverable; after push, zero or one replacement stage is reconciled by exact predecessor/disposition linkage. It never enters external_wait, fails the ReleaseRun, or creates a repair successor. A further distinct canonical descendant is a new frozen-identity transition and follows the same path. Unit29.2 exhausts preflight/process/agent detection, every write/push/stage-start crash boundary, duplicate recovery, chained A->B->C advances, and rejection of code/context drift.

### Round 89 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for D1 seed atomicity, replacement-authorization replay, migration-first User and shopping compatibility, historical User-insertion ownership, Cloudflare REST secret transport, and executable production deployment guards. They replace Round86's acceptedRevision-derived replacement authority, Round87's narrower 0025/0026 validators and post-health order, and Round88's conflicting seed transaction, bearer non-environment, drift evidence, and disposition wording. Every source/config/schema test rejects the superseded forms.

- QA seed atomicity uses the documented D1 batch API, never explicit SQL transaction tokens. Unit5 adds one import-safe Cloudflare D1 query client used by `scripts/seed-qa.mjs`; it resolves the canonical QA account/database from trusted Wrangler read-only JSON, captures the bounded Wrangler OAuth resolver stdout only into parent memory, and sends exactly one `POST /accounts/{accountId}/d1/database/{databaseId}/query` body `{batch:[{sql,params},...]}` per attempt. Each array member is one parameterized statement; `BEGIN`, `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, `COMMIT`, semicolon-joined pseudo-transactions, a temporary SQL file, and multiple write requests are forbidden. D1's batch transaction is the sole commit boundary: any failed member rolls back User, identity, and all seed rows. The final batch member is the only SELECT and returns exact scalar row `{identityCount:1,namespaceValid:1,secretLengthValid:1}`; the parser requires a successful top-level response, one success result per requested member, no top-level errors, and that exact final row, then discards provider metadata and every earlier result. The bearer is never forwarded in argv, an environment, a descriptor child, a file, a log, or durable output; only the bounded resolver capture and in-process Authorization header see it, and both are dropped after fetch settles. At most three complete batch requests are legal. A namespace-unique failure retries the whole batch with newly evaluated database randomness; response loss may replay the idempotent full batch, which preserves an existing identity because the identity statement generates/inserts only when that userId is still missing. Dry-run emits only statement identifiers and parameter arities, never SQL literals, credentials, namespace, or secret bytes. Unit5 tests official response shapes, member failure rollback, response loss after commit, all retry boundaries, malformed/extra results, and byte-for-byte identity stability.

- Historical User inserts are closed without pretending they do not exist. The exact non-test insertion registry includes immutable `migrations/0002_seed.sql` and `migrations/0004_reseed.sql` as `legacy_preidentity_seed` primitives in addition to Round88's runtime/configured-seed entries. They are legal only as already-applied numeric predecessors of 0026 or inside a disposable full-history migration fixture; after 0026 is applied they may not be selected as a pending migration, invoked by a package script/workflow/config, or executed standalone. The reviewed-migration engine reads the complete ordered `d1_migrations` history and requires both names, when present, to precede 0026; an absent historical seed remains absent, but a pending/reordered/duplicate historical seed blocks before mutation. Source tests parse their raw INSERTs, reject any third historical User insertion, and prove 0002/0004 -> 0026 backfill as well as prohibited post-0026 selection.

- Migration 0026, not only the new Worker, owns the live User-creation interval. `CookStorageIdentity` enforces exact database CHECKs `length(userNamespace)=64`, `userNamespace=lower(userNamespace)`, `userNamespace NOT GLOB '*[^0-9a-f]*'`, and `length(userSecret)=32` in addition to its user/unique/not-null constraints. After creating it and backfilling every existing User, 0026 creates exact permanent `AFTER INSERT ON User` trigger `cook_storage_identity_after_user_insert`. When the inserted user lacks an identity, the trigger performs a plain, non-ignoring INSERT with `lower(hex(randomblob(32)))`, `randomblob(32)`, and the database timestamp; a userNamespace collision aborts the parent User statement and cannot commit an identity-less User. The shared provisioning transaction inserts User, then executes idempotent `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(userId) DO NOTHING` only for runtimes whose disposable schema lacks the trigger, rereads exactly one valid identity, and retries the complete User transaction at most three times only for a namespace collision. With the trigger present that fallback is a no-op; every OAuth child remains in the same outer transaction. Migration rollback/rerun, old-Worker signup between migration and deploy, old/new concurrent creation, seed insertion, trigger collision, parent failure, and `SELECT User LEFT JOIN CookStorageIdentity WHERE identity IS NULL` zero after every commit are mandatory tests. The trigger remains permanent rollback defense and 0026's semantic validator requires its exact table checks, SQL, and sole name.

- Migration 0025 closes old shopping writers through a dirty-list compatibility ledger without double-counting new writes. It creates exact `ShoppingListCompatibilityDirty(shoppingListId TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES ShoppingList(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,requiredNextSortIndex INTEGER NOT NULL,latestUpdatedAt TEXT NOT NULL,firstObservedAt TEXT NOT NULL)` plus exact permanent AFTER INSERT, AFTER UPDATE, and AFTER DELETE triggers on `ShoppingListItem`. Each trigger UPSERTs its list, keeps the greatest legal `sortIndex+1` (or the existing parent floor for a delete), keeps the BINARY-greatest row/database timestamp, and preserves the earliest firstObservedAt; invalid/out-of-range input aborts. Before each new shared-helper attempt reads version V, one AtomicSqlPort batch reconciles any dirty row by advancing that list's mutationVersion exactly once, raising nextSortIndex to the recorded floor, raising updatedAt monotonically, and deleting the dirty row. The later lease batch's item statements fire the same triggers; after its one explicit parent version/index/timestamp update, it deletes only its own list's newly created dirty row before commit. D1 batch serialization makes that deletion unable to erase another writer's committed observation. Abort rolls back item, parent, and dirty-row changes together. Old-Worker writes after migration always leave a dirty row; new-helper writes leave none. Tests interleave every old create/update/soft-delete/hard-delete and every new add/check/delete/clear at every transaction boundary and prove no missed mutation, no double version increment, no index regression, and exact retry behavior.

- Post-health shopping reconciliation is a registered release effect. Exact `ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',providerIdentity:CloudflareDatabaseIdentity,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,workerVersionId,deploymentAttestationSha256,observedAt}` and exact `ShoppingCompatibilityCatchupResult={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',operationKeySha256,providerBefore:ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback,providerBeforeSha256,beforeDirtyListCount,reconciledListCount,providerAfter:ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback,providerAfterSha256,afterDirtyListCount:0,disposition:'executed'|'observed_complete',completedAt}` use the same frozen source/tree/version rules as SavedRecipe catch-up. The operation key hashes target, provider identity, frozen source/tree/version, and deployment attestation. `executed` requires positive beforeDirtyListCount, one D1 batch transaction that advances each currently dirty parent once and deletes those ledger rows, reconciledListCount equal to the transaction's positive changed-parent count, and a fresh zero readback; `observed_complete` requires both dirty counts and reconciledListCount zero and performs no write. Before/after readbacks are byte-equal except observedAt. A concurrent old writer producing a nonzero after count fails recoverably and retries. The complete ProcessSpecs are `qa-shopping-compat-catchup={environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-d1',outputPolicy:'json:shopping-compat-catchup.json:ShoppingCompatibilityCatchupResult',operationPhaseCapMs:300000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'idempotent_zero_missing',storedArgv:['pnpm','shopping:compat:catchup','--','--target-env','qa','--source-sha',H,'--source-tree-sha',B,'--out',O],operands:[{index:10,kind:'task_relative'}]}` and the same production row with target `production`, source/tree `DS/DT`, and the same operand. The final Unit33 order inserts `qa-shopping-compat-catchup` immediately after `qa-health` and before `qa-saved-recipe-catchup`; the final Unit37 order inserts `production-shopping-compat-catchup` immediately after `production-health` and before `production-saved-recipe-catchup`. Cleanup and smoke cannot begin before both catch-ups prove zero.

- Script measurement grows with the registered catch-up rather than hiding it. The exact BINARY-sorted pre-visual executable set is Round86's sixteen paths plus `scripts/shopping-compat-catchup.mjs`, for seventeen; Unit31 adds only `scripts/production-visual-dogfood.mjs`, for eighteen final. Pure import-safe `scripts/cloudflare-d1-query.mjs` is additively covered and typechecked but is not an executable member. Unit30/31 source gates reject every old sixteen/seventeen count, omission, substitution, duplicate, or unmeasured task-owned script.

- Replacement authority is an immutable exact-snapshot grant, not a mutable interpretation of acceptedRevision. StartEntry becomes exact `{mutationId,requestHash,attemptId,statusCode:200|201,adoptionOutcome,acceptedRevision,authorizationSnapshotSha256:Sha256|null,acceptedAtMs,expiresAtMs}`; authorizationSnapshotSha256 is nonnull iff the accepted outcome was `server_won` and hashes the snapshot returned at that acceptance. Start success data becomes exact `{snapshot,adoptionOutcome,replacementAuthorization}` where exact `ReplacementAuthorization={authorizationMutationId:UUID,expectedAttemptId:UUID,expectedRevision:nonnegative-safe-integer,snapshotSha256:Sha256,expiresAtMs:nonnegative-safe-integer}` or null. Initial server_won returns a grant matching the entry. Exact replay returns a grant only while current attemptId, revision, and full snapshot hash still byte-equal that immutable entry; if the current snapshot evolved, replay still returns the canonical evolved snapshot and recorded server_won outcome but replacementAuthorization is null. No other outcome returns a grant. LegacyConflict may be created only from a nonnull returned grant. An evolved replay is persisted/read back locally but preserves raw legacy/decision bytes and sends a fresh ordinary adoption probe under a fresh mutation id; that fresh response alone may render a new Keep/Replace prompt and only a new click creates an intent. Replace authorization validates the entry's immutable snapshot hash in addition to id/attempt/revision/expiry before the current-state CAS. Every start envelope, OpenAPI/internal type, raw HTTP projector, and smoke fixture includes and validates the field; the projector may discard it only after validation. In the feature registry legacy_server_seed and legacy_replace carry null, while legacy_use_conflict carries the exact nonnull grant that authorizes the row-4 request. Units8-16 and 29.2/30/31 cover exact no-evolution replay, response loss followed by same-attempt PATCH, evolved replay with no authority, fresh probe/prompt, stale previously displayed grants, both fresh choices, and every missing/extra/hash-mismatched wire field.

- Cloudflare REST secret transport has one explicit gate/child matrix. `cloudflare-rest` and `cloudflare-rest-github` preflight/gate environments contain only their declared public variables plus the decimal descriptor marker; they never contain `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`. After consuming and closing that descriptor, the trusted bootstrap injects `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` only into the registered first-party REST process tree, removes the marker and both descriptor ends, and execs its exact pnpm entry. Only that pnpm entry and its one registered Node script entry may inherit the token; the Node entry copies the bounded token into private memory before its first await, deletes `process.env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` before spawning gh or any other child, and uses only in-memory Authorization headers. `cloudflare-d1` retains its separately declared D1-token channel and matrix. Exact source tests enumerate every policy's gate environment and registered process-tree environment, prove the REST token is present only at this bootstrap -> exact pnpm -> exact Node edge, and reject inheritance by any unregistered descendant, gh, Wrangler, visual agent, log, error, manifest, or output. Round88's phrase `never placed in env` means never placed in a gate, caller, persisted, or unregistered environment; it does not forbid this registered process-tree injection.

- Deployment drift evidence has an executable producer at every later boundary. Unit29.2 defines one immutable `ProductionDeploymentGuardSpec={environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-rest-github',storedArgv:['pnpm','release:provenance','--','probe-production-deployment','--repo','spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2','--task-source-sha',S,'--frozen-deployment',FD,'--script','spoonjoy-v2','--base-url',PB,'--out',O],operands:[{index:9,kind:'task_relative'},{index:15,kind:'task_relative'}],outputBasename:'production-deployment-probe.json',outputSchema:'ProductionDeploymentProbe',operationPhaseCapMs:310000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000}`; FD is the task-relative frozen ProductionStageDeployment path. Exact `ProductionDeploymentProbe={version:1,boundaryOrdinal:positive-safe-integer,phase:'before'|'after',ownerLogicalName,ownerCommandMode:'preflight'|'process'|'agent',ownerAttempt:positive-safe-integer|null,taskSourceSha,frozenDeploymentIdentitySha256,frozenSourceSha,frozenTreeSha,frozenVersionId,readback:ProductionDeploymentReadback|null,readbackSha256:Sha256|null,ancestry:GitAncestryProof|null,ancestrySha256:Sha256|null,relationship:'equal'|'strict_descendant'|'invalid',observedAt}` is no-extra-fields. Equal requires nonnull matching readback/hashes and equal ancestry; strict_descendant requires nonnull readback/hashes plus strict ancestor proof; invalid permits null only for the observation that could not be validated and can never produce drift evidence. Before every post-freeze logical name, including a visual AgentCommand, the executor runs a descriptor-backed read-only guard before reserving/dispatching the owner attempt (`preflight`, null attempt); after every process result or agent report and before owner finish, it runs another guard with that owner's positive attempt/mode. The final health therefore also has a closing after-guard. Equal is the only release condition. Invalid ancestry/provider state is provenance_failed. Strict descendant is projected directly into hash-valid DeploymentDriftEvidence and prevents owner dispatch or acceptance.

- Guard execution is crash-recoverable rather than an unjournaled subprocess. Each stage-relative `deployment-guards/<boundaryOrdinal>-<phase>-<ownerLogicalName>/attempt-<processAttemptOrdinal>/` has immutable intent, descriptor spawn/ready/authorization/run-ack, sanitized stdout/stderr, parsed output when available, terminal command, and exact `DeploymentGuardAttempt={version:1,stageRun,boundaryOrdinal,phase,ownerLogicalName,ownerCommandMode,ownerAttempt,processAttemptOrdinal:positive-safe-integer,executionHost,priorAttemptPaths,priorAttemptSetSha256,probeSpecSha256,intentPath,intentSha256,outputPath:string|null,outputSha256:Sha256|null,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,outcome:'equal'|'strict_descendant'|'invalid'|'indeterminate',completedAt}`. Prior paths are the exact BINARY-sorted terminal GuardAttempt paths for lower ordinals and use the ordinary complete-file set hash; output path/hash are both nonnull exactly for equal/strict_descendant/invalid with a parsed ProductionDeploymentProbe and both null for indeterminate. A crash before run authorization may replace the proven-dead child at the next ordinal; after run authorization the read-only probe may be rerun at the next ordinal because it has no effect, but no historical observation is fabricated or overwritten. At most three fresh process attempts are legal for one boundary; exhausted indeterminate attempts enter the existing typed external-wait path. A retained equal result authorizes only its adjacent boundary and cannot be reused for another ordinal. A strict-descendant result is the sole producer of DeploymentDriftEvidence. Unit29.2 faults every descriptor/process/output/push/readback boundary and rejects missing, skipped, overwritten, stale, wrong-owner, wrong-frozen-identity, or log-inferred guard evidence.

- Drift disposition context is total. `PostMergeFailureDisposition.environmentPolicy` is nullable: it is null iff commandMode is `preflight|agent`, and for process it equals that ProcessSpec's policy. For `deployment_descendant_advanced`, preflight uses `(attempt:null,commandMode:'preflight',environmentPolicy:null)`, process uses its positive attempt and exact policy, and agent uses its positive attempt with null policy. The deployment_refreeze row exists for exactly those tuples and every post-freeze logical name; no wording such as `that row's environmentPolicy` applies to AgentCommand. Probe execution itself always uses the combined policy but does not replace the owner tuple. Unit29.2 exhausts both visual reviewers, every process policy represented after freeze, all three modes, nullability mismatches, and chained refreezes.

### Round 90 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for shopping compatibility trigger/lease safety, old-deployment quiescence and terminal reconciliation, REST child execution, executable coverage closure, deployment-guard context/projection, and command terminalization on post-effect drift. They replace the conflicting Round89 shopping trigger/catch-up, pnpm REST process-tree, script-count, ProductionDeploymentGuardSpec/Probe/Attempt, and drift-boundary sentences.

- Shopping compatibility is cascade-safe and lease-fenced. The AFTER INSERT and AFTER UPDATE triggers retain Round89's exact UPSERT. The AFTER DELETE trigger performs the same UPSERT through `INSERT ... SELECT ... FROM ShoppingList WHERE id=OLD.shoppingListId`; direct item deletion therefore records dirty state while a list/user cascade whose parent is no longer selectable inserts nothing and cannot fail a foreign key. A cascade-created transient dirty row, if the parent remains selectable during child deletion, is itself removed by the same parent cascade. Every shared-helper lease acquisition adds exact predicate `NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ShoppingListCompatibilityDirty WHERE shoppingListId=<listId>)` in the same conditional parent UPDATE as `mutationVersion=V AND mutationLease IS NULL`. If an old Worker commits after pre-reconciliation/snapshot and before lease acquisition, its dirty row makes acquisition affect zero rows; the helper performs no item statement, rereads/reconciles, and restarts under the existing retry schedule. D1 serialization still prevents an old commit between successful lease acquisition and same-transaction own-trigger deletion. Unit1 faults direct item delete, list/user cascades in both SQLite trigger orders, cleanup, migration replay, and the exact old-write-between-snapshot-and-lease interleaving.

- The migration/deploy interval closes with provider quiescence, catch-up, and a terminal read-only barrier while permanent triggers/read repair remain the runtime defense. Exact `WorkerTrafficAllocation={versionId,percentage:integer 0..100}` is BINARY-version-sorted and sums to 100. Exact `ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample={observedAt,script:'spoonjoy-v2',sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,workerVersionId,allocations}` requires the frozen version at 100 and no other positive allocation. Exact `ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceResult={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',operationKeySha256,samples:[ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample,ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample,ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample,ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample,ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample,ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceSample],drainStartedAt,drainEndedAt,disposition:'quiescent',completedAt}` requires `samples[0..2].observedAt=drainStartedAt-10000,drainStartedAt-5000,drainStartedAt`, `drainEndedAt=drainStartedAt+120000`, `samples[3..5].observedAt=drainEndedAt,drainEndedAt+5000,drainEndedAt+10000`, and `completedAt>=samples[5].observedAt`; all six provider reads match the same frozen deployment. A mismatch in the first triple discards that triple and restarts its 0/5/10 sequence; a mismatch during the drain or final triple discards the entire candidate and restarts from the first triple. All retries remain inside the 310000ms operation phase cap. The quiescence operation is read-only and response-loss rerunnable.

- The complete new ProcessSpecs are `qa-shopping-compat-quiescence={environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-rest',outputPolicy:'json:shopping-compat-quiescence.json:ShoppingCompatibilityQuiescenceResult',operationPhaseCapMs:310000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'read_only_refreeze',storedArgv:['node','scripts/shopping-compat-catchup.mjs','--target-env','qa','--mode','wait-for-worker-quiescence','--source-sha',H,'--source-tree-sha',B,'--out',O],operands:[{index:11,kind:'task_relative'}]}` and the same production row with target production and DS/DT. Exact `ShoppingCompatibilityAssertResult={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',operationKeySha256,providerReadback:ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback,providerReadbackSha256,dirtyListCount:0,disposition:'clean',completedAt}` is read-only. `qa-shopping-compat-assert-after={environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-d1',outputPolicy:'json:shopping-compat-assert.json:ShoppingCompatibilityAssertResult',operationPhaseCapMs:300000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'idempotent_zero_missing',storedArgv:['pnpm','shopping:compat:catchup','--','--target-env','qa','--mode','assert-clean','--source-sha',H,'--source-tree-sha',B,'--out',O],operands:[{index:12,kind:'task_relative'}]}` and production substitutes production/DS/DT. The final Unit33 order is `qa-deployment-before,qa-cleanup-reconcile,qa-deploy-ensure,qa-deployment-after,qa-health,qa-shopping-compat-quiescence,qa-shopping-compat-catchup,qa-saved-recipe-catchup,qa-cleanup-apply-before,qa-cleanup-assert-before,qa-api-smoke,qa-lifecycle-smoke,qa-feature-smoke,qa-cleanup-assert-after,qa-shopping-compat-assert-after,qa-final-health`. The final Unit37 order is `ci-workflow-discovery,workflow-discovery,workflow-watch,deploy-job,release-artifact-download,production-deployment-after,production-stage-deployment-freeze,production-health,production-shopping-compat-quiescence,production-shopping-compat-catchup,production-saved-recipe-catchup,production-readiness,production-cleanup-reconcile,production-cleanup-assert-before,production-api-smoke,production-lifecycle-smoke,production-feature-smoke,production-visual-dogfood,production-visual-qa-review,production-visual-design-review,production-cleanup-assert-after,continuation-scan,production-shopping-compat-assert-after,production-final-health`. A late dirty row fails the terminal barrier; no target command follows final health. Every new shopping read/sync/mutation also reconciles its list before deriving version/cursor/output, so a pathological later old write remains safely pending rather than corrupting a new response.

- REST secrets cross no package-manager or shell intermediary. Every ProcessSpec or guard with `cloudflare-rest|cloudflare-rest-github` must have direct StoredArgv `['node',<one exact first-party entry>,...]`; trusted-toolchain resolution pins that Node executable and no pnpm script or shell is launched. Round87's QA broad-apply row is replaced by `{environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-rest',outputPolicy:'json:qa-cleanup-result.json:QaCleanupCommandResult',operationPhaseCapMs:1200000,totalTimeoutMs:1980000,recoveryPolicy:'registered_operation',storedArgv:['node','scripts/cleanup-local-qa-data.mjs','--target-env','qa','--apply','--journal-root',<task-relative operation root>,'--operation-key',<operationKeySha256>,'--out',O],operands:[{index:6,kind:'task_relative'},{index:10,kind:'task_relative'}]}`. Round88's freeze row is replaced by direct `storedArgv:['node','--import','tsx','scripts/release-provenance.ts','freeze-production-stage','--repo','spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2','--task-source-sha',S,'--task-tree-sha',B,'--workflow-result',K,'--script','spoonjoy-v2','--base-url',PB,'--out',O]` with task-relative operands at indices 12 and 18 and otherwise unchanged policy/output/deadlines/recovery. The gate passes only the nonsecret decimal marker plus the one-shot OAuth descriptor to that direct trusted Node process; it never creates `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`. The first-party entry synchronously reads at most 4096 bytes to EOF, closes the descriptor, unsets the marker, retains the bearer only in private memory, and does so before its first network request or child spawn. The pinned Node/tsx loader process is the registered trust boundary, but no package manager, shell, environment bearer, or later child exists. Source tests enumerate every REST-policy registry row and reject pnpm/yarn/npm, shell, another entry, environment injection, leaked descriptor/marker, late consumption, or child inheritance.

- Executable measurement includes the seed work it changes. Round89's pre-visual executable set adds `scripts/seed-qa.mjs` as well as `scripts/shopping-compat-catchup.mjs` to Round86's sixteen, for exact eighteen; Unit31 adds only `scripts/production-visual-dogfood.mjs`, for exact nineteen final. The separate exact seed-entry registry is `[prisma/seed.ts,scripts/seed-qa.mjs]`; Unit5 focused coverage measures both plus pure `scripts/cloudflare-d1-query.mjs` at 100%, and the global script gate independently includes seed-qa. Every old seventeen/eighteen count or omission rejects.

- The deployment child produces only provider observation; Unit29.2 authenticates boundary context. Replacement direct `ProductionDeploymentGuardSpec={environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-rest-github',storedArgv:['node','--import','tsx','scripts/release-provenance.ts','probe-production-deployment','--repo','spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2','--task-source-sha',S,'--frozen-deployment',FD,'--script','spoonjoy-v2','--base-url',PB,'--out',O],operands:[{index:10,kind:'task_relative'},{index:16,kind:'task_relative'}],outputBasename:'production-deployment-observation.json',outputSchema:'ProductionDeploymentObservation',operationPhaseCapMs:310000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000}`. Exact no-extra-fields `ProductionDeploymentObservation={version:1,taskSourceSha,frozenDeploymentIdentitySha256,frozenSourceSha,frozenTreeSha,frozenVersionId,readback:ProductionDeploymentReadback|null,readbackSha256:Sha256|null,ancestry:GitAncestryProof|null,ancestrySha256:Sha256|null,relationship:'equal'|'strict_descendant'|'invalid',observedAt}` retains Round89's relationship/null matrix and contains no caller-owned boundary claim.

- Boundary identity is deterministic and immutable. Let the exact post-freeze suffix be the seventeen Unit37 logical names beginning `production-health` and ending `production-final-health` in Round90's order, indexed `i=0..16`; its before boundaryOrdinal is `2*i+1` and after is `2*i+2`, so all 34 ordinals are fixed. Exact `DeploymentGuardBoundaryContext={version:1,stageRun,boundaryOrdinal,phase:'before'|'after',ownerLogicalName,ownerCommandMode:'preflight'|'process'|'agent',ownerAttempt:positive-safe-integer|null,frozenDeploymentPath,frozenDeploymentSha256,frozenDeploymentIdentitySha256,createdAt}` is pushed/read back before probe intent; before requires preflight/null and after requires the reserved owner's actual mode/positive attempt. Exact parent projection `ProductionDeploymentProbe={version:1,boundaryContext:DeploymentGuardBoundaryContext,boundaryContextSha256,observation:ProductionDeploymentObservation,observationSha256,relationship:'equal'|'strict_descendant'|'invalid',projectedAt}` byte-cross-equals frozen/task identities and relationship. Final exact `DeploymentGuardAttempt={version:1,stageRun,boundaryContextPath,boundaryContextSha256,processAttemptOrdinal:positive-safe-integer,executionHost,priorAttemptPaths,priorAttemptSetSha256,probeSpecSha256,intentPath,intentSha256,observationPath:string|null,observationSha256:Sha256|null,probePath:string|null,probeSha256:Sha256|null,terminalCommandPath,terminalCommandSha256,outcome:'equal'|'strict_descendant'|'invalid'|'indeterminate',completedAt}` contains no duplicate caller-owned owner/boundary fields: observation and probe pairs are both nonnull exactly for equal/strict_descendant/invalid and all four are null for indeterminate. The parent alone builds and validates the projection before terminalizing the attempt. A command may reserve/start only after its exact before ordinal passes equal; an after context cannot exist before that owner attempt/result; every lower ordinal must already be terminal and adjacency is rederived from the closed suffix. Missing/skipped/duplicate/context-forged projection rejects.

- Post-effect drift has an exact owner-terminalization path. ProcessCommand and AgentCommand add nullable `deploymentDriftGuardPath,deploymentDriftGuardSha256`; both are null except the special failed terminal. After a strict-descendant after-guard is pushed/read back, Work Merger invokes exactly one mode-specific vector: `finish-process-command-for-deployment-drift --manifest <M> --writer-id <W> --release-run <R> --unit 37 --stage-run <G> --logical-name <L> --attempt <A> --guard-attempt <P>` or `finish-agent-command-for-deployment-drift` with the same flags. The CLI derives the guard/evidence bytes, requires the owner still running at that positive attempt and its effect result/report already returned but not accepted, and atomically terminalizes it failed with `failureReason:'deployment_descendant_advanced'`, `warningCount:0`, the guard pair, and endedAt. Process output/report bytes remain sanitized attempt evidence but create no accepted Artifact/canonical reviewer report. Exact `DeploymentDriftAbortReceipt={version:1,stageRun,logicalName,commandMode:'process'|'agent',attempt,guardAttemptPath,guardAttemptSha256,evidencePath,evidenceSha256,commandBeforeSha256,commandAfterSha256,terminalizedAt}` is pushed/read back. Recovery adopts an already exact failed after-image; any other owner state fails closed. Only then may record-post-merge-disposition fail the StageRun and create the one refreeze. A strict-descendant before-guard has no owner command and goes directly from its retained preflight evidence/disposition to failed StageRun. Unit29.2 faults guard result, evidence, owner terminalization, receipt, disposition, failed-stage, and replacement-stage boundaries for process and both agents; no failed StageRun retains a running command.

### Round 91 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for D1 cook ownership and replay fencing, shopping rollout closure, tag sync time, terminal tool identity, secret-bearing child execution, path operands, deployment-guard credentials/deadlines/finality, and drifted AgentCommand terminalization. They replace every conflicting Round90 and earlier sentence on those subjects.

- Cook progress is absent from D1, not merely non-canonical there. Final `CookSessionIndex` is exactly `attemptId String @id`, `userId String`, `recipeId String`, nullable unique `activeKey String?`, `status String`, `recipeFingerprint String`, `recipeTitle String`, `recipeCoverImageUrl String?`, `revision Int`, `projectionSeq Int`, `lastProjectionId String @unique`, `startedAt DateTime`, `updatedAt DateTime`, `completedAt DateTime?`, and `abandonedAt DateTime?`, with the existing user/recipe cascades and indexes. `activeStepIndex`, `scaleFactor`, `checkedIngredientCount`, and `checkedStepOutputCount` do not exist in its schema, migration, queue payload, query, evidence, cleanup proof, API, or test fixture. D1 owner-list/history loaders select private attempt/status/recipe/time metadata only; activation of an ACTIVE card always GETs the recipe-keyed Durable Object before rendering progress, and terminal cards never claim resumable progress. The DO and authenticated client record remain the sole current-progress sources.

- D1 projection replay survives physical index deletion. Exact content-free `CookSessionProjectionFence={attemptId,userId,recipeId,projectionSeq,lastProjectionId}` has primary key attemptId, unique lastProjectionId, and user/recipe cascading foreign keys. Final UPSERT payload is exactly `{userId,recipeId,status:'ACTIVE'|'COMPLETED'|'ABANDONED',recipeFingerprint,recipeTitle,recipeCoverImageUrl:string|null,revision,startedAt,updatedAt,completedAt:string|null,abandonedAt:string|null}`; DELETE remains owner/recipe only. One AtomicSqlPort batch reads the fence and applies the existing sequence/projection-id matrix: absent fence permits the first UPSERT or establishes a DELETE fence; matching lastProjectionId is a no-op; lower sequence is a no-op; equal sequence with a different id is corruption; greater UPSERT upserts the progress-free index and fence; greater DELETE deletes the index and advances the fence. Therefore a response-lost DELETE replay and every delayed lower UPSERT remain no-ops after row deletion. Fences persist until User or Recipe cascade; they contain no recipe/checklist/progress content. Exact cleanup addition `CleanupProjectionFenceProof={version:1,userId,reportAttempts,fenceRows,fenceRowsSha256}` uses BINARY-sorted unique reportAttempts and exact same-order `fenceRows:{attemptId,userId,recipeId,projectionSeq,lastProjectionId}[]`; every row belongs to that user, its attemptId set byte-equals reportAttempts, and fenceRowsSha256 hashes compact canonical row-array bytes. Final `CleanupPreDeleteRows` adds `cookSessionProjectionFenceRows:reportAttempts.length` and `projectionFenceProof:{path,sha256}` after `cookSessionIndexRows:0`; final `CleanupPostDeleteRows.productRowCounts` includes `CookSessionProjectionFence:0`; final `CleanupOutcomeProof.proofs` adds the same projectionFenceProof pair. The fence proof is written after terminal-index retirement and before user deletion, is immutable, and post-user-delete proves both tables zero. Unit5/12/13/17/29.1/29.2.4/30/31/33/37 tests independently fault every fence transaction/replay/order/cascade/hash and reject any progress-bearing D1 column or proof.

- Terminal feature evidence separates DO progress from D1 metadata. Exact final `FeatureHistoryProjectionRowProof={attemptId,userId,recipeId,activeKey:null,status:'COMPLETED',recipeFingerprint,recipeTitle:'codex-smoke-feature-recipe',recipeCoverImageUrl:null,revision:1,projectionSeq:1,lastProjectionId,startedAt,updatedAt,completedAt,abandonedAt:null}` and `FeatureHistoryProjectionBarrier={pollCount,row,rowSha256,observedAt}` retain every and only final CookSessionIndex column. The independently captured terminal DO snapshot proves active step, scale, and checked ids; none is copied into or cross-equaled from D1. The barrier query is still exact `(attemptId,userId,recipeId)`, one-row-only, owner-private, hash-complete, and followed by the exact projection-fence proof. Every old all-column schema containing a progress field rejects.

- Shopping old-Worker compatibility closes through a D1 write fence, not elapsed time. Migration 0025 creates singleton `ShoppingListCompatibilityEpoch={id:1,generation:positive-safe-integer,state:'OPEN'|'CLOSED',closureOperationKeySha256:string|null,closedSourceSha:string|null,closedTreeSha:string|null,closedWorkerVersionId:string|null,closedAt:string|null}` at generation 1 OPEN, with all closure fields null exactly when OPEN and all canonical/non-null exactly when CLOSED. While CLOSED, exact BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ShoppingListItem guards abort with `shopping_compatibility_closed` when the selectable parent has `mutationLease IS NULL`; every new helper item statement runs only while holding its validated nonnull lease. Exact BEFORE DELETE ShoppingList guard changes a null lease to literal `cascade-delete` in the deleting transaction so list/user cascades remain legal; child DELETE also remains legal when the parent is no longer selectable. Permanent AFTER dirty triggers and read reconciliation remain.

- Exact `ShoppingCompatibilityClosureResult={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',operationKeySha256,providerBefore:ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback,providerBeforeSha256,beforeDirtyListCount,reconciledListCount,generation:positive-safe-integer,epochBefore:'OPEN'|'CLOSED',providerAfter:ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback,providerAfterSha256,afterDirtyListCount:0,epochAfter:'CLOSED',disposition:'executed'|'observed_closed',completedAt}` binds the frozen deployed source/tree/version and generation into its operation key. `executed` requires OPEN, one D1 batch transaction that reconciles every dirty parent once, deletes all dirty rows, verifies zero, and changes the same generation singleton to CLOSED with this operation/deployment identity. `observed_closed` is response-loss recovery only: the stored generation/operation/deployment identity must match and dirty count is zero, with no write. D1 serialization means an old statement executes before closure and is reconciled or executes after closure and its item guard aborts; request wall time is irrelevant. Unit1/29.1 delays an old request beyond every live smoke/final-health boundary and proves zero post-close item commit. The former six-sample/120-second quiescence result and its ProcessSpecs are removed rather than used as authority.

- Rollback to a Worker that may still use the old item writers must reopen compatibility before any provider traffic mutation. Exact `ShoppingCompatibilityRollbackReopenResult={version:1,targetEnvironment:'qa'|'production',operationKeySha256,providerBefore:ShoppingCompatibilityProviderReadback,providerBeforeSha256,rollbackSourceSha,rollbackTreeSha,closedGeneration:positive-safe-integer,openedGeneration:positive-safe-integer,epochBefore:'CLOSED'|'OPEN',epochAfter:'OPEN',disposition:'executed'|'observed_open',completedAt}` is produced only by the import-safe direct D1 operation `reopen-for-rollback`. Side-effect-free preflight proves providerBefore still byte-matches the CLOSED source/tree/version, the rollback source/tree is the exact trusted rollback target and a schema-compatible ancestor, the epoch is CLOSED at closedGeneration or already OPEN at closedGeneration+1, and openedGeneration is exactly closedGeneration+1 without overflow. `executed` uses one remote AtomicSqlPort batch to verify the CLOSED identity, increment generation, set OPEN, and null every closure field; `observed_open` is response-loss recovery only and requires provider still unchanged plus the exact incremented OPEN row. The provider rollback is forbidden until this immutable result is pushed/read back; failure leaves the current deployment and CLOSED epoch untouched. Any later forward deployment closes the current OPEN generation through the ordinary close operation. Unit1/29.1 tests response loss, duplicate retry, stale/changed provider, wrong/nonancestor target, generation overflow, reopen-before-provider ordering, old writes during reopened rollback, and subsequent re-close.

- The existing production rollback has one concrete reopen owner. `.github/workflows/production-deploy.yml` invokes both normal and rollback orchestration through direct `node --import tsx scripts/deploy-production-canary.ts`, never a package-manager child that inherits its Cloudflare secrets. `runProductionRollback` resolves and ancestry-checks the selected source plus `git rev-parse <rollbackSourceSha>^{tree}`, reads the current deployment, then calls the import-safe in-process `reopenShoppingCompatibilityForRollback` before the first `wrangler versions deploy`; no separate child receives the D1 token. Final `ReleaseArtifact` adds exact nullable `shoppingCompatibilityRollbackReopen` and `shoppingCompatibilityRollbackCompensation` fields. The reopen field becomes nonnull immediately after executed/observed-open and remains in every later success/failure artifact. If candidate promotion never becomes authoritative, or later verification restores the previous CLOSED-source Worker, the orchestrator must run ordinary close on openedGeneration under a deterministic compensation operation and persist the closure result before returning; that result is the nonnull compensation field. A successful rollback leaves OPEN for the old writer and has null compensation. An absent 0025 epoch is allowed only when reviewed migration history proves 0025 has never applied, produces both fields null, and performs no D1 write. Provider ambiguity after reopen is retried to its existing bounded deadline and cannot report rollback success or silently re-close against an unknown Worker. Crash/retry derives one operation key from target/current/generation, observes the epoch and provider first, and never increments twice. Unit29.1 faults every artifact/reopen/deploy/verify/restore/compensating-close boundary and asserts no rollback provider mutation precedes reopen evidence.

- Final shopping ProcessSpecs are direct and closed. `qa-shopping-compat-close={environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-d1',outputPolicy:'json:shopping-compat-closure.json:ShoppingCompatibilityClosureResult',operationPhaseCapMs:300000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'registered_operation',storedArgv:['node','scripts/shopping-compat-catchup.mjs','--target-env','qa','--mode','close-rollout','--source-sha',H,'--source-tree-sha',B,'--out',O],operands:[{index:11,kind:'task_relative'}]}`; production substitutes production/DS/DT. `qa-shopping-compat-assert-after` is the same direct entry with mode `assert-clean`, output `json:shopping-compat-assert.json:ShoppingCompatibilityAssertResult`, recovery `idempotent_zero_missing`, and otherwise the same deadlines/operand; production substitutes production/DS/DT. Final Unit33 order is `qa-deployment-before,qa-d1-atomic-qualification,qa-cleanup-reconcile,qa-deploy-ensure,qa-deployment-after,qa-health,qa-shopping-compat-close,qa-saved-recipe-catchup,qa-cleanup-apply-before,qa-cleanup-assert-before,qa-api-smoke,qa-lifecycle-smoke,qa-feature-smoke,qa-cleanup-assert-after,qa-shopping-compat-assert-after,qa-final-health`. Final Unit37 order is `ci-workflow-discovery,workflow-discovery,workflow-watch,deploy-job,release-artifact-download,production-deployment-after,production-d1-atomic-qualification,production-stage-deployment-freeze,production-health,production-shopping-compat-close,production-saved-recipe-catchup,production-readiness,production-cleanup-reconcile,production-cleanup-assert-before,production-api-smoke,production-lifecycle-smoke,production-feature-smoke,production-visual-dogfood,production-visual-qa-review,production-visual-design-review,production-cleanup-assert-after,continuation-scan,production-shopping-compat-assert-after,production-final-health`.

- Tag changes cannot hide behind a native-sync cursor. In the same AtomicSqlPort transaction as every content-changing controlled-course/custom-tag replacement, update Recipe.updatedAt and NativeSyncClock to canonical `max(databaseNow,completeAccountHighWater+1ms)`. The no-op identical canonical replacement writes nothing. Malformed/noncanonical/out-of-Date-range source time or +1 overflow aborts the tag transaction. Unit24/27.2 covers same-millisecond writes, future skew in every retained source, equal `(kind,resourceId)` cursor identity, +1 boundary/overflow, workerd rollback/serialization, and exact native-sync replay; no separate tag change ledger is introduced.

- Host tool identity matches the pinned machine bytes. `/bin/ps` is root-owned regular no-follow mode `04755` with SHA-256 `b337747e8db7a3443b82ec9f3685432ddf76b971878188c757ca09db1c5a0288`; `/usr/sbin/lsof` is root-owned regular no-follow mode `0755` with SHA-256 `53b9a14df1036dd6d0b7f04d6f212823bddde2e090aaed1e772b73d7d43b2558`. The setuid bit is required only for ps; any normalized-to-0755 or alternate mode rejects.

- Secret-bearing policies use one direct trusted Node process. Final `ProcessPreflightSecret={cloudflareD1ApiToken,cloudflareRestBearer,githubToken}` keeps each string/null, memory-only, and nonempty only for its policy. `cloudflare-rest-github` obtains GitHub token bytes through exact trusted-gh `auth token --hostname github.com` into parent memory with one terminal LF stripped, validates 1..4096 NUL/CR/LF-free UTF-8 bytes, and passes distinct one-shot Cloudflare/GitHub descriptors plus distinct decimal marker variables to the registered direct Node child. The child synchronously consumes both to EOF, closes both, unsets both markers, and retains both tokens only in private memory before any network or spawn. It performs Cloudflare and GitHub HTTP itself and spawns no later child. Every `cloudflare-rest|cloudflare-d1|cloudflare-rest-github` StoredArgv begins exact `['node',<one pinned first-party entry>,...]` or `['node','--import','tsx',<one pinned first-party entry>,...]`; pnpm/npm/yarn/shell/second-entry mediation and token inheritance reject. Source/fault tests enumerate every such registry row and every resolver/read/close/unset/network boundary.

- The four inherited deployment captures are replaced completely. QA before and after are `{environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-rest',outputPolicy:'json:deployments.json:DeploymentSnapshot',operationPhaseCapMs:300000,totalTimeoutMs:1080000,recoveryPolicy:'ordinary',storedArgv:['node','--import','tsx','scripts/release-provenance.ts','capture-deployments','--environment','qa','--script','spoonjoy-v2-qa','--out',O],operands:[{index:10,kind:'task_relative'}]}`. Production before and after use the same row with environment production and script spoonjoy-v2. SavedRecipe catch-up is direct `['node','--import','tsx','scripts/release-provenance.ts','saved-recipes-catchup','--target-env',<qa|production>,'--source-sha',<H|DS>,'--source-tree-sha',<B|DT>,'--out',O]` with operand index 12 and its existing policy/output/caps/recovery. Feature smoke uses direct `['node','scripts/feature-smoke-live.mjs','--target-env',<qa|production>,'--base-url',<QB|PB>,'--source-sha',<H|DS>,'--worker-version-id',<V|DV>,'--release-identity-sha256',X,'--release-attempt-path',Y,'--out',O]`; production visual uses direct `['node','scripts/production-visual-dogfood.mjs','--base-url',PB,'--source-sha',DS,'--worker-version-id',DV,'--release-identity-sha256',X,'--release-attempt-path',Y,'--out',O]`.
