Their prior output policies/caps remain, subject to the post-freeze total deadline replacement below.

- Task-relative operand closure includes `--release-attempt-path`. It joins `--manifest|--out|--intent|--arm|--dispatch|--journal-root|--workflow-result`; no other path flag or sentinel is legal. Final exact task-relative operand arrays are QA/production lifecycle `[{index:13,kind:'task_relative'},{index:15,kind:'task_relative'}]`, QA/production feature `[{index:13,kind:'task_relative'},{index:15,kind:'task_relative'}]`, production visual `[{index:11,kind:'task_relative'},{index:13,kind:'task_relative'}]`, QA cleanup-reconcile `[{index:12,kind:'task_relative'},{index:14,kind:'task_relative'}]`, and production cleanup-reconcile `[{index:10,kind:'task_relative'},{index:12,kind:'task_relative'}]`. The resolved identity hash and canonical failed-target JSON are concrete validated stored bytes rather than path sentinels. Source tests reject every old pnpm vector, old shifted index, undeclared Y, or omitted output operand.

- Deployment guards fit owner deadlines. A before-guard completes before command reservation/start and consumes no owner deadline. Every post-freeze ProcessCommand totalTimeoutMs is its otherwise-final value plus exactly 1200000ms, reserved for the complete after-guard total timeout and terminal checkpoint: ordinary/catch-up/health/assert/readiness/continuation rows become 2280000ms, lifecycle/cleanup-reconcile rows 2340000ms, feature smoke 2580000ms, and production visual 2880000ms. The operation phase cap is unchanged. Unit37 AgentCommand `deadlineAt=deadlineStartedAt+3120000ms`, containing its existing 1800000ms reviewer runtime plus the same after-guard/checkpoint reserve. The owner is still running while its after-guard executes; equality at either deadline fails. Fake-clock tests consume full owner, guard, terminalization, and equality boundaries for process and both agents.

- Agent output is durably received before the after-guard. Exact create-once `AgentResultReceipt={version:1,stageRun,logicalName,attempt,agentId,verdict:'CONVERGED'|'FINDINGS',returnedReportPath,returnedReportSha256,receivedAt}` lives inside the running attempt outPath; agentId is the runtime-returned canonical UUID, returnedReportPath is task-relative inside outPath, and returnedReportSha256 hashes the sanitized schema-valid runtime result bytes. Receipt creation, fsync, Desk commit/push/readback, and byte revalidation happen before the after-boundary context can be created. It does not append an Artifact, populate AgentCommand.report fields, or accept a reviewer verdict. Equal after-guard alone lets ordinary finish validate the receipt against prompt/input and materialize the canonical clean/findings report; strict descendant uses the same receipt only to recover agentId and prove a result returned. A crash at every receipt write/push/readback/guard boundary resumes from the immutable receipt without re-running or accepting the reviewer.

- Drifted agents have an agent-native terminal schema. AgentCommand adds nullable `deploymentDriftGuardPath,deploymentDriftGuardSha256` and verdict domain `CONVERGED|FINDINGS|DEPLOYMENT_DRIFT`. Failed FINDINGS retains the existing report pair and null drift pair. Failed DEPLOYMENT_DRIFT requires nonnull agentId copied from the exact AgentResultReceipt, null report pair, nonnull drift pair, canonical endedAt, and null interruptionReason; it has no warningCount, failureReason, terminalSource, or process fields. `finish-agent-command-for-deployment-drift` validates the persisted receipt, after-boundary context, strict-descendant guard/evidence, and running exact attempt, then writes this after-image plus `DeploymentDriftAbortReceipt`; recovery adopts only byte-equal state. The process vector alone writes process failureReason/warningCount. No CONVERGED report or FINDINGS artifact is accepted from a drifted attempt.

- This historical Round91 production final-close clause is rejected by Round92/Round97. `production-final-health` remains the last application ProcessCommand. Its then-specified mandatory after-guard was boundary 34; passed Unit37 evidence includes its DeploymentGuardAttempt/ProductionDeploymentProbe pair after the HealthSeries, and no application command, smoke, health request, or unguarded target observation follows it. Equal passes and closes the stage; strict descendant terminalizes final-health then refreezes; invalid/indeterminate fails closed. The prior phrase `no target command follows final health` means no further application command and does not exclude this required parent guard. This paragraph is inventory only; final evidence uses boundary 32 exactly as Round97 specifies.

- Execution granularity is final. Unit1.1-1.4 replace historical Unit1a-c as shopping core, migration/compatibility, sync/tombstones, and transport-writer groups. Unit15.1-15.4 replace historical Unit15a-c as record/overlay, locks/generations/logout, bootstrap/reconnect, and legacy adoption groups. Unit31.1-31.5 replace historical Unit31a-c as per-user purge, immutable evidence/recovery, broad reconciliation, feature finalization, and visual/final-gate groups. Only the numbered split groups are executable units; the retained historical blocks are source-detail inventory and cannot receive status, commits, or release evidence.

### Round 92 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for deployed-descendant runner identity, production guard cardinality, GitHub descriptor transport, loader-free secret processes, rollback recovery, client storage migration, and shopping cascade legality. They replace every conflicting Round91 and earlier sentence on those subjects.

- Every post-freeze production runner binds the frozen deployment, never task placeholders. The final direct feature vector uses QA `H/V` or production `DS/DV` positionally, and the final production visual vector uses `DS/DV`; API, lifecycle, readiness, health, catch-up, cleanup, prompts, reports, and observations retain the same frozen identity rule. `S/B` remain only task/workflow provenance. Source tests enumerate every post-freeze StoredArgv and reject task source/tree/version in a live expected-identity slot, including the exact-or-descendant path.

- Guard cardinality follows the actual final order. The exact post-freeze suffix has sixteen owners, in order: `production-health,production-shopping-compat-close,production-saved-recipe-catchup,production-readiness,production-cleanup-reconcile,production-cleanup-assert-before,production-api-smoke,production-lifecycle-smoke,production-feature-smoke,production-visual-dogfood,production-visual-qa-review,production-visual-design-review,production-cleanup-assert-after,continuation-scan,production-shopping-compat-assert-after,production-final-health`. For index `i=0..15`, before is `2*i+1` and after is `2*i+2`; exactly 32 contiguous ordinals exist. The final-health after-guard is boundary 32. Every schema, adjacency proof, test fixture, prompt input, evidence count, and doing/detail reference uses 16/32; 17/34 is rejected.

- Descriptor transport admits GitHub explicitly. Final `DescriptorSecretChannel={role:'cloudflare_oauth'|'cloudflare_d1'|'github_token',childFd:6|7|8,maxBytes:positive-safe-integer}` has the exact role/fd bijection `cloudflare_oauth->6`, `cloudflare_d1->7`, `github_token->8`, each cap `<=4096`, BINARY role order, unique fd, and no secret/hash/length/value. Public marker names are respectively `SPOONJOY_CLOUDFLARE_OAUTH_FD`, `SPOONJOY_CLOUDFLARE_D1_FD`, and `SPOONJOY_GITHUB_TOKEN_FD`, each containing only its exact decimal child fd. `cloudflare-rest-github` requires exactly oauth+github channels; cloudflare-rest requires oauth; cloudflare-d1 requires d1; every other policy rejects a secret channel. DescriptorLaunchSpec, DescriptorSpawnReceipt, ProcessGateSpawn, registration/adoption journals, fd-table probes, and source fixtures accept the public channel projection while persisting no secret. The parent acquires both combined-policy secrets only after paused ready state is pushed/read back, validates both completely, then writes/closes both; any acquisition/write failure closes and zeroizes both and never authorizes run. The child reads both to EOF, closes both, unsets both markers, and only then performs a provider request. Fault tests cross every two-channel acquisition/write/close/order/crash boundary and descriptors 6/7/8.

- No runtime loader executes in a secret-bearing process. `scripts/release-provider-command.mjs` becomes the sole descriptor-backed provider entry, `scripts/release-provenance.mjs` its import-safe token-free business module, and `scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs` the sole production deployment orchestrator; the TypeScript provenance/orchestrator entries and every `node --import tsx` secret vector are historical and absent from production/package/workflow registries. Every secret-bearing StoredArgv is exact `['node',<one reviewed first-party .mjs bootstrap>,...]` with no `--import`, loader, package manager, shell, preload, policy file, or second entry. A bootstrap has no static import except reviewed Node built-ins, synchronously consumes/closes/unsets every required descriptor or copies/deletes the workflow secret environment before its first await/dynamic import, and only then loads reviewed first-party modules. Descriptor-backed business code receives bounded HTTP/D1 capability functions, never raw tokens, descriptors, marker names, or secret environment. The workflow bootstrap alone retains bounded tokens in closure-owned spawn/HTTP capabilities and never exposes them to an imported module or unregistered child.

- Final direct provenance vectors are: capture `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','capture-deployments','--environment',E,'--script',N,'--out',O]` with output operand 8; SavedRecipe catch-up `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','saved-recipes-catchup','--target-env',E,'--source-sha',Q,'--source-tree-sha',T,'--out',O]` with output operand 10; freeze `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','freeze-production-stage','--repo','spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2','--task-source-sha',S,'--task-tree-sha',B,'--workflow-result',K,'--script','spoonjoy-v2','--base-url',PB,'--out',O]` with task-relative operands 10 and 16; and deployment guard `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','probe-production-deployment','--repo','spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2','--task-source-sha',S,'--frozen-deployment',FD,'--script','spoonjoy-v2','--base-url',PB,'--out',O]` with task-relative operands 8 and 14. Exact package aliases are `release:provenance=node scripts/release-provider-command.mjs` and `deploy:auto=node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs`; registered ProcessSpecs still use the direct arrays rather than invoking aliases. `.github/workflows/production-deploy.yml` invokes exact `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs` for normal and rollback flows. TrustedToolchain freezes Node plus every reviewed bootstrap/business source hash and closed first-party/builtin import graph; source gates reject a third-party pre-consumption import, tsx/ts-node/register hook, generated/untracked runtime entry, token-bearing imported capability, or dynamic import before descriptor/environment retirement.

- Executable measurement keeps exact cardinality while changing trust entries. In Round89's eighteen-member pre-visual set, `scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs` replaces the TypeScript deployment entry and `scripts/release-provider-command.mjs` replaces the TypeScript provenance entry; no other member changes. Unit31 still adds only production visual for nineteen final. Token-free pure `scripts/release-provenance.mjs` and `scripts/cloudflare-d1-query.mjs` are additively imported, typechecked, and covered at 100% but are not executable members. Every old TypeScript entry, missing bootstrap/business module, count-only substitution, pre-visual cardinality other than 18, or final cardinality other than 19 rejects.

- Rollback authority survives provider acceptance and artifact loss. Migration 0025 additionally creates content-free `ShoppingListCompatibilityRollbackJournal={operationKeySha256 PRIMARY KEY,targetEnvironment,fromSourceSha,fromTreeSha,fromWorkerVersionId,toSourceSha,toTreeSha,toWorkerVersionId,generationBefore,generationAfter,epochBefore:'OPEN'|'CLOSED',state:'REOPENED'|'PROMOTED'|'COMPENSATED',reopenedAt,promotedAt,compensatedAt}` with nonunique lookup index `(targetEnvironment,state,generationAfter)`. REOPENED has only reopenedAt nonnull; PROMOTED adds promotedAt and keeps compensatedAt null; COMPENSATED has reopenedAt/compensatedAt nonnull and promotedAt nullable exactly when the target had already passed verification before restoration. The deterministic operation key hashes target, exact from/to deployment identities, generationBefore, and epochBefore; it does not depend on one process or artifact attempt. The opening transaction requires zero REOPENED rows for that environment or the one hash-equal row for this operation; a different nonterminal operation blocks before epoch/provider mutation, while terminal PROMOTED/COMPENSATED history remains. One remote AtomicSqlPort batch inserts REOPENED and, only when epochBefore is CLOSED, increments the epoch exactly once and opens it; when already OPEN it records generationAfter equal generationBefore without changing the epoch. Response-loss recovery accepts a hash-equal REOPENED row while provider is exactly from or to. Only the existing exact rollback provider/health verification passing against the to identity permits one remote AtomicSqlPort batch to advance REOPENED to PROMOTED; a provider-version read alone is insufficient. Retry may reconstruct the success artifact only after a fresh read-only verification passes. If verification fails and the provider remains or is restored to the from identity, compensation restores the pre-operation epoch state: a formerly CLOSED epoch is re-closed at generationAfter under a deterministic compensation key, while a formerly OPEN epoch remains OPEN; the same transaction marks COMPENSATED. Unknown/divergent provider state mutates neither epoch nor journal.

- Production rollback has a second recovery owner after process exit. The workflow direct-Node deploy step writes the normal artifact when possible, then an `if: always() && rollback` direct `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs --recover-rollback ...` step reads the exact journal/provider state before artifact upload. No journal means no reopen occurred and recovery performs no D1/provider mutation. REOPENED+target reruns the complete rollback verification and either marks PROMOTED/regenerates success or restores from/compensates/writes truthful failure; REOPENED+from compensates and writes truthful failure; PROMOTED+target reruns read-only verification before regenerating success; COMPENSATED+from is an idempotent failure readback; every other tuple fails closed. A whole-runner loss is recoverable by a rerun because the new process discovers the one exact nonterminal journal by frozen from/to identity and generation before creating another operation. A successful rollback intentionally leaves the epoch OPEN for old writers; a later forward release closes that same generation. Unit1/29.1 faults D1 journal/reopen/provider acceptance/provider readback/full verification/state transition/artifact/process/workflow-step/upload boundaries, successive rollback while already OPEN, and compensation from both prior epoch states.

- Generic client storage migration never owns cook state. Final `APP_STORAGE_SCHEMA_VERSION` is literal `2026-07-18.1`, and final `applyStorageSchemaMigration` has no prefix deletion: its exact incompatible non-cook local key registry is `['ingredient-input-mode']`, its session key registry is empty, and both prefix registries are exact `[]`. Its exact recognized predecessor domain is missing/null or literal `2026-02-17.1`; only those states may remove the one exact non-cook key and then stamp the new schema version. The current version is a no-op. Any other nonempty version is unknown/future and preserves every byte without stamping or deletion. A remove/set storage error likewise leaves the version unstamped for idempotent retry; partial removal may affect only the declared non-cook key. It preserves theme/version plus every `spoonjoy-cook-progress:` anonymous/legacy key and every `spoonjoy-auth-cook:` or `spoonjoy-auth-cook-` local/session record, marker, decision, generation, retirement fence, and client id. Only CookSessionDeviceProvider may validate, migrate, reset, or delete those keys under its exact lifetime client lock and shared/exclusive user-generation protocol. Unknown `spoonjoy-*` keys are preserved rather than guessed; adding one to a later destructive migration requires its own reviewed exact-key migrator. Unit15.2 owns `app/lib/client-storage-schema.ts` integration and tests stale/missing/future versions, every cook key family, raw v1 adoption, cleanup markers, paused writers, storage events/errors, partial exact-key deletion, idempotent retry, and no stale-writer resurrection.

- Shopping cascade authorization never mutates a row in its BEFORE DELETE trigger. Migration 0025 creates content-free no-FK `ShoppingListCompatibilityUserDeleteIntent={userId TEXT PRIMARY KEY,generation INTEGER NOT NULL,markedAt TEXT NOT NULL}` and `ShoppingListCompatibilityDeleteIntent={shoppingListId TEXT PRIMARY KEY,generation INTEGER NOT NULL,markedAt TEXT NOT NULL}`. Exact User BEFORE/AFTER DELETE triggers insert/remove the current-generation user marker around all cascades and abort on an existing stale marker. Exact ShoppingList BEFORE DELETE first aborts while CLOSED when `mutationLease IS NULL` and no exact current-generation user marker exists, then inserts its current-generation list marker; its AFTER trigger removes the list marker. The CLOSED item DELETE guard permits a lease-less child delete only when the parent is absent or an exact current-generation list marker exists; otherwise it aborts. Thus account deletion authorizes list/item cascades, a new helper authorizes direct list/item deletion through its lease, and every delayed old-Worker direct list or item delete after closure aborts. Rollback, statement failure, or crash rolls both markers back; successful list/user deletion leaves both tables zero. Close-rollout, assert-clean, broad cleanup, and final production evidence require both tables empty. Unit1.2 tests direct/list/user deletes with `foreign_keys=ON` and recursive triggers both off/on, every User BEFORE/list BEFORE/child/list AFTER/User AFTER failure point, stale/wrong-generation markers, migration rerun/rollback, late old direct list deletion, and zero undefined target-row mutation.

### Round 95 Quality Closures

These clauses were Round95 authority. Round97 below replaces their native-sync atomicity, high-water, cursor, and feed-compatibility portions; the old-tab storage, rollback ownership/workflow argv, and AgentCommand deadline portions remain cumulative.

- Historical Round95 native sync retained its existing v1 `(updatedAt,kind:resourceId)` cursor and gains one D1-serialized account high-water allocator. Migration 0027 adds exact `NativeSyncClock={accountId String @id,lastIssuedAt DateTime}` with a required User cascade relation and adds required `SavedRecipe.syncUpdatedAt DateTime`; its `(userId,syncUpdatedAt,recipeId)` index is additive to the existing saved-list index. `issueNativeSyncTimestamp(accountId)` executes in the same D1 write transaction as its mutation and returns canonical `max(databaseNow,accountHighWater+1ms)`, where accountHighWater is the maximum valid canonical timestamp across the clock, User.updatedAt, every Recipe.updatedAt owned by the account, every Cookbook.updatedAt owned by the account, the account ShoppingList.updatedAt and ShoppingListItem.updatedAt values, every account NativeSyncTombstone.updatedAt, and every account SavedRecipe.syncUpdatedAt. Missing sources contribute nothing; malformed/out-of-Date-range values or successor overflow abort the entire mutation. D1 write serialization makes the read/clock-write/resource-write indivisible. A content-identical tag replacement, already-saved save, or already-unsaved unsave is a true no-op and allocates nothing.

- Historical Round95 required every content-changing controlled-course/custom-tag replacement sets its Recipe.updatedAt to the issued account timestamp, not merely `max(databaseNow,priorRecipeUpdatedAt+1ms)`. Migration 0027 backfill and its permanent old-Worker RecipeInCookbook compatibility trigger initialize every inserted SavedRecipe.syncUpdatedAt through the same account high-water rule; rows in one deterministic backfill batch may share one issued timestamp because `savedRecipe:<recipeId>` is the stable tie-break. The post-health SavedRecipe catch-up uses that same rule for missing rows and advances NativeSyncClock in its D1 transaction. New shared save writers issue one timestamp and create SavedRecipe with it; explicit unsave issues one timestamp, hard-deletes only SavedRecipe, and upserts the existing NativeSyncTombstone table at `(accountId,'savedRecipe',recipeId)` with null parent/title and that timestamp. Re-save deletes that tombstone and creates the current SavedRecipe with a later issued timestamp in one transaction.

- Historical Round95 extended `NativeSyncEntryKind` with `savedRecipe`; Round97 confines that expanded union to negotiated schema 2. Current rows emit `action:'upsert'`, `kind:'savedRecipe'`, `resourceId:recipeId`, `updatedAt:syncUpdatedAt`, and exact payload `{recipeId,isSaved:true}`. Unsave tombstones emit the existing delete envelope with `resourceType:'savedRecipe'`, `resourceId:recipeId`, null parent/title, and the issued delete time. The account feed queries SavedRecipe by syncUpdatedAt and NativeSyncTombstone by updatedAt, merges them through the unchanged UTF-8 BINARY `(updatedAt,kind:resourceId)` comparator, and keeps v1 ISO/opaque cursor compatibility. A save, unsave, re-save, or tag edit committed after any previously returned account cursor is therefore strictly after that cursor even when another profile/recipe/cookbook/shopping/tombstone timestamp is future-skewed. Unit18 owns schema/backfill/trigger/helper allocation; Unit20.2 owns save/unsave event mutation and replay tests; Unit24 owns tag allocation; Unit27.2 owns feed projection, pagination, OpenAPI, and native-client contract tests.

- Anonymous cook progress is intentionally account-neutral local recipe state, so a pre-release tab running the existing `recipes.$id.tsx` code may recreate `spoonjoy-cook-progress:<recipeId>` after logout/reset. No new code can make that already-loaded writer inspect a future marker. The terminal privacy invariant is therefore exact absence of the departing user id, user namespace secret, reversible authenticated key component, authenticated snapshot/queue/decision/client id, and any authenticated network mutation after the generation barrier; it does not claim absence of an account-neutral anonymous recipe key. Every raw v1/v2 anonymous candidate is untrusted, never auto-adopted, never relabeled as authenticated state, and never sent until a currently authenticated user makes the explicit neutral use/discard choice under the current generation locks. Bootstrap/focus/visibility/storage reconciliation may remove malformed or explicitly discarded anonymous bytes but cannot use their later absence as terminal proof. Tests pause the real old anonymous writer before logout/reset, let it write afterward, and prove the resulting key contains no account identity, cannot resurrect/send authenticated progress, and presents the same explicit neutral choice to a different account. The generic retirement fence and zero-residue assertions apply only to authenticated v1/v2 writers and authenticated key families. This replaces Round86's impossible zero-recipe-association and universal old-parser fail-closed claim.

- Unit1.2 owns only the D1 ShoppingListCompatibilityRollbackJournal schema plus import-safe reopen/promote/compensate transaction semantics: absent journal, create-once identity, REOPENED/PROMOTED/COMPENSATED transitions, already-OPEN prior epoch, generation overflow, response loss, idempotent reread, and compensation to both prior epoch states. It uses an injected provider-state enum only as pure transaction input and never spawns, deploys, verifies a Worker, writes a release artifact, or models process/workflow loss. Unit29.1 exclusively owns provider acceptance/readback, complete rollback verification, restore, artifact regeneration/loss, process exit, always-run workflow recovery, and upload-boundary tests. Each red/green/verification group remains completable in one session.

- `.github/workflows/production-deploy.yml` uses exact deploy argv. Normal is `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --artifact-dir mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts`; rollback is `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --rollback-version-id "$ROLLBACK_VERSION_ID" --artifact-dir mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts`. Immediately afterward and before `Ensure release artifact exists`, exact step name `Recover rollback journal` has literal `if: always() && env.ROLLBACK_VERSION_ID != ''`, the exact no-fifth-key environment map enumerated by Round97, and exact run command `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs --recover-rollback --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --rollback-version-id "$ROLLBACK_VERSION_ID" --artifact-dir mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts`. The bootstrap parser accepts each named flag once, rejects every unknown/duplicate/missing/empty operand, requires canonical lowercase 40-hex source SHA and canonical rollback UUID, requires the literal relative artifact directory, and permits bare `--recover-rollback` only with rollback-version-id. Recovery reads the unique from/to/generation journal, provider state, and existing artifact from that directory; it writes `mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts/production-release.json` atomically before the fallback artifact and upload steps. Secrets remain environment inputs only to the reviewed bootstrap and are retired before its first dynamic import under Round92.

- Unit37 AgentCommand `deadlineAt` is exactly `deadlineStartedAt+3000000ms`: the existing reviewer runtime is 1800000ms and the complete after-guard plus terminal-checkpoint reserve is exactly 1200000ms. No additional 120000ms exists. Equality at 3000000ms fails, and fake-clock tests independently consume the 1800000ms owner boundary, 1200000ms reserve boundary, and exact sum.

## Round 95 Ownership Override

Units18, 20.2, 24, and 27.2 own the account-high-water SavedRecipe/tag native-sync contract above; Unit15.2 owns only authenticated-residue privacy and explicit treatment of account-neutral old-tab anonymous keys; Unit1.2 owns database rollback-journal transactions only; Unit29.1 owns all provider/process/workflow rollback recovery and the literal deploy/recovery workflow; Unit29.2 owns the corrected 3000000ms AgentCommand deadline. Round95 replaces every conflicting Recipe-only sync clock, payload-only isSaved sync, universal old-writer retirement, Unit1.2 provider-loss test, ellipsis recovery argv, or 3120000ms deadline sentence.

### Round 97 Quality Closures

These clauses are final for D1 atomic execution, complete native-sync high-water/cursor compatibility, SavedRecipe catch-up/parser ownership, production workflow environment/order, Unit29.1 granularity, visual reviewer reports, and deployment-guard cardinality. They replace every conflicting Round95 or earlier sentence on those subjects.

- Prisma's exactly pinned `@prisma/adapter-d1@6.19.2` is never an atomicity boundary. Unit0.1 adds exact narrow `AtomicSqlValue=string|finite-number|null`, `AtomicSqlAlias=string matching ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$`, `AtomicSqlStatement={sql:nonempty-NUL-free-single-statement-string,params:readonly AtomicSqlValue[],expectedColumns:readonly unique AtomicSqlAlias[]}`, `AtomicSqlRow=Readonly<Record<AtomicSqlAlias,AtomicSqlValue>>`, `AtomicSqlResult={success:true,rows:readonly AtomicSqlRow[],changes:nonnegative-safe-integer}`, and `AtomicSqlPort.execute(statements:readonly [AtomicSqlStatement,...AtomicSqlStatement[]]):Promise<readonly AtomicSqlResult[]>`. Blob binding is outside this task's port; every task-owned SQL value is bound rather than interpolated. Source validation requires each result-bearing SELECT/RETURNING expression to use an explicit unique alias exactly equal to expectedColumns; returned object keys must byte-equal that vector as a set because D1Result cannot reveal collapsed duplicate labels. The production Worker adapter prepares/binds every statement on the request's exact `env.DB`, calls one `D1Database.batch()`, and normalizes only `results` plus `meta.changes`. The release adapter sends the same bound statement grammar to the frozen remote target, but is release-eligible only after the provider-backed QA qualification below proves first/middle/last rollback for the exact REST endpoint/version in that release; qualification failure blocks every remote mutation and production promotion. Result count must equal statement count, every result must have `success:true`, and every row/value/count/key must validate exactly. The Worker batch executes sequentially and any statement error aborts/rolls back the sequence. The local SQLite adapter is permitted only under test or the existing simultaneous `SPOONJOY_FORCE_SQLITE_LOCAL_DB=1` plus `SPOONJOY_NATIVE_DOGFOOD_API=1` native-dogfood guards; it uses one real local SQLite transaction. Any request with `env.DB` always selects D1 even when either process flag is present. Source gates prove the local adapter is unreachable from every other production path. No task-owned production atomic path may call Prisma `$transaction`, use its D1 transaction object, issue independent Prisma writes around a claimed commit, interpolate values, or swallow the adapter warning; only the guarded local adapter may use the local Prisma transaction primitive.

- The port is the sole application-level transaction primitive for shopping mutation/rollout/rollback, cookbook membership plus SavedRecipe, explicit save/unsave, declarative tag replacement and new tagged recipe creation, CookSessionIndex plus CookSessionProjectionFence, cleanup leases/effects, seed/provisioning, migration materialization/attestation, and QA/production catch-up/close/compensation. The only non-port atomic owners are the exact reviewed migration-defined compatibility/cascade triggers required to protect one-statement writes from old Workers: ShoppingListCompatibilityDirty insert/update/delete, RecipeInCookbook SavedRecipe compatibility, User NativeSyncClock bootstrap, User/list cascade-intent, CookStorageIdentity bootstrap, and schema-attestation immutability. They execute inside the originating D1 statement, expose no application transaction API, and are source-enumerated and workerd-faulted separately. Each port owner constructs a finite parameterized statement list, uses explicit RETURNING rows for every CAS invariant, treats `changes` only as validated telemetry, and performs no user-visible success or provider effect until the batch result validates. Zero-row lease/version acquisition makes every later guarded statement zero-row and is a retry/no-op as already specified; any SQL error rolls back all prior statements. Every shopping command takes a stable mutationId/requestHash. Migration 0025 adds the final cascading expiring ShoppingMutationReceipt schema from Round100; the receipt and, for REST, the already-completed ApiIdempotencyKey response are inserted in the same port batch as the list mutation. Exact live replay returns the stored outcome, hash mismatch conflicts, expiry permits a new effect, and an uncertain response rereads receipt/idempotency rows before retrying; it never deletes a live reservation or reapplies an accepted additive mutation. Unit0.1 source gates enumerate every task-owned application call site and the closed trigger set, and reject a Prisma transaction, unregistered adapter, second atomic implementation, `changes` authority, or existing shopping catch-path reservation deletion. Unit and workerd tests force failure at every statement, concurrent serialization order, pre/post-commit response loss, receipt reread/expiry, count/result corruption, and zero warnings; local fixtures supplement but do not substitute for workerd proof.

- `NativeSyncClock.lastIssuedAt` is a retained account cursor floor as well as a mutation allocator. The exact high-water source set adds every `ShoppingListItemSyncTombstone.updatedAt` whose ShoppingList.authorId is the account; it otherwise retains Round95's sources. Persisted source parsing strictly accepts only canonical `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`, Prisma D1's exact `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sss+00:00`, or SQLite-default `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` interpreted as UTC, all in the inclusive four-digit domain `0000..9999`; each is epoch-normalized before comparison. New sync/clock/resource values emit only canonical `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. Any other offset, precision, separator, malformed value, impossible date, or range failure aborts before writes. Candidate issuance is exact `max(databaseNow,accountHighWater+1ms)`; attempting to succeed the upper bound aborts the whole batch. Migration 0027 backfills one clock row per existing User above all retained source rows, including shopping loser tombstones, and installs the reviewed permanent User AFTER INSERT trigger that initializes a missing clock from normalized NEW.updatedAt for old-Worker signups. Before every cursor comparison, current code atomically upserts an existing or missing clock to `max(existing,complete current source high-water)` and reads all schema-eligible sources in that same AtomicSqlPort batch. Every save/unsave/re-save/catch-up/tag/shopping batch computes the resource timestamp inside its parameterized SQL against that complete current high-water and advances the clock to at least the resulting resource timestamp before commit. Tests use real initial-migration defaults and adapter-emitted `+00:00` fixtures, every existing stale-clock source, plus old-Worker post-migration signup.

- Every native account-sync page with at least one page reference advances NativeSyncClock to `max(existing,lastPageReference.updatedAt)` through the production AtomicSqlPort after response payload derivation but before returning bytes; a failed floor write fails the request and issues no cursor. This makes every successfully emitted post-migration timestamp durable even if its source row is later hard-deleted. Before filtering a supplied cursor, the server repairs/reads the clock from the primary path. A cursor timestamp greater than the retained floor returns private no-store `409 sync_reset_required` with exact message `Native sync cursor is outside the retained account history; clear this account cache and retry without a cursor.` and no entries/cursor; it never advances the floor from caller bytes. Migration/current-source repair makes retained pre-release cursors legal, while an unverifiable removed future row or forged future cursor takes the explicit reset path. Tests cover response loss before/after floor commit, later hard deletion, equal timestamp/id ties, old removed rows, malicious future cursors, old-Worker post-migration signup, and retry without cursor.

- SavedRecipe changes are capability-versioned rather than injected into an old closed union. Request query `schemaVersion` is absent or exact single `1` for the legacy feed and exact single `2` for the expanded first-party feed; blank/repeated/other values are validation errors. Schema 1 preserves the complete existing kind/OpenAPI union, emits no `savedRecipe`, returns freshness.schemaVersion 1, and accepts only legacy ISO/v1 cursors. Schema 2 returns freshness.schemaVersion 2 and adds `savedRecipe`. Schema-specific source-kind filtering happens before comparator sort, after-cursor filtering, page limit, lastPageReference, and nextCursor derivation, so a schema-1-ineligible SavedRecipe can never consume a slot or advance a schema-1 cursor. Schema 2 uses exact opaque cursor `v2.<base64url(JSON.stringify({schemaVersion:2,updatedAt,id}))>`, with that key order, `id` exact `${kind}:${resourceId}`, UTF-8 bytes, RFC 4648 URL-safe alphabet, no padding, and canonical decode/re-encode equality. It accepts no ISO/v1 cursor, and schema 1 accepts no v2 cursor. A schema-2 request with a legacy cursor returns the same `409 sync_reset_required`; the updated first-party client clears its account/environment/schema cache and retries schema 2 without a cursor. Existing clients that omit the parameter can never receive an unknown kind. OpenAPI defines query enum `1|2` default 1 and distinct exhaustive v1/v2 response schemas plus the 409 response. Generated docs/playground and the existing Swift/native sample in `docs/api.md` require explicit schema 2 plus exhaustive savedRecipe upsert/delete handling before that client persists a v2 cursor; no new dogfood document is created.

- In schema 2, current SavedRecipe and delete-tombstone envelopes remain exactly as Round95 specifies. `app/lib/native-sync-invalidation.server.ts` extends both its closed deleted-kind parser and typed upsert input with `savedRecipe`; Unit20.2 owns that module plus `test/lib/native-sync-invalidation.server.test.ts`. Unit18 owns schema/backfill/NativeSyncClock, the permanent old-Worker RecipeInCookbook and User clock triggers, trigger race tests, and ordinary shared save helpers. Unit19 owns all four cookbook writers plus the provider-neutral idempotent catch-up helper and its batch/response-loss tests. Unit29.1.3 owns only the provider-bound catch-up CLI/bootstrap, deployment identity/readback, QA/production invocation, and provider-boundary tests. Unit20.2 owns explicit REST save/unsave mutation events. Unit24 owns tag allocation. Unit27.2 owns schema negotiation, v1/v2 cursor/feed projection, native conformance, OpenAPI, and existing docs. No unit retests or reimplements another owner's layer.

- NativeSyncClock contains no content and cascades with User. Cleanup schemas/proofs add it wherever exact product-row tables are enumerated: CleanupPostDeleteRows.productRowCounts and broad QA/production assertions require `NativeSyncClock:0` for deleted disposable principals, while unrelated retained accounts survive. Migration validators, schema inventories, cleanup tests, feature finalization, and production residue scans include the table and savedRecipe tombstone kind; no cleanup infers progress/content from the clock timestamp.

- Production deploy and recovery environment is exact, not count-shorthand. Both `Deploy staged release to Cloudflare Workers` and rollback-only `Recover rollback journal` have exactly `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID:${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}`, `CLOUDFLARE_D1_API_TOKEN:${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_D1_API_TOKEN }}`, `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_API_TOKEN:${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_API_TOKEN }}`, and `SPOONJOY_MCP_CANARY_BASE_URL:https://spoonjoy.app`; no fifth key or extra environment entry exists. `SPOONJOY_RELEASE_SHA` is removed because exact `--source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA"` is argv. The literal rollback condition and three argv remain Round95. Exact step order is `Deploy staged release to Cloudflare Workers`, `Recover rollback journal`, `Record release source`, `Ensure release artifact exists`, `Upload MCP OAuth canary artifacts`; source tests reject omission, insertion, reordering, environment drift, or recovery after fallback/upload.

- Historical Unit29.1a/b/c is detail inventory only. Executable Units29.1.1-29.1.5 separately own build-SHA/health/canary, reviewed migrations/atomic remote D1, provider provenance/catch-ups, workflow queues/promotion/ensure probes, and rollback deploy/recovery. Every `a` group creates the minimum import-safe uncommitted not_implemented skeletons needed for collection, runs the exact focused tests to behavior-specific sentinels, and records red evidence without commit/push; every `b` group removes only its sentinels and lands tests plus implementation atomically; every `c` group runs focused 100% coverage, zero-warning typecheck/build/source gates and records evidence. No executable group depends on a source module first created by a later group.

- Unit37 canonical CONVERGED visual reviewer reports are the final seventeen LF-terminated lines from Round77 LiveIdentityObservation, including both observed reviewer health operation ids/sequences and source/version values. Every obsolete eight-line report clause is historical and rejected. Unit29.2.2 has exactly sixteen post-freeze owners and thirty-two boundaries; Round91's boundary-34 implementation sentence is historical only, boundary 32 after production-final-health is the sole final target evidence, and every 34-boundary fixture rejects.

## Round 97 Ownership Override
