### Round 99 Quality Closures

These clauses replace every conflicting earlier sentence and are executable authority.

- Timestamp source compatibility is exact as Round99 defines above. Migration 0025/0027 validators normalize the three admitted persisted encodings before comparison and emit only canonical millisecond `Z`; an earlier demand that all legacy database source bytes already be canonical `Z` is rejected. `package.json` and lockfile pin adapter 6.19.2 exactly, and source/fixture tests fail on drift.
- Unit0.0 installs and verifies the official Workers Vitest pool before Unit0.1. Unit0.1's real D1 test path is `test/workers-runtime/d1-atomic.worker.test.ts`; `test/workers/d1-atomic.worker.test.ts` is forbidden. Unit2 is historical inventory only. The remote REST adapter remains provisional until Unit29.1.2 performs a provider-backed QA qualification with a disposable table/rows, one successful control batch, and first/middle/last statement failures followed by independent readback proving total rollback. It records endpoint/API/provider identity and result hashes, cleans all qualification rows, and blocks remote migration/release use if behavior is unproved, partial, changed, or cleanup is incomplete.
- Migration 0025 owns the expiring ShoppingMutationReceipt and complete dedupe/recovery/pruning contract. Every transport carries one stable mutation id across physical retries; REST stores its completed ApiIdempotencyKey response in the same batch. Pre-commit failure leaves neither key, receipt, nor effect. Post-commit response loss recovers the live receipt and byte-identical outcome without reapplying additions. A stale requestHash conflicts while live; equality at expiry permits a fresh mutation. Cleanup/residue/schema inventories include ShoppingMutationReceipt and NativeSyncClock.
- The line-224 `sqlString` interpolation cleanup instruction and line-1682 Wrangler-transaction atomicity assertion are historical and rejected. Cleanup SQL uses bound AtomicSqlStatement values. Reviewed migration application uses the qualified remote AtomicSqlPort, including payload, attestation, and migration ledger in one batch; Wrangler may materialize/configure but is not evidence of rollback. Only the closed migration-defined trigger set may execute outside the port.
- Authenticated privacy cleanup removes all departing identity, recipe association, snapshot, and reversible namespace state before releasing identity confirmation. It may leave only the generic retirement fence, opaque v2 generation fence, and account-neutral anonymous recipe-key state created by a pre-authenticated old tab; that anonymous state contains no authenticated identity or send authority, is never adopted without the explicit conflict flow, and is independently resettable. An earlier universal claim that no recipe association of any kind can remain after logout is rejected.
- Unit18 owns both permanent migration triggers and trigger races; Unit19 owns provider-neutral writer/catch-up behavior; Unit29.1.3 owns provider bootstrap/CLI only. Unit27.2 updates the existing `docs/api.md` Swift sample and generated/developer surfaces; `docs/apple-native-dogfood.md` is not created. Schema filtering precedes sort/cursor/limit, and v2 cursor id/UTF-8/base64url bytes are exact as above.
- Unit29.2.2 accepts only the final eight environment policies, loader-free descriptor model, sixteen owners, and thirty-two guards. Unit30 enforces the literal eighteen-member pre-visual registry in the doing document; Unit31 adds production-visual-dogfood for nineteen. Every fourteen/fifteen count, seven-policy clause, parent projection, boundary 34 implementation, monolithic Unit29.2 green/output/acceptance clause, and `Unit29.2a` acceptance is historical and rejected.

### Round 100 Quality Closures

These clauses are the final executable authority for Round99 findings and replace every conflicting earlier sentence.

- Shopping receipts are bounded canonical state. Migration 0025 defines exact cascading `ShoppingMutationReceipt={authorId,mutationId,requestHash,outcomeJson,acceptedAt,expiresAt}` with primary key `(authorId,mutationId)` and index `(authorId,expiresAt,mutationId)`. `acceptedAt` and `expiresAt` are canonical millisecond timestamps whose epoch values satisfy exact `expiresAtMs=acceptedAtMs+86_400_000`; overflow aborts before mutation. A receipt is live exactly while `nowMs < expiresAtMs`, expires at equality, and the same mutation id may be reused only after expiry. Before each author's shopping command, the owning AtomicSqlPort batch deletes at most 100 expired rows for that author in `(expiresAt,mutationId)` order. `workers/app.ts` adds one scheduled handler and `wrangler.json` defines production `17 3 * * *` plus QA `29 3 * * *`; each invocation performs at most twenty ordered 500-row expired-receipt deletes through AtomicSqlPort, then stops even if residue remains. Scheduled cleanup is storage maintenance only, emits no user-visible sync event, and runs through `ctx.waitUntil`. Tests cover the live/expiry boundary, reuse after expiry, response loss on both sides of expiry, author/global bounds, inactive accounts, overlapping cron/mutation cleanup, user cascade, rollback, and exact top-level/environment trigger configuration.

- REST shopping bypasses generic pre-reservation completely. `runAtomicShoppingApiMutation` first reads any live ShoppingMutationReceipt and ApiIdempotencyKey; matching completed rows replay, mismatched operation/request hash conflicts, and an expired pair is removed only inside the new attempt batch. A fresh REST attempt generates one ApiIdempotencyKey row id and inserts the already-completed exact response row in the same AtomicSqlPort batch as the list lease/effect and ShoppingMutationReceipt. Lease acquisition and every effect/receipt statement are guarded by `EXISTS` on that exact generated id; a concurrent unique-key loser therefore changes no list row and rereads the winner. For web/MCP the receipt and effect share the list lease/version guard; a no-op receipt is guarded by the exact observed list version or observed-list-absence predicate. Every correctness-bearing insert/lease/CAS uses explicit `RETURNING` rows, never `meta.changes`. Failure before any statement or at every statement boundary leaves no key, receipt, lease, or effect; commit followed by response loss leaves both completed records and one effect. Generic reservation behavior remains unchanged for non-shopping APIs, but shopping source gates reject `reserveIdempotencyKey`, `completeIdempotencyKey`, reservation deletion, an incomplete key row, or an independently committed response. Race tests cross same/different hashes, another operation using the same client key, no-op/content-changing paths, process death after the former reservation point, and both D1 serialization orders.

- Stable shopping mutation carriers are transport-exact. REST continues to require `clientMutationId`. Web uses `app/lib/shopping-mutation-client.ts`: it persists one lowercase UUID-v4 under the authenticated account plus logical-action/payload-hash namespace before serialization, sends that same value through reload/network/unknown-response retries, removes it only after a definitive response, and creates a fresh id for a later intentional action even when the payload is identical. Logout/account-switch retirement removes this account namespace. MCP adds backward-compatible optional `clientMutationId` to every shopping write tool. `JsonRpcToolRouter.callTool` additionally receives exact `{requestId:number|string|null}`; HTTP threads it through `app/lib/mcp/json-rpc.server.ts`, `app/lib/mcp/http-mcp.server.ts`, `app/lib/spoonjoy-api-request.server.ts`, and `SpoonjoyApiContext`, while the stdio process owns one random process-session UUID. On HTTP initialize without an inbound session, the response carries a newly generated lowercase UUID-v4 `Mcp-Session-Id`; later requests may echo it, and the endpoint remains stateless. Omitted clientMutationId derives a non-egressing SHA-256 mutation id from principal id, credential identity, tool name, canonical request hash, typed JSON-RPC id, and MCP session id. A missing HTTP session uses an explicit `legacy` namespace and therefore requires the client's JSON-RPC ids to remain unique for intentional writes during the 24-hour receipt window; a reused id with changed input conflicts. Tests prove retries serialize identical bytes, string `"1"` differs from number `1`, two sessions using the same id remain distinct, two intentional post-success calls get distinct ids, old sessionless clients retain request-id fallback, notifications cannot write, malformed/absent ids fail before mutation, and no principal/session/raw credential enters stored outcome data.

- Every content-changing shopping add/restore/check/uncheck/delete/clear, including recipe aggregate and web/REST/MCP writers, allocates its resource timestamp inside the same AtomicSqlPort batch as exact `max(databaseNow,completeAccountHighWater+1ms)` and advances NativeSyncClock before commit. The complete account high-water is the Round99 source set, not merely ShoppingList/item local time. Parent and every changed item use the one issued canonical timestamp; no-op, validation failure, lost lease, receipt replay, conflict, or rollback allocates no native timestamp. Migration reconciliation and old-Worker dirty-list reconciliation use the same account allocator. Future-skew fixtures for every retained source cross every shopping operation and prove the next native page observes the change.

- Native clock repair and feed reads are one consistent D1 snapshot. Before validating a cursor, every schema-1/schema-2 request executes one AtomicSqlPort batch that atomically upserts NativeSyncClock to `max(existingClock,completeCurrentSourceHighWater)`, returns the repaired floor, and reads every schema-eligible source table. This repair runs even when the clock row already exists. All source result sets belong to that one D1 transaction; only after batch validation may application code merge, schema-filter, BINARY-sort, cursor-filter, and page. The existing page-floor advancement is a second AtomicSqlPort write after immutable payload derivation and before response bytes. A row committed between any source statement is impossible inside the snapshot; a row committed after it carries an account-issued timestamp and appears on the next request. Workerd tests pause a writer at every source-statement boundary, exercise every old-Worker-visible source against an existing stale clock, and prove no item is skipped across pages.

- SavedRecipe compatibility never resurrects an explicit unsave. A compatibility-eligible row is an active RecipeInCookbook membership for an active recipe with neither SavedRecipe nor an existing `NativeSyncTombstone(accountId,'savedRecipe',recipeId)`. Catch-up inserts only that set; its final invariant is zero compatibility-eligible missing rows, not zero membership rows missing SavedRecipe. Every explicit membership-add/re-save helper and the permanent old-Worker RecipeInCookbook insert trigger atomically deletes that savedRecipe tombstone before or with SavedRecipe creation and its later issued timestamp. A passive catch-up never deletes a tombstone. Explicit unsave still deletes only SavedRecipe and upserts the tombstone without removing memberships. Tests force catch-up versus unsave in both commit orders, response loss at each boundary, duplicate existing membership plus explicit add, old-Worker insert, re-save, and a later catch-up; intentional unsave remains unsaved until a new save action.

- AtomicSqlResult.changes is validated nonnegative-safe-integer telemetry only and never authorizes correctness. Every lease, reservation, dedupe winner, version update, migration ledger/attestation insert, and cleanup CAS uses explicit `INSERT|UPDATE|DELETE ... RETURNING` aliases and decides from exact returned row count/keys/values. Unit0.1 rejects a caller that branches on `changes`, a REST adapter that omits or invents RETURNING rows, or any mismatch among statement count, expectedColumns, result keys, value grammar, and typed bindings. Exact binding qualification covers string, finite integer, finite fractional number, and null; nonfinite/unsafe/unsupported values fail before network.

- Remote D1 qualification is release-executable and source-bound. Exact no-extra-fields `D1AtomicQualification={version:1,releasePhase:'implementation'|'qa'|'production_workflow'|'production_validation',ownerKeySha256,targetEnvironment:'qa',providerIdentity:CloudflareDatabaseIdentity,endpointIdentity,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,adapterPath:'scripts/cloudflare-d1-query.mjs',adapterSha256,atomicContractSha256,qualificationKeySha256,cases,cleanupProof,qualifiedAt}` uses exact `endpointIdentity={origin:'https://api.cloudflare.com',pathTemplate:'/client/v4/accounts/{accountId}/d1/database/{databaseId}/query',apiContract:'d1-rest-query-v1'}`. `qualificationKeySha256` hashes the complete owner/release/provider/endpoint/source/tree/adapter/contract identity. `cases` is the BINARY-id-sorted closed set of success plus injected-failure-at-every-ordinal cases over qualification-only table/index/trigger/data/attestation/ledger statements, exact string/finite-number/null bindings, expectedColumns/result count/key/value validation, and one success/zero-row CAS using RETURNING. Each case records exact statement/result/readback hashes and expected `committed|rolled_back`; independent schema/data readback must prove total commit or rollback. Qualification-only object identifiers are `q_` plus the first 32 lowercase hex characters of qualificationKeySha256, are quoted by one closed identifier helper, and are the sole identifier interpolation exception. Migration 0024 defines the permanent empty bootstrap table; before 0024, one exact idempotent single-statement CREATE of that same schema is the sole prequalification mutation. The successful control is independently read back, then every qualification row/object is removed through the now-qualified adapter; `cleanupProof` hashes independent zero-row/zero-object/schema-inventory readback. Any partial/malformed/stale/provider-drift/source-drift/adapter-drift/endpoint-drift case or residue blocks.

- The stable direct qualifier ProcessSpec is `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','qualify-d1-atomicity','--release-phase',P,'--target-env','qa','--source-sha',Q,'--source-tree-sha',T,'--out',O]` with output operand index 12, `environmentPolicy:'cloudflare-d1'`, output `json:d1-atomic-qualification.json:D1AtomicQualification`, recovery `qualified_current_owner`, 300000ms phase cap, and 1080000ms total timeout. Unit29.1.2c runs releasePhase implementation against its exact clean commit/tree. Unit33 order begins `qa-deployment-before,qa-d1-atomic-qualification,qa-cleanup-reconcile`; qualification is before the first QA mutation. The production workflow's direct `scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs` internally runs releasePhase production_workflow against QA and embeds/hash-binds that proof in production-workflow-result.json before any production D1 migration. Unit37 order places `production-d1-atomic-qualification` immediately before `production-stage-deployment-freeze`; it uses releasePhase production_validation and QA, and precedes production catch-up/cleanup mutations. Every remote mutation command cross-links the current qualifying path/hash in its ProcessCommand or workflow result. Qualification artifacts live at the owning attempt's stable `out/d1-atomic-qualification.json`, enter the release manifest, and are valid only for that owner/source/tree/provider/endpoint/adapter/contract tuple. QA qualification and the pre-freeze production-validation qualifier add no post-freeze owner, so the exact sixteen-owner/thirty-two-guard suffix remains unchanged.

- Qualification evidence has exact inner schemas. `D1AtomicQualificationCase={caseId,program:'binding'|'returning_cas'|'atomic_program',failureOrdinal,statementCount,expectedOutcome:'committed'|'rolled_back',statementSetSha256,resultSetSha256,readbackSha256,passed:true}` is exact/no-extra-fields; statementCount is positive safe integer and failureOrdinal is null or an integer in `1..statementCount`. The BINARY-caseId-sorted closed set is exactly `binding-control`, `returning-cas-success`, `returning-cas-zero`, `atomic-control`, and `atomic-failure-<NNNN>` for every statement ordinal in the canonical atomic program. The first four have null failureOrdinal and committed outcomes except returning-cas-zero, which is a committed zero-row RETURNING result; every atomic-failure case has its matching ordinal and rolled_back. `D1AtomicQualificationCleanupProof={qualificationKeySha256,baselineSchemaInventorySha256,finalSchemaInventorySha256,remainingQualificationRowCount:0,remainingQualificationObjectNames:[],readbackSha256,cleanedAt}` is exact; baseline and final inventory hashes must equal. `cases` and `cleanupProof` in D1AtomicQualification are exactly these types. Any duplicate/missing/extra case, noncontiguous failure matrix, false pass, hash mismatch, surviving row/object, or inventory drift rejects.

- Qualification ownership is canonical rather than descriptive. Exact `D1AtomicQualificationOwner` is one of `{releasePhase:'implementation',unit:'29.1.2c',sourceSha,sourceTreeSha}`, `{releasePhase:'qa',releaseRun,stageRun,logicalName:'qa-d1-atomic-qualification',attempt,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha}`, `{releasePhase:'production_validation',releaseRun,stageRun,logicalName:'production-d1-atomic-qualification',attempt,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha}`, or `{releasePhase:'production_workflow',repository:'spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2',workflowRunId,runAttempt,jobName:'deploy',sourceSha,sourceTreeSha}`. Run/stage/attempt/workflow numbers are positive safe integers. `ownerKeySha256` hashes compact canonical owner bytes in displayed field order. `qualificationKeySha256` hashes compact canonical `{owner,providerIdentity,endpointIdentity,sourceSha,sourceTreeSha,adapterPath,adapterSha256,atomicContractSha256}` bytes in displayed field order. `atomicContractSha256` hashes the compact canonical exported AtomicSql contract descriptor used by both qualifier and adapter. A proof is current only when every owner, source/tree, provider, endpoint, adapter, and contract byte revalidates at consumption time.

- Release registration and cross-linking are exact. `D1AtomicQualificationRef={path,sha256}` is exact and resolves to one hash-valid D1AtomicQualification. Final ProcessSpec adds required `requiresQualifiedRemoteD1:boolean`; it is true exactly for `qa-cleanup-reconcile`, `qa-deploy-ensure`, `qa-shopping-compat-close`, `qa-saved-recipe-catchup`, `qa-cleanup-apply-before`, `production-shopping-compat-close`, `production-saved-recipe-catchup`, and `production-cleanup-reconcile`, and false for every other registered row including each qualifier and every read-only assertion. Final ProcessCommand adds `d1AtomicQualificationRef:D1AtomicQualificationRef|null`; it is nonnull exactly when its ProcessSpec flag is true, points to the current same-stage QA or production-validation qualifier Artifact, and is null otherwise. That Artifact is exact `{kind:'json',path:<qualifier attempt task-relative out/d1-atomic-qualification.json>,sha256}` and is appended only after output schema/hash/current-owner validation. Unit29.1.2c's implementation proof is the exact verification output `unit-29.1.2-qa-atomic-qualification.json` named by the doing document and predates the release manifest; it is not falsely represented as a StageRun Artifact. The production workflow has no ProcessCommand cross-link: final ProductionWorkflowResult instead adds required embedded `d1AtomicQualification:D1AtomicQualification` and `d1AtomicQualificationSha256`, with releasePhase production_workflow and hash over the complete embedded bytes. These fields are validated before reviewed migration apply and again after Unit37 artifact download. Missing, stale, cross-stage, self-referential, or read-only-command qualification refs reject.

- The direct qualifier is the only owner that may use the unqualified remote adapter, and it may execute only the closed qualification program plus its cleanup. Before migration 0024, the one bootstrap CREATE is byte-equal the reviewed 0024 bootstrap-table statement and is followed by independent schema readback before any multi-statement qualification. After 0024, even that bootstrap path is forbidden. The implementation proof is captured at its exact clean commit/tree; the QA and production-validation proofs are normal StageRun JSON Artifacts; the workflow proof is embedded in production-workflow-result.json. Every later remote AtomicSqlPort mutation consumes its exact current ref before descriptor opening or network, while source/tree/provider/adapter/contract drift requires a fresh qualifier. QA qualification and the pre-freeze production-validation qualifier add no post-freeze owner, so the exact sixteen-owner/thirty-two-guard suffix remains unchanged.

- Secret-bearing registered child vectors are direct and loader-free. Final QA broad cleanup is `['node','scripts/cleanup-local-qa-data.mjs','--target-env','qa','--apply','--journal-root',J,'--operation-key',K,'--out',O]` with task-relative operands 6 and 10. Final SavedRecipe catch-up is `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','saved-recipes-catchup','--target-env',E,'--source-sha',Q,'--source-tree-sha',T,'--out',O]` with operand 10. Capture/freeze/deployment-probe remain the final direct vectors in Round99, and production visual is `['node','scripts/production-visual-dogfood.mjs','--base-url',PB,'--source-sha',DS,'--worker-version-id',DV,'--release-identity-sha256',X,'--release-attempt-path',Y,'--out',O]` with task-relative operands 11 and 13. Every displayed pnpm/package-alias/TypeScript-loader form for those registered children, including Round87 line 1706 and the Unit33/37 narrative commands, is historical documentation only and rejected by source/registry tests. Package aliases may remain human conveniences but never appear in StoredArgv or a secret-bearing process tree.

- Feature workerd evidence is mandatory rather than inferred from SQLite fixtures. Unit1.2 owns `test/workers-runtime/shopping-migration.worker.test.ts` over real 0025 SQL, every compatibility/cascade trigger, allocator/receipt expiry, scheduled prune, first/middle/last failure, and both serialization orders. Unit18 owns `test/workers-runtime/saved-recipe-migration.worker.test.ts` over real 0027 SQL, both permanent triggers, tombstone/catch-up races, clock repair, cascades, three timestamp encodings, and allocator serialization. Unit24 owns `test/workers-runtime/recipe-tag-migration.worker.test.ts` over real 0028 SQL, trigger/lease/limit conflicts, account-clock allocation, rollback, and both replacement orders. Unit27.2's native tests use the worker pool for one-snapshot paging. Each owning verification unit runs its worker file at 100% with zero warnings; local migration tests supplement but cannot satisfy these acceptances.

### Round 101 Full-System Audit Closures

These clauses are the latest executable authority. They replace every conflicting Round100-or-earlier transaction, anonymous-candidate, local-storage, native-feed, receipt-retention, projection-retention, D1 REST, qualification, registry, workflow-deploy, rollback, feature-smoke, and authorization-boundary instruction. Source/config/schema tests reject the superseded forms rather than merely ignoring them.

- `AtomicSqlPort` is the sole application multi-statement primitive, including User plus CookStorageIdentity provisioning, CookSessionIndex plus fence projection, cookbook membership plus SavedRecipe, new Recipe plus RecipeTag, declarative tag replacement, shopping mutations, cleanup, and migration attestation. Exact exported `AtomicSqlContractDescriptor={version:1,placeholderGrammar:'numbered_once',resultAuthority:'returning_rows_only',statements:'ordered_atomic_batch',bindings:['text','integer','real','null'],remoteEncoding:'d1_rest_string_v1'}` is compact-canonical hashed. Each statement uses contiguous `?1..?N`, and a structural SQL tokenizer proves every ordinal appears exactly once outside literals/comments/quoted identifiers. The Worker adapter binds typed values directly. The REST compiler deterministically rewrites text to `CAST(? AS TEXT)`, safe integer to `CAST(? AS INTEGER)`, finite non-integer to `CAST(? AS REAL)`, and null to `NULLIF(?,'')`, emitting one wire string per nonliteral placeholder and `''` for null. It rejects anonymous/bare/repeated/skipped/out-of-range placeholders, unsafe/nonfinite values, unsupported types, comments containing fake binds, and any result whose statement count, expected columns, row keys, or typed values differ. Prisma transactions, nested creates, `meta.changes` authority, and a second batch implementation are source-rejected.

- Remote qualification has one exact canonical program in execution order: create qualification data table; create its index; create qualification ledger; create qualification trigger; create qualification attestation table; insert one data row with text/integer/real/null bindings and RETURNING; insert one attestation row with RETURNING; read back joined data/schema/ledger. Cases are execution-ordered exactly `binding-control`, `atomic-failure-0001` through `atomic-failure-0008`, `atomic-control`, `returning-cas-success`, `returning-cas-zero`; case meaning comes from `caseId`, never sort position. Each failure case replaces exactly its named ordinal with a syntactically valid guaranteed-failing statement and proves all prior statements rolled back. Every statement, typed descriptor, compiled REST SQL, wire string vector, expected columns, returned rows, and independent readback contributes to the case hashes. Qualification identifiers use the closed quoted `q_<first32 qualificationKeySha256>` grammar; no other identifier interpolation is legal.

- Migration 0024 permanently creates empty `D1AtomicQualificationBootstrap`, `D1AtomicQualificationJournal`, and `D1AtomicQualificationReceipt` tables. Before 0024 only the byte-equal idempotent single-statement bootstrap CREATE sequence plus independent schema readback is legal. Exact receipt identity is the complete owner/provider/endpoint/source/tree/adapter/contract hash and expires after 24 hours; exact current receipt replays without mutation. Otherwise one single-statement prepare insert creates a journal with the complete case-set hash and `nextCaseOrdinal`. Each case atomically advances only its matching journal ordinal after independent readback; response loss rereads journal/residue and resumes. Foreign owner, malformed ordinal, unexpected object, hash drift, or contradictory receipt blocks. The final now-qualified batch deletes all qualification objects/rows, inserts the completed receipt, deletes the journal, and prunes up to 100 expired receipts in qualification time/key order; a permanent trigger caps receipts at 2048 and rejects before program mutation when expired pruning cannot make room. Response loss rereads the receipt. `cleanupProof` excludes the permanent empty bootstrap/journal/receipt schemas and proves no owner-scoped row/object residue. `qualified_current_owner` recovery is legal only for the four qualifier ProcessSpecs and means exactly this receipt/journal state machine; it never applies to an ordinary command.

- Final no-extra-fields `ProcessSpec` is ordered exactly `{environmentPolicy,outputPolicy,operationPhaseCapMs,totalTimeoutMs,recoveryPolicy,requiresQualifiedRemoteD1,storedArgv,operands}`. `environmentPolicy` is exactly `base|local-db|github|cloudflare|cloudflare-rest|cloudflare-d1|cloudflare-github|cloudflare-rest-github`; `recoveryPolicy` is exactly `ordinary|registered_operation|idempotent_zero_missing|read_only_refreeze|qualified_current_owner`. Final no-extra-fields `ProcessCommand` is ordered exactly `{mode:'process',logicalName,attempt,reservationToken,executionHost,status,command,storedCommandSha256,envNames,timeoutMs,deadlineStartedAt,deadlineAt,deploymentIdentitySha256,d1AtomicQualificationRef,spawnGenerations,spawnWaitPaths,spawnWaitSetSha256,spawnBlockerPath,spawnBlockerSha256,terminalSource,outPath,intentPath,armPath,dispatchPath,deploymentDriftGuardPath,deploymentDriftGuardSha256,startedAt,endedAt,exitCode,warningCount,failureReason,interruptionReason,stdoutPath,stderrPath}`. Existing field domains/nullability remain authoritative; qualification/deployment/drift members obey their later exact owners. Schema tests compare ordered key arrays and reject every shorter historical shape.

- Unit29.1.2 creates and 100%-covers the minimal loader-free `scripts/release-provider-command.mjs` descriptor bootstrap together with the qualifier before Unit29.1.2c invokes it. Unit29.1.3 extends that already-green bootstrap with provenance/catch-up commands; it does not create or replace the qualifier entry. The stable qualifier argv is exact `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs','qualify-d1-atomicity','--release-phase',<literal release phase>,'--target-env','qa','--source-sha',<exact owner source SHA>,'--source-tree-sha',<exact owner tree SHA>,'--out',<task-relative output>]`; no `P/Q/T` shorthand is stored. Its sole operand is the zero-based output index 12, policy is `cloudflare-d1`, output is `json:d1-atomic-qualification.json:D1AtomicQualification`, caps are 300000/1080000, recovery is `qualified_current_owner`, and qualification-required is false.

- The immutable registry stores only direct first-party Node vectors. Exact compile-time sentinel enum is `BRANCH_HEAD_SHA|BRANCH_HEAD_TREE_SHA|TASK_MERGE_SHA|TASK_MERGE_TREE_SHA|DEPLOYED_SOURCE_SHA|DEPLOYED_TREE_SHA|DEPLOYED_VERSION_ID|RELEASE_PHASE|RELEASE_OUT|RELEASE_INTENT|RELEASE_ARM|RELEASE_DISPATCH|RELEASE_ATTEMPT_PATH|RELEASE_IDENTITY_SHA256|CLEANUP_TARGETS_JSON`; these names are disjoint and resolve once from validated immutable evidence before ProcessCommand hashing. Every old `P/Q/T`, package alias, `pnpm release:provenance`, `pnpm feature:smoke`, `pnpm production:readiness`, TypeScript loader, and runtime-discovered argv is illegal. Exact provider bootstrap prefix is `['node','scripts/release-provider-command.mjs',<subcommand>]`; the closed subcommands/argument suffixes are: `capture-deployments --environment <qa|production> --script <spoonjoy-v2-qa|spoonjoy-v2> --out RELEASE_OUT`; `ensure-qa-deploy --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --base-url https://spoonjoy-v2-qa.mendelow-studio.workers.dev --script spoonjoy-v2-qa --intent RELEASE_INTENT --arm RELEASE_ARM --dispatch RELEASE_DISPATCH --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-health --base-url <target> --source-sha <branch|deployed source> --worker-version-id <frozen version> --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-drift`, `ensure-pr`, `verify-protection`, `verify-checks`, `verify-review-comments`, `verify-merge-ready`, and `merge-pr` with the exact prior reviewed suffixes; `discover-ci`, `discover-production`, `watch-workflow`, `verify-deploy-job`, `download-artifact`, and `continuation-scan` with the exact prior reviewed suffixes but output basename `production-workflow-result.json`; `saved-recipes-catchup` with target/source/tree/out; `freeze-production-stage` and `probe-production-deployment` with the exact Round99 suffixes; and `production-readiness --base-url <target> --source-sha DEPLOYED_SOURCE_SHA --worker-version-id DEPLOYED_VERSION_ID`. Every row retains its final prior output policy/caps/recovery/operands except the explicit Round101 changes, and sets `requiresQualifiedRemoteD1` true only for the eight mutation rows already named by Round100.

- Provider bootstrap vectors are complete, not shorthand. In addition to the preceding sentinel enum define disjoint `TESTED_BASE_SHA|PR_NUMBER|HEAD_REF|PR_TITLE|PR_BODY|CI_DATABASE_ID|CI_RUN_ATTEMPT|WORKFLOW_DATABASE_ID|WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT|EXCLUDED_HEAD_REF|FROZEN_DEPLOYMENT_PATH|WORKFLOW_RESULT_PATH`. Exact argv after the common Node/bootstrap prefix is: `verify-drift --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --branch main --tested-base-sha TESTED_BASE_SHA --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --tree-sha BRANCH_HEAD_TREE_SHA --out RELEASE_OUT`; `ensure-pr --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --head-ref HEAD_REF --head-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --base-ref main --base-sha TESTED_BASE_SHA --title PR_TITLE --body PR_BODY --intent RELEASE_INTENT --arm RELEASE_ARM --dispatch RELEASE_DISPATCH --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-protection --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --branch main --required-context workers-coverage --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-checks --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --pr-number PR_NUMBER --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-review-comments --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --pr-number PR_NUMBER --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-merge-ready --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --pr-number PR_NUMBER --tested-base-sha TESTED_BASE_SHA --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --tree-sha BRANCH_HEAD_TREE_SHA --required-context workers-coverage --out RELEASE_OUT`; `merge-pr --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --pr-number PR_NUMBER --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --tested-base-sha TESTED_BASE_SHA --tree-sha BRANCH_HEAD_TREE_SHA --method squash --intent RELEASE_INTENT --arm RELEASE_ARM --dispatch RELEASE_DISPATCH --out RELEASE_OUT`; `discover-ci --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --workflow-path .github/workflows/ci.yml --out RELEASE_OUT`; `discover-production --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --workflow-path .github/workflows/production-deploy.yml --triggering-ci-database-id CI_DATABASE_ID --triggering-ci-run-attempt CI_RUN_ATTEMPT --out RELEASE_OUT`; `watch-workflow --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --run-id WORKFLOW_DATABASE_ID --run-attempt WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT --source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --workflow-path .github/workflows/production-deploy.yml --out RELEASE_OUT`; `verify-deploy-job --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --run-id WORKFLOW_DATABASE_ID --run-attempt WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT --source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --job-name deploy --out RELEASE_OUT`; `download-artifact --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --run-id WORKFLOW_DATABASE_ID --run-attempt WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT --source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --tree-sha TASK_MERGE_TREE_SHA --artifact-name mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts --out RELEASE_OUT`; `freeze-production-stage --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --task-source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --task-tree-sha TASK_MERGE_TREE_SHA --workflow-result WORKFLOW_RESULT_PATH --script spoonjoy-v2 --base-url https://spoonjoy.app --out RELEASE_OUT`; `probe-production-deployment --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --task-source-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --frozen-deployment FROZEN_DEPLOYMENT_PATH --script spoonjoy-v2 --base-url https://spoonjoy.app --out RELEASE_OUT`; and `continuation-scan --repo spoonjoy/spoonjoy-v2 --pr-number PR_NUMBER --source-sha BRANCH_HEAD_SHA --tree-sha BRANCH_HEAD_TREE_SHA --merge-sha TASK_MERGE_SHA --excluded-head-ref EXCLUDED_HEAD_REF --out RELEASE_OUT`. Stored argv contains enum tokens, never their uppercase text; resolution emits only validated literal operands before hashing.

- The remaining provider-bound vectors are also direct: shopping close/assert use `node scripts/shopping-compat-catchup.mjs`; broad cleanup/apply/assert use `node scripts/cleanup-local-qa-data.mjs`; API smoke uses `node scripts/smoke-api-live.mjs`; lifecycle/reconcile uses `node scripts/smoke-live.mjs`; feature smoke uses `node scripts/feature-smoke-live.mjs`; production visual uses `node scripts/production-visual-dogfood.mjs`. Their exact target/base/source/tree/version/identity/attempt/output flags remain in the prior final order. Unit29.2 registry tests enumerate every Unit32-37 logical name, complete eight-field ProcessSpec, resolved argv, operand index, output basename/schema, environment policy, caps, recovery, and qualification flag; an omitted row, indirect prefix, extra child, or historical narrative vector fails. Unit33 and Unit37 `What` paragraphs are inventory where they conflict with this registry and the exact Round100 orders.

- Production Durable Object lifecycle is an irreversible release boundary. This release never invokes `wrangler versions upload`, gradual deployments, traffic percentages, rollback-version-id, or a prior Worker rollback because those operations cannot apply or safely cross the new SQLite DO migration. QA uses one direct `wrangler deploy --env qa` behind `qa-deploy-ensure`. Production is the top-level Wrangler configuration, so its workflow validates the exact task source/tree and qualification, applies reviewed D1 migrations while rollback remains possible, then invokes exactly one direct bare `wrangler deploy` with no `--env` flag for the source/tree that declares the CookSession Durable Object migration. Any production environment flag is source-rejected. Before deploy dispatch, D1 failure may compensate to its pre-workflow state. After provider acceptance or indeterminate dispatch, the old Worker is never restored: recovery independently reads source/tree/version, DO namespace/migration state, D1 attestations, and health, then either reconciles exact success or requires a forward repair/deploy from a verified descendant. Divergent/ancestor/unknown state fails closed.

- Workflow qualification evidence exists before rollout effects. Exact no-extra-fields `ProductionWorkflowQualificationArtifact={version:1,taskSourceSha,taskTreeSha,workflowRunId,runAttempt,jobName:'deploy',d1AtomicQualification,d1AtomicQualificationSha256,createdAt}` is atomically written as `production-workflow-qualification.json`; a distinct `actions/upload-artifact@v4` step uploads immutable artifact name `production-workflow-qualification` and a following download/readback step rehashes it before the first production rollout D1 mutation. It cannot be appended or replaced. Exact final `ProductionWorkflowResult={version:1,outcome:'promoted'|'reconcile_exact'|'superseded_by_descendant',taskSourceSha,taskTreeSha,workflowRunId,runAttempt,phase:'complete',triggeringCiDatabaseId,triggeringCiRunAttempt,qualificationArtifactPath:'production-workflow-qualification.json',qualificationArtifactSha256,d1AtomicQualification,d1AtomicQualificationSha256,promotionDecision,promotionDecisionSha256,postDecisionReadback,postDecisionReadbackSha256,reviewedMigrations,migrationApply,databaseRollbackSupported:false,durableObjectLifecycleApplied,workerDeployDispatchSha256,deployedSourceSha,deployedTreeSha,deployedVersionId,previousVersionId,candidateVersionId,promotionReceiptSha256,zeroMutationProof}`. Its workflowRunId/runAttempt equal the qualification artifact and selected workflow. Promoted requires `durableObjectLifecycleApplied:true`, a nonnull sole dispatch hash, exact deployed task source/tree, and the immutable qualification bytes/hash equal the separately downloaded pre-effect artifact. Reconcile/superseded require the existing zero-mutation proof and may report lifecycle true only from independent provider readback. Recovery and artifact fallback write `production-workflow-result.json` only when independently proven; failures write only the failure artifact. `production-release.json` is illegal.

- The workflow bootstrap has exact normal `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --source-tree-sha "$SOURCE_TREE_SHA" --artifact-dir mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts` and recovery `node scripts/deploy-production-canary.mjs --recover-forward --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --source-tree-sha "$SOURCE_TREE_SHA" --artifact-dir mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts` forms. There is no rollback form. The only pre-mutation artifact is the qualification artifact; the final result is produced only after all fields are independently knowable. Workflow tests crash at every qualification write/upload, D1 statement, deploy dispatch/response/readback, health, final-result write/upload, and recovery boundary, and prove no second deploy for an exact accepted source.

- Cook client v2 storage names are exact: session client id `spoonjoy-cook-client:v2`; client lock `spoonjoy-cook-client:v2:<clientId>`; user lock `spoonjoy-cook-user:v2:<userNamespace>`; record `spoonjoy-cook-record:v2:<userNamespace>:<recipeNamespace>:<clientId>`; reset `spoonjoy-cook-reset:v2:<userNamespace>:<recipeNamespace>`; user generation `spoonjoy-cook-user-generation:v2:<userNamespace>`; recipe generation `spoonjoy-cook-recipe-generation:v2:<userNamespace>:<recipeNamespace>`; global cleanup lock `spoonjoy-cook-cleanup:v2`; anonymous decision `spoonjoy-cook-anonymous-decision:v2:<userNamespace>:<recipeNamespace>`. Components are the existing opaque nonreversible encodings; delimiters are forbidden inside them. Only the generic v1 retirement fence remains executable from v1. Source tests enumerate every create/read/delete/lock call and reject reversible or undeclared prefixes.

- Local cook storage is bounded and loss-averse. Each authenticated user namespace permits at most eight records and 4,194,304 aggregate UTF-8 bytes; automatic eviction may remove only stale, fully synchronized, default/terminal records after canonical server readback. A nonempty orphan requires an explicit named discard under the user lock and otherwise blocks a ninth record. Anonymous storage permits at most sixteen recipe entries and 2,097,152 aggregate bytes; no nondefault anonymous progress is silently evicted, and the UI exposes explicit discard when capacity blocks a new recipe. Persisted metadata indexes are checksummed hints only and are rebuilt by a bounded exact-prefix scan after missing/corrupt/crash states. Quota/write failure preserves the last durable record/queue prefix, surfaces retry/discard state, sends nothing newer than durable bytes, and never clears a candidate.

- Anonymous adoption is a no-extra-fields discriminated union. `AnonymousRawCandidate` is `{version:1,storageVersion:'v1',recipeId,rawSha256,rawBytes}` or `{version:1,storageVersion:'v2',recipeId,rawSha256,rawBytes,recordGeneration}`. `AnonymousCandidateDecision={version:1,storageVersion:'v1'|'v2',userNamespace,recipeNamespace,rawSha256,recordGeneration:number|null,userGeneration,choice:'pending'|'use'|'keep_server'|'replace_server'|'discard',mutationId:string|null,resolutionProofSha256:string|null,createdAt,updatedAt}`; v1 requires null recordGeneration and v2 requires the exact observed generation. Every use/discard/replace rechecks version/hash/generations under the exclusive user lock. Raw and decision bytes survive reload, account switch, conflict, network/unknown response, and stale authorization; they clear only after discard is durably chosen or exact authenticated DO plus local-record readback proves accepted adoption. Tests and QA live smoke execute both versions through use, keep, replace, discard, reload, account switch, conflict, cleanup, and delayed old-writer recreation without prior-account disclosure.

- Web shopping mutation identity is per action instance, not payload. Exact persisted `ShoppingActionIntent={version:1,accountNamespace,userGeneration,actionInstanceId,logicalAction,payloadSha256,clientMutationId,state:'prepared'|'dispatched',createdAt}` uses independent lowercase UUID-v4 values for actionInstanceId and clientMutationId. Creation, lookup, serialization, definitive-success removal, logout, and account-switch retirement all run under the exact account generation lock; immediately before persistence and immediately before dispatch the current loader identity and generation must still match. Retries reuse the same intent, while two concurrent identical intentions create distinct ids. Tab/logout/account-switch races cannot write or dispatch for a retired identity.

- Every native-visible writer allocates through the account clock in its AtomicSqlPort batch: profile create/update, Recipe create/update/delete, Cookbook create/update/delete, all four cookbook membership writers, SavedRecipe save/unsave/catch-up, RecipeTag replacement/new-recipe tags, shopping current writers/reconciliation, and every NativeSyncTombstone writer. Migration triggers fence old-Worker User, Recipe, Cookbook, membership, and tombstone writes by advancing/repairing NativeSyncClock in the originating statement; after rollout closure, any old write that cannot execute the allocator is rejected by its compatibility epoch guard. Workerd tests future-skew every retained source against every writer and both old/current Worker serialization orders.

- Native feed work is bounded inside one snapshot. The structural cursor is decoded and grammar/range-validated before D1 access; only its retained-floor semantic check waits for the batch. That AtomicSqlPort batch repairs the complete source high-water and issues one indexed `WHERE accountId=? AND (updatedAt,kind,resourceId) > cursorTuple ORDER BY updatedAt,kind,resourceId LIMIT limit+1` query per schema-eligible source, including a deterministic impossible sentinel tuple when no cursor exists. The application performs a bounded global merge and emits at most limit. No source table is read wholesale. Malformed cursors perform zero D1 calls; stale/future persisted floors follow the existing explicit reset contract. Workerd tests prove snapshot consistency and query/result cardinality bounds at limit 1, maximum limit, ties, empty sources, and concurrent commits.

- Mutation receipts and idempotency keys are bounded together. `ShoppingMutationReceipt` adds nullable `idempotencyKeyId` with cascading foreign key to `ApiIdempotencyKey` for REST and null for web/MCP. Migration 0025 adds singleton `ShoppingReceiptQuota={id:1,totalCount,limit:250000}` plus triggers that maintain exact count and reject a new receipt before any list effect when the global cap would be exceeded. Each author may retain at most 2048 live receipts; the owning mutation batch first deletes up to 100 expired receipt/key pairs, and if 2048 unexpired receipts remain it applies backpressure before the list effect rather than weakening replay semantics. Scheduled cleanup deletes expired pairs atomically in resumable `(expiresAt,authorId,mutationId)` pages, records its next cursor, uses backpressure when the global cap remains full, and never commits an orphan ApiIdempotencyKey. Tests prove inactive-account/global convergence after expiry, exact cap equality, high-ingress backpressure, cascading user cleanup, and no effect when quota rejects.

- Private projection state is bounded by epoch. `CookProjectionEpoch={userId,epoch}` has one cascading row per user. Every DO projection UPSERT/DELETE carries the exact current epoch. In the same AtomicSqlPort batch as an accepted projection, D1 retains at most 64 ACTIVE index rows and 20 terminal history rows per user, prunes corresponding attempt fences, and rotates the epoch whenever pruning occurs. A delayed request from the old epoch returns `stale_epoch` with no write; the DO refreshes the epoch and reprojects only its current attempt. Tests serialize projection, prune, delayed retry, DO eviction, and response loss, and prove at most 84 index rows plus their exact fences with no progress-bearing field.

- The deployed authenticated feature registry has exactly 42 exchanges. Rows 1-4 are anonymous adoption, 5-15 shopping, 16 native schema-2 baseline cursor, 17 REST SavedRecipe PUT using `savedPutMutationId`, 18 owner blank-search positive, 19 owner filtered positive, 20 visitor list empty, 21 authenticated REST saved list positive, 22 authenticated REST detail `isSaved:true`, 23 public-read bearer REST detail `isSaved:null`, 24 native schema-2 SavedRecipe upsert after row-16 cursor, 25 REST SavedRecipe DELETE using `savedDeleteMutationId`, 26 native schema-2 SavedRecipe delete after row-24 cursor, 27 owner saved list empty, 28 owner tag update, 29 owner tag filter, 30 owner tag search, 31 native schema-2 recipe upsert with course/tags after row-26 cursor, 32-33 existing REST read compatibility rows, 34-35 existing MCP read rows, and 36-42 existing cook-history rows. Unit tests instrument row 23 to prove zero SavedRecipe lookup without exposing a debug field live. `FeatureMutationIds` is exact `{legacySeedMutationId,legacyConflictMutationId,legacyReplaceMutationId,restCheckMutationId,restDeleteMutationId,restRestoreMutationId,savedPutMutationId,savedDeleteMutationId,historyCompleteMutationId}` with nine pairwise-distinct UUID-v4 values. The identity index has 42 exchange refs plus two boundary-health refs; semantic/final aggregate file counts remain unchanged. QA and production run this full matrix against the frozen deployment, so web-only assertions or a REST 401 cannot satisfy SavedRecipe/tag parity.

- Terminal process authorization language is unambiguous. Every historical pre-dispatch `run-authorization-receipt` basename/schema is renamed `run-preauthorization-receipt`; it proves only that the paused child may be considered for dispatch and grants no provider/product/message effect. The immutable dispatch-intent publication is the sole effect/run authorization boundary. A provably dead/reaped child may be replaced only before dispatch-intent; after that boundary no replacement attempt may execute, and recovery is read/reconcile-only. State names may retain `run_authorized` solely as a historical internal enum alias mapped to preauthorization, but source tests reject treating it as effect authority. Slugger and process tests prove at most one attempt crosses dispatch-intent.

### Round 102 Complete E2E Authority

These clauses are the sole latest executable authority. They replace every conflicting earlier qualification, migration-0024 ownership, native-time ordering, projection-retention, anonymous-storage, shopping-intent, feature-registry, production-workflow, rollback, overlap, executable-coverage membership, output-policy grammar, ProcessSpec registry, and process-authorization sentence. In particular the imperative rollback/reopen/restore clauses near the historical Round 91, Round 92, and Round 95 sections and every `deploy-production-canary` executable/coverage instruction are rejected inventory; no implementation or release test may execute them.

- Migration ownership is immutable. Unit0 creates final byte-identical `migrations/0024_qa_cleanup_coordination.sql` and Prisma mirror containing only the already-declared legacy-helper removals plus `QaCleanupLease`, `SpoonjoyMigrationAttestation`, `D1AtomicQualificationBootstrap`, `D1AtomicQualificationJournal`, and `D1AtomicQualificationReceipt` with their declared indexes/triggers. Unit29.1.2 consumes and validates those bytes, source-rejects any Unit29 diff to them, and never edits either 0024 file. `D1AtomicQualificationBootstrap` is exact singleton `(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK(id=1),schemaVersion INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(schemaVersion=1),combinedCapacity INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(combinedCapacity=2048),journalTtlMs INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(journalTtlMs=900000),receiptTtlMs INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(receiptTtlMs=86400000),nextReservationGeneration INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(nextReservationGeneration BETWEEN 1 AND 9007199254740991),generationClaimSha256 TEXT NULL)` with row `(1,1,2048,900000,86400000,1,NULL)`. `D1AtomicQualificationJournal` is exact `(qualificationKeySha256 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,ownerKeySha256 TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,reservationGeneration INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE CHECK(reservationGeneration BETWEEN 1 AND 9007199254740991),ownerTokenSha256 TEXT NOT NULL,identityJson TEXT NOT NULL,identitySha256 TEXT NOT NULL,caseSetSha256 TEXT NOT NULL,nextCaseOrdinal INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(nextCaseOrdinal BETWEEN 1 AND 13),objectPrefix TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,createdAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL,updatedAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL,expiresAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(expiresAtMs=updatedAtMs+900000))` with index `(expiresAtMs,qualificationKeySha256)`. `objectPrefix` is exactly `q_<reservationGeneration as 13 lowercase zero-padded hex>_<first16 qualificationKeySha256>`. `D1AtomicQualificationReceipt` is exact `(qualificationKeySha256 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,ownerKeySha256 TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,identityJson TEXT NOT NULL,identitySha256 TEXT NOT NULL,qualificationJson TEXT NOT NULL,qualificationSha256 TEXT NOT NULL,qualifiedAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL,expiresAtMs INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(expiresAtMs=qualifiedAtMs+86400000))` with index `(expiresAtMs,qualificationKeySha256)`. Lowercase SHA, compact-canonical JSON, canonical identity equality, safe-integer times, owner token, generation, and objectPrefix grammar are enforced by the reviewed validator. BEFORE INSERT triggers on both journal and receipt abort unless combined journal plus receipt count is below 2048; the final transaction deletes its journal before inserting the replacing receipt. Before remote 0024 application, only the byte-identical idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`/index/trigger materialization of these three qualification tables and their singleton row is legal; every statement is independently schema-read back, and 0024 then validates/adopts those exact objects. No cleanup lease or migration attestation is bootstrapped early.

- Qualification reserves capacity and a unique generation before creating any qualification object. A current exact receipt replays after identity/hash validation without mutation. Otherwise recovery first prunes at most 100 expired receipts in `(expiresAtMs,qualificationKeySha256)` order. It then reads at most 100 expired journals in that order, validates each complete identity/generation/token/objectPrefix, drops only that generation's closed qualification objects, independently proves zero residue, and deletes only the matching expired journal. Reservation is one AtomicSqlPort batch: (1) CAS-update the singleton only at caller-read generation `G`, below combined capacity, and below generation exhaustion to set `nextReservationGeneration=G+1,generationClaimSha256=<claim>` RETURNING both; (2) `INSERT ... SELECT` the journal with generation G/token/prefix only while the singleton carries that claim, RETURNING its complete reservation; (3) clear that exact claim RETURNING id. A zero-row/mismatched result rolls back the batch and returns backpressure or generation exhaustion before program mutation. D1 statement serialization makes concurrent final-slot contenders single-winner. Every journal counts against the cap. Before every binding, atomic-failure/control, RETURNING setup/CAS, cleanup, and finalization batch, statement 1 renews only the unexpired matching key/generation/token to `updatedAtMs=nowMs,expiresAtMs=nowMs+900000` and RETURNING validates it; statement 2 is an abort guard that attempts to duplicate singleton id 1 only when that renewed live row is absent, so a lost/expired generation aborts the complete D1 batch before any DDL/DML. The canonical case payload follows those two control statements in the same atomic batch. Recovery of a live journal resumes only its exact ordinal/generation. The final guarded AtomicSqlPort batch drops owner-generation objects, deletes that journal, inserts the receipt, and returns the inserted receipt; rollback restores the journal and objects if receipt insertion fails. Response loss adopts only the independently reread exact receipt. A journal expires after 15 minutes from its last successful guarded renewal and is never deleted before its generation's closed objects are independently removed. Tests fill exact cap-minus-one/cap, race two reservations from the same G, suspend an old owner across expiry/cleanup/new generation, and crash at every renew/guard/cleanup/program/final boundary to prove no stale DDL, stranded object, prefix collision, or over-cap state.

- Exact `D1AtomicQualification` changes `targetEnvironment` to `'qa'|'production'`. `implementation` and `qa` owners require QA; `production_workflow` and `production_validation` owners require the exact production database identity and production target. `production_workflow` identity additionally includes explicit positive-safe-integer workflowRunId/runAttempt and literal jobName `deploy`, byte-equal to its immutable qualification artifact and workflow invocation. No production proof may cite QA or omit workflow identity. Qualification executes cases in order `binding-control`, `atomic-failure-0001` through `atomic-failure-0008`, `atomic-control`, `returning-cas-success`, `returning-cas-zero`; serialized `cases` remains BINARY-caseId sorted. Every case batch begins with the two generation/token renewal-and-abort-control statements above. `binding-control` then sends exact SQL `SELECT ?1 AS textValue,?2 AS integerValue,?3 AS realValue,?4 AS nullValue` with bindings `['spoonjoy-d1-atomic',900719925474099,3.25,null]`, expected columns in that order, and that one exact row. The canonical eight-statement atomic payload over the journal's quoted generation-qualified objectPrefix is:

  1. `CREATE TABLE "<q>_data"(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,textValue TEXT NOT NULL,integerValue INTEGER NOT NULL,realValue REAL NOT NULL,nullValue TEXT)`
  2. `CREATE INDEX "<q>_data_integer_text_idx" ON "<q>_data"(integerValue,textValue)`
  3. `CREATE TABLE "<q>_ledger"(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,dataId INTEGER NOT NULL,state TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(state IN ('inserted','attested')),FOREIGN KEY(dataId) REFERENCES "<q>_data"(id) ON DELETE CASCADE)`
  4. `CREATE TRIGGER "<q>_data_ai" AFTER INSERT ON "<q>_data" BEGIN INSERT INTO "<q>_ledger"(id,dataId,state) VALUES(NEW.id,NEW.id,'inserted'); END`
  5. `CREATE TABLE "<q>_attestation"(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,dataId INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE,proof TEXT NOT NULL,FOREIGN KEY(dataId) REFERENCES "<q>_data"(id) ON DELETE CASCADE)`
  6. `INSERT INTO "<q>_data"(id,textValue,integerValue,realValue,nullValue) VALUES(?1,?2,?3,?4,?5) RETURNING id,textValue,integerValue,realValue,nullValue` with `[7,'spoonjoy-d1-atomic',900719925474099,3.25,null]` and that exact row
  7. `INSERT INTO "<q>_attestation"(id,dataId,proof) VALUES(?1,?2,?3) RETURNING id,dataId,proof` with `[11,7,'attested']` and that exact row
  8. `SELECT d.id,d.textValue,d.integerValue,d.realValue,d.nullValue,l.id AS ledgerId,l.state,a.id AS attestationId,a.proof FROM "<q>_data" d JOIN "<q>_ledger" l ON l.dataId=d.id JOIN "<q>_attestation" a ON a.dataId=d.id WHERE d.id=?1` with `[7]` and exact joined values.

  Atomic control commits the two controls plus all eight payload statements, then independent schema/data readback reproduces them. Failure ordinal N replaces only payload statement N with exact guaranteed-failing `INSERT INTO "<q>_missing_NNNN"(id) VALUES(1)` and independent readback proves both control renewals and every earlier payload statement rolled back. Returning setup/CAS use their own guarded batches: setup creates `<q>_cas(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,value INTEGER NOT NULL)` and row `(7,41)` through independently read-back statements; success sends `UPDATE "<q>_cas" SET value=value+1 WHERE id=?1 AND value=?2 RETURNING id,value` with `[7,41]` and row `(7,42)`; zero sends the same bindings again, returns zero rows, and readback remains `(7,42)`. Every control/payload SQL byte, typed binding, expected-column vector, compiled REST SQL, wire string, result, generation/token identity, and independent readback enters the case hashes. The only interpolated identifiers are the closed validated journal objectPrefix/suffix set above.

- Production qualification statements are an isolated pre-rollout evidence boundary: they may mutate only generation-qualified temporary qualification objects plus permanent qualification journal/receipt rows, must end with zero temporary residue, and never change product schema/data or deploy a Worker. The field `firstProductionD1StatementAccepted` means the first non-qualification rollout/migration/catch-up D1 statement, never a qualification statement. Production rollout D1 is forward-only from that first accepted rollout statement. Before it, failure proves zero product-schema/data rollout mutation, while independently reporting qualification receipt state. Once any rollout statement is accepted or response-indeterminate, `databaseRollbackSupported:false`, no compensation/reopen/restore/old-Worker rollback is attempted, and recovery may only reread or resume the same idempotent migration journal, issue the one Worker deploy only when no prior or indeterminate Worker dispatch exists, or reconcile exact/already-canonical state. An already-canonical strict descendant may supersede a selected older source with zero mutation; a descendant requiring a new deploy requires a separately reviewed repair-successor release and cannot be deployed by this workflow. The older source is not required to deploy first. Divergent, ancestor, or unclassifiable state fails closed.

- Native ordering never compares raw timestamp text. Migration 0027 adds each source that exists at 0027 a VIRTUAL generated `nativeSortMs` using exact deterministic expression `CAST(strftime('%s',<timeColumn>) AS INTEGER)*1000+CASE WHEN substr(<timeColumn>,20,1)='.' THEN CAST(substr(<timeColumn>,21,3) AS INTEGER) ELSE 0 END`. Source-specific indexes are User `(nativeSortMs,id)`, Recipe `(chefId,nativeSortMs,id)`, Cookbook `(authorId,nativeSortMs,id)`, SavedRecipe `(userId,nativeSortMs,recipeId)`, ShoppingListItem `(shoppingListId,nativeSortMs,id)`, ShoppingListItemSyncTombstone `(authorId,nativeSortMs,resourceId)`, and NativeSyncTombstone `(accountId,nativeSortMs,resourceType,resourceId)`; the snapshot first resolves the account's ShoppingList id. Guards admit only canonical millisecond `Z`, millisecond `+00:00`, and SQLite `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, require a nonnull safe integer within years 0001..9999, and abort malformed source writes. Exact source rows are profile/User `(id,updatedAt,'profile')`, Recipe `(chefId,updatedAt,'recipe')`, Cookbook `(authorId,updatedAt,'cookbook')`, SavedRecipe `(userId,syncUpdatedAt,'savedRecipe')`, ShoppingListItem via its list author `(updatedAt,'shoppingItem')`, ShoppingListItemSyncTombstone `(authorId,updatedAt,'shoppingItem')`, and NativeSyncTombstone `(accountId,updatedAt,resourceType)`. RecipeInCookbook is a SavedRecipe compatibility writer rather than a feed row. RecipeStep, StepOutputUse, Ingredient, and every recipe-content table already present at 0027 has a permanent source-specific trigger that touches parent Recipe.updatedAt. RecipeTag is created later: 0028 atomically creates RecipeTag and its own parent-touch/account-clock trigger, and Unit24 owns that trigger's workerd proof. All profile, Recipe, Cookbook, membership/SavedRecipe, shopping, and tombstone old/current writers have permanent source-specific clock triggers. For accepted source time `T` and prior clock `C`, the trigger always stores clock `max(C,T)`; when `T<=C` it instead allocates `C+1` and writes that value to both source and clock, while when `T>C` it preserves the source time and advances the clock to T. Only the source-row rewrite may no-op for a future T; the clock advancement may not. Unit18 owns 0027 migration/workerd tests, Unit24 owns RecipeTag/0028, and Unit27.2 owns numeric cursor/feed tests. No generic compatibility epoch remains.

- Native schema-2 cursor bytes carry `{version:2,nativeSortMs,kind,resourceId}` in that field order; no undefined floor epoch exists. `nativeSortMs` is a safe integer in the admitted 0001..9999 instant domain, kind belongs to the schema-2 union, and resourceId is nonempty; malformed or noncanonical bytes fail before D1. The repaired `NativeSyncClock.lastIssuedAt` is the retained account high-water: it is parsed through the same strict timestamp-to-millisecond function, parse/range failure is an invariant error, and a cursor nativeSortMs greater than that parsed millisecond value returns the existing private `409 sync_reset_required`, while equal/lower proceeds. In one AtomicSqlPort snapshot batch, each query binds `(cursorMs,cursorKind,cursorId,limitPlusOne)` and uses its real owner predicate plus exact BINARY tuple: User `id=? AND (nativeSortMs,'profile' COLLATE BINARY,id COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`; Recipe `chefId=? AND (nativeSortMs,'recipe' COLLATE BINARY,id COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`; Cookbook `authorId=? AND (nativeSortMs,'cookbook' COLLATE BINARY,id COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`; SavedRecipe `userId=? AND (nativeSortMs,'savedRecipe' COLLATE BINARY,recipeId COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`; ShoppingListItem `shoppingListId=? AND (nativeSortMs,'shoppingItem' COLLATE BINARY,id COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`; ShoppingListItemSyncTombstone `authorId=? AND (nativeSortMs,'shoppingItem' COLLATE BINARY,resourceId COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`; NativeSyncTombstone `accountId=? AND (nativeSortMs,resourceType COLLATE BINARY,resourceId COLLATE BINARY)>(?,?,?)`. Each orders by the identical tuple and limits to `limit+1`; only the final bounded k-way merge happens in application. No query names `kindKey` or substitutes `accountId` for another table's owner column. Equal instants encoded by the three admitted text formats compare identically. Tests cover ties across literal/dynamic kinds, cursor deletion, page limits, old/current writer serialization, future-clock repair, and all malformed forms without a D1 call.

- `CookProjectionEpoch` is exact D1 table `(userId TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES User(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,epoch INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(epoch BETWEEN 0 AND 9007199254740991))`; migration and every User provisioning batch create epoch zero. Every projection request carries `expectedEpoch`, operation `UPSERT|DELETE`, and durable operation authority. In one AtomicSqlPort batch D1 validates epoch, applies the progress-free fence/index mutation, and if a terminal write makes more than 20 terminal rows, retains the newest 20 by `(terminalAt DESC,attemptId COLLATE BINARY DESC)`, deletes only losing terminal rows and matching fences, and increments epoch exactly once. `terminalAt` is completedAt for COMPLETED and abandonedAt for ABANDONED. ACTIVE rows are never capped, pruned, or hidden. Epoch exhaustion returns 503 before mutation. A mismatched request returns exact `{code:'stale_epoch',epoch}` with no write. For stale UPSERT, the DO refreshes and reprojects only a still-current matching attempt at that epoch; if no matching current attempt remains, it converts to/reissues the durable DELETE authority. For stale DELETE, the persisted purge/replacement/deletion queue item retains user/recipe/attempt/sequence and reissues DELETE at the refreshed epoch even when current state is absent; it is not removed until D1 confirms deletion/fence settlement. Response-loss replay, delayed old-epoch UPSERT/DELETE after purge, pruning ties, DO eviction, and concurrent terminal writes prove no progress in D1 and no surviving ghost ACTIVE row or missing active continuation.

- Anonymous storage uses global exclusive Web Lock `spoonjoy-cook-anonymous-quota:v2`. The realizable total acquisition order is lifetime client, cleanup, anonymous quota, user, recipe, with any unneeded lock skipped; the lifetime client lock remains outermost for its browsing context and is never acquired while another listed lock is held. Reverse or nested inversion is source-rejected. Every anonymous v1/v2 prefix scan, metadata-index rebuild, capacity check, write, and explicit discard occurs under the anonymous lock. An anonymous entry is exactly one localStorage key whose name begins `spoonjoy-cook-progress:` and has a nonempty suffix; v1/v2 share that entry identity. Entry count is the number of those keys. Aggregate bytes are `sum(TextEncoder(key).byteLength + TextEncoder(value).byteLength)` for every entry plus the same key+value byte count for sole hint key `spoonjoy-cook-anonymous-index:v2`; decision/authenticated-generation keys are charged to the authenticated-user budget, not double-counted here. The hint is compact canonical `{version:2,entries:[{key,valueSha256,bytes}]}` in BINARY key order, checksummed and fully rebuilt from the exact prefix scan after absence/corruption/crash. Replacement computes postwrite bytes by subtracting the old same-key contribution and old hint bytes, adding the new key/value and newly serialized hint; updating an existing entry does not consume another count. The 16-entry/2,097,152-byte cap accepts exact equality and rejects one byte/entry above before write. A malformed empty-suffix prefix key is preserved, marks the index corrupt, and blocks writes until explicit management; it is never silently ignored or evicted. At capacity, existing state stays usable, a new recipe cannot start, and desktop/mobile UI exposes explicit discard management; no nondefault state is evicted. Tests race tabs, delayed storage events, crash rebuild, exact equality, v1/v2 replacement, generation-bearing v2 values, quota errors, account switch, and unlock cleanup.

- Anonymous decision bytes include recipe generation. Exact `AnonymousCandidateDecision={version:1,storageVersion:'v1'|'v2',userNamespace,recipeNamespace,rawSha256,recordGeneration:number|null,userGeneration,recipeGeneration,choice:'pending'|'use'|'keep_server'|'replace_server'|'discard',mutationId:string|null,resolutionProofSha256:string|null,createdAt,updatedAt}` requires nonnegative safe generations and rechecks raw version/hash plus both generations under exclusive authority before every effect. QA and production additionally write exact no-extra-fields `AnonymousAdoptionLiveProof={version:1,scenarios:AnonymousAdoptionScenario[],scenarioSetSha256,passed:true}` as `anonymous-adoption-live.json`, separate from the 42 exchange count. `AnonymousAdoptionScenario={ordinal,storageVersion:'v1'|'v2',scenario:'use'|'keep'|'replace'|'discard'|'reload'|'account_switch'|'conflict'|'cleanup'|'delayed_writer',rawBeforeSha256:string|null,decisionBeforeSha256:string|null,authenticatedSnapshotSha256:string|null,rawPresentAfter:boolean,decisionPresentAfter:boolean,networkMutationCount,priorAccountDisclosureCount:0,passed:true}` has the literal matrix below; `sha` means one schema-valid lowercase SHA-256 derived from captured bytes, `null` is literal null, and v2 rows 10..18 repeat row semantics 1..9 with storageVersion v2. Every field/count must byte-equal the row before `passed:true`; no implied nullability or arbitrary nonnegative count is legal.

| ordinals | scenario | rawBefore | decisionBefore | authenticatedSnapshot | rawAfter | decisionAfter | networkMutations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| 1,10 | `use` | sha | sha | sha | false | false | 1 |
| 2,11 | `keep` | sha | sha | sha | false | false | 1 |
| 3,12 | `replace` | sha | sha | sha | false | false | 2 |
| 4,13 | `discard` | sha | sha | null | false | false | 0 |
| 5,14 | `reload` | sha | sha | null | true | true | 0 |
| 6,15 | `account_switch` | sha | sha | null | true | false | 0 |
| 7,16 | `conflict` | sha | sha | sha | true | true | 1 |
| 8,17 | `cleanup` | sha | sha | null | false | false | 0 |
| 9,18 | `delayed_writer` | null | null | null | true | false | 0 |

  Use clears only after adopted authenticated persistence; keep's one probe returns server_won and explicit keep clears with no second cook mutation; replace is one conflict probe plus one authorized replacement and clears only after authenticated persistence; discard/cleanup perform zero network mutation and clear; reload preserves raw/decision; account_switch preserves only account-neutral raw bytes and removes the departing userNamespace-bearing decision before exposing the new identity; conflict preserves raw/decision after its one server_won probe; delayed_writer begins with no raw key and recreates only raw account-neutral prompt state after the identity barrier, with no decision or authenticated send. Every row has `priorAccountDisclosureCount:0`; enumerating all post-switch keys/values must find no departing userNamespace, recipeNamespace, mutationId, or authenticated snapshot. `scenarioSetSha256` hashes the complete no-extra-fields ordinal array.

- Web shopping action intent is terminally bounded and owned entirely by Unit1.4. `accountNamespace` is the current opaque 64-lowercase-hex CookStorageIdentity userNamespace. The only intent key is exact `spoonjoy-shopping-intent:v1:<accountNamespace>:<actionInstanceId>`; its two components forbid delimiters, and the exact account prefix scan is the sole enumeration source. Exact `ShoppingActionIntent={version:1,accountNamespace,userGeneration,actionInstanceId,logicalAction,payloadSha256,clientMutationId,state:'prepared'|'dispatched'|'manual_reconcile',createdAt,dispatchedAt,expiresAt,lastAttemptAt}` uses distinct lowercase UUID-v4 action/mutation IDs, the closed shared-shopping-command logicalAction discriminator, lowercase SHA-256 payload bytes, canonical times, null dispatchedAt/lastAttemptAt only while prepared, and `expiresAt=createdAt+86400000ms`. At most 64 intents and 262144 compact UTF-8 bytes may exist per account, where bytes are the sum of each exact storage key and compact value; the account-generation lock serializes exact-prefix scan/create/dispatch/settle/retire, validates key/value namespace and action id equality, and computes replacement delta before write. Malformed keys/values block new writes and require explicit management; they are never silently ignored or evicted. Every server shopping mutation has a hard 30000ms request deadline and retains the existing atomic 24-hour ShoppingMutationReceipt. Private no-store `GET /api/shopping-list/mutations/<clientMutationId>` returns exact `{clientMutationId,state:'applied',outcomeSha256}` for a live matching current-user receipt or `{clientMutationId,state:'absent',outcomeSha256:null}`; malformed/cross-user input follows normal private auth/validation without revealing existence. A received success or application 4xx is definitive and removes the intent after canonical revalidation. Network loss, 5xx, process death, or unknown response retains dispatched and retries the same id only while now is before expiresAt. At or after equality it never retries and atomically becomes manual_reconcile. Exact `noInFlightAt=max(expiresAt,lastAttemptAt+60000ms)` dominates the server deadline. Before equality the UI may reload/status-check but cannot stop; at/after noInFlightAt it performs receipt-status read then a full canonical list reload. Applied settles from the receipt/reload; absent enables `Stop retrying`, whose explicit click removes the intent. A prepared never-dispatched intent may be cancelled. App-initiated logout cancels prepared intents and must settle or reach noInFlightAt/status/reload for dispatched/manual intents before submitting logout, otherwise it displays `Finish shopping sync before signing out.` and remains authenticated. Server-401/external-cookie account switch performs no retry under the new identity, deletes departing intent bytes under cleanup/generation fencing, and requires the next authenticated mount to reload canonical list state. Cap blocks new actions with management UI rather than eviction.

  The existing shopping projection file embeds exact `ShoppingIntentLiveProof={version:1,scenarios,scenarioSetSha256,passed:true}` without adding a semantic file. Scenarios are exactly `[prepared_cancel,success_settle,application_4xx_settle,response_loss_replay,expiry_manual_stop,account_switch_fence]` in that order with expected `(dispatchCount,retryCount,statusReadCount,reloadCount,intentPresentAfter,networkUnderNewIdentity)` equal respectively `(0,0,0,0,false,0)`, `(1,0,0,1,false,0)`, `(1,0,0,1,false,0)`, `(1,1,1,1,false,0)`, `(1,0,1,1,false,0)`, and `(1,0,0,1,false,0)`. Response-loss replay uses the same clientMutationId and the status result is applied; expiry status is absent and stop occurs only at/after noInFlightAt; account switch's reload is under the later rightful identity and sends no old mutation. Unit30 derives this proof from captured browser/storage/status/reload bytes, while Unit1.4 owns every state, equality, cap, reload, duplicate click, logout, account switch, and crash test.

- The deployed feature registry is exactly the following 42 rows. Shared `CookFeatureDefaultState`, `CookFeatureState`, Shopping projections, `NeutralMetadataProjection` with exact course `main`, NeutralScaleProjection, terminal row/barrier, sanitized IDs, identity observations, and request/response hash rules retain their latest exact definitions. Exact sanitized `PublicProfileProjection={profileUserId,recipeIds,cookbookIds,recentSpoonIds}` contains only schema-valid opaque public ids, with each array in rendered order; saved-recipe and private cook-session/history fields are structurally impossible. `FeatureMutationIds={legacySeedMutationId,legacyConflictMutationId,legacyReplaceMutationId,restCheckMutationId,restDeleteMutationId,restRestoreMutationId,savedPutMutationId,savedDeleteMutationId,historyCompleteMutationId}` contains nine pairwise-distinct lowercase UUID-v4 values. Cook rows' response column is the retained sanitized HTTP start projection only. Separate exact `FeatureLocalPostcondition={operationId,rawAnonymousPresent,decisionPresent,authenticatedRecordPersisted,priorAttemptId}` is stored in the adoption projection and never mixed into response bytes: row 2 is `(false,false,true,null)`, row 3 `(true,true,true,null)`, row 4 `(false,false,true,seedAttemptId)`.
