  Projection arrays are adoption 1-4, shopping 5-15, saved 16-27, tags 28-31, REST 32-33, MCP 34-35, and history 36-42. The shopping projection additionally embeds the exact ShoppingIntentLiveProof without adding an exchange or file. The identity index has exactly 42 exchange refs plus two boundary-health refs. Rows 20/23/38/39 prove zero private lookup through test-only instrumentation that is absent from live response bytes. The semantic set is now the prior eight files plus `anonymous-adoption-live.json`, BINARY-path sorted and hash-bound. The provisional fixture is exactly 10 files, final feature output exactly 22 files, and primary API+lifecycle+feature aggregate exactly 36 files; every stored count/hash is rederived from these bytes. No label-only row, implied prior row, hidden extra, or old 8/21/35 count is accepted.

- Production workflow uses separate script processes with an Actions artifact boundary. The implementation is `scripts/deploy-production.mjs`. Qualify runs exact `node scripts/deploy-production.mjs --phase qualify --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --source-tree-sha "$SOURCE_TREE_SHA" --workflow-run-id "$GITHUB_RUN_ID" --run-attempt "$GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT" --job-name deploy --out production-workflow-qualification.json`, executes only the isolated production qualification state machine, and exits before any production rollout/migration D1 statement or Worker dispatch. A distinct `actions/upload-artifact@v4` uploads immutable name `production-workflow-qualification`; a distinct download step downloads it to a fresh directory and hashes/parses it. Deploy runs exact `node scripts/deploy-production.mjs --phase deploy --source-sha "$SOURCE_SHA" --source-tree-sha "$SOURCE_TREE_SHA" --workflow-run-id "$GITHUB_RUN_ID" --run-attempt "$GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT" --job-name deploy --qualification-path <downloaded>/production-workflow-qualification.json --artifact-dir mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts`. Recovery is exact `--phase recover-forward` with the same source/tree/workflow/job/qualification/artifact operands. There is no rollback phase. Every phase validates positive safe workflow IDs/attempt, literal job, and equality with artifact/current Actions identity before capability use. Unit37 separately downloads artifact name `production-workflow-qualification` through logical name `release-qualification-artifact-download` before downloading `mcp-oauth-canary-artifacts` through `release-artifact-download`.

- Workflow outputs are truthful. Successful deploy/reconcile/already-canonical supersession writes the latest exact `ProductionWorkflowResult`; a successful workflow job requires it. Any failed qualify/upload/readback/migrate/deploy/verify/result-write/result-upload/recover-forward path instead writes exact `ProductionWorkflowFailure={version:1,outcome:'failed',taskSourceSha,taskTreeSha,workflowRunId,runAttempt,jobName:'deploy',phase:'qualify'|'qualification_upload'|'qualification_readback'|'migrate'|'deploy'|'verify'|'result_write'|'result_upload'|'recover_forward',failureCode:'credential'|'qualification'|'provider'|'migration'|'deployment'|'health'|'artifact'|'indeterminate',qualificationArtifactPath:string|null,qualificationArtifactSha256:string|null,firstProductionD1StatementAccepted:boolean,databaseRollbackSupported:false,forwardRecoveryRequired:boolean,observedStateSha256:string|null,failedAt}` to `production-workflow-failure.json` and uploads failure artifact `production-workflow-failure`. The boolean uses the non-qualification rollout boundary defined above. It never fabricates `production-workflow-result.json`. `forwardRecoveryRequired` equals firstProductionD1StatementAccepted or any indeterminate Worker/provider rollout dispatch. Recover-forward may resume the same journal or issue the sole Worker dispatch only when no prior/indeterminate dispatch exists; otherwise it is read-only and a required new descendant deploy becomes a repair-successor release.

- Workflow secret transport is exact. The qualify step's explicit credential/source/identity input set is `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`, `CLOUDFLARE_D1_API_TOKEN`, `SOURCE_SHA`, `SOURCE_TREE_SHA`, `GITHUB_RUN_ID`, and `GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT`; deploy/recover add only `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_API_TOKEN`. `GITHUB_RUN_ID`/attempt are public identity inputs, not secrets, and literal job travels only in argv. The synchronous bootstrap validates/copies the exact public identity and tokens into bounded closure capabilities, deletes all three Cloudflare variables and the two consumed GitHub identity variables from `process.env` before dynamic import, and passes business modules only validated identity, a D1 request capability, and Worker spawn capability. The D1 token is used only by the D1 REST closure. Exact Wrangler child environment is a sanitized allowlisted base plus `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` and `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<workers token>`; no D1 token, original Workers variable, or unrelated Actions environment reaches it. The parent clears closure references after request/spawn completion. Tests inspect dynamic-import environment, child env, logs, errors, crash artifacts, and `/proc`-style argv projections for leakage.

- Process authorization keeps existing artifact names. Historical Round101's rename to `run-preauthorization-receipt` is rejected. Existing `run-authorization-intent` and `run-authorization-receipt` paths/schemas remain byte-stable and mean only pre-dispatch eligibility. Existing immutable `runDispatchIntent` is the sole effect boundary. A dead/reaped authorization-only child may be replaced before dispatch; no child may be replaced, replayed, redelivered, or respawned after dispatch. Every earlier generic/PR/merge/QA clause permitting dispatch-host post-dispatch sequential replay is rejected inventory. After dispatch, exact-effect provider readback may only materialize; absence/indeterminate state waits/fails truthfully without a second child. Tests prove terminology cannot grant effect authority and at most one generation crosses dispatch.

- Final `ProcessSpec` and `ProcessCommand` key orders remain the Round101 displayed orders. `requiresQualifiedRemoteD1` is true for every direct or HTTP-mediated remote D1 mutator, including lifecycle/feature/visual cleanup, and false for qualifiers/read-only assertions/discovery. The qualifier subcommand is in the closed provider command set. All rows below use exact eight-field order `[environmentPolicy,outputPolicy,operationPhaseCapMs,totalTimeoutMs,recoveryPolicy,requiresQualifiedRemoteD1,storedArgv,operands]`; uppercase names are the existing compile-time sentinel enum, not runtime text. `O` is forbidden shorthand in stored data; the tables spell `RELEASE_OUT`. Final output policy grammar is only `logs`, `empty`, `json:<basename>:<schema>`, `ensure:<basename>:<schema>`, `api-smoke`, `lifecycle-smoke`, `feature-smoke`, `visual`, and `cleanup-reconcile`. `logs` permits only sanitized stdout/stderr Artifacts and no out payload; `ensure` owns intent/arm/dispatch plus one exact typed result; `api-smoke`, `lifecycle-smoke`, `feature-smoke`, and `visual` validate respectively the exact 1-, 13-, 22-, and 38-file latest closed sets; no `none` or `directory:*` policy exists.

- Final environment policy adds exact `cloudflare-deploy`, used only by `qa-deploy-ensure`. It combines the existing Cloudflare account/OAuth and D1 visibility preflight proofs; `ProcessPreflightSecret` has both cloudflareRestBearer and cloudflareD1ApiToken nonnull and githubToken null. Descriptor transport requires exactly OAuth fd6 plus D1 fd7, consumed/closed/unset before business import. The provider module receives only bounded D1 request and Worker-spawn capabilities, applies reviewed migrations through the qualified D1 capability, and may spawn exactly one child `wrangler deploy --env qa` with only OAuth mapped to its sanitized `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`; the D1 token never reaches Wrangler. Every other policy retains its prior secret nullability/channel set. Missing either proof/channel blocks before attempt creation.

- Executable coverage membership is exactly the doing document's BINARY-sorted eighteen pre-visual entries with `scripts/deploy-production.mjs`, plus only `scripts/production-visual-dogfood.mjs` for nineteen final. Any `scripts/deploy-production-canary.*` membership or file is historical and rejected.

- The exact closed `release-provider-command.mjs` subcommand union is `capture-deployments|qualify-d1-atomicity|ensure-qa-deploy|verify-health|verify-drift|ensure-pr|verify-protection|verify-checks|verify-review-comments|verify-merge-ready|merge-pr|discover-ci|discover-production|watch-workflow|verify-deploy-job|download-artifact|saved-recipes-catchup|freeze-production-stage|probe-production-deployment|production-readiness|continuation-scan`. Unknown, duplicate, package-aliased, loader-mediated, or runtime-discovered subcommands fail before environment consumption or output creation.
